idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/kelly-overbet-ruin-2026-07-17.md
question: Round-22 UNRELATED-domain closer (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated). Domain: information theory / mathematical finance ‚Äî growth-optimal capital allocation (Kelly 1956), a fleet-external pure-mechanism head. On a favorable repeated even-money bet (win prob p=0.6) where you stake a fixed FRACTION f of current wealth each round (win‚Üí√ó(1+f), lose‚Üí√ó(1‚àíf)), the arithmetic-mean (ensemble) wealth grows monotonically in f (E[mult]=1+f¬∑(2p‚àí1)), so a naive expected-value maximizer bets bigger ‚Äî but is the growth-optimal stake fraction strictly INTERIOR, and does the naive double-Kelly overbet f=2f* have NEGATIVE long-run growth (the typical gambler goes broke while the ensemble average balloons)? Pinned: SEED=20260717, P_WIN=0.6, N_PATHS=100000, T_ROUNDS=400, GRID_STEP=0.01, F_UNDER=0.02, F_OVER=0.40 (=2¬∑f*), F_MAX=0.90; derived f*=2p‚àí1=0.20 (analytic argmax of g(f)=p¬∑ln(1+f)+q¬∑ln(1‚àíf)), ruin boundary f_c=0.389391. APPROVE iff ALL: G1 overbet-trap ‚Äî g(f*)‚àíg(F_OVER) ‚â• 3œÉ AND g(F_OVER)<0<g(f*) (double-Kelly ruins); G2 interior ‚Äî g(f*) beats timid F_UNDER AND near-all-in F_MAX, each ‚â• 3œÉ; G3 anchor MATCH ‚Äî sim mean growth at f* vs closed form g(f*)=p¬∑ln(1+f*)+q¬∑ln(1‚àíf*), |z| < 3œÉ. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/kelly_overbet_ruin.py (random,math,json,hashlib). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): f*=0.20, g(f*)=0.020134 (closed 0.020136); G1 z=311.63œÉ (g(0.40)=‚àí0.002451<0), G2 z=519.58œÉ (vs f=0.02) / 2424.85œÉ (vs f=0.90), G3 |z|=0.06œÉ ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0; ergodicity gap at f=0.40: ensemble-mean wealth ‚âà10^13.4√ó after 400 rounds while median path = 0.375√ó and 55.9% of paths lose money; expected results-dict sha256=d6e489de505de1d44a24f0b5164aec83233d8a82fc864dfbb5356c2b75ad65a5.

## PROPOSAL 101 ¬∑ 2026-07-17T22:56:45Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 114, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/winners-curse-task-auction-2026-07-17.md
question: Round-23 FLEET-domain opener (rotation reopens at fleet: fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated). Domain: multi-agent fleet economics ‚Äî the winner's curse in a common-value auction (Capen-Clapp-Campbell 1971; Milgrom-Weber 1982). N agents compete to CLAIM one task of unknown COMMON value V; each sees an unbiased noisy private signal s_i=V+Uniform(-w,w) and the highest signal wins the claim. Is the naive "bid your own estimate" policy a value trap ‚Äî does the winner (the max-signal agent) earn NEGATIVE expected profit despite every signal being individually UNBIASED, because winning is evidence your estimate was the most upward-biased of the N ‚Äî and does shading every bid by the N-dependent order-statistic bias w*(N-1)/(N+1) restore break-even? Pinned: SEED=20260717, N_BIDDERS=8, W_NOISE=1.0, V_TRUE=10.0, N_AUCTIONS=200000, sweep N in {2,4,8,16}; derived shade*=w*(N-1)/(N+1)=0.777778, naive closed-form winner profit=-0.777778. APPROVE iff ALL: G1 winner's-curse ‚Äî naive mean winner profit < 0 at >=3œÉ (the winner systematically loses despite unbiased signals); G2 shading-cure ‚Äî the shaded policy beats naive by >=3œÉ AND is not significantly negative (mean_shaded >= -3*se, break-even or better); G3 anchor MATCH ‚Äî sim naive mean profit vs closed form -w*(N-1)/(N+1), |z|<3œÉ. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/winners_curse_task_auction.py (random,math,json,hashlib). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): shade*=0.777778, mean_naive=-0.778285 (se 0.000443), mean_shaded=-0.000250 (break-even, se 0.000445); G1 z=-1755.27, G2 z_vs_naive=1238.54, G3 |z|=1.14 ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0; curse deepens monotonically over the sweep N=2->16 (-0.333, -0.600, -0.778, -0.883; each matching its closed form -w*(N-1)/(N+1)); expected results-dict sha256=df79ddf35990ae250d096ada20a7f10d1e5320b2f83f49fc551d97415b98eb54.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 114 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 101 ‚Üí VERDICT 114). Offset pinned ‚Äî the +13 offset's row for P101 (immediate predecessor row P100 ‚Üí V113, "sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 100 ‚Üí VERDICT 113). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V114 for P101; then round-23 venture-slot P102"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P101/V114 offset line ‚Äî it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059 (proposal/verdict ledger only through P049/V059); the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER ‚Äî fleet-economics pure-mechanism head (common-value auction / order-statistic selection bias); verdict co-consumer = any fleet surface that selects among many noisy-but-unbiased estimates by the extreme (task dispatch to the cheapest/most-confident bid, lead routing to the most-confident agent, highest-confidence ensemble vote) ‚Äî the transferable correction is "the winning estimate is optimistic by an order-statistic gap that widens with the number of RIVALS; shade the winning commitment by ~E[max noise over N rivals] (or set a reserve / require independent corroboration), scaling with rival count not own-estimate quality." idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/kelly-overbet-ruin-2026-07-17.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (favorable even-money bet, P_WIN=0.6, stake a fixed FRACTION f of current wealth each round: win‚Üí√ó(1+f), lose‚Üí√ó(1‚àíf); T_ROUNDS=400, N_PATHS=100000), the growth-optimal stake fraction f*=2p‚àí1=0.20 is strictly INTERIOR (analytic argmax of g(f)=p¬∑ln(1+f)+q¬∑ln(1‚àíf)) and the naive double-Kelly overbet F_OVER=0.40 has NEGATIVE time-average growth (g(0.40)=‚àí0.002451 < 0), sitting just past the ruin boundary f_c=0.389391. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3, never softened (first-failing-gate None).
- G1 overbet-trap headline: PASS ‚Äî g(f*)=0.020134 > g(F_OVER=0.40)=‚àí0.002451, z=311.63œÉ ‚â•3œÉ, and g_over=‚àí0.002451 < 0 < g_star=0.020134 ‚Äî double-Kelly is a ruin trap, not merely lower growth.

- G3 analytic-anchor MATCH: PASS ‚Äî sim mean growth at f* vs closed form g(f*)=p¬∑ln(1+f*)+q¬∑ln(1‚àíf*)=0.020136, |z|=0.06œÉ <3œÉ ‚Äî the sim implements the process the closed form rests on.

## VERDICT 114 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-17T23:22:57Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P101 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 101 ¬∑ 2026-07-17T22:56:45Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the winner's curse in a common-value task auction: N agents compete to CLAIM one task of unknown COMMON value V, each sees an unbiased noisy private signal s_i=V+Uniform(‚àíw,w), and the highest signal wins the claim; the winner (the max-signal agent) earns NEGATIVE expected profit despite every signal being individually UNBIASED ‚Äî winning is evidence your estimate was the most upward-biased of the N ‚Äî and shading every bid by the N-dependent order-statistic bias w¬∑(N‚àí1)/(N+1) restores break-even.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/winners-curse-task-auction-2026-07-17.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (N_BIDDERS=8 agents compete to CLAIM one task of unknown COMMON value V_TRUE=10.0; each sees an unbiased noisy private signal s_i=V+Uniform(‚àíw,w), W_NOISE=1.0; the highest signal wins the claim; N_AUCTIONS=200000), the naive "bid your own estimate" policy earns NEGATIVE expected profit (mean_naive=‚àí0.778285 < 0) even though every signal is individually UNBIASED ‚Äî conditioning on winning selects the most upward-biased of the N estimators (order-statistic selection bias), so the winner is systematically the loser. ## PROPOSAL 102 ¬∑ 2026-07-17T23:24:36Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 115, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/growth-cash-trough-trap-2026-07-17.md
question: Round-23 VENTURE-domain proposal (rotation: fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; P101 opened fleet, this is the venture slot). Domain: startup unit-economics / cash-flow dynamics ‚Äî the growth cash-trough trap. Take TWO subscription ventures with IDENTICAL, POSITIVE per-customer unit economics (LTV=m/c > CAC). Vary ONLY the acquisition growth rate g. Because CAC is paid UPFRONT while margin m accrues over the customer's lifetime (churn c, survival s=1-c), does the faster grower have a strictly DEEPER cumulative-cash trough and higher ruin probability for equal starting capital ‚Äî and is there a closed-form critical rate g_crit=CAC*s/(CAC-m) above which per-period cash flow is PERMANENTLY negative despite every customer being profitable, so "positive LTV/CAC, scale hard" is a ruin trap driven by cash TIMING, not unit economics? Fluid-limit anchor E[active_k]=A0*(g^{k+1}-s^{k+1})/(g-s). Pinned: SEED=20260717, T=24, A0=10, M=10, c=0.10 (s=0.90), CAC=60, N_REPS=4000, K0=3000, growth sweep g in {1.05,1.08,1.15,1.30}; derived g_crit=1.080000, LTV=100, per-customer profit=40, cohort-0 truncated pi0(T)=32.023356. APPROVE iff ALL (order R1->R2->R3): R1 cash-trough trap ‚Äî mean trough(g_high=1.30) more negative than mean trough(g_low=1.05) by >=3œÉ; R2 identical positive unit economics (control) ‚Äî cohort-0 realized per-customer profit matches truncated analytic pi0(T) within 3œÉ in BOTH regimes, >0 by >=3œÉ in BOTH, and low-vs-high do NOT differ (|z|<3) ‚Äî proving the trough gap is TIMING/scale, not a unit-economics difference; R3 fluid anchor MATCH ‚Äî sim mean cumulative cash at each regime's fluid-trough period k* matches closed-form E[C_{k*}] within 3œÉ. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/venture-lab/growth_cash_trough_trap.py (random,math,json,hashlib). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): trough_low=-3064.51 (se 6.69), trough_high=-498627.20 (se 82.16) ‚Äî ~163x deeper; ruin_prob 0.533->1.000; R1 z=6011.93, R2 pi0-anchor |z|=0.66/0.83, positivity z=83.87/83.00, low-vs-high |z|=1.05, R3 |z|=0.89 (g_low k*=11) / 1.14 (g_high k*=23) ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; trough deepens monotonically over the sweep g=1.05->1.30 (-3064.51, -5071.33, -29062.01, -498627.20); expected results-dict sha256=5e6b4ce7cf3a58e6c5fa912ee5365ff4152c162818113320a8c2332195bc4d95.

## PROPOSAL 103 ¬∑ 2026-07-17T23:43:56Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 116, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/streak-shield-regression-2026-07-17.md
question: Round-23 GAME-domain proposal (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; P101 opened fleet, P102 took venture, this is the game slot). Domain: competitive-game rating dynamics / anti-tilt mechanic incidence ‚Äî the streak-shield regressive-amplification trap. A competitive game grants a "streak shield": after W consecutive true wins a player banks a shield that negates their next loss (the loss is scored as a WIN for rating/streak, a +2 rating swing). It is marketed as anti-tilt PROTECTION for STRUGGLING players. Claim under test: the shield is REGRESSIVE ‚Äî because length-W win-run frequency is monotone increasing in per-game win probability p, an already-high-skill cohort banks and spends more shields than the low-skill cohort it is sold to protect, so the mean rating UPLIFT (shield-on minus shield-off, paired on the SAME per-game outcomes) is strictly larger for the high-skill cohort. Pinned: SEED=20260717, N_PER_COHORT=10000, GAMES=200, STREAK_W=3, LOW_P=0.45, HIGH_P=0.55, SIGMA_GATE=3.0. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1‚ÜíG2): G1 regressive amplification ‚Äî high-cohort mean uplift exceeds low-cohort mean uplift by ‚â•3œÉ (œÉ = two-sample SE ‚àö(se_low¬≤+se_high¬≤)); G2 both-positive / strict ordering ‚Äî low-cohort mean uplift >0 AND high-cohort mean uplift > low-cohort mean uplift (the buff is positive-sum, the regressivity is in the derivative, not a sign flip). Design fix (one line): grant shields on LOSS streaks, not win streaks ‚Äî funds the safety net from the cold-run distress signal it targets, making it progressive. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/superbot-games/streak_shield_regression.py (random,math,json,hashlib; paired Monte Carlo, same draws scored shield-on/shield-off). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): low_uplift_mean=21.6436 (se 0.05745), high_uplift_mean=35.313 (se 0.068073), gap=13.6694 (~1.63√ó more protection to the high cohort); G1 uplift_gap_sigma=153.4585, G2 low>0 and high>low ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=0294f75799989decfe6c0166136691509239a91ac8eaba75d1932074022c09b4.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, both gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 116 is mirrored back.

Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V115 for P102; then round-23 game-slot P103"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P103/V116 offset line ‚Äî it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER beyond the design surface ‚Äî superbot-games competitive-balance / anti-tilt mechanic design (this head prices a rating-protection mechanic); verdict co-consumer = any competitive-game surface adding a loss-protection / MMR-decay / streak-forgiveness resource ‚Äî the transferable correction is "audit a protection mechanic by cohort-conditional incidence, not average benefit; if the resource is gated on a SUCCESS signal (win streak / high MMR / spend) the net flows uphill and widens the skill-to-rating gap it claims to close ‚Äî gate it on the DISTRESS signal instead (grant on a loss streak) to make the floor progressive." Grounding idea-engine@dc75cb3 ¬∑ pinned world above.

target: sim-lab (VERDICT 117, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/epidemic-overshoot-2026-07-17.md
question: Round-23 UNRELATED-domain closer (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; P101 opened fleet, P102 took venture, P103 took game, this closes the round at the unrelated slot). Domain: epidemiology ‚Äî Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick (1927) SIR final-size theory; a fleet-external pure-mechanism head, deliberately a DIFFERENT unrelated domain from the round-22 closer P100 (information theory / Kelly betting). The "overshoot past herd immunity" trap: the herd-immunity threshold h*=1‚àí1/R0 is the susceptible fraction at which transmission stops GROWING (R_eff falls to 1 ‚Äî the epidemic PEAK), but the epidemic does not stop there. The infections already in flight at that moment keep transmitting, so the final attack rate z* is the nonzero root of the final-size equation z=1‚àíe^{‚àíR0¬∑z}, which strictly EXCEEDS h* for every R0>1; the overshoot z*‚àíh* is the fraction infected UNNECESSARILY past the threshold. Claim under test: a stochastic SIR epidemic settles at z*‚âà0.893, not at the herd threshold h*=0.60, overshooting it by ‚âà0.29 (at R0=2.5). Pinned: SEED=20260717, R0=2.5, N_POP=5000, I0=1, N_EPIDEMICS=4000, MAJOR_CUTOFF=0.10, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; derived h*=0.60, z*=0.892645, overshoot=0.292645. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3): G1 overshoot headline ‚Äî mean final attack rate over major outbreaks z_sim exceeds h*=1‚àí1/R0 by ‚â•3œÉ AND z_sim>h* (the epidemic does NOT stop at herd immunity); G2 final-size anchor MATCH ‚Äî z_sim matches the closed-form root z* of z=1‚àíe^{‚àíR0¬∑z} within 3œÉ (|z|<3, reproduces Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick); G3 post-threshold burn ‚Äî mean fraction infected AFTER the susceptible pool first drops below 1/R0 is ‚â•3œÉ above 0 (the in-flight-transmission mechanism, not just the magnitude). Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/epidemic_overshoot.py (random,math,json,hashlib; stochastic Reed‚ÄìFrost chain-binomial SIR, exact geometric-gap Binomial sampler, major/minor split). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): n_major=3574/n_minor=426 (major_frac=0.8935 = branching major-outbreak prob for R0=2.5), z_sim=0.892632 (se 0.000101), overshoot_sim=0.292632; G1 z=2883.28œÉ, G2 |z|=0.13œÉ (z_sim=0.892632 vs closed z*=0.892645), G3 burn=0.194675 (se 0.000869) z=224.07œÉ ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=2ecda33ccab80be04904c973478093bb50a51cde8e5964da9d35e1ca4d340c3f.

## VERDICT 116 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-18T00:08:18Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P103 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 103 ¬∑ 2026-07-17T23:43:56Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the streak-shield regressive-amplification trap: a competitive game grants a "streak shield" that, after W=3 consecutive true wins, negates the player's next loss (the loss is scored as a WIN for rating/streak, a +2 rating swing), marketed as anti-tilt PROTECTION for STRUGGLING players ‚Äî but because length-W win-run frequency is monotone increasing in per-game win probability p, the already-high-skill cohort (p=0.55) banks and spends more shields than the low-skill cohort (p=0.45) it is sold to protect, so the mean rating UPLIFT (shield-on minus shield-off, paired on the SAME per-game draws) is strictly LARGER for the high cohort ‚Äî a mechanic that WIDENS the skill-to-rating gap it claims to close.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/streak-shield-regression-2026-07-17.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N_PER_COHORT=10000, GAMES=200, STREAK_W=3, LOW_P=0.45, HIGH_P=0.55, SIGMA_GATE=3.0), the high-skill cohort's mean rating uplift (35.313) strictly exceeds the low-skill cohort's (21.6436) by gap 13.6694 (~1.63√ó more protection to the already-strong cohort) at 153.4585œÉ ‚Äî the shield is regressive, its net benefit flowing UPHILL to the cohort it was not sold to protect. Both pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2 (proposal labels G1/G2), never softened (first-failing-gate None). regressive=true, passed=true, exit 0.
- G1 regressive amplification: PASS ‚Äî high-cohort mean uplift 35.313 (se 0.068073) exceeds low-cohort mean uplift 21.6436 (se 0.05745) by gap 13.6694 at uplift_gap_sigma=153.4585œÉ ‚â•3œÉ (œÉ = two-sample SE ‚àö(se_low¬≤+se_high¬≤)) ‚Äî length-W win-run frequency rises with p, so the strong cohort banks and spends more shields.
- G2 both-positive / strict ordering: PASS ‚Äî low-cohort mean uplift 21.6436 >0 AND high-cohort mean uplift 35.313 > low-cohort 21.6436 ‚Äî the buff is positive-sum, the regressivity is in the derivative (the cross-cohort gap), not a sign flip.

source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 104 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T00:00:45Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the epidemic-overshoot-past-herd-immunity trap: reaching the herd-immunity threshold h*=1‚àí1/R0 (the susceptible fraction at which R_eff falls to 1 ‚Äî the epidemic PEAK) does NOT stop the epidemic, because the infections already in flight keep transmitting, so the final attack rate z* is the nonzero root of the Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick final-size equation z=1‚àíe^{‚àíR0¬∑z}, which strictly EXCEEDS h* for every R0>1; on the pinned world (R0=2.5) a stochastic Reed‚ÄìFrost chain-binomial SIR settles at z_sim‚âà0.893 ‚Äî NOT at h*=0.60 ‚Äî overshooting it by ‚âà0.29 (~29% of the population infected UNNECESSARILY past the threshold), with the mean post-threshold burn ‚â•3œÉ above 0; the transferable correction is to size a cutoff/quota/kill-switch/intervention target by the SETTLING fixed point z*, not the growth-stops threshold h* ‚Äî budget for the overshoot.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/epidemic-overshoot-2026-07-17.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, R0=2.5, N_POP=5000, I0=1, N_EPIDEMICS=4000, MAJOR_CUTOFF=0.10, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; derived h*=1‚àí1/R0=0.60, closed-form final-size root z*=0.892645, overshoot*=0.292645), a stochastic Reed‚ÄìFrost chain-binomial SIR does NOT stop at the herd-immunity threshold h*=0.60 ‚Äî the infections already in flight at the peak keep transmitting, so the mean final attack rate over major outbreaks settles at z_sim=0.892632 (se 0.000101), overshooting h* by ‚âà0.29 (~29% of the population infected unnecessarily past the threshold). ## PROPOSAL 105 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T00:33:24Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 118, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/delegation-loop-collapse-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-24 FLEET-domain opener (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; this opens round-24 at the fleet slot). Domain: multi-agent governance / coordination economics ‚Äî transitive proxy voting ("liquid democracy") modelled as a uniform random mapping, deliberately DISTINCT from prior fleet slots (winner's-curse task auction P101, Braess selfish-routing P092). The "delegation loop collapse" trap: N agents each delegate their vote to ONE uniformly-random peer (self = voting directly), so the delegation graph is a random mapping (Flajolet‚ÄìOdlyzko 1990) ‚Äî every chain ends in a cycle, only cyclic nodes decide, and each weakly-connected component is one voting bloc. Uniform random delegation "feels" egalitarian, but the random-mapping statistics bite: the effective electorate COLLAPSES to ~‚àö(œÄN/2)=O(‚àöN) cyclic deciders, one accidental component (the giant) captures a Œò(N) share of all voting weight (asymptotic constant ‚âà0.7582), and the cycle controlling that bloc is only O(‚àöN) agents ‚Äî a sub-5% clique. Claim under test: individually-rational one-hop uniform delegation silently concentrates ‚â•30% of all voting weight into a sub-5% clique while shrinking the effective electorate below 10%, purely from graph structure (no preferential attachment, no coordination). Pinned: SEED=20260717, N=4096, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0; theory anchors cyclic_expected_frac=‚àö(œÄN/2)/N=0.019583, largest_component_frac_asymptotic=0.7582. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3): G1 electorate collapse ‚Äî mean(cyclic/N) ‚â§ 0.10 by ‚â•3œÉ (only an O(‚àöN) minority decides); G2 power concentration ‚Äî mean(largest bloc/N) ‚â• 0.30 by ‚â•3œÉ (one accidental component holds a Œò(N) weight share); G3 controlling clique ‚Äî mean(core cycle/N) ‚â§ 0.05 by ‚â•3œÉ (the cycle controlling that bloc is a sub-5% clique). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/‚àöTRIALS), the P104 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/delegation_loop_collapse.py (random,math,json,hashlib; one O(N) functional-graph pass extracting cyclic nodes, weakly-connected blocs, and each bloc's core cycle). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): G1 mean_cyclic_frac=0.01967 (se 0.000495) z=162.4318œÉ, G2 mean_largest_bloc_frac=0.748063 (se 0.009785) z=45.7889œÉ, G3 mean_core_cycle_frac=0.010834 (se 0.000419) z=93.4923œÉ ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=c3e5e6c15bee39165e3ccfb3967135c06df2ccfd60af7d727b918cda47d49ea6.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 118 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 105 ‚Üí VERDICT 118). Offset pinned ‚Äî the +13 offset's row for P105 (immediate predecessor row P104 ‚Üí V117, "sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 104 ‚Üí VERDICT 117). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V118 for P105; then round-24 venture-slot P106"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P105/V118 offset line ‚Äî it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER ‚Äî fleet-domain governance mechanism (liquid-democracy random mapping); verdict co-consumer = any fleet surface that routes decisions/tasks/trust one hop to "whoever seems best" (escalation chains, proxy approvals, trust delegation) ‚Äî the transferable correction is "uniform one-hop delegation is NOT egalitarian: it collapses the electorate to O(‚àöN) cyclic deciders and hands a Œò(N) weight share to a sub-5% controlling clique; cap chain depth or forbid cycles (bounded delegation) and measure electorate/concentration recovery." Grounding idea-engine@22b7cfc ¬∑ pinned world above.

## PROPOSAL 106 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T00:53:58Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 119, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/retention-survivorship-mirage-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-24 VENTURE-domain proposal (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; P105 opened round-24 at the fleet slot, this is the venture slot). Domain: subscription/SaaS churn-cohort unit economics ‚Äî the retention-survivorship mirage. A subscriber base is a STATIC MIXTURE of a loyal segment (share w_A, constant monthly retention r_A) and a churny segment (w_B, constant r_B < r_A); every customer's hazard is constant for all time, nobody ever gets stickier. Claim under test: the OBSERVED cohort month-over-month retention R(t)=(w_A¬∑r_A^{t+1}+w_B¬∑r_B^{t+1})/(w_A¬∑r_A^t+w_B¬∑r_B^t) ‚Äî a survivor-weighted average R(t)=p_A(t)¬∑r_A+(1‚àíp_A(t))¬∑r_B with the surviving-loyal mix p_A(t) strictly increasing to 1 ‚Äî RISES monotonically with tenure toward max(r_A,r_B), so a firm that changes NOTHING watches its cohort retention curve climb, purely because the churny segment burns off first (mover-stayer / unobserved-heterogeneity / shifted-beta-geometric); reading the rising curve as real stickiness/cohort-quality improvement is a survivorship-bias mirage. Pinned: SEED=20260717, N=200000, T=24, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0, T_LATE=12, W_A=0.5 (N_A=100000/N_B=100000), R_A=0.97, R_B=0.55, LIFT_MIN=0.05; closed-form anchors R_cf(0)=0.760000, R_cf(12)=0.969537, lift_cf=0.209537, asymptote 0.97. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3): G1 survivorship lift (headline) ‚Äî mean observed lift R_obs(T_LATE)‚àíR_obs(0) ‚â• LIFT_MIN=0.05 by ‚â•3œÉ (the curve strictly rises); G2 individual-constant control ‚Äî tenure-pooled within-segment observed retention matches the constant r_A AND r_B within 3œÉ (|z|<3 for BOTH), proving no customer's hazard rises with tenure so G1's lift is 100% composition/mixing; G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ‚Äî mean observed R_obs(t) reproduces the mixture formula R_cf(t) at t=0 and t=T_LATE within 3œÉ (|z|<3 each). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/‚àöTRIALS), the P104/P105 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/venture-lab/retention_survivorship_mirage.py (random,math,json,hashlib; count-level cohort Monte Carlo advancing per-segment alive counts by Binomial survival). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): G1 lift_mean=0.209617 (se 5.2e-05) z=3079.6379œÉ, G2 pooled_A=0.969995 |z|=0.7328 / pooled_B=0.549955 |z|=0.8081 (both <3), G3 R_obs(0)=0.759975 vs 0.76 |z|=0.5941 / R_obs(12)=0.969593 vs 0.969537 |z|=1.672 (both <3) ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=1dd09b8d06cfbd0155e5822f60f5c1fbf091bf192d4395e503c4f889dd6856d6.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 119 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 106 ‚Üí VERDICT 119). Offset pinned ‚Äî the +13 offset's row for P106 (immediate predecessor row P105 ‚Üí V118, "sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 105 ‚Üí VERDICT 118). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V119 for P106; then round-24 game-slot P107"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P106/V119 offset line ‚Äî it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER ‚Äî venture-economics pure-mechanism head (subscription churn-cohort survivorship / duration-analysis heterogeneity); verdict co-consumer = any operator surface that reads a cohort metric computed over an attriting population (retention curves, actives-only ARPU, engagement-of-remaining, seasoning default rates) ‚Äî the transferable correction is "a rising aggregate cohort metric under attrition is the DEFAULT signature of a static heterogeneous base, not evidence of behavioural improvement; measure the metric WITHIN homogeneous segments (or fit a heterogeneity model) and forecast off segment hazards, not the survivorship-inflated late-tenure aggregate." Grounding idea-engine@a0564e3 ¬∑ pinned world above.

## PROPOSAL 107 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T01:12:18Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 120, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/energy-cap-overflow-forfeiture-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-24 GAME-domain proposal (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; P105 opened round-24 at the fleet slot, P106 took venture, this is the game slot). Domain: free-to-play energy/stamina pacing economics ‚Äî the energy-cap overflow-forfeiture tax. A stamina bar regenerates 1 unit per period up to a cap C; players log in at a Poisson rate (inter-login gaps iid Exponential mean Œº) and spend on login, so the regen banked between logins is min(gap, C) and the overflow max(0, gap‚àíC) that accrues while the bar is full is silently FORFEITED. Claim under test: the fraction of all regenerated stamina a player can actually SPEND is the exact closed form U(Œº)=E[min(G,C)]/E[G]=1‚àíe^{‚àíC/Œº} ‚Äî a CONCAVE, saturating function of the cap ‚Äî so (a) raising the cap yields exponentially diminishing usable stamina once C~Œº (doubling the cap barely helps), and (b) the overflow-forfeited fraction W(Œº)=e^{‚àíC/Œº} rises monotonically with the login gap Œº, so the busy/casual player the cap is sold to "protect" forfeits the LARGEST fraction while the always-online player captures nearly all of it; the cap converts absence into permanent forfeiture and is throughput-limited by login frequency, not by the cap. On the pinned world a frequent player (Œº=60) captures ~86% of regen while a casual player (Œº=480) captures only ~22%, forfeiting ~78%. Pinned: SEED=20260717, C=120, MU_FREQUENT=60, MU_CASUAL=480, N_GAPS=5000, TRIALS=400, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, WASTE_MIN=0.70; closed-form anchors U_frequent=1‚àíe^{‚àí2}=0.864665, U_casual=1‚àíe^{‚àí0.25}=0.221199, W_frequent=0.135335, W_casual=0.778801. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3): G1 casual-forfeiture headline ‚Äî casual (Œº=480) mean overflow-forfeited fraction W_casual ‚â• WASTE_MIN=0.70 by ‚â•3œÉ (the cap forfeits most of a busy player's regen); G2 frequency-regressivity control ‚Äî casual waste fraction strictly exceeds the frequent (Œº=60) player's by ‚â•3œÉ (two-sample se), so the tax is on ABSENCE (W‚àùe^{‚àíC/Œº}), not a cap-independent constant; G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ‚Äî mean usable fraction U_sim matches the exact 1‚àíe^{‚àíC/Œº} within 3œÉ (|z|<3) for BOTH scenarios, proving G1 is 100% the overflow mechanism, not a sampling artifact. Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/‚àöTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/superbot-games/energy_cap_overflow_forfeiture.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-scenario Monte Carlo of exponential login gaps, bank min(gap,C) and forfeit overflow, ratio-of-sums usable/waste fraction). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): U_frequent=0.864359 (se 0.000296) / U_casual=0.221325 (se 0.000135); G1 W_casual=0.778675 (se 0.000135) z=581.5691œÉ (‚â•0.70), G2 W_casual 0.778675 vs W_frequent 0.135641 z=1973.299œÉ, G3 U_frequent |z|=1.0304 / U_casual |z|=0.9307 (both <3) ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=2e30020af5a97661506c29c962d047fb9561948098b09f5a826084b013f3ce29.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 120 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 107 ‚Üí VERDICT 120). Offset pinned ‚Äî the +13 offset's row for P107 (immediate predecessor row P106 ‚Üí V119, "sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 106 ‚Üí VERDICT 119). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V120 for P107; then round-24 unrelated-slot P108"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P107/V120 offset line ‚Äî it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER ‚Äî game-economy pure-mechanism head (free-to-play energy/stamina-pacing overflow forfeiture / renewal-reward theory); verdict co-consumer = any operator surface that meters a regenerating resource against a discard-on-overflow cap consumed at irregular intervals (energy/stamina/lives bars, rate-limit token buckets, accrued-credit/points caps, PTO/leave accrual caps, retry/quota buckets) ‚Äî the transferable correction is "a discard-on-overflow cap makes the USABLE fraction of a regenerating budget 1‚àíe^{‚àíC/Œº} of the amount generated ‚Äî concave in the cap and decreasing in the consumer's inter-arrival gap Œº ‚Äî so raising the cap saturates fast and the forfeiture is a regressive tax on infrequent/absent consumers; measure the cadence-conditional usable fraction, and bank overflow into an uncapped reserve (or scale the cap to realized cadence) to make throughput rate-limited, not arrival-frequency-limited." Grounding idea-engine@36f5a6f ¬∑ pinned world above.

## PROPOSAL 108 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T01:33:34Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 121, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/regression-to-mean-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-24 UNRELATED-domain closer (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; P105 opened round-24 at the fleet slot, P106 took venture, P107 took game, this closes the round at the unrelated slot). Domain: statistics / experimental design / measurement ‚Äî Galton's regression to the mean (1886); a fleet-external pure-mechanism head, deliberately a DIFFERENT unrelated domain from the round-22 closer P100 (information theory / Kelly betting) AND the round-23 closer P104 (epidemiology / SIR final-size). The "spurious treatment effect" trap: take a noisy repeated measure ‚Äî a pre score X and a post score Y of the SAME unit with test-retest reliability rho<1 (so (X,Y) is standard bivariate normal with correlation rho, E[Y|X=x]=rho¬∑x) and NOTHING done between the reads; select the "underperformers" (units with X below a fixed cutoff c=‚àí1 SD), apply an INERT intervention, and re-measure. The selected group's mean IMPROVES, strongly and reproducibly, purely from regression to the mean ‚Äî their extreme-low X was partly luck that does not repeat, so Y drifts back toward the mean. Claim under test: the apparent before/after improvement of the selected underperformers is exactly Delta*=(rho‚àí1)¬∑E[X|X<c]>0 (with E[X|X<c]=‚àíphi(c)/Phi(c) the inverse Mills ratio), a large hundred-sigma "effect" from an intervention that does nothing, while the WHOLE population shows zero change (no time trend) and the over-performer top tail shows a symmetric spurious DECLINE. On the pinned world (rho=0.5, c=‚àí1) E[X|X<c]=‚àí1.525135 and the spurious improvement is +0.762568 SD. Pinned: SEED=20260717, RHO=0.5, C_THRESH=‚àí1.0, N_UNITS=20000, TRIALS=400, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, IMPROVE_MIN=0.50; derived m_sel=E[X|X<c]=‚àí1.525135, Delta*=(rho‚àí1)¬∑m_sel=0.762568, top-tail Delta_top*=‚àí0.762568. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3): G1 spurious-improvement headline ‚Äî the selected underperformers' mean improvement Delta_sel ‚â• IMPROVE_MIN=0.50 by ‚â•3œÉ AND Delta_sel>0 (an inert intervention on the bottom tail shows a large significant "effect"); G2 selection-necessity control ‚Äî the whole-population before/after change Delta_pop is within 3œÉ of 0 (|z|<3), so there is no global time trend and G1 is 100% selection-on-extremes + regression, not drift; G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ‚Äî Delta_sel matches the exact Galton prediction Delta*=(rho‚àí1)¬∑E[X|X<c] within 3œÉ (|z|<3), reproducing the regression coefficient so the effect IS the RTM mechanism. Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/‚àöTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/regression_to_mean.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-trial Monte Carlo of standard bivariate-normal repeated measures Y=rho¬∑X+‚àö(1‚àírho¬≤)¬∑Z, select X<c with NO intervention, Phi via math.erf ‚Äî no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): m_sel=‚àí1.525135, Delta*=0.762568; Delta_sel=0.762986 (se 0.000842) / Delta_pop=4.5e-05 (se 0.000372) / Delta_top=‚àí0.764375 (se 0.000738); G1 z=312.4059œÉ (‚â•0.50), G2 |z|=0.1204 (<3), G3 |z|=0.4969 (<3) ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=35d65a5060d0582a5fd0d041c2d72635657c7f8c62ac024d4ee004b285ed8aed.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 121 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 108 ‚Üí VERDICT 121). Offset pinned ‚Äî the +13 offset's row for P108 (immediate predecessor row P107 ‚Üí V120, "sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 107 ‚Üí VERDICT 120). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V121 for P108; then round-25 fleet-slot P109"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P108/V121 offset line ‚Äî it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER ‚Äî statistics/measurement pure-mechanism head (Galton regression to the mean / selection-on-extremes illusion); verdict co-consumer = any operator surface that judges an intervention by the before/after change of a group SELECTED for being extreme on a noisy metric (bottom-decile remediation, worst-performer triage, seasoning/vintage effects, "we fixed the worst-performing X and it improved") ‚Äî the transferable correction is "a selected-because-extreme group regresses toward the mean by (1‚àírho) of its selection gap, manufacturing a spurious before/after shift of exactly (rho‚àí1)¬∑E[X|selected]; use a randomized control (or the unselected population) as the counterfactual, or subtract the regression prediction before crediting the intervention ‚Äî never the naked before/after delta." Grounding idea-engine@a2c7162 ¬∑ pinned world above.

ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, C=120, MU_FREQUENT=60, MU_CASUAL=480, N_GAPS=5000, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0, WASTE_MIN=0.70; closed-form anchors U(Œº)=1‚àíe^{‚àíC/Œº}: U_frequent=0.864665, U_casual=0.221199, W_casual=0.778801, W_frequent=0.135335), a stdlib Monte-Carlo over exponential login gaps (bank min(gap,C), forfeit overflow, ratio-of-sums usable/waste fraction) confirms the tax: a frequent player captures ~86% of regen while a casual/busy player captures only ~22%, forfeiting ~78%. - G2 frequency-regressivity: PASS ‚Äî mean W_casual=0.778675 vs mean W_frequent=0.135641 separated by z=1973.299œÉ ‚â•3œÉ ‚Äî the forfeiture e^{‚àíC/Œº} is a regressive tax on absence: the casual cohort wastes ~5.7√ó the fraction the frequent cohort (Œº=60) does, so the cap is login-frequency-throughput-limited, not cap-limited.
- G3 closed-form anchor: PASS ‚Äî sim vs exact U(Œº)=1‚àíe^{‚àíC/Œº}: U_frequent sim 0.864359 vs anchor 0.864665 |z|=1.0304, U_casual sim 0.221325 vs anchor 0.221199 |z|=0.9307 (both <3) ‚Äî the Monte-Carlo usable-fraction means match the concave saturating law to within noise, proving G1 is 100% the overflow mechanism, not a sampling artifact.

## PROPOSAL 109 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T01:54:38Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 122, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/correlated-fleet-variance-floor-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-25 FLEET-domain opener (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; round-24 is complete on the proposal side through P108, so the rotation reopens at the fleet slot and this opens round-25). Domain: multi-agent estimation / wisdom-of-crowds coordination economics ‚Äî the correlated-fleet variance floor, deliberately DISTINCT from prior fleet slots (common-value auction winner's curse P101, liquid-democracy random mapping P105, sequential information cascades P064, Braess selfish routing P092). The trap: a fleet of N agents each estimates a common truth Œ∏ (WLOG Œ∏=0) with error Œµ_i=‚àöœÅ¬∑C+‚àö(1‚àíœÅ)¬∑Z_i ‚Äî a SHARED common-mode draw C~N(0,œÉ¬≤) (identical for every agent: same config, upstream, prior, dataset) plus an idiosyncratic Z_i~N(0,œÉ¬≤) iid ‚Äî so every agent's error has variance œÉ¬≤ and every pair has correlation œÅ. The naive "wisdom of crowds" reading says the fleet mean cuts the error variance to œÉ¬≤/N‚Üí0. It does NOT: the common-mode term does not average away, so the fleet-mean error variance is the exact equicorrelation identity MSE_N(œÅ)=œÅ¬∑œÉ¬≤+(1‚àíœÅ)¬∑œÉ¬≤/N, which FLOORS at œÅœÉ¬≤ as N‚Üí‚àû, and the effective independent count N_eff=œÉ¬≤/MSE_N saturates at 1/œÅ. Claim under test: on the pinned world (œÅ=0.25, N=400) a fleet of 400 correlated agents has MSE‚âà0.2519 (floor œÅœÉ¬≤=0.25) ‚Äî worth only ~3.97 independent ones (asymptote 1/œÅ=4) ‚Äî while independence predicts œÉ¬≤/N=0.0025, so ~99% of the promised variance reduction never materializes and scaling the fleet past N‚âà1/œÅ buys essentially no precision. Pinned: SEED=20260717, SIGMA_ERR=1.0 (œÉ¬≤=1), RHO=0.25, N_FLEET=400, TRIALS=400, BATCH=200, SIGMA=3.0; closed-form anchors MSE_corr=œÅœÉ¬≤+(1‚àíœÅ)œÉ¬≤/N=0.251875, MSE_indep=œÉ¬≤/N=0.0025, N_eff_cf=3.970223 (asymptote 1/œÅ=4.0). APPROVE iff ALL (order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3): G1 correlated-floor headline ‚Äî the correlated-fleet mean MSE ‚â• FLOOR=0.10 by ‚â•3œÉ (the aggregate error floors ~100√ó above the 0.0025 independence predicts, bounded away from 0 despite N=400); G2 correlation-necessity control ‚Äî the SAME fleet size with œÅ=0 (no shared component) collapses to œÉ¬≤/N=0.0025 within 3œÉ (|z|<3), proving G1's floor is 100% the common-mode correlation, not fleet size or a sim artifact; G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ‚Äî the correlated MSE reproduces the exact equicorrelation variance œÅœÉ¬≤+(1‚àíœÅ)œÉ¬≤/N within 3œÉ (|z|<3), so the effect IS the equicorrelation mechanism. Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/‚àöTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/correlated_fleet_variance_floor.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-trial Monte Carlo drawing one shared common-mode C and N EXPLICIT idiosyncratic errors per fleet realization, averaging them so the 1/N reduction emerges ‚Äî no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): G1 mean_mse_corr=0.250281 (se 0.001198) z=125.4097œÉ (‚â•0.10), G2 mean_mse_indep=0.002502 (se 1.3e-05) vs 0.0025 |z|=0.1469 (<3), G3 mean_mse_corr=0.250281 vs 0.251875 |z|=1.3306 (<3), N_eff(sim)=3.995517 ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=77ab05b0c61ce782d90f51a747cd4a96f5445f4df10f982a506f346a60513903.

Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V122 for P109; then round-25 venture-slot P110"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P109/V122 offset line ‚Äî it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER ‚Äî multi-agent-estimation pure-mechanism head (equicorrelated-error aggregation / wisdom-of-crowds diversification limit); verdict co-consumer = any fleet surface that averages or votes over many agents/replicas/models to beat noise while those units share an error source (replicas on one stale cache, monitor quorums on one upstream, model ensembles sharing a training prior or feature, retry fan-out hitting one failing dependency, poller pools on one clock) ‚Äî the transferable correction is "a fleet with residual pairwise error-correlation œÅ has an aggregate MSE that floors at œÅœÉ¬≤ and an effective independent count N_eff=1/[œÅ+(1‚àíœÅ)/N]‚Üí1/œÅ, so budget precision by N_eff (measure œÅ), decorrelate the shared input/config/prior before scaling the count, and stop adding agents past N‚âà1/œÅ ‚Äî the count grows but the precision does not." Grounding idea-engine@d20e881 ¬∑ pinned world above.

## VERDICT 121 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-18T02:10:10Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P108 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 108 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T01:33:34Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the regression-to-the-mean selection illusion: take a noisy repeated measure (a pre score X and a post score Y of the SAME unit with test-retest reliability rho=0.5, so (X,Y) is standard bivariate normal with E[Y|X=x]=rho¬∑x, and NOTHING done between the reads), select the "underperformers" (units with X below a fixed cutoff c=‚àí1 SD), apply an INERT intervention, and re-measure; the selected group's mean IMPROVES by exactly Delta*=(rho‚àí1)¬∑E[X|X<c]=+0.762568 SD ‚Äî a hundred-sigma "effect" from an intervention that does nothing ‚Äî purely from regression to the mean (their extreme-low X was partly luck that does not repeat, so Y drifts back toward the mean by the (1‚àírho) fraction), while the WHOLE population shows zero change (no time trend) and the over-performer top tail shows a symmetric spurious DECLINE; the transferable correction is that a group SELECTED for being extreme on a noisy metric regresses toward the mean by (1‚àírho) of its selection gap, manufacturing a spurious before/after shift of exactly (rho‚àí1)¬∑E[X|selected] ‚Äî use a randomized control (or the unselected population) as the counterfactual, or subtract the regression prediction before crediting the intervention, never the naked before/after delta.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/regression-to-mean-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, RHO=0.5, C_THRESH=‚àí1.0, N_UNITS=20000, TRIALS=400, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, IMPROVE_MIN=0.50; derived m_sel=E[X|X<c]=‚àíphi(c)/Phi(c)=‚àí1.525135, Delta*=(rho‚àí1)¬∑m_sel=+0.762568, top-tail Delta_top*=‚àí0.762568), a stdlib Monte-Carlo over standard bivariate-normal repeated measures Y=rho¬∑X+‚àö(1‚àírho¬≤)¬∑Z (select X<c with NO intervention, Phi via math.erf) confirms the illusion: the selected underperformers' mean IMPROVES by ~0.763 SD purely from regression to the mean, the whole population shows no change, and the top tail declines symmetrically. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 spurious-improvement: PASS ‚Äî Delta_sel=0.762986 (se 0.000842) ‚â• floor IMPROVE_MIN 0.50 by z=312.4059œÉ ‚â•3œÉ AND Delta_sel>0 ‚Äî an INERT intervention on the bottom tail (X<‚àí1 SD) shows a large, significant "effect" (closed-form anchor +0.762568; the sim mean sits right on it).
- G2 selection-necessity control: PASS ‚Äî the whole-population before/after change Delta_pop=4.5e-05 (se 0.000372) is within 3œÉ of 0 by |z|=0.1204 (<3) ‚Äî there is NO global time trend, so G1 is 100% selection-on-extremes + regression to the mean, not drift.
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ‚Äî Delta_sel=0.762986 vs the exact Galton prediction Delta*=(rho‚àí1)¬∑E[X|X<c]=0.762568 |z|=0.4969 (<3) ‚Äî the Monte-Carlo improvement matches the regression coefficient to within noise, proving the effect IS the RTM mechanism (not merely the inequality). Top tail Delta_top=‚àí0.764375 (se 0.000738), the symmetric spurious decline.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/fleet/regression_to_mean.py ‚Äî random, math, json, hashlib; per-trial Monte Carlo of standard bivariate-normal repeated measures Y=rho¬∑X+‚àö(1‚àírho¬≤)¬∑Z, select X<c with NO intervention, Phi via math.erf ‚Äî no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source, blob e3340104dc2f2ab630b3fb842088dc981dd58369, file sha256 ffd91dd4c5abf957915c2700b27e157f718fa8b6dfb18989a67c4870490e71c2; diff exit 0; reproduced deterministically across two fresh python3 invocations, stdout + results-json byte-identical run-to-run, exit 0). NOTE: like P104/P107 (and unlike P105), the P108 results dict DOES carry a results_sha256 self-field ‚Äî the verifier computes digest=sha256(json.dumps(results,sort_keys=True,separators=(",",":"))) BEFORE inserting results["results_sha256"]=digest (main() lines 174‚Äì176), so the preimage is the compact canonical blob of the results dict with the results_sha256 field OMITTED (field-omission step required; resolved against the script text, not assumed). ## PROPOSAL 110 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T02:12:57Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 123, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/discount-breakeven-trap-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-25 VENTURE-domain proposal (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; P109 opened round-25 at the fleet slot, this is the venture slot). Domain: retail/DTC promotional-pricing unit economics ‚Äî the discount-depth breakeven trap, deliberately DISTINCT from prior venture slots (referral-bonus value trap P098, growth cash-trough trap P102, retention-survivorship mirage P106). The trap: a price cut of depth d on a product with gross margin m = (P0‚àíC)/P0 sets the promo unit margin to P0(m‚àíd) ‚Äî every point of discount comes one-for-one off the unit margin because cost C is fixed ‚Äî so holding gross profit constant requires a unit-volume UPLIFT of exactly u*(d)=m/(m‚àíd)‚àí1=d/(m‚àíd). This breakeven uplift is CONVEX and increasing in d, with a vertical asymptote at d=m: for any d‚â•m no finite volume breaks even (selling at/below cost). Under an explicit demand curve (exponential willingness-to-pay D(P)=e^{‚àíŒªP}, point elasticity ŒªP) the REALIZED uplift from P0 to P0(1‚àíd) is e^{ŒªP0d}‚àí1 and the exact gross-profit ratio is GP(d)/GP(0)=e^{ŒªP0d}¬∑(m‚àíd)/m; the discount is profit-neutral only when the realized uplift factor e^{ŒªP0d} equals the breakeven factor m/(m‚àíd), so below the breakeven elasticity Œª*=ln(m/(m‚àíd))/(P0¬∑d) the SAME discount destroys profit and above it helps. Claim under test: on the pinned INELASTIC world (m=0.40, d=0.20, elasticity ŒªP0=0.5) a "reasonable" 20%-off promotion needs +100% units to break even, realizes only ~+10.5%, and DESTROYS ~45% of gross profit (GP ratio e^{0.1}¬∑0.5=0.5526) even though unit sales rise ‚Äî reading higher promo volume as success is a margin-dilution mirage. Pinned: SEED=20260717, N=200000, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0, P0=1.0, MARGIN=0.40 (cost 0.60), DISCOUNT=0.20 (price 0.80), LAMBDA_LO=0.5 (inelastic), LAMBDA_HI=5.0 (elastic control), EROSION_MIN=0.10; nested-buyer count sampling n_disc~Binomial(N,e^{‚àíŒªP_disc}), n_full~Binomial(n_disc,e^{‚àíŒªP0d}); closed-form anchors breakeven uplift d/(m‚àíd)=1.000000, realized uplift (inelastic) e^{0.1}‚àí1=0.105171, GP ratio (inelastic) 0.552585, GP ratio (elastic) 1.359141, breakeven table u*(0.10)=0.333333/u*(0.20)=1.000000/u*(0.30)=3.000000. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3): G1 margin-dilution erosion (headline) ‚Äî mean gross-profit erosion 1‚àíGP(d)/GP(0) on inelastic demand ‚â• EROSION_MIN=0.10 by ‚â•3œÉ (a discount that raises units still destroys a large fraction of gross profit); G2 elasticity-necessity control ‚Äî the SAME discount at the SAME margin on ELASTIC demand (Œª_HI) has mean GP ratio > 1 by ‚â•3œÉ (the same promotion RAISES gross profit), proving G1's erosion is the margin/elasticity breakeven, not discounting per se or a sim artifact ‚Äî flip the elasticity across the breakeven and the sign of the effect flips; G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ‚Äî the mean simulated GP ratio reproduces e^{ŒªP0d}¬∑(m‚àíd)/m for BOTH curves and the realized inelastic uplift reproduces e^{ŒªP0d}‚àí1 within 3œÉ (|z|<3 each). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/‚àöTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/venture-lab/discount_breakeven_trap.py (random,math,json,hashlib; count-level nested-buyer Monte Carlo over an exponential-WTP population ‚Äî no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): G1 mean erosion 0.447445 (se 2.6e-05) z=13287.66œÉ (‚â•0.10), G2 mean GP ratio (elastic) 1.362147 (se 0.001446) z=250.42œÉ (>1), G3 |z|=1.17 (inelastic GP ratio) / 2.08 (elastic GP ratio) / 1.17 (realized uplift) ‚Äî all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=fd870ec6310b687a0a7ad966773404db56401b882116239c9803b405ce927ee0.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 123 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 110 ‚Üí VERDICT 123). Offset pinned ‚Äî the +13 offset's row for P110 (immediate predecessor row P109 ‚Üí V122, "sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 109 ‚Üí VERDICT 122). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V123 for P110; then round-25 game-slot P111"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P110/V123 offset line ‚Äî it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER ‚Äî retail/DTC promotional-pricing pure-mechanism head (discount breakeven / margin dilution); verdict co-consumer = any surface running depth-based discounts, coupons, or promotional price cuts and judging them by unit volume or top-line revenue ‚Äî the transferable correction is "a discount of depth d on gross margin m needs a unit-volume uplift of exactly d/(m‚àíd) (convex, impossible for d‚â•m) just to hold gross profit, so size promotions against the breakeven uplift for the actual margin and measured elasticity ‚Äî reserve deep cuts for SKUs whose elasticity clears Œª*=ln(m/(m‚àíd))/(P0¬∑d), and treat 'units went up' as the null expectation of any discount, not proof it paid." Grounding idea-engine@d445094 ¬∑ pinned world above.

## VERDICT 122 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-18T02:21:40Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P109 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 109 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T01:54:38Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the correlated-fleet variance floor: a fleet of N agents each estimates a common truth Œ∏ (WLOG Œ∏=0) with error Œµ_i=‚àöœÅ¬∑C+‚àö(1‚àíœÅ)¬∑Z_i, a SHARED common-mode draw C~N(0,œÉ¬≤) (identical for every agent: same config, upstream, prior, dataset) plus an idiosyncratic Z_i~N(0,œÉ¬≤) iid, so every agent's error has variance œÉ¬≤ and every pair has correlation œÅ; naive "wisdom of crowds" says averaging N estimates cuts the error variance to œÉ¬≤/N‚Üí0, but the common-mode term never averages away, so the fleet-mean MSE is the exact equicorrelation identity MSE_N(œÅ)=œÅœÉ¬≤+(1‚àíœÅ)œÉ¬≤/N which FLOORS at œÅœÉ¬≤ as N‚Üí‚àû and the effective independent count N_eff=œÉ¬≤/MSE saturates at 1/œÅ ‚Äî on the pinned world (œÅ=0.25, N=400, œÉ=1) the floor is 0.251875 while independence predicts 0.0025, so 400 correlated agents are worth only ~3.97 independent ones and ~99% of the promised variance reduction never materializes; the transferable correction is that correlated errors cap a fleet's precision at N_eff‚Üí1/œÅ regardless of headcount, so diversify the common-mode (independent upstreams/configs/priors) rather than scaling the fleet past N~1/œÅ.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/correlated-fleet-variance-floor-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, RHO=0.25, N_FLEET=400, SIGMA_ERR=1.0 so œÉ¬≤=1, TRIALS=400, BATCH=200, SIGMA=3.0; closed-form anchors MSE_corr=œÅœÉ¬≤+(1‚àíœÅ)œÉ¬≤/N=0.251875, MSE_indep=œÉ¬≤/N=0.0025, N_eff_cf=3.970223 ‚Üí asymptote 1/œÅ=4.0), a stdlib Monte-Carlo over œÅ-equicorrelated N-agent fleets (each realization: a shared common-mode C plus n idiosyncratic Z_i averaged EXPLICITLY over the n agents, fleet-mean error E=‚àöœÅ¬∑C+‚àö(1‚àíœÅ)¬∑mean(Z), average E¬≤ over BATCH realizations per trial) confirms the floor: a fleet of 400 correlated agents has MSE‚âà0.25 ‚Äî worth only ~3.97 independent ones ‚Äî while independence promises 0.0025, so ~99% of the promised variance reduction never materializes. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 correlated-floor headline: PASS ‚Äî mean(MSE_corr)=0.250281 (se 0.001198) ‚â• FLOOR=0.10 by z=125.4097œÉ ‚â•3œÉ ‚Äî the fleet-mean MSE of 400 correlated agents floors at œÅœÉ¬≤, nowhere near the œÉ¬≤/N=0.0025 independence promises; the common-mode term does not average away.
- G2 correlation-necessity control: PASS ‚Äî the SAME 400-agent fleet at œÅ=0 collapses to œÉ¬≤/N: mean(MSE_indep)=0.002502 (se 1.3e-05) vs anchor 0.0025, |z|=0.1469 (<3) ‚Äî with no shared component the full 1/N wisdom-of-crowds reduction is recovered, so G1's floor is 100% the common-mode correlation, not fleet size or a sim artifact.
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ‚Äî mean(MSE_corr)=0.250281 vs the exact equicorrelation formula œÅœÉ¬≤+(1‚àíœÅ)œÉ¬≤/N=0.251875, |z|=1.3306 (<3) ‚Äî reproduces the equicorrelation identity, and the effective independent count N_eff(sim)=3.995517 lands on the theory N_eff_cf=3.970223 ‚Üí asymptote 1/œÅ=4.0.

## VERDICT 119 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-18T02:33:41Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P106 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 106 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T00:53:58Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the retention-survivorship mirage: a subscriber base is a STATIC MIXTURE of a loyal segment (share w_A, constant monthly retention r_A=0.97) and a churny segment (w_B=1‚àíw_A, constant r_B=0.55<r_A) where every customer's hazard is constant for all time and nobody ever gets stickier, yet the OBSERVED cohort month-over-month retention R(t)=(w_A¬∑r_A^{t+1}+w_B¬∑r_B^{t+1})/(w_A¬∑r_A^t+w_B¬∑r_B^t) ‚Äî a survivor-weighted average R(t)=p_A(t)¬∑r_A+(1‚àíp_A(t))¬∑r_B with the surviving-loyal mix p_A(t) strictly rising to 1 ‚Äî climbs monotonically with tenure toward max(r_A,r_B)=0.97 (0.760‚Üí0.9695 over 12 months) purely because the churny segment burns off first, so a firm that changes NOTHING watches its cohort retention curve rise; reading that rising aggregate as real stickiness/cohort-quality improvement is a survivorship-bias mirage (mover-stayer / unobserved-heterogeneity / shifted-beta-geometric); the transferable correction is that a rising aggregate cohort metric under attrition is the DEFAULT signature of a static heterogeneous base, so measure the metric WITHIN homogeneous segments (or fit a heterogeneity model) and forecast off segment hazards, not the survivorship-inflated late-tenure aggregate.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/retention-survivorship-mirage-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N=200000 customers/cohort, T=24 months, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0, T_LATE=12, W_A=0.5 ‚Üí N_A=100000/N_B=100000, R_A=0.97, R_B=0.55, LIFT_MIN=0.05; closed-form anchors R_cf(0)=0.760000, R_cf(12)=0.969537, lift_cf=0.209537, asymptote max(r_A,r_B)=0.97), a stdlib count-level cohort Monte-Carlo ‚Äî advancing per-segment alive counts by Binomial survival ‚Äî confirms the mirage: the aggregate cohort month-over-month retention curve strictly RISES with tenure (R_obs 0.759975 at t=0 ‚Üí 0.969593 at t=T_LATE=12), climbing toward the loyal-segment asymptote 0.97 while no customer's hazard ever changes. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.

## VERDICT 123 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-18T02:39:20Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P110 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 110 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T02:12:57Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the discount-depth breakeven trap: a price cut of depth d (fraction off the full price P0) on a product with gross margin m=(P0‚àíC)/P0 sets the promo unit margin to P0(m‚àíd) ‚Äî because unit cost C is fixed, every point of discount comes one-for-one off the unit margin ‚Äî so holding gross profit constant requires a unit-volume UPLIFT of exactly u*(d)=m/(m‚àíd)‚àí1=d/(m‚àíd), CONVEX and increasing with a vertical asymptote at d=m (for any d‚â•m no finite volume breaks even, you are selling at or below cost); under an explicit exponential willingness-to-pay curve D(P)=e^{‚àíŒªP} (point elasticity ŒªP) the realized uplift from P0 to P0(1‚àíd) is e^{ŒªP0d}‚àí1 and the exact gross-profit ratio is GP(d)/GP(0)=e^{ŒªP0d}¬∑(m‚àíd)/m, so the discount pays only above the breakeven elasticity Œª*=ln(m/(m‚àíd))/(P0¬∑d) ‚Äî below it the SAME discount destroys profit; on the pinned INELASTIC world (m=0.40, d=0.20, elasticity ŒªP0=0.5) a "reasonable" 20%-off promo needs +100% units to break even, realizes only ~+10.5%, and DESTROYS ~45% of gross profit (GP ratio e^{0.1}¬∑0.5=0.5526) even though unit sales rise ‚Äî reading higher promo volume as success is a margin-dilution mirage; the transferable correction is to size promotions against the convex breakeven uplift d/(m‚àíd) for the actual margin and MEASURED elasticity, reserve deep cuts for SKUs whose elasticity clears Œª*, and treat "units went up" as the null expectation of any discount, not proof it paid.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/discount-breakeven-trap-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N=200000, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0, P0=1.0, MARGIN=0.40 so cost C=0.60, DISCOUNT=0.20 so P_disc=0.80, LAMBDA_LO=0.5 inelastic, LAMBDA_HI=5.0 elastic control, EROSION_MIN=0.10; closed-form anchors gp_ratio_lo=0.552585, gp_ratio_hi=1.359141, realized_uplift_lo=0.105171, breakeven_uplift d/(m‚àíd)=1.000000, breakeven table u*(0.10)=0.333333 / u*(0.20)=1.000000 / u*(0.30)=3.000000), a stdlib count-level nested-buyer Monte-Carlo over an exponential-WTP population (n_disc~Binomial(N, e^{‚àíŒªP_disc}), n_full~Binomial(n_disc, e^{‚àíŒªP0d}), gross profit GP=n¬∑unit_margin at each price) confirms the trap: on inelastic demand a 20%-off promo raises unit sales ~+10.5% yet destroys ~44.7% of gross profit (GP ratio 0.552555), while the SAME discount at the SAME margin on elastic demand RAISES gross profit ~+36% (GP ratio 1.362147) ‚Äî the sign flips across the elasticity breakeven. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 margin-dilution erosion (headline): PASS ‚Äî mean(1‚àíGP_ratio) on inelastic demand = 0.447445 (se 2.6e-05) ‚â• EROSION_MIN=0.10 by z=13287.6583œÉ ‚â•3œÉ ‚Äî a discount that RAISES units still destroys ~45% of gross profit; higher promo volume is not success.
- G2 elasticity-necessity control: PASS ‚Äî the SAME discount at the SAME margin on ELASTIC demand (Œª_HI=5.0) has mean GP ratio = 1.362147 (se 0.001446) > 1 by z=250.4225œÉ ‚Äî the identical promotion RAISES gross profit, so G1's erosion is the margin/elasticity breakeven, not discounting per se or a sim artifact; flip the elasticity across Œª* and the sign of the effect flips.
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ‚Äî the mean simulated GP ratios reproduce e^{ŒªP0d}¬∑(m‚àíd)/m for BOTH curves and the realized inelastic uplift reproduces e^{ŒªP0d}‚àí1 within 3œÉ: z_ratio_lo=1.1706 (sim 0.552555 vs CF 0.552585), z_ratio_hi=2.0785 (sim 1.362147 vs CF 1.359141), z_uplift_lo=1.1706 (sim 0.105110 vs CF 0.105171) ‚Äî all |z|<3, and the breakeven table reproduces u*(d)=d/(m‚àíd) EXACTLY (0.333333 / 1.000000 / 3.000000).
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/venture-lab/discount_breakeven_trap.py ‚Äî random, math, json, hashlib; count-level nested-buyer Monte-Carlo over an exponential-WTP population, no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source, blob 6afc0177ac56b959ae3b8bd6c2de8061fca2985d, file sha256 dae00e3128d8b02283f655c61f7ad62ef248fda9d9038361a7d7989efe53f80d; diff exit 0; reproduced deterministically across two fresh python3 invocations, stdout byte-identical run-to-run, plus an in-process double run() identical, exit 0). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = fd870ec6310b687a0a7ad966773404db56401b882116239c9803b405ce927ee0 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex) ‚Äî verified two ways: the script-printed sha256: line, and an independent recompute of json.dumps(sort_keys=True,compact) over the results dict. NOTE the digest posture: like P105/P109 (and unlike P104/P107/P108 which carry a results_sha256 SELF-field requiring the P104-style field-omission step), P110 hashes the results dict AS-IS and prints the digest as a sibling stdout `sha256:` line with NO self-field inside results ‚Äî so NO field-omission is needed (resolved against the script text main(), not assumed). All three gate sigmas match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=13287.6583œÉ, G2 z=250.4225œÉ, G3 z_ratio_lo=1.1706 / z_ratio_hi=2.0785 / z_uplift_lo=1.1706); means match (G1 erosion 0.447445, G2 GP ratio elastic 1.362147, G3 GP ratio inelastic 0.552555 / realized uplift 0.105110); verifier exits 0, all_pass=true. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114‚ÄìV122, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ‚Äî and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a discount of depth d on gross margin m needs a unit-volume uplift of exactly d/(m‚àíd) (convex, impossible for d‚â•m) just to hold gross profit, so size promotions against the breakeven uplift for the actual margin and measured elasticity ‚Äî reserve deep cuts for SKUs whose elasticity clears Œª*=ln(m/(m‚àíd))/(P0¬∑d), and treat "units went up" as the null expectation of any discount, not proof it paid.
