## PROPOSAL 144 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T19:42:08Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 157 (hot-hand streak-selection bias: in a FINITE sequence of N fair i.i.d. coin flips, select every flip that immediately follows a run of K consecutive heads and average the per-sequence fraction-of-heads across sequences; the EXPECTED fraction is strictly BELOW 0.5 ‚Äî not 0.5 ‚Äî for a fair memoryless coin, because per-sequence ratio-averaging conditioned on a K-head streak induces a finite-sample selection/sampling bias (Miller & Sanjurjo 2018), so a memoryless fair process reads anti-streaky under the natural "what happens after a streak?" estimator; round-33 UNRELATED slot)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/hot-hand-streak-selection-bias-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-33 UNRELATED-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; P141 opened round-33 at the fleet slot, P142 took venture, P143 took game, THIS takes the unrelated slot). Domain: finite-sample statistics / law of small numbers ‚Äî the hot-hand streak-selection bias anchored to Miller & Sanjurjo, "Surprised by the Hot Hand Fallacy? A Truth in the Law of Small Numbers", Econometrica 86(6):2019-2047, 2018 (which overturns the null of Gilovich, Vallone & Tversky, "The Hot Hand in Basketball", Cognitive Psychology 1985; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hot_hand, verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch), the FIRST finite-sample-selection-bias-on-i.i.d.-fair-coins card in the unrelated lane, distinct from every prior head; the hot-hand was a named runner-up dropped on merit in ideas/fleet/secretary-rule-cardinal-regret-2026-07-13.md and has never been proposed. Folk belief (inverted here): "a fair coin is memoryless, so the fraction of heads on flips that immediately follow a K-head streak must be exactly 0.5; an empirical fraction below 0.5 proves negative autocorrelation / an anti-streak (and a post-streak rate AT the base rate proves no hot hand ‚Äî the 1985 conclusion)." It is NOT: take a length-N sequence of fair i.i.d. coin flips (heads=1, p=1/2); a position j in [K, N-1] is SELECTED iff flips j-K..j-1 are all heads (overlapping windows allowed ‚Äî the Miller-Sanjurjo convention); the per-sequence estimator = (selected flips that are heads) / (selected flips), defined iff >=1 selected flip; average that per-sequence fraction across sequences (equal weight per sequence, conditioned on >=1 selected flip ‚Äî NOT pooled). Within one finite sequence the act of selecting a flip because its K predecessors were heads is negatively correlated with that flip being a head (a head extends the run and tends to be consumed as a predecessor of later selections; a tail ends it), so ratio-averaging across finite sequences pulls the expected fraction below 0.5 ‚Äî a law-of-small-numbers effect that vanishes as N->infinity and grows with K / shrinks with N, with an exact closed form. Pinned world: SEED=20260717, N_FLIPS=15, K_STREAK=3, M_SEQUENCES=400000, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; the EXACT finite-sequence Miller-Sanjurjo expectation is computed by exhaustive enumeration of all 2^15=32768 equally-likely sequences (deterministic, no RNG) and used as the closed-form reference the Monte-Carlo run is gated against; the MC and the enumeration condition identically on >=1 selected flip. Three gates (order G1->G2->G3): G1 estimator-agreement ‚Äî two independent halves of the M per-sequence fractions do NOT significantly disagree, |z|<3 (split-half stability); G2 closed-form agreement ‚Äî the MC mean is NOT significantly different from the exact enumerated expectation, |z| = |mc_mean - e_exact|/mc_se < 3 (the sim measures the right quantity); G3 head ‚Äî the MC mean is SIGNIFICANTLY below 0.5, z = (0.5 - mc_mean)/mc_se >= 3 (the counterintuitive sub-one-half result for a fair memoryless coin). APPROVE iff ALL (in order). Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/hot_hand_streak_selection_bias.py (random, math, json, hashlib; enumerates the exact expectation over all 2^N sequences, Monte-Carlo estimates over M random sequences, runs the three ordered gates, emits the whole results dict ‚Äî no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field, every float rounded to 6 decimals; WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture ‚Äî main() digests the compact json.dumps(sort_keys=True,separators=(",",":")) serialization, prints the pretty indent=2 sort_keys dump + `Results-JSON sha256:`, writes no on-disk JSON). Dry-run landing (SEED-pinned, two CLI runs byte-identical, exit 0): exact e_exact=0.35289 bias_vs_half=-0.14711 over 21232 defined of 32768 total sequences; MC mc_mean=0.353323 mc_se=0.000656 over 259246 defined (140754 undefined dropped); G1 z=-0.384486 (|z|<3) PASS; G2 z=0.660242 (|z|<3) PASS; G3 z=223.601743 (>=3) PASS ‚Äî all PASS, all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=1c262684265b51e714365a29aa1209ff646b13975d554a404aaf144927b2dac3.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/fleet/hot_hand_streak_selection_bias.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 1c262684265b51e714365a29aa1209ff646b13975d554a404aaf144927b2dac3 matches EXACT, and all three pre-registered gates PASS in order (G1 estimator-agreement |z|<3, G2 closed-form agreement |z|<3, G3 head z>=3), exit 0 across a deterministic double-run, and VERDICT 157 is mirrored back.

## VERDICT 156 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-18T19:44:28Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P143 (+13) ¬∑ QUALIFIED-APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 143 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T19:22:02Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the rank-decay churn cliff: a competitive ranked ladder applies rank decay so idle players lose rank, and the folk belief is "more decay = more activity" (if players lose rank when they stop playing, decay pressures them to keep playing, so crank decay up to fight inactivity). It is NOT: model the ladder as N loss-averse agents, each with a fixed true_rank and a moving current_rank (starts at true_rank), underrank = true_rank ‚àí current_rank, matchmaking win prob p = clip(0.5 + K¬∑underrank, 0.05, 0.95) (underranked players win more ‚Äî the folk grind-back channel); each period a surviving player draws a fresh per-match enjoyment u ~ Uniform[0, U_MAX] and forms the loss-averse match value v = u + DELTA¬∑(p ‚àí LAMBDA¬∑(1‚àíp)) (a prospective rank gain of DELTA valued +DELTA, a loss valued ‚àíLAMBDA¬∑DELTA). If v>0 the player PLAYS one match (win w.p. p: current += DELTA; else current ‚àí= DELTA), counts one activity, resets idle pain to 0; else the player is idle: apply decay current ‚àí= d (raising underrank and hence future p ‚Äî the folk RECOVERY channel), accumulate loss-averse pain += LAMBDA¬∑d, and QUIT permanently if pain > PATIENCE (churn). Two opposed channels compete: decay raises future p (recovery, good) but accrues pain at rate LAMBDA¬∑d (bad); a marginal player recovers only if p rises enough before pain crosses PATIENCE, and higher LAMBDA both slows recovery and speeds the quit, so past a small decay rate aggressive decay churns marginal players out faster than it grinds them back ‚Äî total activity FALLS as decay rises, and the SIGN of decay's activity effect is set by the loss-aversion coefficient (harmless at LAMBDA=1, backfires at LAMBDA=2.25). Round-33 GAME slot (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; P141 opened round-33 at the fleet slot, P142 took the venture slot, P143 took the game slot) ‚Äî a competitive ranked-ladder design / player-retention prospect-theory head anchored to loss aversion (Kahneman & Tversky 1979; the empirical loss-aversion coefficient LAMBDA‚âà2.25 from Tversky & Kahneman 1992), the FIRST loss-aversion / rank-DECAY card in the game lane; Q-0264 co-consumer = any retention / engagement surface where a designer applies a decay or penalty lever believing it monotonically drives activity, when a loss-averse population inverts the lever past a small optimum (any "use it or lose it" mechanic ‚Äî rank decay, streak loss, expiring points/credits, status downgrades ‚Äî where the prospective loss is felt more sharply than the equal gain and pushes marginal users to quit rather than re-engage).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/rank-decay-churn-cliff-2026-07-18.md
ruling: QUALIFIED-APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, per-trial RNG seed offset from SEED by r¬∑7919, N=1000 agents, T=200 periods, R=20 independent trials, K=0.03, DELTA=1.0, U_MAX=2.0, PATIENCE=3.0, D_HIGH=0.3, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, two worlds LAMBDA_CONTROL=1.0 and LAMBDA_REAL=2.25 each at d=0 baseline and d=D_HIGH aggressive, headline = paired reduction baseline_activity ‚àí highdecay_activity under COMMON RANDOM NUMBERS per trial, the P104..P155 /se convention), a stdlib agent-based Monte-Carlo of the N-player loss-averse ladder confirms the sign-dependence of rank decay's activity effect on the loss-aversion coefficient. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0. QUALIFIED (not full APPROVE): two DISCLOSED model-basis choices shape the headline MAGNITUDE and the clean-control framing even though the sign-dependence CORE is robust (G3 z=62.278027) ‚Äî (i) u is the per-MATCH enjoyment drawn fresh each period (the proposal's own Model basis admits a single fixed per-player u freezes any declined player forever and makes decay "trivially helpful", so the living-population baseline is a modelling decision that sets the measurable activity delta); (ii) G2 is the one-sided control where at LAMBDA=1 aggressive decay significantly HELPS (reduction=‚àí3.4845, z=‚àí13.502832), so the head is a "helpful-vs-harmful" contrast rather than "harmless-vs-harmful". The SIGN flip with the loss-aversion coefficient is robustly established; the ~11.6% magnitude and the clean-control narrative are model-choice-contingent ‚Äî hence QUALIFIED.
- G1 estimator-agreement: PASS ‚Äî two independent halves of the R headline reductions do NOT significantly disagree, z=‚àí0.835097 (|z|<3 two-sided) ‚Äî the paired common-random-number headline estimator is internally consistent across the trial set.
- G2 mechanism-control: PASS (not significantly positive) ‚Äî in the LAMBDA=1.0 world aggressive decay does NOT reduce activity: reduction=‚àí3.4845, z=‚àí13.502832 (z<3 one-sided, in fact significantly NEGATIVE ‚Äî decay mildly HELPS absent loss aversion), control_base_activity=963.0025 control_high_activity=966.487, control churn 1000‚Üí1000 (zero quits at LAMBDA=1) ‚Äî folk belief holds absent loss aversion, isolating loss aversion as the cause of the backfire.
- G3 head backfire: PASS ‚Äî in the LAMBDA=2.25 world aggressive decay SIGNIFICANTLY reduces activity: reduction=+76.051, z=+62.278027 (‚â•3 one-sided), real_base_activity=657.044 real_high_activity=580.993, real churn 1000‚Üí709 (291 quits at LAMBDA=2.25) ‚Äî aggressive decay cuts steady-state activity ~11.6% by churning marginal players out, the counterintuitive backfire the "more decay = more activity" folk belief misses.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/superbot-games/rank-decay-churn-cliff-2026-07-18.py ‚Äî random, math, json, hashlib; per-trial seed offset from SEED, simulates the N-player ladder over T periods for both LAMBDA worlds at both decay levels with R paired common-random-number trials, runs the three ordered gates, emits the whole results dict; no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field, every float rounded to 6 decimals) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at main 9678a2b, git blob bf119ea47e4a86c6c3badb073467b7ec5860ef86, file sha256 6c3e1b07914c6dcb1dbba53ba0ab5311c984b73abeb0b02ce1855de025ea6a0e, 283 lines / 11162 bytes; verifier landed via idea-engine PR #590 (P143); diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across two fresh python3 invocations (stdout byte-identical run-to-run, exit 0). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = ea060663fecf79059bedeb98d43c4f010d2daca6ff273ebf6a97545ddc83ac59 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P143 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132/P134/P135/P136/P137/P138/P139/P140/P141/P142 family) with the P127+ TWIST ‚Äî main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key (every float rounded to 6 dp), computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ‚Äî so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form (the stdout DUMP is pretty indent=2 while the hashed payload is compact). All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=‚àí0.835097 / G2 reduction=‚àí3.4845 z=‚àí13.502832 with control_base_activity=963.0025 & control_high_activity=966.487, control churn 1000‚Üí1000 / G3 reduction=+76.051 z=+62.278027 with real_base_activity=657.044 & real_high_activity=580.993, real churn 1000‚Üí709, 291 quits). REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114‚ÄìV155, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ‚Äî and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): in a loss-averse population the SIGN of rank decay's effect on activity is set by the loss-aversion coefficient ‚Äî decay is a recovery lever only while its pain accrual (LAMBDA¬∑d per idle period) stays below patience; past a small optimal rate, aggressive decay churns marginal players out faster than it grinds them back, so "more decay = more activity" is FALSE beyond that optimum. Measure the loss-aversion regime before cranking decay to fight inactivity; a ladder tuned as if Œª‚âà1 will churn its marginal population when Œª‚âà2.25. Anchors prospect theory / loss aversion (Kahneman & Tversky 1979; Œª‚âà2.25 from Tversky & Kahneman 1992, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Loss_aversion, verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch). QUALIFICATION (why QUALIFIED-APPROVE, not full APPROVE): two disclosed model-basis choices ‚Äî per-match enjoyment u redrawn fresh each period (a fixed-u baseline would make decay trivially helpful), and the one-sided G2 where LAMBDA=1 decay significantly HELPS (z=‚àí13.5, so the head is helpful-vs-harmful) ‚Äî shape the ~11.6% headline magnitude and the clean-control framing; the sign-dependence core is robust (z=62.278027), the magnitude/framing are model-choice-contingent.

## VERDICT 157 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-18T20:06:59Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P144 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 144 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T19:42:08Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the hot-hand streak-selection bias: in a FINITE sequence of N fair i.i.d. coin flips, select every flip that immediately follows a run of K consecutive heads and average the per-sequence fraction-of-heads across sequences; the EXPECTED fraction is strictly BELOW 0.5 ‚Äî not 0.5 ‚Äî for a fair memoryless coin, because per-sequence ratio-averaging conditioned on a K-head streak induces a finite-sample selection/sampling bias (Miller & Sanjurjo 2018), so a memoryless fair process reads anti-streaky under the natural "what happens after a streak?" estimator. Folk belief (inverted here): "a fair coin is memoryless, so the fraction of heads on flips that immediately follow a K-head streak must be exactly 0.5; an empirical fraction below 0.5 proves negative autocorrelation / an anti-streak (and a post-streak rate AT the base rate proves no hot hand ‚Äî the 1985 Gilovich‚ÄìVallone‚ÄìTversky conclusion)." It is NOT: take a length-N sequence of fair i.i.d. flips (heads=1, p=1/2); a position j in [K, N‚àí1] is SELECTED iff flips j‚àíK..j‚àí1 are all heads (overlapping windows allowed ‚Äî the Miller‚ÄìSanjurjo convention); the per-sequence estimator = (selected flips that are heads) / (selected flips), defined iff ‚â•1 selected flip; average that per-sequence fraction across sequences (equal weight per sequence, conditioned on ‚â•1 selected flip ‚Äî NOT pooled). Within one finite sequence the act of selecting a flip because its K predecessors were heads is negatively correlated with that flip being a head (a head extends the run and tends to be consumed as a predecessor of later selections; a tail ends it), so ratio-averaging across finite sequences pulls the expected fraction below 0.5 ‚Äî a law-of-small-numbers effect that vanishes as N‚Üí‚àû and grows with K / shrinks with N, with an exact closed form. Round-33 UNRELATED slot (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; P141 opened round-33 at the fleet slot, P142 took venture, P143 took game, P144 takes the unrelated slot) ‚Äî a finite-sample-statistics / law-of-small-numbers head anchored to Miller & Sanjurjo, "Surprised by the Hot Hand Fallacy? A Truth in the Law of Small Numbers", Econometrica 86(6):2019-2047, 2018 (which overturns the null of Gilovich, Vallone & Tversky, "The Hot Hand in Basketball", Cognitive Psychology 1985), the FIRST finite-sample-selection-bias-on-i.i.d.-fair-coins card in the unrelated lane, distinct from every prior head; Q-0264 co-consumer = any "what happens after a streak / after event E?" surface where a per-sample ratio-average conditioned on a preceding run is read as an autocorrelation signal (streak detection, momentum/anti-momentum studies, any conditional-frequency estimator on finite samples where selection on the conditioning event is negatively correlated with the outcome). Distinct from every prior head ‚Äî this is a finite-sample SELECTION artifact on genuinely i.i.d. fair coins, not a process with memory.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/hot-hand-streak-selection-bias-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N_FLIPS=15, K_STREAK=3, M_SEQUENCES=400000, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, the P104..P156 /se convention), the EXACT finite-sequence Miller‚ÄìSanjurjo expectation is computed by exhaustive enumeration of all 2^15=32768 equally-likely sequences (deterministic, no RNG) and used as the closed-form reference the Monte-Carlo run is gated against; the MC and the enumeration condition identically on ‚â•1 selected flip. Exact enumeration: e_exact=0.35289, bias_vs_half=‚àí0.14711 over 21232 defined sequences of 32768 total. Monte-Carlo over M_SEQUENCES=400000: mc_mean=0.353323, mc_se=0.000656 over 259246 defined (140754 undefined dropped ‚Äî sequences with no K-head streak followed by a flip). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 estimator-agreement: PASS ‚Äî two independent halves of the M per-sequence fractions do NOT significantly disagree, z=‚àí0.384486 (|z|<3 two-sided) ‚Äî split-half stability of the per-sequence ratio-average estimator.
- G2 closed-form agreement: PASS ‚Äî the MC mean is NOT significantly different from the exact enumerated Miller‚ÄìSanjurjo expectation, z=0.660242 (|z|=|mc_mean‚àíe_exact|/mc_se<3) ‚Äî the sim measures the right quantity (mc_mean=0.353323 vs e_exact=0.35289).
- G3 head sub-one-half: PASS ‚Äî the MC mean is SIGNIFICANTLY below 0.5, z=(0.5‚àímc_mean)/mc_se=223.601743 (‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî the counterintuitive sub-one-half result for a fair memoryless coin, a ~0.147 gap below 0.5 that is a finite-sample selection bias, not negative autocorrelation.

The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 1c262684265b51e714365a29aa1209ff646b13975d554a404aaf144927b2dac3 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P144 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132/P134/P135/P136/P137/P138/P139/P140/P141/P142/P143 family) with the P127+ TWIST ‚Äî main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key (every float rounded to 6 dp), computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ‚Äî so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form (the stdout DUMP is pretty indent=2 while the hashed payload is compact). All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=‚àí0.384486 / G2 z=0.660242 with mc_mean=0.353323 & e_exact=0.35289 / G3 z=223.601743 with mc_mean=0.353323, mc_se=0.000656); exact e_exact=0.35289, bias_vs_half=‚àí0.14711 over 21232 defined of 32768, MC over 259246 defined (140754 dropped). REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114‚ÄìV156, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ‚Äî and it landed to the digit (diff exit 0, cmp exit 0, double-run identical). Design correction (transferable, Q-0264): a post-streak fraction below 0.5 for a fair memoryless coin is a finite-sample Miller‚ÄìSanjurjo selection artifact, NOT negative autocorrelation; a base-rate post-streak read does NOT disprove a hot hand (it overturns the 1985 Gilovich‚ÄìVallone‚ÄìTversky null). Any "what happens after a streak?" estimator that ratio-averages per-sequence fractions on finite samples must correct for this selection bias before reading the residual as evidence of streakiness or anti-streakiness. Anchors Miller & Sanjurjo, Econometrica 86(6):2019-2047, 2018 + Gilovich‚ÄìVallone‚ÄìTversky 1985 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hot_hand, verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch). Clean APPROVE ‚Äî the head is gated against a deterministic closed-form enumeration (all 2^15 sequences, no RNG) via G2, so the sub-0.5 result is a verified property of the fair coin, not a model-choice-contingent magnitude; no qualification warranted.

## PROPOSAL 145 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T20:15:47Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 158 (cache-stampede / request-coalescing herd law: when a HOT cached key expires it is stale for one recompute window T, and under a plain TTL cache WITHOUT coordination every request arriving in [0,T) independently recomputes it at the origin, so the per-expiry origin herd is exactly Poisson(ŒªT) ‚Äî expected size ŒªT, which GROWS LINEARLY with request rate (a hotter key stampedes harder, not softer; the Wikipedia example verbatim: 10 req/s √ó 3 s render = 30 simultaneous recomputes). Request coalescing (single-flight / dogpile lock) lets only the FIRST in-window request recompute and makes the rest wait, collapsing the herd to exactly 1 (E = 1‚àíe^{‚àíŒªT} ‚âà 1) ‚Äî INDEPENDENT of Œª; the stampede cure is coalescing, not a longer TTL (which leaves herd size ŒªT untouched); round-34 FLEET slot)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/cache-stampede-coalescing-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-34 FLEET-domain slot OPENER (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-33 closed P141 fleet / P142 venture / P143 game / P144 unrelated, so round-34 opens at the FLEET slot). Domain: caching / concurrency (thundering-herd) ‚Äî the cache-stampede / request-coalescing herd law anchored to cache stampede / dogpile + probabilistic early expiration (Vattani, Chierichetti & Lowenstein, "Optimal Probabilistic Cache Stampede Prevention", VLDB 2015; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cache_stampede, verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch ‚Äî the page's own example, 10 req/s √ó 3 s render = 30 concurrent recomputes, is ŒªT=30 verbatim), the FIRST cache-stampede / single-flight-coalescing card in the fleet lane, distinct from every prior head. Folk belief (inverted here): "a cache with a near-100% hit ratio protects the origin, and a HOTTER key is SAFER because it is almost always served from cache, so to tame origin load you raise the hit ratio or lengthen the TTL; origin load per key is bounded by the miss rate, which caching drives toward zero." It is NOT: model one expiry event as a window [0,T) of length T = the recompute-and-repopulate time; requests to the key arrive as a Poisson process of rate lambda; under a plain TTL cache WITHOUT coordination every request arriving while the value is stale (in [0,T), before the first recompute has repopulated) independently launches its OWN origin recompute ‚Äî none can see the others in flight ‚Äî so the number that pile onto the origin in one expiry is exactly the count of Poisson arrivals in [0,T): Poisson(lambda*T), expected herd E = lambda*T. The herd SCALES with request rate: doubling traffic doubles the per-expiry origin burst even as the cache hit ratio climbs toward 100% (a hotter key stampedes HARDER ‚Äî the opposite of the folk intuition). Request coalescing (single-flight / dogpile lock) lets the FIRST in-window request acquire the recompute and makes every subsequent request wait on that single in-flight result, collapsing the herd to exactly 1 recompute per expiry (E = 1 ‚àí e^{-lambda*T} ‚âà 1 for any hot key) ‚Äî INDEPENDENT of lambda; a longer TTL lowers expiry FREQUENCY but leaves each expiry's herd size lambda*T untouched, so the stampede cure is coalescing, not TTL length. Pinned world: SEED=20260717, T_RECOMPUTE=1.0, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; one random.Random(SEED) drawn sequentially in fixed config order; three configs (name, lambda, T, R, coalesce): CFG_LOW lambda=5.0 T=1.0 R=4000 naive (anchor = exact Poisson mean lambda*T = 5), CFG_HIGH lambda=30.0 T=1.0 R=4000 naive (anchor = exact Poisson mean lambda*T = 30), CFG_HEAD lambda=30.0 T=1.0 R=4000 coalesced (head statistic = paired amplification vs zero). Estimator: per replication draw a Poisson(lambda) arrival process on [0,T) by summing interarrival ~ Exp(lambda) until the clock passes T; naive herd = count of arrivals strictly inside the window (exactly Poisson(lambda*T)); coalesced recomputes = 1 if herd>=1 else 0; se = sample-std/sqrt(R); z = (mean - anchor)/se. Three gates (order G1->G2->G3): G1 CFG_LOW verifier-correctness ‚Äî MC naive herd matches exact Poisson mean lambda*T=5, |z|<3; G2 CFG_HIGH verifier-correctness ‚Äî MC naive herd matches exact Poisson mean lambda*T=30, |z|<3 (6x the CFG_LOW herd for 6x the rate ‚Äî the mechanism control that the herd is Poisson(lambda*T) and scales linearly with rate); G3 head ‚Äî with coalescing the paired amplification (naive_herd - coalesced_recomputes) per expiry sits z = (mean-0)/se >= 3 (coalescing removes ~lambda*T-1 redundant recomputes per expiry, a ~30x -> 1x collapse). APPROVE iff ALL (in order). Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/cache_stampede_coalescing.py (random, json, hashlib, math; per-replication Poisson-process arrival draw, three ordered gates, emits the whole results dict ‚Äî no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field, every float rounded to 6 decimals; WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture with the P127+ TWIST ‚Äî main() digests the compact json.dumps(sort_keys=True,separators=(",",":")) serialization, prints the pretty indent=2 sort_keys dump + `Results-JSON sha256:`, writes no on-disk JSON). Dry-run landing (SEED-pinned, two CLI runs byte-identical, exit 0): CFG_LOW herd=4.91225 vs anchor 5.0 z=-2.522567 (|z|<3) PASS; CFG_HIGH herd=30.083 vs anchor 30.0 z=+0.963546 (|z|<3) PASS; CFG_HEAD amplification=29.13825 (naive 30.13825 - coalesced 1.0, se 0.085288, amplification_factor ~30.14x) z=+341.643962 (>=3) PASS ‚Äî all PASS, all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=81929730f04afec22fcacffd0d44d81609c6d2d2e395dd0803ec77ee29f19422.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/fleet/cache_stampede_coalescing.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 81929730f04afec22fcacffd0d44d81609c6d2d2e395dd0803ec77ee29f19422 matches EXACT, and all three pre-registered gates PASS in order (G1 CFG_LOW verifier-correctness |z|<3, G2 CFG_HIGH verifier-correctness |z|<3, G3 head coalescing amplification z>=3), exit 0 across a deterministic double-run, and VERDICT 158 is mirrored back.

## VERDICT 158 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-18T20:41:40Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P145 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 145 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T20:15:47Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî the cache-stampede / request-coalescing herd law: when a HOT cached key expires it is stale for one recompute window T, and under a plain TTL cache WITHOUT coordination every request arriving in [0,T) independently recomputes it at the origin, so the per-expiry origin herd is exactly Poisson(ŒªT) ‚Äî expected size ŒªT, which GROWS LINEARLY with request rate (a hotter key stampedes HARDER, not softer; the Wikipedia example verbatim: 10 req/s √ó 3 s render = 30 simultaneous recomputes). Folk belief (inverted here): "a cache with a near-100% hit ratio protects the origin, and a HOTTER key is SAFER because it is almost always served from cache, so to tame origin load you raise the hit ratio or lengthen the TTL; origin load per key is bounded by the miss rate, which caching drives toward zero." It is NOT: model one expiry event as a window [0,T) of length T = the recompute-and-repopulate time; requests arrive as a Poisson process of rate Œª; under a plain TTL cache WITHOUT coordination every request arriving while the value is stale (in [0,T), before the first recompute repopulates) independently launches its OWN origin recompute ‚Äî none can see the others in flight ‚Äî so the number that pile onto the origin in one expiry is exactly the count of Poisson arrivals in [0,T): Poisson(ŒªT), expected herd E = ŒªT. The herd SCALES with request rate: doubling traffic doubles the per-expiry origin burst even as the cache hit ratio climbs toward 100%. Request coalescing (single-flight / dogpile lock) lets the FIRST in-window request acquire the recompute and makes every subsequent request wait on that single in-flight result, collapsing the herd to exactly 1 recompute per expiry (E = 1 ‚àí e^{‚àíŒªT} ‚âà 1 for any hot key) ‚Äî INDEPENDENT of Œª; a longer TTL lowers expiry FREQUENCY but leaves each expiry's herd size ŒªT untouched, so the stampede cure is coalescing, not TTL length. Round-34 FLEET slot OPENER (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; round-33 closed P141 fleet / P142 venture / P143 game / P144 unrelated, so round-34 opens at the FLEET slot) ‚Äî a caching / concurrency (thundering-herd) head anchored to cache stampede / dogpile + probabilistic early expiration (Vattani, Chierichetti & Lowenstein, "Optimal Probabilistic Cache Stampede Prevention", VLDB 2015; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cache_stampede, verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch ‚Äî the page's own example, 10 req/s √ó 3 s render = 30 concurrent recomputes, is ŒªT=30 verbatim), the FIRST cache-stampede / single-flight-coalescing card in the fleet lane, distinct from every prior head; Q-0264 co-consumer = any per-expiry / cache-miss coordination surface where a near-100% hit ratio is read as origin-load protection while the worst-case per-expiry recompute herd (Poisson(ŒªT), rate-scaling) is the real load, and single-flight coalescing (not a longer TTL) is the cure. Distinct from every prior fleet head ‚Äî metastable-retry-storm-collapse (bistable retry congestion-collapse, not a single-expiry herd), hedged-request-tail-cure (duplicates ISSUED as a tail-latency cure ‚Äî opposite direction), erlang-b-trunking / checkout-pooling (queueing loss/delay systems), fan-out-tail / two-choices (fan-out / placement objects), epidemic-overshoot (SIR contagion) ‚Äî this is the first per-EXPIRY Poisson(ŒªT) origin-recompute herd with an O(1)-under-coalescing collapse.

Per replication a Poisson(Œª) arrival process on [0,T) is drawn by summing interarrival ~ Exp(Œª) until the clock passes T; naive herd = count of arrivals strictly inside the window (exactly Poisson(ŒªT)); coalesced recomputes = 1 if herd‚â•1 else 0; se = sample-std/sqrt(R); z = (mean ‚àí anchor)/se. Observed: CFG_LOW naive herd 4.91225 (z=-2.522567), CFG_HIGH naive herd 30.083 (z=+0.963546, 6√ó the CFG_LOW herd at 6√ó the rate), CFG_HEAD paired amplification 29.13825 (naive 30.13825 ‚àí coalesced 1.0, se 0.085288, amplification_factor ~30.14√ó, z=+341.643962). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate null). all_gates_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 CFG_LOW verifier-correctness: PASS ‚Äî the MC naive herd is NOT significantly different from the exact Poisson mean ŒªT=5, z=-2.522567 (|z|<3) ‚Äî the estimator measures the right quantity at the low rate (naive herd 4.91225 vs anchor 5.0).
- G2 CFG_HIGH verifier-correctness: PASS ‚Äî the MC naive herd is NOT significantly different from the exact Poisson mean ŒªT=30, z=+0.963546 (|z|<3) ‚Äî the mechanism control that the herd is Poisson(ŒªT) and scales LINEARLY with rate: 6√ó the CFG_LOW herd (30.083 vs 4.91225) for 6√ó the rate (Œª 30 vs 5).
- G3 head coalescing amplification: PASS ‚Äî with coalescing the paired amplification (naive_herd ‚àí coalesced_recomputes) per expiry is SIGNIFICANTLY positive, z=(mean‚àí0)/se=+341.643962 (‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî single-flight collapses a ~30-herd to exactly 1 recompute per expiry (amplification 29.13825 = naive 30.13825 ‚àí coalesced 1.0), a ~30.14√ó ‚Üí 1√ó reduction independent of Œª.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/fleet/cache_stampede_coalescing.py ‚Äî random, json, hashlib, math; per-replication Poisson-process arrival draw on [0,T), three ordered gates, emits the whole results dict ‚Äî no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field, every float rounded to 6 decimals) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at main 92c9e8a, git blob e024c61e599999dcb7d81767ff78554219058f9f, file sha256 4d2febd01d1710d19824d9e8f2b14bdddaaad8fb34b474f31e333c96440f5b20, 194 lines / 8144 bytes; verifier landed via idea-engine PR #594 (P145); byte-for-byte reproduction), reproduced deterministically across a cross-invocation double-run (stdout byte-identical run-to-run, exit 0, identical digest). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 81929730f04afec22fcacffd0d44d81609c6d2d2e395dd0803ec77ee29f19422 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P145 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132/P134/P135/P136/P137/P138/P139/P140/P141/P142/P143/P144 family) with the P127+ TWIST ‚Äî main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key (every float rounded to 6 dp), computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ‚Äî so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form (the stdout DUMP is pretty indent=2 while the hashed payload is compact). All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=-2.522567 with naive herd 4.91225 vs anchor 5.0 / G2 z=+0.963546 with naive herd 30.083 vs anchor 30.0 / G3 z=+341.643962 with amplification 29.13825, se 0.085288); all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114‚ÄìV157, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ‚Äî and it landed to the digit (byte-for-byte, double-run identical). Design correction (transferable, Q-0264): a near-100% cache hit ratio does NOT bound worst-case origin load ‚Äî it CONCENTRATES it into the per-expiry recompute herd Poisson(ŒªT), which grows LINEARLY with request rate (a hotter key stampedes harder); the cure is request coalescing / single-flight (collapses the herd to 1 independent of Œª), NOT a longer TTL (which leaves each expiry's herd size ŒªT untouched, only lowering expiry frequency). Two declared modelling choices flagged in the doc's Model basis (both CONSERVATIVE): (i) the recompute window is a FIXED length T rather than "until first refill lands" ‚Äî relaxing it only makes the naive herd LARGER, so the coalescing benefit is understated; (ii) arrivals are Poisson/independent ‚Äî real hot-key streams can be bursty/super-Poisson, which makes the herd worse, not better. Anchors Vattani, Chierichetti & Lowenstein, VLDB 2015 + https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cache_stampede (verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch). Clean APPROVE ‚Äî the naive herd is gated against the EXACT Poisson mean ŒªT at two rates (G1 |z|<3 at Œª=5, G2 |z|<3 at Œª=30 with the linear-scaling mechanism control), so the coalescing collapse (G3 z=+341.643962) is a verified property of single-flight, not a model-choice-contingent magnitude; no qualification warranted.

## PROPOSAL 146 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T20:49:07Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 159 (quota-attainment cliff bunching: a sales-comp ACCELERATOR with a CLIFF at 100% quota ‚Äî marginal deals below quota pay the base rate, above quota the accelerated rate ‚Äî is a convex kink in each rep's payoff, so rational reps RE-TIME deals across the period boundary; a rep just under quota pulls next-period deals FORWARD to clear the cliff and land in the bunching band, and a rep past the cliff pulls forward MORE deals to book at the accelerated rate NOW ("a hot period borrows from the next"). Even when TRUE per-rep per-period demand is i.i.d. across periods, OBSERVED bookings show three artifacts absent from the TRUE counterfactual: (1) BUNCHING ‚Äî excess mass in the [Q,Q(1+eps)] attainment band; (2) SPURIOUS NEGATIVE lag-1 autocorrelation of team-total bookings; (3) horizon demand CONSERVED (relocation moves timing not magnitude), so the volatility/mean-reversion a forecaster reads off QoQ booking growth is a comp-plan ARTIFACT, not the market; round-34 VENTURE slot)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/quota-attainment-cliff-bunching-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-34 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-34 opened at the FLEET slot with P145, so the venture slot is P146). Domain: sales-ops / sales-comp design ‚Äî quota-attainment cliff bunching, a NEW venture-domain mechanism (sales-comp INCENTIVE-TIMING distortion), anchored to bunching at kink points (Saez, "Do Taxpayers Bunch at Kink Points?", NBER w7366, https://www.nber.org/papers/w7366, verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch) + tiered sales commission / quota accelerators (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Commission_(remuneration)) + serial correlation (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autocorrelation), the FIRST comp-plan deal-timing / bunching-at-notch card in the venture lane, distinct from every prior head. Folk belief (inverted here): "a sales-comp cliff at 100% quota just sharpens focus on hitting quota; it changes how reps are PAID, not what/when they sell ‚Äî aggregate bookings and their trend are unaffected, and most reps landing right at quota means the plan is well-calibrated." It is NOT: the accelerator is a convex kink in the payoff (marginal payout jumps from base rate c to accelerated c*a at B=Q), so a rep who can shift a deal's TIMING across the t/t+1 boundary re-times to maximize commission. Two rational relocations follow: (i) a rep just under quota (attainment in [1-PULL_WINDOW,1)) pulls next-period deals FORWARD, smallest-first, just enough to CLEAR the cliff -> lands just above quota (BUNCHING); (ii) a rep comfortably over quota (attainment >= 1+OVER_THRESH, in the accelerator zone) pulls forward MORE next-period deals, largest-first up to ACCEL_BANK_CAP quota-units, to book at the accelerated rate NOW ("a hot period borrows from the next" -> depletes the next period). Aggregated over N_REPS x N_PERIODS with TRUE demand i.i.d. across periods, the OBSERVED (relocated) series shows: BUNCHING (excess band mass), a SPURIOUS NEGATIVE lag-1 autocorrelation of team totals (a hot period is followed by a depleted one), and EXACT horizon CONSERVATION (relocation only moves deals between existing adjacent periods, no leak past the last period). Pinned world: SEED=20260717, N_REPS=50, N_PERIODS=8, QUOTA=12.0, DEALS_PER=12 (Dist A amounts ~ Exponential(mean 1.0)), PULL_WINDOW=0.20, OVER_THRESH=0.20, ACCEL_BANK_CAP=0.50, BAND_EPS=0.10, M_TRIALS=800, SIGMA=3.0, CONSERVATION_TOL=1e-6; one random.Random(SEED) single stream, Dist A then Dist B (poisson_count_unit: deal count ~ Poisson(12), unit amounts). Estimator: PAIRED Monte-Carlo difference z on the /se convention ‚Äî per trial re-draw true demand, derive the observed grid by the deterministic relocation rule, compute (observed - counterfactual) for each statistic; z = mean_diff / (stdev_diff / sqrt(M_TRIALS)). Three ORDERED gates (order G1->G2->G3, APPROVE iff ALL): G1 BUNCHING ‚Äî observed [Q,Q(1+eps)] band excess z >= 3 (one-sided); G2 SPURIOUS NEGATIVE lag-1 AUTOCORR ‚Äî observed lag-1 autocorr of team totals strictly MORE negative than counterfactual (diff < 0), magnitude z >= 3; G3 CONSERVATION + ROBUSTNESS ‚Äî horizon demand conserved in BOTH worlds (|observed_total-true_total|/true_total <= 1e-6), AND the G1/G2 effect survives under the SECOND true-demand distribution (Dist B) still >= 3sigma. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/venture-lab/quota_cliff_bunching.py (hashlib, json, math, random; paired M-trial relocation, three ordered gates, emits the whole results dict ‚Äî no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field, every float rounded to 6 decimals; WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture with the P127+ TWIST ‚Äî main() digests the compact json.dumps(sort_keys=True,separators=(",",":")) serialization, prints the pretty indent=2 sort_keys dump + `Results-JSON sha256:`, writes no on-disk JSON). Dry-run landing (SEED-pinned, two CLI runs byte-identical, exit 0): G1 BUNCHING obs_band_mean=126.16375 vs true_band_mean=51.66 (diff 74.50375) z=+239.736603 (>=3) PASS; G2 AUTOCORR obs_ac_mean=-0.275185 vs true_ac_mean=-0.129676 (diff -0.145509) z=-13.149401 (magnitude >=3, strictly more negative) PASS; G3 conservation_max_rel=0.0 for Dist A and 0.0 for Dist B (<=1e-6), robustness Dist B band_z=+175.223184 ac_z=-13.109365 (both >=3sigma) PASS ‚Äî all PASS, all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=90e5ea44d1b7ccd2a230d4c3dd74eef8798ec56cf54794553f9e434efce00ea4.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/venture-lab/quota_cliff_bunching.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 90e5ea44d1b7ccd2a230d4c3dd74eef8798ec56cf54794553f9e434efce00ea4 matches EXACT, and all three pre-registered gates PASS in order (G1 BUNCHING band-excess z>=3, G2 SPURIOUS NEGATIVE lag-1 autocorr magnitude z>=3 and diff<0, G3 CONSERVATION <=1e-6 both worlds + ROBUSTNESS Dist B >=3sigma), exit 0 across a deterministic double-run, and VERDICT 159 is mirrored back.

## PROPOSAL 147 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T21:15:02Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 160 (Gresham currency hoarding: a game economy issues TWO currencies at nominal parity ‚Äî a STABLE "good" currency and a DEPRECIATING "bad" one (a holding penalty melts a fraction GAMMA of any UNSPENT bad balance each period) ‚Äî with SYMMETRIC nominal faucets and both settling today's expense at par; the folk belief is "a premium hard currency at par circulates and drives the economy," but under Gresham's law rational players SETTLE with the melting bad currency FIRST and HOARD the stable good one, so the good currency's circulation velocity COLLAPSES toward zero (settles only ~12.9% of real expense, velocity 0.129 vs a naive-spender 0.698 ‚Äî an ~81.5% collapse) while the bad currency does almost all the settlement; the SIGN of the circulation gap (share settled in bad minus share settled in good) depends on spender rationality ‚Äî with naive proportional spenders good circulates MORE (gap negative, folk belief holds), with rational hoarders good is driven OUT of circulation (gap +0.741); a two-currency game-economy / monetary-microeconomics head, round-34 GAME slot)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/gresham-currency-hoard-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-34 GAME-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-34 opened at the FLEET slot with P145, VENTURE slot took P146, THIS takes the game slot). Domain: two-currency game-economy design / monetary microeconomics ‚Äî a Gresham's-law head anchored to "bad money drives out good" (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gresham%27s_law, verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch), the FIRST two-currency / currency-hoarding card in the game lane, distinct from every prior game head. Folk belief (inverted here): "issue a premium hard currency at par with the soft currency and it will circulate and drive the economy ‚Äî two interchangeable currencies at parity spend equally." It is NOT: model N players each holding a good-currency wallet (stable) and a bad-currency wallet (a holding penalty melts a fraction 1-GAMMA of any UNSPENT bad balance each period); both faucets mint SYMMETRIC nominal income (each period draw good income g~Uniform[0,INC_MAX] and bad income b~Uniform[0,INC_MAX] from the SAME distribution) and both settle a per-period real expense E~Uniform[0,E_MAX] at par (1 unit of either = 1 real). RATIONAL spenders pay the melting bad currency first and hoard good (from_bad=min(pay,bad_wallet), from_good=pay-from_bad); NAIVE spenders pay in proportion to unit holdings. Circulation share_c = real settled in c / total real settled; velocity_c = settled in c / received in c. Two worlds (naive control, rational head) share the per-trial seed (common random numbers). Three ordered gates on the /se convention (z=mean/se, SIGMA_GATE=3.0): G1 estimator-agreement (two-half split of the rational gaps, |z|<3) -> G2 mechanism-control (naive world, circulation gap NOT significantly positive, z<3) -> G3 head (rational world, circulation gap SIGNIFICANTLY positive, z>=3, good money driven out). A model-dependence result (P024): Gresham hoarding is a property of rational agents facing a holding penalty, not of the (symmetric) currencies. ONE declared modelling choice flagged in the doc's Model basis: depreciation is a per-period MELT on the UNSPENT bad balance (a holding penalty) ‚Äî the cleanest way to make bad strictly worse to HOLD while keeping it at par to SPEND today (the Gresham premise), isolating the hoarding decision from any settlement-price asymmetry.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/superbot-games/gresham_currency_hoard.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 90995ccd7c152402a6189a3fbdb60a401d04f750767127eae449cf8f67f7b969 matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the /se margin: G1 estimator-agreement z=-0.69753 (|z|<3) / G2 naive-control gap=-0.398765 z=-1442.35432 (not-sig-positive, z<3, good circulates more) / G3 rational-head gap=+0.741351 z=+1701.028769 (>=3, good driven out of circulation), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded to 6 dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage ‚Äî the P127+ TWIST). Headline: rational hoarders collapse the good currency's velocity from 0.69817 (naive) to 0.129105 ‚Äî it settles only 12.93% of real expense (share_bad 0.870675) despite symmetric faucets.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 147 -> VERDICT 160). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P146 -> V159).
loop: round-34 GAME slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-34 opened at the FLEET slot with P145, VENTURE slot took P146, this takes the game slot). next is V160 (sim-lab reproduce + rule), then round-34 unrelated-slot P148.

## PROPOSAL 148 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T21:36:27Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 161 (the Will Rogers phenomenon / stage migration: reclassifying a member across a group boundary can raise the mean of BOTH groups at once while the population mean is EXACTLY unchanged and no member's value changes; move a member x from the higher-mean group HIGH into the lower-mean group LOW where LOW_mean < x < HIGH_mean, and HIGH sheds a below-its-mean member (its mean rises) while LOW gains an above-its-mean member (its mean rises), yet the pooled mean is identically conserved because the member was only RELABELLED; the folk belief is "if the average improves within EVERY subgroup, the population as a whole must have improved," but every subgroup can improve while the whole does not move a hair ‚Äî "every subgroup improved" and "the population improved" are DIFFERENT claims that coincide only when group membership is fixed; the serious form is medical STAGE MIGRATION (Feinstein, Sosin & Wells, NEJM 1985) where finer diagnostics reclassify borderline patients from an early to a late stage and stage-specific survival rises in BOTH stages with no change to any patient; a classification / measurement-artifact head, round-34 UNRELATED slot)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/will-rogers-stage-migration-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-34 UNRELATED-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-34 opened at the FLEET slot with P145, VENTURE slot took P146, GAME slot took P147, THIS completes the rotation at the unrelated slot). Domain: classification / measurement artifacts (outside fleet/venture/game) ‚Äî the Will Rogers phenomenon / stage migration, anchored to "moving an element from one set to another can raise the average of both sets" (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Will_Rogers_phenomenon, verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch), the FIRST reclassification-artifact card in the lane, distinct from every prior unrelated head. Folk belief (inverted here): "if the average outcome improves within EVERY subgroup, the population as a whole must have improved ‚Äî you cannot make every part better without making the whole better." It is NOT: model N members each with a FIXED value v ~ Uniform[0,1]; a COARSE classifier splits them at T_COARSE into LOW (v<T_COARSE) and HIGH (v>=T_COARSE); a FINER classifier then MIGRATES the bottom slice of HIGH (members with T_COARSE<=v<T_FINE) DOWN into LOW (the stage migration ‚Äî borderline members reclassified into the group whose mean they beat). Because a migrated member is below HIGH's mean and above LOW's mean, removing it raises HIGH's mean and adding it raises LOW's mean; the value multiset is never modified so the pooled mean is conserved EXACTLY. Two states (before/after migration) are computed per trial (paired ‚Äî same members). Three ordered gates on the /se convention (z=mean/se, SIGMA_GATE=3.0): G1 estimator-agreement (two-half split of the R low-group improvements, |z|<3) -> G2 conservation-control (pooled mean unchanged: cons_max_rel<=1e-9 with count conservation AND pooled change NOT significantly positive, z<3) -> G3 head (BOTH subgroup means rise, min(z_low,z_high)>=3). A model-dependence result (P024): "every subgroup improved" and "the population improved" coincide only when group membership is fixed. ONE declared modelling choice flagged in the doc's Model basis: the migration direction is HIGH->LOW (the bottom slice of the higher group moves into the lower group whose mean it beats) ‚Äî the stage-migration direction and the cleanest way to make both means rise at once; a migrated slice above HIGH's mean would flip HIGH's sign (disclosed model-dependence).
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/fleet/will_rogers_stage_migration.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 79b7443f3c993d830de4cd9d63c2ce826111e45ec426c3fd8bf17a8c33306470 matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the /se margin: G1 estimator-agreement half_a=+0.049696 half_b=+0.049445 z=+0.361594 (|z|<3) / G2 conservation-control pooled_impr=+0.000000 cons_max_rel=0.0 z=+0.010159 (not-sig-positive, z<3, count_conserved) / G3 head low_impr=+0.049571 z=+144.191642, high_impr=+0.049516 z=+133.926813, min z=+133.926813 (>=3, BOTH subgroup means rise), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded to 6 dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage ‚Äî the P127+ TWIST). Headline: migrating the bottom slice of HIGH into LOW raises BOTH subgroup means ~+0.0496 (closed form LOW 0.25->0.30, HIGH 0.75->0.80) while the pooled population mean is EXACTLY conserved (0.5->0.5) ‚Äî every subgroup improves >=3sigma on a population that did not change at all.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 148 -> VERDICT 161). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P147 -> V160).
loop: round-34 UNRELATED slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-34 opened at the FLEET slot with P145, VENTURE slot took P146, GAME slot took P147, this completes the rotation at the unrelated slot). round-34 proposals COMPLETE. next is V161 (sim-lab reproduce + rule), then round-35 fleet-slot P149.

## VERDICT 160 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-18T21:38:28Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P147 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 147 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T21:15:02Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî Gresham currency hoarding: a game economy issues TWO currencies at nominal parity ‚Äî a STABLE "good" currency (holds its value) and a DEPRECIATING "bad" currency (a holding penalty melts a fraction 1‚àíGAMMA of any UNSPENT bad balance each period) ‚Äî with SYMMETRIC nominal faucets (each period every player draws good units and bad units from the SAME Uniform[0,INC_MAX]) and both settling a per-period real expense E~Uniform[0,E_MAX] at par (1 unit of either = 1 real). Under Gresham's law (bad money drives out good) rational players SETTLE the melting bad currency FIRST (from_bad=min(pay,bad_wallet), from_good=pay‚àífrom_bad) and HOARD the stable good one, so the good currency's circulation velocity COLLAPSES toward zero (0.69817 naive ‚Üí 0.129105 rational, ~81.5% collapse, settling only 12.93% of real expense) while the bad currency does almost all the settlement ‚Äî even though the faucets are symmetric. Folk belief (inverted here): "issue a premium hard currency at par with the soft currency and it will circulate and drive the economy ‚Äî two interchangeable currencies at parity spend equally." It is NOT: model N players each holding a good wallet (never melts) and a bad wallet (unspent balance melts by GAMMA each period); symmetric faucets; par settlement capped at holdings; RATIONAL spenders pay bad-first and hoard good, NAIVE spenders pay in proportion to unit holdings; the SIGN of the circulation gap (share_bad ‚àí share_good) flips with spender rationality ‚Äî NEGATIVE under naive proportional spending (good circulates MORE because it accumulates unspent; folk belief holds), strongly POSITIVE +0.741351 under rational hoarding (good driven OUT of circulation). A model-dependence result (the P024 discipline): Gresham hoarding is a property of rational agents facing a holding penalty, not of the (symmetric) currencies. Round-34 GAME slot (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; round-34 opened at the FLEET slot with P145 ‚Üí V158, VENTURE slot took P146 ‚Üí V159, this GAME slot takes P147) ‚Äî a two-currency game-economy / monetary-microeconomics head anchored to Gresham's law "bad money drives out good" (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gresham%27s_law, verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch), the FIRST two-currency / currency-hoarding card in the game lane, distinct from every prior head; Q-0264 co-consumer = any game/economy designer who issues a second currency at nominal parity expecting it to circulate while one currency depreciates when held (an inflating soft currency, a decaying resource, a token with an expiry/wallet cap). Distinct from every prior game head ‚Äî shop-reroll (optimal stopping), streak-shield (regression), energy-cap (renewal overflow), matchmaking-winrate-mirage (Elo/SBMM), compounding-reward-inequality (log-normal Gini), PRD proc compression, snipe-clearing-leak (auction microstructure), guild-volunteer-dilemma (N-player public goods), single-elim favorite-collapse (order statistics), balance-triangle pick-rate inversion (zero-sum matrix equilibrium), Ringelmann raid-coordination overhead (group-size output decay), rank-decay churn cliff (loss-aversion churn), pity-anticipation-collapse (gacha pity), rubber-band PID instability ‚Äî none model a two-currency ECONOMY, currency hoarding, Gresham's law, or a circulation-velocity collapse whose sign flips with spender rationality.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/gresham-currency-hoard-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N=1000, T=100, R=40, INC_MAX=2.0, E_MAX=2.0, GAMMA=0.90, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, TRIAL_STRIDE=7919, the P104..P157 /se convention z=mean/se): each trial r seeds random.Random(SEED + r¬∑TRIAL_STRIDE); the naive and rational worlds share that per-trial seed (common random numbers ‚Äî identical income/expense draws), so their circulation-gap contrast is paired at fixed randomness. Observed: NAIVE world share_good 0.699383 / share_bad 0.300617 / vel_good 0.69817 / vel_bad 0.300463 / gap ‚àí0.398765 (z=‚àí1442.35432); RATIONAL world share_good 0.129325 / share_bad 0.870675 / vel_good 0.129105 / vel_bad 0.870242 / gap +0.741351 (z=+1701.028769). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate null). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 estimator-agreement: PASS ‚Äî two independent halves of the R=40 rational-world circulation gaps agree within noise, half_a gap 0.741044 vs half_b gap 0.741657, z=‚àí0.69753 (|z|<3) ‚Äî the head estimator is internally stable before it is read.
- G2 mechanism-control: PASS ‚Äî in the NAIVE proportional world the circulation gap (share_bad ‚àí share_good) is NOT significantly positive, gap ‚àí0.398765, se 0.000276, z=‚àí1442.35432 (z<3) ‚Äî without rational hoarding the good currency circulates MORE (share_good 0.699383 vs share_bad 0.300617); the folk belief holds, NO Gresham effect. This gate pins the sign flip to spender rationality.
- G3 head: PASS ‚Äî in the RATIONAL hoarder world the circulation gap is SIGNIFICANTLY positive, gap +0.741351, se 0.000436, z=+1701.028769 (‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî good money is driven OUT of circulation (share_good 0.129325 vs share_bad 0.870675); its velocity collapses to 0.129105 (from naive 0.69817), settling only 12.93% of real expense despite symmetric faucets.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/superbot-games/gresham_currency_hoard.py ‚Äî random, json, hashlib, math; N players √ó T periods √ó R paired trials under common random numbers, three ordered gates on the /se convention, emits the whole results dict ‚Äî no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field, every float rounded to 6 decimals) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at main b948e5c, git blob aad2303e100da26881d5fdd113eb6e01cb97f327, file sha256 335f1ce4a5128c84c52c98976d997a725a4a28a3c028ec3895242ab39d4ffd3d, 285 lines / 11206 bytes; verifier landed via idea-engine PR #597 (P147); byte-for-byte reproduction), reproduced deterministically across a cross-invocation double-run (stdout byte-identical run-to-run, exit 0, identical digest) and an in-process double-run (identical results dict). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 90995ccd7c152402a6189a3fbdb60a401d04f750767127eae449cf8f67f7b969 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P147 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132/P134/P135/P136/P137/P138/P139/P140/P141/P142/P143/P144/P145 family) with the P127+ TWIST ‚Äî main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key (every float rounded to 6 dp), computes payload=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(payload), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ‚Äî so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form (the stdout DUMP is pretty indent=2 while the hashed payload is compact ‚Äî the pretty dump hashes to 23d8c61e731267dcec51483d5a66792f0f6db79b7a170caf00833bd8469bd019). All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=‚àí0.69753 / G2 naive gap=‚àí0.398765 z=‚àí1442.35432 / G3 rational gap=+0.741351 z=+1701.028769); all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114‚ÄìV158, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ‚Äî and it landed to the digit (byte-for-byte, double-run identical). Design correction (transferable, Q-0264): two currencies at nominal parity do NOT circulate equally when one depreciates when HELD ‚Äî rational players spend the depreciating (bad) currency first and hoard the stable (good) one, so the good currency's circulation velocity collapses toward zero (0.69817 ‚Üí 0.129105, ~81.5%) and the bad currency does almost all the settlement, the reverse of the design intent (Gresham's law). The disclosed P024 MODEL-DEPENDENCE is handled honestly, not hidden: the head (G3, rational-world circulation gap positive) rests on a behavioral model (rational bad-first hoarding), so the result is CONDITIONAL on rational hoarding ‚Äî and that conditionality IS the disclosed finding, PROVEN by the G2 mechanism-control (naive proportional world, gap NEGATIVE, no Gresham effect) vs the G3 head (rational world, gap POSITIVE) under common random numbers; the single declared modelling choice (per-period melt on the UNSPENT bad balance, GAMMA=0.90, flagged in the doc's Model basis) sets the collapse MAGNITUDE, not its sign (as GAMMA‚Üí1 the rational gap shrinks toward the naive gap). APPROVE of a transparently model-dependent result ‚Äî the reproduction is EXACT and the sign flip is gate-verified, not asserted; unlike a closed-form-anchored head (e.g. V158's Poisson herd) the head is behavioral-model-contingent by construction and disclosed as such, so the ruling records the model-dependence rather than downgrading below APPROVE.

All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate null). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 estimator-agreement: PASS ‚Äî two independent halves of the R low-group improvements agree within noise, half_a=+0.049696 vs half_b=+0.049445, z=+0.361594 (|z|<3) ‚Äî the head estimator is internally stable before it is read.
- G2 conservation-control: PASS ‚Äî the pooled population mean is unchanged by the RELABELLING: pooled_impr=+0.000000, cons_max_rel=0.0, count conserved, and the pooled change is NOT significantly positive, z=+0.010159 (z<3) ‚Äî migration moves labels, not the value multiset; the WHOLE did not improve. This gate pins the artifact: every part rises while the whole holds still.
- G3 head: PASS ‚Äî BOTH subgroup means rise after migration, low_impr=+0.049571 z=+144.191642 and high_impr=+0.049516 z=+133.926813, min z=+133.926813 (‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî every subgroup improves ‚â•3œÉ on a population that did not move a hair.

Design correction (transferable, Q-0264): "every subgroup improved" and "the population improved" are DIFFERENT claims ‚Äî a reclassification that moves a member below the higher group's mean and above the lower group's mean raises BOTH group means while the pooled population mean is EXACTLY conserved (no member's value changes, only its label). The disclosed P024 MODEL-DEPENDENCE is handled honestly, not hidden: the both-means-rise result requires migrating the BOTTOM slice of HIGH (the direction that is below HIGH's mean and above LOW's mean; a slice above HIGH's mean would flip HIGH's sign), the single declared modelling choice flagged in the doc's Model basis ‚Äî and that choice sets WHICH subgroups rise, not WHETHER the pooled mean is conserved (it always is, because only labels change), PROVEN by the G2 conservation-control (pooled change ~0) vs the G3 head (both subgroup means rise). ## VERDICT 159 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-19T00:40:38Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P146 (+13) ¬∑ QUALIFIED-APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 146 ¬∑ 2026-07-18T20:49:07Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî quota-attainment cliff bunching: a sales-comp ACCELERATOR with a CLIFF at 100% quota (marginal deals below quota pay the base rate, above quota the accelerated rate) is a convex kink in each rep's payoff, so rational reps RE-TIME deals across the period boundary ‚Äî a rep just under quota (attainment in [1‚àíPULL_WINDOW, 1)) pulls next-period deals FORWARD, smallest-first, just enough to CLEAR the cliff and land in the [Q, Q(1+eps)] band (BUNCHING), and a rep comfortably past the cliff (attainment ‚â• 1+OVER_THRESH) pulls forward MORE next-period deals, largest-first up to ACCEL_BANK_CAP quota-units, to book at the accelerated rate NOW ("a hot period borrows from the next" ‚Äî the successor period is depleted). Even when TRUE per-rep per-period demand is i.i.d. across periods, the OBSERVED (relocated) bookings show three artifacts absent from the TRUE counterfactual: (1) BUNCHING ‚Äî excess mass in the attainment band just above quota; (2) a SPURIOUS NEGATIVE lag-1 autocorrelation of team-total bookings (a hot period is followed by a depleted one); (3) EXACT horizon CONSERVATION (relocation moves timing, not magnitude) ‚Äî so the QoQ volatility / mean-reversion a forecaster reads off bookings is a comp-plan ARTIFACT, not the market. Folk belief (inverted here): "a comp cliff at 100% quota just sharpens focus on hitting quota; it changes how reps are PAID, not what/when they sell ‚Äî aggregate bookings and their trend are unaffected, and most reps landing right at quota means the plan is well-calibrated." Round-34 VENTURE slot (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; round-34 opened at the FLEET slot with P145 ‚Üí V158, this VENTURE slot P146 ‚Üí V159 was the LAST round-34 verdict gap ‚Äî V158/V160/V161 landed first, so V159 is a BACKFILL below the V161 high-water) ‚Äî a sales-ops / sales-comp incentive-timing head anchored to bunching at kink points (Saez, "Do Taxpayers Bunch at Kink Points?", NBER w7366, https://www.nber.org/papers/w7366) + tiered commission / quota accelerators (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Commission_(remuneration)) + serial correlation (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autocorrelation), the FIRST comp-plan deal-timing / bunching-at-notch card in the venture lane; Q-0264 co-consumer = any operator who reads demand signal off incentive-timed bookings (sales forecasting, quota planning, revenue smoothing) ‚Äî a convex comp kink re-times deals it does not create, so comp-period-boundary volatility is plan-contingent.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/quota-attainment-cliff-bunching-2026-07-18.md
ruling: QUALIFIED-APPROVE ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N_REPS=50, N_PERIODS=8, QUOTA=12.0, DEALS_PER=12 with Dist A amounts ~ Exponential(mean 1.0), PULL_WINDOW=0.20, OVER_THRESH=0.20, ACCEL_BANK_CAP=0.50, BAND_EPS=0.10, M_TRIALS=800, SIGMA=3.0, CONSERVATION_TOL=1e-6, Dist B = poisson_count_unit; one random.Random(SEED) single stream, Dist A then Dist B; PAIRED Monte-Carlo difference estimator on the P104..P158 /se convention, z = mean_diff / (stdev_diff/sqrt(M_TRIALS)) ‚Äî the pairing nets out the shared finite-sample lag-1 autocorrelation bias ‚âà ‚àí1/(P‚àí1)), a stdlib Monte-Carlo of the N_REPS √ó N_PERIODS relocation world confirms all three pre-registered artifacts. All three gates pass in order G1‚ÜíG2‚ÜíG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate null). all_pass=true, exit 0. QUALIFIED (not full APPROVE, the V156 precedent): the proposal's OWN Model basis declares the LOAD-BEARING modelling choice ‚Äî a pure above-quota ACCELERATOR with NO quota ratchet (œÅ=0), under which the rational over-quota re-timing is PULL-FORWARD (which is exactly what produces the G2 negative sign) ‚Äî and concedes that under a QUOTA-RATCHET plan the over-quota incentive inverts to defer/sandbag, pushing the observed lag-1 autocorrelation toward positive/smoothing; the verifier ships NO ratchet-control world gating that dependence (the œÅ* sign-flip sweep is the named next venture slice). BUNCHING (G1), EXACT CONSERVATION (G3a) and distribution-robustness (G3b) are ratchet-robust and unconditional; the SPURIOUS-NEGATIVE-autocorrelation headline SIGN (G2) ‚Äî the leg powering the forecaster-facing "QoQ mean-reversion is the comp plan" punchline ‚Äî is regime-dependent, hence QUALIFIED.
- G1 BUNCHING: PASS ‚Äî observed [Q, Q(1+eps)] band mass significantly exceeds the paired counterfactual's: obs_band_mean=126.16375 vs true_band_mean=51.66 (excess 74.50375, se 0.310773), z=+239.736603 (‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî pull-to-clear reps manufacture excess band mass the true world does not have (the gate is the excess, not the level).
- G2 SPURIOUS NEGATIVE lag-1 AUTOCORR: PASS ‚Äî observed team-total lag-1 autocorr strictly more negative than the counterfactual's: obs_ac_mean=‚àí0.275185 vs true_ac_mean=‚àí0.129676 (diff ‚àí0.145509 < 0, se 0.011066), z=‚àí13.149401 (magnitude ‚â•3) ‚Äî the true world sits at the finite-sample bias (‚âà ‚àí1/(P‚àí1)); the paired difference isolates the relocation's increment, nearly doubling the raw level.
- G3 CONSERVATION + ROBUSTNESS: PASS ‚Äî conservation_max_rel=0.0 (Dist A) and 0.0 (Dist B), both ‚â§ 1e-6 (relocation moves whole deal amounts between existing adjacent periods, no leak past the horizon ‚Äî "timing not magnitude" as a checked invariant); robustness under Dist B (poisson_count_unit ‚Äî count randomness, not amount randomness): band_z=+175.223184 and ac_z=‚àí13.109365, both ‚â•3œÉ, same signs ‚Äî distribution-general.

NOTE the digest posture: P146 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132/P134/P135/P136/P137/P138/P139/P140/P141/P142/P143/P144/P145/P147/P148 family) with the P127+ TWIST ‚Äî main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key (every float rounded to 6 dp), computes payload=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(payload), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ‚Äî so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form (the stdout DUMP is pretty indent=2 while the hashed payload is compact). All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=+239.736603 with obs_band_mean 126.16375 vs true 51.66 / G2 z=‚àí13.149401 with diff ‚àí0.145509 / G3 conservation 0.0 + 0.0, Dist B band_z=+175.223184 ac_z=‚àí13.109365); all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114‚ÄìV161, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ‚Äî and it landed to the digit (byte-for-byte, diff exit 0, triple-run identical). Design correction (transferable, Q-0264): a convex comp kink re-times deals it does not create ‚Äî observed bookings BUNCH just above quota and ANTI-CORRELATE period-to-period while horizon demand is EXACTLY conserved, so QoQ booking volatility / mean-reversion measured on comp-period boundaries is a property of the COMP PLAN, not the market; read attainment histograms for band excess just above 100% (Saez's kink-point signature) before trusting them as calibration evidence, and treat comp-period-boundary autocorrelation as plan-contingent ‚Äî its SIGN flips between accelerator regimes (pull-forward ‚Üí negative) and ratchet regimes (sandbagging ‚Üí positive/smoothing). QUALIFICATION (why QUALIFIED-APPROVE, not full APPROVE): the disclosed LOAD-BEARING no-ratchet accelerator choice sets the G2 headline sign and carries no ratchet-control gate ‚Äî unlike V160/V161 (model-dependence proven by a control-vs-head contrast) and unlike V157/V158 (closed-form anchors); per the V156 precedent a disclosed model-basis choice shaping the headline warrants the downgrade, while the G1 bunching and G3 conservation legs are unconditional. Anchors Saez NBER w7366 (https://www.nber.org/papers/w7366) + https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Commission_(remuneration) + https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autocorrelation.
