## PROPOSAL 149 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T01:21:17Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 162 (coordinated omission: a closed-loop / synchronous load generator waits for each request to return before issuing the next, so during a service stall it has exactly ONE request outstanding and CANNOT send the requests it was scheduled to send ‚Äî it silently OMITS the coordinated backlog those requests would have measured, so the measured latency distribution is biased LOW by orders of magnitude: the closed loop records ~1 inflated sample per stall where the true user experience is a pile-up of ~D/œÑ backlogged requests, so the p99/p99.9 it prints is set by the MEASUREMENT METHOD, not the system under test; both loops run on the byte-identical server, stall schedule and service-time list ‚Äî only the pacing (open vs synchronous) differs, and that alone moves the reported p99 by ~104√ó ‚Äî and an HdrHistogram-style CO-correction (back-fill the omitted samples at expected interval œÑ=1/Œª) lifts the measured tail back toward the open-loop truth; a performance-measurement / load-generation-artifact head, round-35 FLEET slot)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/coordinated-omission-2026-07-19.md
question: Round-35 FLEET-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-35 OPENS at the FLEET slot with P149; round-34 COMPLETE, P145->V158 / P146->V159 / P147->V160 / P148->V161). Domain: performance measurement ‚Äî the load generator itself, not the system under test ‚Äî coordinated omission (Gil Tene / HdrHistogram, "How NOT to Measure Latency"), the FIRST coordinated-omission / closed-loop-tail-blindness card in the fleet lane, distinct from every prior fleet head; a grep of ideas/ and control/ at HEAD for "coordinated omission" returned zero prior heads. Folk belief (inverted here): "my load generator drives the system at rate Œª and records every request's latency, so the p99/p99.9 it reports IS the tail my users experience ‚Äî a closed-loop harness (fire a request, wait for the response, fire the next) is a faithful sampler of the latency distribution." It is NOT: model ONE single-server FIFO system measured two ways over the SAME per-replicate stall schedule and the SAME pre-drawn service-time list ‚Äî an OPEN-LOOP drive (arrivals on a fixed schedule k¬∑œÑ regardless of completions ‚Äî the ground truth of what a user experiences) and a CLOSED-LOOP drive (synchronous, dispatch_{i+1}=completion_i, exactly one request outstanding ‚Äî the common but flawed harness); the server is FROZEN for a fixed stall duration D, and because the closed loop has one request outstanding it fires exactly one request into each stall, blocks for the whole stall, and issues NOTHING else, so it OMITS the ~D/œÑ requests that a fixed-schedule drive would have queued behind the stall ‚Äî the missing samples are precisely the ones that would have measured the tail, and they are missing BECAUSE the load generator's pacing is coordinated with (backs off during) the very stalls it is meant to measure. The CO-correction (HdrHistogram's recordValueWithExpectedInterval): for each recorded closed sample L>œÑ, back-fill L‚àíœÑ, L‚àí2œÑ, ‚Ä¶ down to >œÑ and union with the originals ‚Äî synthesising the samples a coordinated (evenly-paced) load would have recorded during the stall. Three ordered gates on the /se convention (z=mean/se, z_gate=3.0, require z>=3): G1 tail-blindness (z = mean(log(open_p99/closed_p99))/se ‚Äî the closed-loop p99 understates the open-loop p99) -> G2 omitted-backlog (z = mean(open_stall_count ‚àí closed_stall_count)/se ‚Äî the open loop carries ~D/œÑ backlogged requests per stall that the closed loop omits, ~1 per stall) -> G3 correction-recovery (z = mean(log(corrected_p99/closed_p99))/se ‚Äî the CO-correction lifts the measured tail back up toward the truth). A model-dependence result (the P024 discipline): the tail understatement is a property of the MEASUREMENT METHOD (closed-loop pacing), not the system ‚Äî the open-loop latencies ARE the truth and are unchanged; declared choices flagged in the doc's Model basis (server FROZEN for fixed D ‚Äî relaxing to a load-dependent slowdown only ENLARGES the open-loop backlog, deepening the gap, so the closed-loop understatement is conservative; base service Exp(Œº_s) at low utilisation so the between-stall path is fast and the closed loop's few inflated samples fall above its own p99; the CO-correction uses expected interval œÑ=1/Œª exactly per HdrHistogram) change neither the SIGN nor the order-of-magnitude of the understatement. Q-0264 co-consumer = any operator who reads a p99/p99.9 off a synchronous/closed-loop harness (JMeter/wrk/ab-style benchmarks, RPC/DB-driver latency dashboards, browser synthetic monitors, agent-loop / tool-call round-trip timing) and trusts it as the user-experienced tail ‚Äî a closed-loop harness backs off in lockstep with the exact GC/safepoint/failover/lock-convoy/cron stalls a load test exists to catch, so its tail is not evidence about the user-experienced tail during any stall.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/fleet/coordinated_omission.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 12d3ce4fce9a4f1cc218be0ee6f5dbc945d42a5e45173a46044704524dbfab5d matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the /se margin: G1 tail-blindness mean log(open/closed p99)=+4.643332 z=+2505.733369 (>=3) / G2 omitted-backlog mean(open‚àíclosed stall count)=+294.635 z=+5148.278412 (>=3) / G3 correction-recovery mean log(corr/closed p99)=+4.408601 z=+2606.670555 (>=3), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded to 6 dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage ‚Äî the P127+ TWIST). Headline: the closed loop reports p99 ‚âà 0.47 ms while the true open-loop p99 is ‚âà 48.6 ms ‚Äî a ~104√ó understatement (mean_open_over_closed_p99 = 103.855766) produced by the pacing alone; the open loop carries ~300 backlogged requests across the 6 stalls (~50 = D/œÑ each) where the closed loop carries ~6 (one frozen sample per stall); the CO-correction lifts the measured p99 from 0.47 ms to 38.4 ms ‚Äî recovery fraction 0.788774 of the way back to the open-loop truth.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 149 -> VERDICT 162). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P148 -> V161).
loop: round-35 FLEET slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-35 OPENS at the FLEET slot with P149; round-34 COMPLETE ‚Äî FLEET-slot P145 -> V158, VENTURE-slot P146 -> V159, GAME-slot P147 -> V160, UNRELATED-slot P148 -> V161). next is V162 (sim-lab reproduce + rule; P149 -> V162, +13 ‚Äî mapping cited, verdict NOT written here), then round-35 venture-slot P150.

## PROPOSAL 150 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T01:57:53Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 163 (founder-dilution liquidation waterfall: between two term sheets raising the SAME amount for the SAME company, the one with the HIGHER headline pre-money valuation can leave founders with LESS money at exit ‚Äî across a strict MAJORITY of realistic exit outcomes and at the typical/median exit ‚Äî because it carries a PARTICIPATING preferred with a liquidation MULTIPLE whose participation drag (a fixed multiple off the top, THEN the investor also shares the remainder pro-rata) dominates the founder-friendly effect of the smaller investor ownership fraction the higher valuation buys, everywhere below an analytic crossover exit of $200M; Offer A pre=30 raise=10 1x-non-participating (f_A=0.25) vs Offer B pre=50 raise=10 2x-participating-uncapped (f_B=1/6), founder_A(X)=X-max(10,0.25X), founder_B(X)=(1-1/6)(X-20), equal at X*=200 exactly; a cap-table / exit-economics head, round-35 VENTURE slot)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/founder-dilution-waterfall-2026-07-19.md
question: Round-35 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-35 opened at the FLEET slot with P149 -> V162, this VENTURE slot is P150 -> V163). Domain: startup finance / cap-table & exit economics ‚Äî the liquidation-preference waterfall, the FIRST such head in the venture lane; a grep of ideas/venture-lab/ and control/ at HEAD shows every prior venture head is an operating-metric / growth / revenue / cash / sales-ops result, none touching the cap table. Folk belief (inverted here): "a higher pre-money valuation is unambiguously better for founders ‚Äî same cash in, less dilution; between two offers raising the same money, take the higher valuation, the liquidation-preference fine print only bites in a fire sale." It is NOT: model ONE company raising RAISE=10 under TWO term sheets evaluated on the SAME exit draw (paired) ‚Äî Offer A (LOWER valuation, CLEAN terms: pre 30 -> post 40, investor owns f_A=0.25, 1x NON-PARTICIPATING) and Offer B (HIGHER valuation, STACKED terms: pre 50 -> post 60, investor owns f_B=1/6, 2x PARTICIPATING uncapped); founder proceeds at exit X are founder_A(X)=X-max(10,0.25X) (non-participating: investor takes the GREATER of preference or as-converted common) and founder_B(X)=(1-1/6)(X-20) for X>20 (participating: 2x preference off the top THEN pro-rata share of the remainder); the two are equal at X*=200 (0.75X=0.8333(X-20)), so BELOW $200M exit Offer A pays founders strictly more and only extreme moonshots reverse it. Three ordered gates on the /se convention (z=(mean-H0)/se, z_gate=3.0, require z>=3): G1 majority-inversion (fraction of exits with founder_A>founder_B exceeds 0.5) -> G2 typical-outcome gap (conditional on a non-moonshot exit X<X*, the paired gap founder_A-founder_B>0) -> G3 robustness (both effects survive a SECOND heavier-tailed exit distribution ‚Äî Dist B median 120 sigma 1.3, MORE moonshots, the case most favourable to Offer B). A model-dependence result (the P024 discipline): the head is a BODY/typical-exit claim, not universal ‚Äî above X*=200 Offer B wins and under the moonshot-heavy Dist B the raw full-distribution MEAN flips to B (207.803124 vs 215.568532), disclosed as a descriptive field; declared choices flagged in the doc's Model basis (uncapped participation ‚Äî a 3x cap only weakens B's tail win; single preferred round / no option pool ‚Äî a multi-round stack only deepens the drag on common; log-normal exit calibration ‚Äî signs are calibration-general per G3) change neither the SIGN nor the order-of-magnitude of the below-crossover founder-A advantage. Q-0264 co-consumer = any founder, operator, or angel weighing a term sheet by its headline valuation (Series-A/seed negotiations, secondary/exit modelling, employee-vs-founder waterfall analysis) ‚Äî a participating multiple is a large first-order founder haircut while a headline-valuation bump is a small tail-only credit, so the valuation number is nearly orthogonal to realised founder proceeds.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/venture-lab/founder_dilution_waterfall.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 bf4042bdc18bf2c5ff3d1530a97ad2a7ead43038407f095ff2b5304df4f3b7c1 matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the /se margin: G1 majority-inversion win_rate=0.7983 z=+105.128721 (>=3) / G2 typical-outcome gap gap_mean=+8.787216 z=+300.536834 with frac_below_cross=0.8153 (>=3) / G3 robustness (Dist B) win_rate=0.62305 z=+35.907274 and gap_mean=+8.22916 z=+234.981636 (both >=3), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded 6 dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage ‚Äî the P127+ TWIST). Headline: the higher-valuation 2x-participating sheet loses to the lower-valuation clean sheet in ~80% of exits and by ~$8.8M at the typical (X<$200M) exit, median founder proceeds 60.243238 (A) vs 50.270264 (B); it wins only above the $200M analytic crossover.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 150 -> VERDICT 163). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P149 -> V162).
loop: round-35 VENTURE slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-35 FLEET-slot P149 -> V162, this VENTURE-slot P150 -> V163). next is V163 (sim-lab reproduce + rule; P150 -> V163, +13 ‚Äî mapping cited, verdict NOT written here), then round-35 game-slot P151.

## VERDICT 162 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-19T02:08:04Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P149 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 149 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T01:21:17Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî coordinated omission: a closed-loop / synchronous load generator waits for each request to return before issuing the next, so during a service stall it has exactly ONE request outstanding, fires one request that blocks for the whole stall, and OMITS the ~D/œÑ requests a fixed-schedule open-loop drive would have queued behind the stall ‚Äî the missing samples are precisely the ones that measure the tail, so the closed-loop p99 is set by the fast between-stall path while the true open-loop p99 sits deep in the stall tail; the reported tail is biased LOW by orders of magnitude and is set by the MEASUREMENT METHOD (closed-loop pacing), not the system under test. An HdrHistogram-style CO-correction (recordValueWithExpectedInterval: back-fill each closed sample L>œÑ with L‚àíœÑ, L‚àí2œÑ, ‚Ä¶ at expected interval œÑ=1/Œª) repopulates the omitted band and lifts the measured tail back toward the open-loop truth. Folk belief (inverted here): "my load generator drives the system at rate Œª and records every request's latency, so the p99 it prints IS the tail my users experience ‚Äî a closed-loop harness (fire, wait, fire) is a faithful sampler." Round-35 FLEET slot (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; round-35 OPENS at the FLEET slot with P149 ‚Üí V162; round-34 COMPLETE V158‚ÄìV161) ‚Äî a performance-measurement / load-generation-artifact head anchored to coordinated omission (Gil Tene / HdrHistogram, "How NOT to Measure Latency"), the FIRST coordinated-omission / closed-loop-tail-blindness card in the fleet lane; Q-0264 co-consumer = any operator who reads a p99/p99.9 off a synchronous/closed-loop harness (JMeter/wrk/ab benchmarks, RPC/DB-driver latency dashboards, browser synthetic monitors, agent-loop / tool-call round-trip timing) and trusts it as the user-experienced tail.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/coordinated-omission-2026-07-19.md
ruling: APPROVE (clean) ‚Äî on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, R=200 replicates seeded random.Random(SEED+i), Œª=1000 ‚Üí œÑ=1 ms, horizon T=1.0 s, base service Exp(mean Œº_s=0.1 ms) utilisation 0.1, n_stalls=6 of D=50 ms so D/œÑ=50, N_svc=12000, q=0.99 nearest-rank, z_gate=3.0; server FROZEN during each stall, both loops consume the SAME per-replicate service-time list, only the pacing differs) sim-lab reproduced the committed verifier byte-for-byte and confirmed all three ordered /se gates PASS. all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0. CLEAN (not QUALIFIED, unlike the V159 precedent): the proposal's OWN Model basis declares three choices (server FROZEN for fixed D, low base utilisation, œÑ=1/Œª back-fill) but each is CONSERVATIVE-DIRECTION ‚Äî relaxing the frozen-server abstraction only ENLARGES the open-loop backlog (deepening the gap), higher utilisation lifts the closed baseline while the open-vs-closed p99 gap persists as long as D‚â´œÑ, and the œÑ=1/Œª back-fill is exactly HdrHistogram's expected-interval ‚Äî none flips a gate SIGN or the order-of-magnitude; every gate is a definitional contrast on the byte-identical server / stall schedule / service-time list, so the ~104√ó tail understatement rests on the load generator's PACING alone. No headline is regime-contingent ‚Üí clean APPROVE (contrast V159, whose G2 autocorrelation SIGN inverted between accelerator and ratchet regimes with no control world).
- G1 tail-blindness: PASS ‚Äî mean log(open_p99/closed_p99)=+4.643332, se 0.001853, z=+2505.733369 (‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî the closed-loop p99 understates the open-loop p99.
- G2 omitted-backlog: PASS ‚Äî mean(open_stall_count ‚àí closed_stall_count)=+294.635, se 0.05723, z=+5148.278412 (‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî the open loop carries ~D/œÑ‚âà50 backlogged requests per stall (300.635 total across 6 stalls) that the closed loop omits (~1 per stall, 6.0 total).
- G3 correction-recovery: PASS ‚Äî mean log(corrected_p99/closed_p99)=+4.408601, se 0.001691, z=+2606.670555 (‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî the HdrHistogram-style CO-correction lifts the measured tail toward the truth; recovery fraction 0.788774 (descriptive companion, not gated).
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/fleet/coordinated_omission.py ‚Äî hashlib, json, math, random; open + closed loops over the identical server/stalls/service-times, HdrHistogram back-fill correction, three ordered /se z-gates; emits the whole results dict ‚Äî no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field, every float rounded to 6 dp) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at main d615489, git blob d276fb5047473a5bc569f8747c1dd7a04311bbc5, file sha256 6780d06a213ad27d218331447bb922898a1eb8aa716396bf343590c16eda762c, 323 lines / 12310 bytes; verifier landed via idea-engine PR #602 (P149); diff exit 0, byte-for-byte reproduction), reproduced deterministically across a fresh cross-invocation double-run + the byte-identical copy + the script's in-process double-run assert (stdout byte-identical, cmp exit 0, exit 0, identical digest). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: results-dict sha256 = 12d3ce4fce9a4f1cc218be0ee6f5dbc945d42a5e45173a46044704524dbfab5d matched to the digit (all 64 hex). Digest posture: WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY (the P105‚Ä¶P149 family) with the P127+ TWIST ‚Äî main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key (floats 6 dp), computes payload=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(payload), PRINTS the pretty json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ‚Äî so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict (the stdout dump is pretty indent=2). Descriptive headline reproduced: mean_open_p99 0.048583 s vs mean_closed_p99 0.000468 s (mean_open_over_closed_p99 103.855766 ‚Äî ~104√ó understatement from pacing alone); mean_open_stall_count 300.635 vs mean_closed_stall_count 6.0 (excess 294.635 ‚âà 6 stalls √ó ~50 = D/œÑ); mean_corrected_p99 0.03842 s (recovery 0.788774). REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114‚ÄìV161): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction ‚Äî and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable, Q-0264): a closed-loop / synchronous load generator cannot measure the tail it stalls in ‚Äî during any stall it has one request outstanding and omits the ~D/œÑ coordinated backlog, so its reported p99 is set by the MEASUREMENT METHOD, not the system, and understates the truth by orders of magnitude; drive load OPEN-LOOP (decouple issue time from completion time) or apply an HdrHistogram-style CO-correction (recordValueWithExpectedInterval) ‚Äî either restores the tail; more samples / more warm-up does NOT. External anchor: Gil Tene, "How NOT to Measure Latency" / HdrHistogram.

Offer A pre=30 raise=10 1x-NON-participating (f_A=0.25, founder_A(X)=X‚àímax(10,0.25X)) vs Offer B pre=50 raise=10 2x-PARTICIPATING-uncapped (f_B=1/6, founder_B(X)=(1‚àí1/6)(X‚àí20) for X>20), equal at the analytic crossover X*=200 exactly (0.75X=0.8333(X‚àí20)) ‚Äî below $200M Offer A pays founders strictly more, only extreme moonshots reverse it. On the pinned world (SEED=20260717, stdlib-only, the /se convention z=(mean‚àíH0)/se, z_gate=3.0, require z‚â•3; Offer A pre=30 raise=10 1x-non-participating vs Offer B pre=50 raise=10 2x-participating-uncapped, both evaluated on the SAME paired exit draw; analytic crossover X*=200) sim-lab reproduced the committed verifier byte-for-byte and confirmed all three ordered /se gates PASS. all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0. The head is a BODY / typical-exit claim (the P024 model-dependence discipline, disclosed not hidden): above the $200M crossover the higher-headline participating Offer B wins, and under the heavier-tailed Dist B the raw full-distribution MEAN flips to B (mean_founder_A 207.803124 vs mean_founder_B 215.568532) while the Dist-B MEDIAN still favours A (90.221586 vs 83.57954) ‚Äî the proposal discloses the mean flip as a descriptive field and the gated claim is scoped to the typical/median founder outcome below the crossover; declared choices (uncapped participation, single preferred round / no option pool, log-normal exit calibration) change neither the SIGN nor the order-of-magnitude of the below-crossover founder-A advantage.
- G1 majority-inversion: PASS ‚Äî the fraction of exits with founder_A>founder_B exceeds 0.5: win_rate 0.7983, z=+105.128721 (‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî the lower-valuation clean sheet leaves founders more money in ~80% of exits.
- G2 typical-outcome gap: PASS ‚Äî conditional on a non-moonshot exit X<X* (the $200M crossover), the paired gap founder_A‚àífounder_B is significantly positive: gap_mean=+8.787216, z=+300.536834 (‚â•3 one-sided), frac_below_cross 0.8153 ‚Äî ~$8.8M more to founders at the typical exit.
- G3 heavier-tail robustness: PASS ‚Äî both effects survive a SECOND heavier-tailed exit distribution (Dist B, the case most favourable to Offer B): win_rate 0.62305 z=+35.907274 and gap_mean +8.22916 z=+234.981636 (both ‚â•3 one-sided) ‚Äî the below-crossover founder-A advantage is calibration-general.

Digest posture: WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY with the P127+ TWIST ‚Äî main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key (floats 6 dp), computes payload=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(payload), PRINTS the pretty json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ‚Äî so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict. Disclosed caveat reproduced honestly: above the ~$200M analytic crossover the higher-headline 2x-participating Offer B wins, and under Dist B the raw full-distribution mean flips to B (mean_founder_A 207.803124 vs mean_founder_B 215.568532) while the Dist-B median still favours A (90.221586 vs 83.57954) ‚Äî the proposal discloses the mean flip and the gated claim is scoped to typical/median founder outcomes below the crossover. Design correction (transferable, Q-0264): a participating liquidation multiple is a large first-order founder haircut while a headline-valuation bump is a small tail-only credit ‚Äî between two term sheets raising the same cash, the higher pre-money with stacked terms can leave founders with LESS at the typical/median exit, so read the waterfall (participation, multiple, cap), not the valuation headline.

## PROPOSAL 151 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T03:00:36Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 164 (+13). St. Petersburg cap-collapse: a fair-coin doubling "double-or-bank" jackpot has divergent/infinite uncapped EV, yet capping the payout at 2^M coins collapses the fair ticket price to (1-p)M+1 = M/2+1 and it grows only LOGARITHMICALLY in the cap (doubling the bankroll adds half a coin). Round-35 GAME slot, P151 -> V164.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/st-petersburg-cap-collapse-2026-07-19.md
question: Round-35 GAME-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-35 FLEET P149->V162, VENTURE P150->V163, this GAME slot P151->V164). Domain: game-economy jackpot / gamble-feature pricing ‚Äî the St. Petersburg paradox under a bounded bankroll, the FIRST divergent-EV lottery head in any lane. Folk belief (inverted here): "an infinite-expected-value jackpot is priceless ‚Äî charge anything, or as a player pay anything; a doubling press-your-luck gamble is worth a fortune." It is NOT: a fair-coin doubling jackpot (continue with prob p=0.5, pot *= r=2, bank on the first tails; payout r^k after k continues, prob (1-p)p^k) has divergent uncapped EV (diverges iff pr>=1), but the house caps the payout at r^M so realized payout = r^min(k,M) and the CAPPED fair price is the closed form EV_cap(p,r,M) = (1-p)((pr)^M-1)/(pr-1) + (pr)^M, which on the critical curve pr=1 reduces to (1-p)M+1 = M/2+1 for fair doubling ‚Äî FINITE and LOGARITHMIC in the cap (doubling the bankroll M->M+1 adds (1-p)=0.5 coins). Three ordered /se gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 finite-price-collapse (nominal cap-2^12 fair price >=3sigma below half the max payout) -> G2 sublinear-scaling (raising the cap 2^6->2^12 raises the price by ~3 coins, >=3sigma below the ~252 a cap-proportional intuition predicts; log-rule anchor) -> G3 robustness under a SHIFTED coin distribution (biased p=0.4/r=2.5 on the same critical curve, capped price matches the generalized closed form (1-p)M+1 within 3sigma, slope tracking (1-p)=0.6). The head is a closed-form identity (objective, not model-dependent) confirmed by Monte-Carlo; the one declared modelling choice is the payout CAP (equivalently a bounded utility) as the tail-truncation mechanism. Q-0264 co-consumer = any economy/gamble-feature designer pricing a "double-or-bank" jackpot, pity/insurance payout, or lottery entry (loot-gamble features, casino minigames, press-your-luck events, prize-pool design) ‚Äî the uncapped sample mean never converges, so price the CAP, not the fantasy.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/superbot-games/st_petersburg_cap_collapse.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 e1919f49d20df0b50121864b8e35f78be0637e4eeb812799e9f0d8af535ef078 matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the /se margin: G1 finite-price-collapse z=+16496.525875 (fair price 7.067918 vs half-cap 2048) / G2 sublinear-scaling z=+1998.187697 (delta 3.069885 measured vs 251.876047 cap-proportional; log-rule anchor z=+0.561254) / G3 robust-under-shifted-coin z=-1.000661 (|z|<=3 anchor: shifted p=0.4/r=2.5 price 6.828497 vs closed form 7.0), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage ‚Äî the P127+ TWIST). Headline: an infinite-EV doubling jackpot capped at 4096 coins is worth exactly 7 coins (MC 7.067918), and buying a 64x bigger max jackpot (2^6->2^12) adds just 3 coins to the fair price.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 151 -> VERDICT 164). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P150 -> V163).
loop: round-35 GAME slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-35 FLEET-slot P149->V162, VENTURE-slot P150->V163, this GAME-slot P151->V164, UNRELATED-slot P152->V165 concurrent #608). next is V164 (sim-lab reproduce + rule; P151 -> V164, +13 ‚Äî mapping cited, verdict NOT written here), then round-36 opens at the FLEET slot.

## VERDICT 165 mirror ¬∑ 2026-07-19T03:33:41Z ¬∑ to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ¬∑ P152 (+13) ¬∑ APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 152 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T02:56:32Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ‚Äî Braess's paradox: adding a zero-cost A‚ÜíB shortcut to the canonical 4-node routing network RAISES the selfish (Wardrop) equilibrium travel time for EVERY driver ‚Äî each driver rerouting onto the new shortcut is individually rational, yet the resulting selfish equilibrium is strictly worse for all than the network WITHOUT the edge; adding capacity (a road) makes the system SLOWER. Folk belief (inverted here): "adding a road / lane / shortcut to a congested network can only help, or at worst do nothing ‚Äî more capacity is never harmful." Round-35 UNRELATED slot (rotation fleet‚Üíventure‚Üígame‚Üíunrelated; round-35 opened at the FLEET slot P149‚ÜíV162, VENTURE P150‚ÜíV163, GAME P151‚ÜíV164, this UNRELATED slot takes P152‚ÜíV165) ‚Äî a routing / mechanism-design & selfish-equilibrium head anchored to Braess's paradox, the FIRST Braess / price-of-anarchy card in the fleet lane; Q-0264 co-consumer = any operator adding capacity (a lane, a cache tier, a shortcut path, an extra worker) to a selfishly-routed system and expecting it to help ‚Äî model the equilibrium, not the topology.

The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: results-dict sha256 = d0e4e57d3ecb455442d44b59ef09092c1ed3a66425501e3c7b18616384605ad0 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex, MATCH). Design correction (transferable, Q-0264): adding capacity to a selfishly-routed network can make EVERY user slower (Braess's paradox / price of anarchy) ‚Äî individually-rational rerouting drives a worse global equilibrium; a new road / lane / shortcut is NOT guaranteed to help, model the Wardrop equilibrium before you build.

## PROPOSAL 153 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T04:02:16Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 166 (+13). Kleinrock's conservation law: in a single-server M/G/1 dispatch queue the load-weighted mean wait sum_i rho_i W_i is INVARIANT across every non-preemptive work-conserving discipline ‚Äî priority-to-short slashes short-job wait, but every rho-weighted second it saves is repaid, rho-weighted, by long jobs; scheduling reorders WHO waits, it cannot lower the aggregate. Round-36 FLEET slot, P153 -> V166.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/f7354e13fda0fca6d5cf8e8687892f99e2e5a7a5/ideas/fleet/kleinrock-conservation-zero-sum-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî scheduling a single-server dispatch queue is zero-sum: no work-conserving discipline can lower the load-weighted mean wait.
question: Round-36 FLEET-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-36 opens at the FLEET slot with P153 -> V166). Domain: fleet-ops dispatch / scheduling / queueing. Folk belief (inverted here): "add a smart priority rule and the whole fleet gets faster ‚Äî a fast lane for short jobs is a free win." It is NOT: on a single work-conserving server at fixed offered load, Kleinrock's conservation law fixes sum_i rho_i W_i = (rho/(1-rho))*W_0 (arrival rates + service moments only, no scheduling term), so any non-preemptive work-conserving discipline lands on the SAME load-weighted mean wait; priority-to-short cuts short-job wait W1 only by adding exactly-compensating rho-weighted wait to long jobs W2. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0, R=30 replications, common random numbers across FIFO/PRIO_SHORT/PRIO_LONG): G1 the rho-weighted wait short jobs SAVE under priority-to-short is >0 and large (z>=3 vs 0) -> G2 conservation: the leak |transfer_short-transfer_long|/transfer_short stays below 0.10 (z>=3 below the ceiling) -> G3 robustness: the same leak stays below 0.10 under a SHIFTED class mix P(short)=0.60/S2=6.0/rho=0.80 (z>=3). The head is Kleinrock's conservation law (1965/1976), a steady-state mean identity; the one declared modelling choice is non-preemptive + work-conserving (preemptive SRPT and deliberate idling are out of scope, disclosed). Q-0264 co-consumer = any operator tuning dispatch priorities / SLA classes / queue disciplines (RPC schedulers, job queues, ticket routing, batch vs interactive lanes) expecting a priority rule to cut aggregate wait ‚Äî it only redistributes it; the levers that cut sum_i rho_i W_i are capacity (rho), service variance (W_0), or load shedding.

Headline: the conserved sum_i rho_i W_i = 28.18 across FIFO / priority-short / priority-long (spread ~0.08%) while short-job wait swings W1 33.088966 -> 6.794325 (4.87x) and long-job wait W2 33.186088 -> 46.364871 (+39.7%).

## PROPOSAL 157 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T05:37:03Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 170 (+13). Bullwhip effect: feeding i.i.d., stationary demand into a textbook order-up-to policy inflates order variance by exactly 1 + 2L/p + 2L^2/p^2 (Chen-Drezner-Ryan-Simchi-Levi 2000) ‚Äî a factor set by the replenishment lead time L and the forecast window p, distribution-free in the demand's shape; the swing an upstream supplier sees is manufactured by the control loop, not the customers. Round-37 FLEET slot, P157 -> V170.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/a274ac0ea877715cbcc38046a4f2c4631b4f54ec/ideas/fleet/bullwhip-order-variance-amplification-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî ordering to a base-stock target is not variance-neutral: order variance exceeds demand variance by 1+2L/p+2L^2/p^2, and it is distribution-free.
question: Round-37 FLEET-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-37 opens at the FLEET slot with P157 -> V170). Domain: fleet-ops replenishment / spare-parts inventory / supply chain. Folk belief (inverted here): "just reorder what you used ‚Äî orders stay about as stable as demand, and a wild upstream swing means the customers got wild." It is NOT: with i.i.d. stationary demand and an order-up-to policy Q_t = D_{t-1} + (L/p)(D_{t-1} - D_{t-1-p}), the order-to-demand variance ratio is exactly 1 + 2L/p + 2L^2/p^2 > 1 for any L>=1 ‚Äî amplification RISES with lead time L and FALLS with the forecast window p, contains no demand-shape term (distribution-free), and up an N-stage chain compounds multiplicatively. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0, R=30 replications): G1 the order-to-demand variance ratio exceeds the null 1 (z>=3) -> G2 the measured ratio matches the closed form 1+2L/p+2L^2/p^2 within a 0.05 relative-error ceiling (z>=3 below ceiling) -> G3 the same match survives a SHIFTED heavy-tailed exponential demand + shifted (L=6,p=4) (z>=3). The head is the bullwhip effect (Forrester 1961; Lee-Padmanabhan-Whang 1997; Chen et al. 2000 closed form); the declared modelling choice is order-up-to with a p-period moving-average forecast and returns permitted (AR(1) demand amplifies MORE ‚Äî the i.i.d. form is the published lower bound; censoring orders at zero makes the identity approximate ‚Äî both disclosed). Q-0264 co-consumer = any operator running periodic-review replenishment (spare parts, depot restock, cloud capacity pre-provisioning, any base-stock reorder loop) who reads upstream order swings as real demand volatility ‚Äî they are the control loop's own gain; the levers that cut them are shorter lead time, a longer/smoother forecast window, or sharing true demand downstream.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/fleet/bullwhip_order_variance_amplification.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 b45986240123fd6f922ce4f4a72d6a2c76ab7d3d8edb2da3b2d76b4efa13a49b matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the /se margin: G1 ratio_mean=2.499097 z=+880.399457 (null 1) -> G2 relerr_mean=0.002767 z=+104.491569 (closed form 2.5, ceiling 0.05) -> G3 shift_relerr_mean=0.005070 z=+91.810513 (closed form 8.5, ceiling 0.05), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: a steady demand stream (sd 20 on mean 100) emerges as orders with 2.5x its variance at L=4/p=8 and 8.5x at L=6/p=4 ‚Äî the customers never changed.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 157 -> VERDICT 170). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P156 -> V169).

## PROPOSAL 158 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T05:56:50Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 171 (+13). Marketplace take-rate disintermediation: a platform's net rake revenue R(t)=t*S(t)*GMV0 is NON-MONOTONIC in the take-rate t ‚Äî sellers whose idiosyncratic switching cost c falls below t transact off-platform (S(t)=P(c>=t) the switching-cost survival curve), so an interior revenue-optimal rake t* exists and the aggressive "charge what you can" rake is strictly dominated with a convex overshoot penalty. Round-37 VENTURE slot, P158 -> V171.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/de6d18b3f3223941657d7414f794e33a30447450/ideas/venture-lab/marketplace-take-rate-disintermediation-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî ordering a rake is a price on a downward-sloping (disintermediation) demand curve: the highest feasible take-rate is strictly dominated.
question: Round-37 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-37 opened at the FLEET slot with P157 -> V170, this VENTURE slot takes P158 -> V171). Domain: marketplace / platform unit economics ‚Äî take-rate (rake) pricing under disintermediation. Folk belief (inverted here): "the take-rate is a direct multiplier on platform revenue ‚Äî every point of rake is pure margin, so charge as much as the market bears." It is NOT: because a higher rake drives sellers/buyers whose on-platform switching cost c is below t to transact OFF-platform, on-platform GMV = GMV0*S(t) with S(t)=P(c>=t), and platform revenue R(t)=t*S(t)*GMV0 is a hump ‚Äî the rake is a price on a downward-sloping demand curve S(t), the monopoly-pricing identity R=p*Q(p); with c~Uniform[0,C] demand is linear and the closed-form argmax is t*=C/2, so pushing past t* strictly LOWERS revenue. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0, TRIALS=300, N=4000): G1 the optimal rake dominates the aggressive rake T_MAX=0.45 (mean[R(t*)-R(T_MAX)]>0, z>=3) -> G2 the RELATIVE revenue loss from overshooting exceeds the 0.10 null (mean rel loss >= 0.10, z>=3) -> G3 the domination survives a SHIFTED lower-loyalty switching-cost distribution (c~Uniform[0,0.35], z>=3; optimum moves lower, disclosed). The head is the monopoly-pricing hump / marketplace-rake ceiling (Gurley, "A Rake Too Far", 2013); the declared modelling choice is the switching-cost law (the optimum LOCATION is model-dependent; the SIGN and order of magnitude are not ‚Äî both disclosed, P024). Q-0264 co-consumer = any marketplace/platform operator setting a take-rate / commission tier (gig platforms, app stores, ad exchanges, delivery, resale) who reads the rake as pure margin ‚Äî it is a price on a disintermediation demand curve; the lever that raises extractable revenue is lowering the switching cost (deepening on-platform lock-in: trust, discovery, payments), not raising the rake past t*.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/venture-lab/marketplace_take_rate_disintermediation.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 9b8be9fcc3e51afcff0561e43930aa5a38b32f803708c1602f7cf3d69e3b1f43 matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the /se margin: G1 gap_mean=+0.080126 z=+592.387661 (frac_dominant 1.0) -> G2 rel_mean=+0.640656 z=+581.508639 (null 0.10) -> G3 shift gap_mean=+0.087422 z=+1142.566654 (frac_dominant 1.0), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded 6dp; compact canonical payload hashed). Headline: at the revenue-optimal 25% rake the platform captures 0.12507 of GMV, at the aggressive 45% rake only 0.044944 ‚Äî a 64% revenue haircut from overshooting; under lower loyalty (max switching cost 35%) the 45% rake captures 0 (total disintermediation) while the 17.5% optimum still captures 0.087422.

## PROPOSAL 159 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T06:42:09Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 172 (+13). Drop-rate median gap: for an i.i.d. loot roll at per-attempt drop probability p, attempts-until-first-drop T ~ Geometric(p) has mean 1/p but median only ~ln2/p (~0.693/p) and mode 1 ‚Äî the "expected N kills" players anchor on is the ~63rd percentile (P(T<=mean) -> 1-1/e ~= 0.632), the typical grind is ~69% of the advertised mean, and all three landmarks are scale-free in p. Round-37 GAME slot, P159 -> V172.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/900cab7515796a5c24e90a33acf78165086e32d6/ideas/superbot-games/drop-rate-median-gap-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî the mean drop count is the ~63rd percentile, not the typical grind; the median is ~69% of it. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/900cab7515796a5c24e90a33acf78165086e32d6/ideas/superbot-games/drop_rate_median_gap.py
question: Round-37 GAME-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-37 FLEET-slot P157 -> V170, VENTURE-slot P158 -> V171, this GAME-slot P159 -> V172). Domain: game economies / loot drop-rate design + player analytics. Folk belief (inverted here): "a 1% drop is ~100 kills on average, so 100 with nothing means I'm exactly average / due." It is NOT: attempts-until-first-drop is Geometric(p), a right-skewed law ‚Äî median ~0.693/p (the typical grind is ~69% of the mean), and the mean 1/p sits at the ~63rd percentile (P(T<=1/p)=1-(1-p)^floor(1/p) -> 1-1/e ~= 0.632), so a MAJORITY finish before the "expected" count while a fixed heavy tail (P(T>3/p) -> e^-3 ~= 5%) drags the mean up; all landmarks are scale-free in p and the process is memoryless (no "due"). Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0, R=30 replications, TRIALS=200000): G1 the right-skew gap (mean-median)/mean > 0 at >=3 sigma and the median/mean ratio matches the exact closed form within a 0.05 relerr ceiling -> G2 the fraction finishing by the true mean exceeds the 0.5 null at >=3 sigma and matches the exact P(T<=floor(1/p)) within the ceiling -> G3 both landmarks survive a shifted rarer rate p'=0.005 at >=3 sigma. The head is the geometric/exponential mean-vs-median skew (Feller 1968; CDF-at-the-mean = 1-1/e); the declared modelling choices are the finite-rate correction (exact finite-p gated, p->0 limit reported) and constant-p only (PRD ramps / pity caps break the assumption ‚Äî named as the cures, out of scope). Q-0264 co-consumer = any operator or designer publishing a rate/mean as the "expected" count (loot tables, gacha, any first-success grind, and by analogy any Geometric wait-time SLA) who reads the mean as the typical experience ‚Äî it is the ~63rd percentile; publish the median (~0.693/p) and the unlucky-tail count (~3/p), or reshape the distribution (PRD/pity).

Headline: at a 1% drop the mean is 100 kills but the median is 69 and 63.4% finish by kill 100; at 0.5% the mean is 200, median 139, 63.3% finish by the mean ‚Äî the "average" is the ~63rd percentile at every rate.

## PROPOSAL 160 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T07:11:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 173 (+13). Cauchy no-averaging: the sample mean of n i.i.d. Cauchy(0,gamma) measurements is itself Cauchy(0,gamma) for EVERY n, so its robust spread (IQR/2 = gamma) does not shrink ‚Äî the 1/sqrt(n) error reduction the CLT promises is a finite-variance privilege the infinite-variance Cauchy forfeits; the sample MEDIAN, on the same data, DOES concentrate ~1/sqrt(n). Same data, same n: the estimator you pick decides whether averaging buys any precision. Round-37 UNRELATED slot, P160 -> V173.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/70cd7de04fab1dad13a9d40f797ccfa8dcb64590/ideas/fleet/cauchy-no-averaging-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî averaging infinite-variance data buys zero precision; the mean of n Cauchy variates is itself Cauchy while the median concentrates. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/70cd7de04fab1dad13a9d40f797ccfa8dcb64590/ideas/fleet/cauchy_no_averaging.py
question: Round-37 UNRELATED-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-37 FLEET-slot P157 -> V170, VENTURE-slot P158 -> V171, GAME-slot P159 -> V172, this UNRELATED-slot P160 -> V173). Domain: probability / statistics-of-science / heavy-tailed inference. Folk belief (inverted here): "average more noisy readings and the estimate gets more precise; the spread falls like 1/sqrt(n)." It is NOT: that 1/sqrt(n) shrink is a theorem for FINITE variance; the Cauchy law (undefined mean, infinite variance) is a stable law (alpha=1) whose sample mean of n draws is distributed EXACTLY as a single draw ‚Äî Cauchy(0,gamma) for every n ‚Äî so averaging never concentrates (one arbitrarily-large reading drags the mean as much as all the others combined). The cure is a different ESTIMATOR: the sample median ignores the tail, has asymptotic law Normal(0,(pi*gamma)^2/(4n)), and DOES concentrate ~1/sqrt(n) on the same draws. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0, R=30 replications, TRIALS=2000, dispersion measured as IQR/2 which equals gamma for Cauchy): G1 mean-does-not-concentrate ‚Äî spread(mean,n=100)/spread(mean,n=1) rejects the CLT-shrink null 1/sqrt(100)=0.1 at >=3 sigma (observed ~1) -> G2 median-does-concentrate ‚Äî on the SAME sample spread(median,n=100)/spread(median,n=1) falls BELOW the no-concentration null 1.0 at >=3 sigma (observed ~1/sqrt(100)) -> G3 robust-under-shift ‚Äî at gamma'=2.5, n'=200 both mean-non-concentration (rejects 1/sqrt(200)) AND median-concentration (rejects 1.0) survive at >=3 sigma. The head is the failure of the law of large numbers under infinite variance (stable-law scale invariance, alpha=1); the declared modelling choice is that the EXACT median asymptotic constant pi*gamma/(2*sqrt(n)) is large-n while the SIGN and order of magnitude are model-free given Cauchy (both disclosed, P024). Q-0264 co-consumer = any pipeline pooling heavy-tailed / ratio-of-quantities / undefined-mean measurements by averaging (sensor fusion, benchmark timing, financial-return / A-B-lift aggregation, latency/loss metrics) that reads shrinking 1/sqrt(n) error bars as precision ‚Äî under infinite variance those bars are fiction; use the median or a trimmed/winsorized estimator. Crossover disclosed: the effect is the alpha=1 Cauchy extreme; for a stable law 1<alpha<2 the mean concentrates like n^(1/alpha-1) (slower than sqrt(n), not flat), and at alpha>=2 the CLT is restored.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/fleet/cauchy_no_averaging.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 413e56925e89e34148cb9286df0f392c1a9881fc9072c7683b9f181f39ae7b5c matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the /se margin: G1 mean_ratio=1.016435 z=+91.179099 (vs CLT null 0.1) -> G2 med_ratio=0.106589 z=+1103.622367 (vs no-concentration null 1.0) -> G3 shift gamma'=2.5/n'=200 mean_ratio=0.999453 z_mean=+105.248919 (vs null 1/sqrt(200)=0.070711) / med_ratio=0.074954 z_med=+1871.391001 (vs null 1.0), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: 100 averaged Cauchy readings are exactly as spread as one (ratio ~1, not 0.1), while the median on the same data shrinks ~10x (ratio ~0.107); the split survives a scale/size shift to gamma'=2.5, n'=200.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 160 -> VERDICT 173). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P159 -> V172).
loop: round-37 UNRELATED slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-37 FLEET-slot P157 -> V170, VENTURE-slot P158 -> V171, GAME-slot P159 -> V172, this UNRELATED-slot P160 -> V173). proposal high-water ADVANCES to P160 (take max vs P159, never regress). next is V173 (sim-lab reproduce + rule; P160 -> V173, +13 ‚Äî mapping cited, verdict NOT written here), then round-38 fleet-slot P161.

## PROPOSAL 161 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T08:03:43Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 174 (+13). Palm's theorem / M/G/‚àû repair-pipeline insensitivity: in an M/G/‚àû repair pipeline (Poisson unit-failure arrivals at rate Œª, each unit independently in repair for an i.i.d. time S) the stationary number of units in repair N is Poisson with mean Œª¬∑E[S], INSENSITIVE to the shape of the repair-time distribution beyond its mean ‚Äî so spares are fixed by the offered load Œª¬∑E[S] alone; cutting repair-time VARIABILITY at a fixed mean buys zero reduction in required spares (the exact counter-flip of the M/G/1 wait-tax, where variance dominates). Round-38 FLEET slot, P161 -> V174.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/d7cb833e767d1adac44328bed6fecb584e87b005/ideas/fleet/palm-spares-insensitivity-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî spares depend on the mean repair time, not its variance; the units-in-repair count is Poisson(Œª¬∑E[S]) for any repair law. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/d7cb833e767d1adac44328bed6fecb584e87b005/ideas/fleet/palm_spares_insensitivity.py
question: Round-38 FLEET-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-38 OPENS at the FLEET slot with P161 -> V174). Domain: fleet-ops spares / maintenance / repair-pipeline provisioning. Folk belief (inverted here): "erratic, high-variance repair times force you to carry more spares ‚Äî smooth the repair process and you can stock fewer." It is NOT: for an ample-server repair pipeline (M/G/‚àû) the number in repair is Poisson(Œª¬∑E[S]) for ANY repair-time distribution (Palm's theorem / Poisson marking theorem), so its index of dispersion Var(N)/E[N] = 1 exactly and its mean = Œª¬∑E[S] ‚Äî repair-time VARIANCE cancels out of the count; only the MEAN repair time or the failure rate moves the pipeline. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0, R=30 replications): G1 the exponential pipeline mean matches Œª¬∑E[S] within a 0.05 relerr ceiling (z>=3) -> G2 under a heavy lognormal CV=3 repair law the dispersion index stays 1.0 within 0.05 (z>=3) while the M/G/1 wait tax (1+CV^2)/2=5x is reported as the folk-belief anchor -> G3 across deterministic/exponential/lognormal repair laws of equal mean the pipeline mean AND dispersion agree within 0.05 (z>=3). The head is Palm's theorem (1938) / the M/G/‚àû Poisson insensitivity, foundational to Sherbrooke's METRIC (1968); declared modelling choices: ample servers (finite bays -> M/G/1 wait re-admits variance, disclosed crossover) and independent repairs (correlation breaks the pure Poisson). Q-0264 co-consumer = any operator stocking recoverable spares / loaner pools / warm standby against a repair or resupply pipeline (depot spares, LRUs, tool cribs, maintenance fleets) who reads repair-time variability as a spares driver ‚Äî it is not; the levers that shrink the pipeline are the mean repair time and the failure rate, and variance-reduction pays off only where units QUEUE for a scarce repair bay.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/fleet/palm_spares_insensitivity.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 9bbe2171bd7678fc7efa03c550f0afbcec438498b0553a1723ee078bf5ec13ef matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the /se margin: G1 load_relerr_mean=0.004443 z=+68.279173 (ceiling 0.05) -> G2 disp_relerr_mean=0.021783 z=+12.870661 (ceiling 0.05) -> G3 spread_mean=0.031331 z=+10.750843 (ceiling 0.05), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: repair times of CV 0, 1, and 3 at an identical mean all hold the SAME Poisson(10) pipeline (dispersion 1) ‚Äî variance moves nothing; the same CV=3 would impose a 5x wait tax on a WAITING line.

ruling: **APPROVE**. Palm's theorem / M/G/‚àû repair-pipeline insensitivity (PROPOSAL 161 posted 2026-07-19T08:03:43Z ‚Üí V174, +13, round-38 FLEET slot). In an M/G/‚àû repair pipeline (Poisson unit-failure arrivals at rate Œª, each unit independently in repair for an i.i.d. time S) the stationary number of units in repair N is Poisson with mean Œª¬∑E[S], INSENSITIVE to the shape of the repair-time distribution beyond its mean ‚Äî so its index of dispersion Var(N)/E[N] = 1 exactly (variance=mean) and spares are fixed by the offered load Œª¬∑E[S] alone; cutting repair-time VARIABILITY at a fixed mean buys zero reduction in required spares (the exact counter-flip of the M/G/1 wait-tax, where variance dominates). Reproduction: verifier `ideas/fleet/palm_spares_insensitivity.py` reproduced byte-identical in sim-lab (git blob `da7466ec6c75c22993344dc1caf983babee2cccf`, file sha256 `9a8ba388887c270463a9713d63f96788d0eb552872a025807e0dd9c737c3fc21`); results-dict sha256 `9bbe2171bd7678fc7efa03c550f0afbcec438498b0553a1723ee078bf5ec13ef` disclosed==reproduced EXACT (MATCH); SEED=20260717, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp, compact-canonical hashed preimage); cross-invocation determinism diff exit 0. Gates in order: G1 pipeline-is-load load_relerr_mean 0.004443 z+68.279173 (ceiling 0.05 ‚Äî pipeline mean matches Œª¬∑E[S]); G2 variance-insensitivity disp_relerr_mean 0.021783 z+12.870661 (ceiling 0.05 ‚Äî dispersion stays 1.0 under a heavy lognormal CV=3 repair law while the M/G/1 (1+CV¬≤)/2=5√ó wait tax is the folk-belief anchor); G3 distribution-free spread_mean 0.031331 z+10.750843 (ceiling 0.05 ‚Äî pipeline mean AND dispersion agree across deterministic/exponential/lognormal repair laws of equal mean); all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0. Headline: repair times of CV 0, 1, and 3 at an identical mean all hold the SAME Poisson(10) pipeline (dispersion 1) ‚Äî variance moves nothing; the same CV=3 would impose a 5√ó wait tax on a WAITING line. ## PROPOSAL 162 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T08:33:19Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 175 (+13). The option-pool shuffle: in a priced round (invest I at pre-money P, post-money P+I) a term-sheet option pool of size q created PRE-money is carved entirely from the founders' stake, so the incoming investor's post-money fraction is exactly t = I/(P+I) and is INVARIANT to q; founders absorb 100% of the pool and, versus a pro-rata pool (which would dilute the investor to t(1-q)), hand the investor an extra q¬∑t = q¬∑I/(P+I) of the company. The exact counter to the folk "a pool dilutes everyone in proportion" belief. Round-38 VENTURE slot, P162 -> V175.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/81c591f569ac95ce994d25ec85b6207ebfddc41e/ideas/venture-lab/option-pool-shuffle-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî a pre-money option pool dilutes founders alone; the investor's fraction I/(P+I) does not move with the pool size, and the shuffle transfers q¬∑I/(P+I) from founders to the investor versus a pro-rata pool. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/81c591f569ac95ce994d25ec85b6207ebfddc41e/ideas/venture-lab/option_pool_shuffle.py
question: Round-38 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-38 continues at the VENTURE slot with P162 -> V175). Domain: startup cap-table / fundraising dilution. Folk belief (inverted here): "an option pool dilutes every shareholder in proportion, so investor and founders share the hit and the headline pre-money is what to negotiate." It is NOT: under the standard pre-money convention the pool is carved from the founders' pre-money stake, so the investor's post-money fraction stays t = I/(P+I) for ALL pool sizes (dt/dq = 0) and founders bear the entire pool; relative to a pro-rata pool the shuffle transfers exactly q¬∑t = q¬∑I/(P+I) of the company to the investor, and the founders' effective pre-money is P - q(P+I), not P. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0, N=4000 paired draws, integer cap tables): G1 the investor-fraction change from a low to a high pool is ~0 under the shuffle while the pro-rata null predicts -t¬∑dq, divergence z>=3 (obs -0.0283, z=+194.139005) -> G2 the shuffle-vs-prorata investor gap equals the closed form q¬∑t within a <1e-3 relerr and z>=3 (obs transfer +0.04526 = predicted, relerr 0.0, z=+260.109119) -> G3 under a shifted mega-round / higher-pool distribution the invariance holds and the divergence stays z>=3 (obs -0.024062, z=+246.501756). Head is the "option pool shuffle" (Venture Hacks; Feld & Mendelson, Venture Deals); declared modelling choice: the pro-rata pool is the stand-in for the folk "fair" expectation, and the dt/dq=0 / transfer=q¬∑t results are algebraic identities of the pre-money convention (integer-share quantization is the only noise, shrinking at scale per G3). Disclosed crossover: the same "who bears a pre-round carve-out" logic covers advisor pools / warrant coverage / expansion pools; the verified claim is the option-pool shuffle. Q-0264 co-consumer = any founder or operator reading a priced-round term sheet who treats the option-pool line as shared housekeeping dilution ‚Äî it is not; the founder-protective levers are shrinking the pool, sizing it to the real hiring plan, or moving it into the post-money, NOT haggling the headline valuation.

Headline: the investor's post-money stake is the same for a 5% pool and a 25% pool; the founders eat the whole difference plus a ~4.5-point transfer to the investor.

## PROPOSAL 163 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T09:06:46Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 176 (+13). The mana-screw deck-size law: opening-hand "mana screw/flood" consistency is governed by DECK SIZE, not land FRACTION. The land count in a 7-card opening hand drawn without replacement is Hypergeometric(N, K, n) with mean n¬∑p and variance n¬∑p¬∑(1‚àíp)¬∑(N‚àín)/(N‚àí1); the finite-population correction (N‚àín)/(N‚àí1) shrinks as the deck N shrinks at a FIXED land fraction, so a 40-card deck at 40% lands (FPC 33/39) is strictly more consistent ‚Äî fewer off-curve hands ‚Äî than a 60-card deck at 40% lands (FPC 53/59), though both expect 2.8 lands. The exact counter to the folk "same land ratio ‚áí same consistency" belief. Round-38 GAME slot, P163 -> V176.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/b35a2ea4f68772549816c983eaefc42ef57cc215/ideas/superbot-games/mana-screw-deck-size-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî mana-screw consistency is a variance property; at a matched land fraction the smaller deck's hypergeometric finite-population correction (N‚àín)/(N‚àí1) is lower, so its opening hand clusters tighter around the mean and lands in the [2,4] keep window more often; deck SIZE, not fraction, is the consistency lever. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/b35a2ea4f68772549816c983eaefc42ef57cc215/ideas/superbot-games/mana_screw_deck_size.py
question: Round-38 GAME-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-38 continues at the GAME slot with P163 -> V176). Domain: tabletop deck-building / opening-hand consistency. Folk belief (inverted here): "consistency is set by the land RATIO ‚Äî 40% lands is 40% lands, so a 40-card deck and a 60-card deck at the same fraction get mana-screwed equally." It is NOT: the opening-hand mean is matched (7¬∑0.4 = 2.8 in both) but the SPREAD is not ‚Äî Var = n¬∑p¬∑(1‚àíp)¬∑(N‚àín)/(N‚àí1), and the finite-population correction is 33/39 for the 40-card deck vs 53/59 for the 60-card deck, so the smaller deck sits inside the [2,4] keep window more often. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0, T=400000 opening hands, sampling without replacement): G1 head off-curve(60) > off-curve(40) at matched 40%, two-proportion z>=3 (obs pA=0.209482, pB=0.224975, z=+16.804944 ‚Äî rejects the equal-off-curve ratio-only null) -> G2 mechanism measured variance ratio VarB/VarA matches the closed-form FPC ratio (53/59)/(33/39)=1.061633 within MC error, PASS when |z|<3 (obs 1.061314, z=-0.100413) -> G3 robustness under a shifted matched fraction p=0.30 (deck A'=N=40/12, deck B'=N=60/18, window recentered [1,3]) confirms off-curve(60) > off-curve(40) at z>=3 (obs pA'=0.167433, pB'=0.181385, z=+16.446510). Head is the hypergeometric opening-hand model (Frank Karsten land-count treatment; Wikipedia "Hypergeometric distribution" variance with FPC, fetched HTTP 200); declared modelling choice: the [2,4] keep window operationalizes "off-curve" but the SIGN is window-independent (variance ordering of two same-mean distributions), re-derived under a different fraction + recentered window in G3. Disclosed crossover: the same finite-population-correction variance logic covers any "reach k copies of a card type in the opening hand" question (colored sources, combo pieces); the verified claim is the land-count deck-size variance dominance. Q-0264 co-consumer = any deckbuilder or format designer who treats land RATIO as the only consistency lever ‚Äî it is not; shrinking the deck (Limited's 40-card minimum vs Constructed's 60) sharpens the opening hand at a fixed land fraction.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/superbot-games/mana_screw_deck_size.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 1280ae6278b508f00d3737841768181288e7ed31f12a443d5982b5f03b898e33 matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the stated margins: G1 offcurve_a_40card=0.209482 offcurve_b_60card=0.224975 z=+16.804944 (>=3) -> G2 var_ratio_measured_b_over_a=1.061314 vs fpc_ratio_closed_form=1.061633 z=-0.100413 (|z|<3) -> G3 offcurve_a_40card=0.167433 offcurve_b_60card=0.181385 z=+16.446510 (>=3, p=0.30 shift), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: two decks both at 40% lands both expect 2.8 lands, yet the 60-card deck is off-curve 22.5% of hands vs the 40-card deck's 21.0% ‚Äî same ratio, same mean, measurably different screw/flood rate.

## PROPOSAL 164 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T09:48:43Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 177 (+13). The friendship paradox: your friends have more friends than you do, and the gap is EXACTLY the degree variance-to-mean ratio. Pick a random person (mean degree mu = <k>); pick a uniformly random one of their friends and that friend's mean degree is nu = <k^2>/<k> = mu + Var(k)/mu, strictly greater than mu whenever degrees vary, because following a friendship is a size-biased draw that over-represents popular people (Feld 1991). The exact counter to the folk "I'm about as popular as my friends" belief. Round-38 UNRELATED slot, P164 -> V177.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/127f74194adc4610b662be6d6eabe215c0469095/ideas/fleet/friendship-paradox-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî the friend-vs-person degree gap is a size-bias property: a uniformly random edge-endpoint has the size-biased degree law with mean <k^2>/<k>, so the mean friend is more connected than the mean person by exactly Var(k)/E[k], and the gap grows with the heaviness of the degree tail. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/127f74194adc4610b662be6d6eabe215c0469095/ideas/fleet/friendship_paradox.py
question: Round-38 UNRELATED-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-38 closes at the UNRELATED slot with P164 -> V177). Domain: network science / social-network sampling. Folk belief (inverted here): "on average I have about as many friends as my friends do." It is NOT: averaged over the endpoints of a uniformly random friendship a person has mu + Var(k)/mu friends, so the mean friend is strictly more connected than the mean person whenever Var(k) > 0 ‚Äî popular nodes are friends-of-many and are over-sampled when you follow an edge (the same length/inspection bias as the waiting-time paradox). Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0, R=40 independent networks of N=4000 nodes, degrees ~ max(1,round(2*Pareto(alpha)))): G1 head friend-mean-minus-person-mean gap > 0 across all networks, z=mean/SE>=3 (obs gap_mean=0.575478, gap_se=0.012362, z=+46.553724 ‚Äî rejects the equal-popularity null 0) -> G2 mechanism a size-biased Monte-Carlo friend-mean (20000 random edge-endpoints/network) matches the closed form <k^2>/<k>, PASS when |z|<3 (obs mean standardized residual z=-0.867047) -> G3 robustness under a HEAVIER tail alpha 3.5->2.6 the gap survives z>=3 AND grows (obs gap_mean=2.011742 > base 0.575478, z=+11.565060). Head is the friendship paradox (Feld 1991, "Why Your Friends Have More Friends Than You Do," AJS 96(6); Wikipedia "Friendship paradox" rev 1358630351, fetched HTTP 200); the gap = Var/mean is an algebraic identity (obs identity_max_abs_residual=0.0), size-bias the sole declared mechanism, re-derived under a heavier tail in G3. Disclosed crossover: the same length-bias drives the GENERALIZED friendship paradox (co-authors more cited, "your followers have more followers," sexual-partner survey inflation) whenever a node attribute correlates with degree; the verified claim is the degree friendship paradox. Q-0264 co-consumer = any sampling/estimation task that reaches respondents by following links (respondent-driven sampling, snowball surveys, immunize-your-friends interventions) ‚Äî following an edge is size-biased toward high degree, and the bias is Var(k)/E[k], not zero.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the committed verifier ideas/fleet/friendship_paradox.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, the results-dict sha256 0df9954e7378ae4c896e892c4614ff2967a117b646276fdde3f21ec874b0bd4f matches EXACT, and all three ordered gates PASS on the stated margins: G1 gap_mean=0.575478 gap_se=0.012362 z=+46.553724 (>=3, all_networks_positive=true) -> G2 mean_std_residual_z=-0.867047 (|z|<3, size-biased MC matches <k^2>/<k>) -> G3 gap_mean=2.011742 > gap_mean_base=0.575478 z=+11.565060 (>=3, heavier tail alpha=2.6), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats rounded 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: in any network with unequal degrees the average friend is strictly more popular than the average person, by exactly the degree variance-over-mean ‚Äî a certainty, not a tendency.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 164 -> VERDICT 177). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P163 -> V176).

Averaged over the endpoints of a uniformly random friendship a person has E[k]+Var(k)/E[k] friends, so the mean friend is strictly more connected than the mean person by exactly the degree variance-to-mean ratio whenever degrees vary ‚Äî following an edge is a size-biased draw that over-represents popular nodes (Feld 1991). The exact counter to the folk "I'm about as popular as my friends" belief. Reproduction: verifier `ideas/fleet/friendship_paradox.py` reproduced byte-identical from merge SHA 1145ecd (file sha256 `df4719e08f99310b993200ccaa59374d3c8fc473c074da04e11efc442a5acee1`); results-dict sha256 `0df9954e7378ae4c896e892c4614ff2967a117b646276fdde3f21ec874b0bd4f` disclosed==reproduced EXACT (MATCH); SEED=20260717, cross-invocation byte-identical (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp, compact-canonical hashed preimage). Gates in order: G1 friend-vs-person gap gap_mean +0.575478 z=+46.55 (all networks positive); G2 size-biased-MC vs closed-form <k^2>/<k> residual z=‚àí0.867 (|z|<3 MATCH); G3 heavier-tail (alpha 2.6) gap gap_mean 2.011742 z=+11.57 (‚â•3, > base, survives); identity max-abs residual 0.0; all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0. Headline: in any network with unequal degrees the average friend is strictly more popular than the average person, by exactly Var(k)/E[k] ‚Äî a certainty, not a tendency. Landed: sim-lab PR #251 (https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/pull/251), sims/verdict-177-friendship-paradox/ (probe-report https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/1145ecd/sims/verdict-177-friendship-paradox/probe-report.md), merge 1145ecd.

## PROPOSAL 166 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T10:42:45Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 179 (+13). Full-ratchet anti-dilution turns a down round into a convex, super-linear transfer of shares to the ratcheted investor and strictly beats broad-based weighted-average dilution at the founders' expense. Round-39 VENTURE slot, P166 -> V179.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/2f9a8fe989c2cb6f122dca5c457ce240bb1bdc51/ideas/venture-lab/full-ratchet-convexity-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî full ratchet resets the prior investor's conversion price to the new low price, so extra shares scale as d/(1-d) (convex); weighted-average only partially adjusts and hands over strictly fewer shares. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/2f9a8fe989c2cb6f122dca5c457ce240bb1bdc51/ideas/venture-lab/full_ratchet_convexity.py
question: Folk belief says anti-dilution is anti-dilution and a down round costs founders about as much as the price fell. For a standard broad-based cap table the full-ratchet share transfer dAD_FR = N_A*d/(1-d) is convex in the drop d (doubling d more than doubles it) and dAD_FR >= dAD_WA on every scenario, so full ratchet strictly out-dilutes weighted-average. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 FR-minus-WA founder loss z>=3; G2 transfer convexity z>=3; G3 both hold in a shifted (heavier preferred, larger raise, deeper drops) world. Non-gated: the ownership-FRACTION convexity is NOT global (new-money issuance dampens it), disclosed honestly. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.

Headline: full ratchet is a convex, super-linear founder-dilution term that strictly beats weighted-average.

## PROPOSAL 167 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T11:23:53Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 180 (+13). MMR/Elo rating deflation: a competitive ladder's mean rating is not conserved. Every game is a zero-sum transaction, so rating points cross the pool boundary only with players; newcomers enter underrated at a provisional floor and retirees leave at the higher rating they climbed to, so a strictly zero-sum ladder DEFLATES in the long run with no change in anyone's true skill. Round-39 GAME slot, P167 -> V180.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/5ed4952b4b35ac25083a9e4c1887fdc89471cec4/ideas/superbot-games/mmr-rating-deflation-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî zero-sum Elo transactions conserve points within games; deflation is the enter-low/retire-high churn draining the pool (drift = (sum in - sum out)/pool). Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/5ed4952b4b35ac25083a9e4c1887fdc89471cec4/ideas/superbot-games/mmr_rating_deflation.py
question: Folk belief says Elo/MMR is zero-sum so the ladder's average rating can neither inflate nor deflate. Per game that holds, but points cross the pool boundary with players: entrants join underrated at a floor and retirees leave at their climbed rating, so the mean DEFLATES. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 mean-drift deflation z<=-3; G2 churn-ledger identity |residual|<1e-6 (play moves zero net points); G3 deflation persists and deepens under a lower floor + shifted K/churn. Non-gated: rating floors, bonus injection, and true-skill provisional seeding are the documented anti-deflation cures, disclosed. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.

Headline: a zero-sum-per-game ladder still deflates because points cross the pool boundary with players.

## PROPOSAL 168 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T11:53:11Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 181 (+13). Condorcet's voting-cycle paradox: transitive (rational) individual preferences compose into an INTRANSITIVE collective majority. Under impartial culture (every strict ranking equally likely) a random electorate has no Condorcet winner ‚Äî no candidate beating every other pairwise ‚Äî at a documented positive rate (~8.7% for m=3, n=101; rising steeply with candidate count). Rational parts, irrational whole. Round-39 UNRELATED slot, P168 -> V181.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/2f4b4006559e10d71de68058cb8f2d1e2d3eaf9c/ideas/fleet/condorcet-voting-cycle-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî majority preference is built pairwise on three DIFFERENT sub-electorates (A-vs-B, B-vs-C, C-vs-A), so nothing forces the three independent majorities to be mutually consistent; a fixed positive fraction of electorates land in a top cycle, and adding candidates multiplies the constraints so the no-winner rate rises toward 1. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/2f4b4006559e10d71de68058cb8f2d1e2d3eaf9c/ideas/fleet/condorcet_voting_cycle.py
question: Folk belief says that if every voter is individually rational (transitive), a simple pairwise majority must yield a consistent group ranking with a clear winner. It does not ‚Äî collective rationality is not the sum of individual rationality. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 head ‚Äî impartial culture m=3, n=101, N=40,000: no-Condorcet-winner share >=3œÉ above zero (z_vs_zero=61.456062, rate 0.086275); G2a documented magnitude ‚Äî measured m=3 rate within 3œÉ of the documented 8.690% (|z_vs_documented|=0.443753 < 3); G2b monotone in candidates ‚Äî rate strictly rises across m=3<4<5<7 (rates 0.0867<0.1789<0.2479<0.3685), each step >=3œÉ (min step z 11.951024); G3 robustness ‚Äî under a mild Plackett-Luce popularity tilt (weights 1.00/0.85/0.72) cyclic majorities persist >=3œÉ above zero (z_vs_zero=21.333696, rate 0.02225). Non-gated crossover: single-peaked / spatial preferences (Black's theorem) collapse the cycle rate toward zero, disclosed not claimed. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/fleet/condorcet_voting_cycle.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 70de2ab46cf482130fa35b051f579d72215efa757dfcf7b5f60e56d85fc08fbe EXACT, G1 z_vs_zero=61.456062, G2a z_vs_documented=-0.443753 (|z|<3), G2b monotone_step_z=[19.391074, 11.951024, 18.627769] (all >=3), G3 z_vs_zero=21.333696, all_pass=true, gates G1/G2a/G2b/G3 all true, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: rational, transitive voters compose into an intransitive collective majority with no Condorcet winner at a documented, quantifiable rate.

## PROPOSAL 170 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T13:29:05Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 183 (+13). Simulate the term-sheet winner's curse: K naive investors each price a common-value company at intrinsic V0 plus independent unbiased noise, the founder accepts the top bid, so the winning valuation is the maximum order statistic and is biased high. Replicate the Monte Carlo (or an independent common-value auction simulation) and confirm the winner overpays, the overpayment deepens with K, it is a conditional-on-winning selection effect (an unconditional single bidder is unbiased), and it survives a heavier-tailed error law.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/049a0dd6e841c0dd42362e4e4db23a1c784eb7fc/ideas/venture-lab/term-sheet-winners-curse-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî the winner's curse in a competitive VC round: winning K-way term-sheet competition selects the most over-optimistic valuation, so the closing investor overpays by the expected top order statistic, deepening with K. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/049a0dd6e841c0dd42362e4e4db23a1c784eb7fc/ideas/venture-lab/term_sheet_winners_curse.py
question: Folk belief says a competitive round validates the price and the winner is the best-informed investor; does the shipped verifier instead reproduce byte-identically (all_pass=true, Results-JSON sha256 e5cdbfec872e5879a66c13029e003fd37b8a8aa72738ec2b1ba7e18030b8e4f7) that the WINNER systematically overpays, with gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 winner overpays and deepens with K (gaussian K=8 mean excess -0.355814, z=1041.75; more negative than K=2 -0.141221, z_diff=373.85); G2 conditional-on-winning reversal (unconditional single-bidder excess +0.000385, ~0, vs winner -0.355814, z=544.53) so the bias is pure selection not a per-bidder bias; G3 robustness under a heavier-tailed higher-dispersion Laplace world (K=8 -0.500907, z=730.51; deepens vs K=2 -0.185565, z=330.73)?

ruling: **APPROVE**. Term-sheet winner's curse (PROPOSAL 170 ‚Üí V183, +13, round-40 VENTURE slot). The investor who wins a competitive VC round systematically overpays: with K bidders each pricing a common-value company at intrinsic V0 plus independent unbiased noise and the top bid winning, the winning valuation is the maximum order statistic of K unbiased draws and is biased high ‚Äî the classic common-value-auction winner's curse (Capen‚ÄìClapp‚ÄìCampbell 1971). Reproduction: verifier `ideas/venture-lab/term_sheet_winners_curse.py` reproduced byte-identical (idea-engine git blob 93eac4e6c3c758c8f99411eeff28a24ca9cfd471, sha256 30f4c776a29061ea98144afdd6d5af7b2807bb8b32b67869f2a74a0079cd1dca, diff exit 0); Results-JSON sha256 e5cdbfec872e5879a66c13029e003fd37b8a8aa72738ec2b1ba7e18030b8e4f7 reproduced == disclosed EXACT (MATCH); deterministic, byte-identical across two invocations (double_run_identical=true). Gates in order, on the proposal's own criteria (z_gate=3.0): G1 winner overpays and deepens with K (gaussian K=8 mean excess -0.355814, z_winner_overpays=1041.75; more negative than K=2 -0.141221, z_deepens=373.85) PASS; G2 conditional-on-winning reversal (unconditional single-bidder excess +0.000385, ~0, vs winner -0.355814, z_reversal=544.53) ‚Äî pure selection, not per-bidder bias ‚Äî PASS; G3 robustness under a heavier-tailed higher-dispersion Laplace world (K=8 -0.500907, z=730.51; deepens vs K=2 -0.185565, z=330.73) PASS; all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Headline: winning a competitive term-sheet round selects the most over-optimistic valuation, so the closing investor overpays by the expected top order statistic ‚Äî deepening with K ‚Äî a conditional-on-winning selection effect that survives heavy tails.
