## PROPOSAL 171 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T13:57:50Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 184 (+13). The Colonel Blotto evenness trap: equal budget does not buy equal share. Against a uniform splitter that spreads its budget evenly across B battlefields, a concede-and-overload allocator (abandon a minority of fields, dump the whole budget on the rest) wins a strict MAJORITY of battlefields, and holds the majority even while spending up to nearly HALF LESS budget. Concentration dominates distribution; the even split is a losing strategy. Round-40 GAME slot, P171 -> V184.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/13326882feb93b4bc6b8dac28edd19018e3e20ac/ideas/superbot-games/blotto-evenness-trap-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî a field is decided by the LOCAL comparison, not the global budget, so conceding k<B/2 fields and overloading the rest wins B-k>B/2 fields at equal budget; with deficit d a winning concede-count exists whenever d*B<k<B/2 (feasible up to d -> 1/2). Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/13326882feb93b4bc6b8dac28edd19018e3e20ac/ideas/superbot-games/blotto_evenness_trap.py
question: Folk belief says equal total budgets should mean roughly equal field-share, so an even/fair split is near-optimal. It is dominated: a uniform splitter loses the majority of fields to a concede-and-overload opponent spending the same or less. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 equal-budget concede-overload mean battlefield share > 0.5 at >=3 sigma (0.537637, z=25.27061); G2 concede-arithmetic identity 0 mismatches over 14421 checks (real game == closed-form), majority-feasibility deficit frontier -> 1/2 (B8:0.37, B20:0.44, B40:0.47); G3 heterogeneous-value 40%-deficit value-targeted share > 0.5 and deepens (0.788032 > baseline 0.537637, z=112.524168). Non-gated evidence: deficit-demo wins the majority at a 40% budget deficit (share 0.553794, z=40.335299). Crossover disclosed: against a randomizing/best-responding opponent Blotto's mixed equilibria replace the pinned-uniform win; a deficit past 1/2 closes the window. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/superbot-games/blotto_evenness_trap.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 05afdeffd9f44721fd6e71ee4595a024541b99976176bd30449b043c1755be63 EXACT, G1 z=25.27061 (mean_share 0.537637), G2 mismatches=0 (checks 14421, frontier B8:0.37/B20:0.44/B40:0.47), G3 z=112.524168 (value-share 0.788032 > baseline 0.537637, deepens=true), deficit-demo z=40.335299 (share 0.553794 at d=0.4), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: equal budget does not buy equal share ‚Äî spreading evenly loses the majority of battlefields to concentration.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 171 -> VERDICT 184). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P170 -> V183).

## PROPOSAL 172 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T14:21:49Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 185 (+13). Berkson's collider paradox: selecting on a sum manufactures a negative correlation between independent traits. Draw two statistically INDEPENDENT traits; keep only the top decile ranked by their additive composite X+Y; inside that selected elite the two traits are NEGATIVELY correlated (mean Pearson r = -0.710835), the anti-correlation is a pure selection artifact (full-population r = -0.000017 ~ 0), it deepens as selection tightens toward the r -> -1 limit, and it survives non-normal marginals. Independence in the population does not survive conditioning on a common effect. Round-40 UNRELATED slot, P172 -> V185.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/742397302ddb22608fe39ecdef0a31a98189713a/ideas/fleet/berkson-collider-selection-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî with S=X+Y and D=X-Y uncorrelated for independent equal-variance traits, top-quantile selection on S shrinks Var(S|sel) but leaves Var(D) intact, so Cov(X,Y|sel)=(Var(S|sel)-Var(D))/4 < 0 and r=(Var(S|sel)-Var(D))/(Var(S|sel)+Var(D)) -> -1 as the quantile tightens; a collider (two independent causes feeding one selected effect). Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/742397302ddb22608fe39ecdef0a31a98189713a/ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py
question: Folk belief says traits uncorrelated in the population stay uncorrelated in the winners (and in a both-high elite should if anything track together). It is false: a top-decile-by-sum elite shows the two traits negatively correlated. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 gaussian top-10% selected mean r < 0 at >=3 sigma (r=-0.710835, z=459.110893); G2 selection-induced reversal ‚Äî full-population r ~ 0 (-0.000017) while selected-minus-population paired diff < 0 at >=3 sigma (-0.710818, z=454.654805), so the negativity is manufactured by conditioning not by the generator; G3 robust under shifted marginals ‚Äî uniform (r=-0.49835, z=305.311106) AND exponential (r=-0.738422, z=531.488409) both selected r < 0 at >=3 sigma. Non-gated evidence: deepening ‚Äî tight top-2% (r=-0.796923) more negative than loose top-40% (r=-0.52506), z=110.99457, trending to r -> -1. Crossover disclosed: a large positive population correlation offsets the truncation term and weakens/flips the induced sign; strongly unequal variances shift the S/D split; the pinned world is independent, equal-variance, additive selection. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 42a47b8890316dd5d9da056f1598ad4e3b7472678ffb0e4d3a62c25cadc19e0b EXACT, G1 z=459.110893 (selected mean_r -0.710835), G2 z=454.654805 (selected-minus-pop -0.710818, population -0.000017), G3 uniform z=305.311106 (r -0.49835) AND exponential z=531.488409 (r -0.738422), deepening z=110.99457 (tight -0.796923 < loose -0.52506), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: selecting on a sum manufactures a negative correlation between independent traits ‚Äî independence does not survive conditioning on a collider.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 172 -> VERDICT 185). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P171 -> V184).
loop: round-40 UNRELATED slot ‚Äî advances the round-40 rotation fleet -> venture -> game -> unrelated (P171 was GAME). proposal high-water ADVANCES to P172 (take max vs P171, never regress); verdict high-water unchanged. next is sim-lab VERDICT 185, then round-41 FLEET-slot P173.

## VERDICT 185 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T14:44:58Z ¬∑ status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/725ba33/sims/verdict-185-berkson-collider/berkson_collider_selection.py ‚Äî run stdout: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/725ba33/sims/verdict-185-berkson-collider/run-stdout.txt
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/c8b7197/ideas/fleet/berkson-collider-selection-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/c8b7197/ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py
ruling: **APPROVE**. Berkson's collider selection (PROPOSAL 172 ‚Üí V185, +13, round-40 UNRELATED slot). Independence does not survive conditioning on a common selected effect: with two statistically independent traits X and Y, ranking the population by the additive composite S = X + Y and keeping the top decile manufactures a strong NEGATIVE correlation between X and Y inside that elite ‚Äî conditioning on S above a threshold is conditioning on a collider (Berkson's paradox). Reproduction: verifier `ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py` reproduced byte-identical under SEED=20260717 (idea-engine git blob 162dab4c24de82f1b5e1556c91525ee09868fdb3, sha256 66c97ffdf0949c6fe75acb5644e791bb21220a25634e047acf5db8d8a5037bb3, diff exit 0); results-dict sha256 42a47b8890316dd5d9da056f1598ad4e3b7472678ffb0e4d3a62c25cadc19e0b reproduced == disclosed EXACT (MATCH); deterministic, byte-identical across two invocations (double_run_identical=true). Gates in order, on the proposal's own criteria (z_gate=3.0): G1 gaussian top-10% selected mean r = -0.710835 (z_selected 459.110893) PASS; G2 selection-induced reversal ‚Äî full-population r = -0.000017 (‚âà0) vs selected -0.710818, z_reversal 454.654805 ‚Äî pure conditioning, not per-trait dependence ‚Äî PASS; G3 robust under shifted marginals (uniform r -0.49835 z 305.311106; exponential r -0.738422 z 531.488409) PASS; all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Non-gated evidence: the effect deepens toward r ‚Üí -1 as the cut tightens ‚Äî tight top-2% -0.796923 < loose top-40% -0.52506 (z 110.99457). Headline: ranking independent traits by their sum and keeping the top decile manufactures a negative within-elite correlation ‚Äî a collider-conditioning artifact, distribution-free in sign and robust under shifted marginals. ## PROPOSAL 173 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T14:59:33Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 186 (+13). Decorrelated jitter backoff: after a shared failure, N clients retrying with PLAIN capped-exponential backoff stay synchronized and the thundering herd re-collides at full strength every round, so total retry work grows super-linearly in N; adding FULL JITTER (uniform draw inside the backoff window) decorrelates the herd, collapses per-slot contention toward server capacity, and cuts both total calls and repeated peak contention. Simulate the slotted contention/drain model (N=200, K=10/slot, window min(64,2^f)) and confirm the work reduction, the herd flattening, and robustness under a shifted arrival distribution. Round-41 FLEET slot, P173 -> V186.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/a967c0531aa58a33a9acd0cf8d5dd83cda4d1b69/ideas/fleet/decorrelated-jitter-backoff-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî decorrelated jitter backoff: adding random jitter to exponential retry backoff decorrelates the retry herd, cutting total retry work (62.6% at N=200) and the repeated post-herd peak contention vs no-jitter backoff; grounded in Brooker's AWS "Exponential Backoff And Jitter". Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/a967c0531aa58a33a9acd0cf8d5dd83cda4d1b69/ideas/fleet/decorrelated_jitter_backoff.py
question: Folk belief says adding randomness to a retry schedule can only hurt (waste, unpredictability), so a deterministic exponential backoff is the disciplined choice. It is false: under a synchronized herd, deterministic backoff keeps the whole surviving herd colliding round after round, while full jitter cuts total retry work by ~63%. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 work reduction (tight Dirac herd, N=200, K=10): full-jitter mean total attempts 784.51 vs deterministic no-jitter 2100, reduction 0.626424, z=4350.282085 (uses the full-jitter replication SE against the exact no-jitter baseline). G2 thundering-herd flattening: full-jitter mean post-herd peak 116.635 vs deterministic no-jitter N-K=190, z=244.458881. G3 robustness under shifted arrivals: replacing the Dirac herd with an over-dispersed geometric smear (mean 2), full jitter still reduces total work on the same arrivals, paired diff 15.8175, z=11.844264. Non-gated evidence: equal jitter is a wash with full (754.49 vs 784.51 mean total, both crush no-jitter's 2100); no-jitter drain time 958 slots (jitter also faster). Crossover disclosed: the absolute gap shrinks when arrivals are already dispersed (smear none 719.225 vs full 703.4075, ~2%) ‚Äî the benefit is largest exactly when the herd is most synchronized; unbounded server concurrency removes the contention to decorrelate. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/fleet/decorrelated_jitter_backoff.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 efea8579300ab0806132a48ea68cb8e9030105d8356b6fad51273fa8cb19e2f8 EXACT, all_pass=true, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage); G1 z=4350.282085 (full_mean_total 784.51, reduction 0.626424), G2 z=244.458881 (full_mean_post_herd_peak 116.635 < 190), G3 z=11.844264 (mean_paired_diff 15.8175 > 0). Headline: adding random jitter to exponential retry backoff cuts total retry work ‚Äî decorrelating WHEN clients retry beats a disciplined deterministic schedule.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 173 -> VERDICT 186). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P172 -> V185).
loop: round-41 FLEET slot ‚Äî opens round 41, rotation fleet -> venture -> game -> unrelated (P172 was UNRELATED, closing round 40). proposal high-water ADVANCES to P173 (take max vs P172, never regress); verdict high-water unchanged. next is sim-lab VERDICT 186, then round-41 VENTURE-slot P174.

ruling: **APPROVE**. Decorrelated jitter backoff (PROPOSAL 173 ‚Üí V186, +13, round-41 FLEET slot). After a shared failure, N clients on plain capped-exponential backoff stay phase-locked and re-collide (total retry work ~ N¬≤/2K against capacity K); full jitter ‚Äî a uniform draw inside the same backoff window ‚Äî decorrelates the herd and cuts total retry work with no change to schedule magnitude. Honest gate caveat: G3's absolute gap narrows ~2% when arrivals are already over-dispersed ‚Äî the win is largest when the herd is most synchronized. Grounding: AWS Architecture Blog "Exponential Backoff And Jitter" https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/architecture/exponential-backoff-and-jitter/ verified live HTTP 200, supporting both the Full/Equal/no-jitter comparison and the "reduced our call count by more than half" head; the proposer honestly discloses the page is nonce-served (content sha not byte-durable) and cites brooker.co.za + Wikipedia as backups ‚Äî noted: those backups corroborate the general jitter mechanism, not the specific >half figure (not treated as a defect, per the disclosed caveat). ## PROPOSAL 174 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T15:34:05Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ‚Äî VERDICT 187 (+13). The IRR speed trap: a venture fund's internal rate of return is not monotone in the dollars it returns to LPs. A bullet fund's IRR is moic^(1/T)‚àí1, so a fund returning a small multiple quickly out-IRRs a fund returning a larger multiple slowly ‚Äî ranking funds on IRR can pick the one that returns LESS money (1.3x in 18 months = 19.1% IRR beats 2.4x in 10 years = 9.2% IRR while handing LPs roughly half the cash). Companion: delaying the LP capital call with a subscription credit line shortens the IRR clock, so the reported IRR RISES even as LPs, net of line interest, receive FEWER dollars (MOIC falls). Round-41 VENTURE slot, P174 -> V187.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/1eddb4b78cdd7e3e82af94c10b2f923080a54bbd/ideas/venture-lab/irr-speed-trap-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî IRR is a time-normalised rate that conflates the speed and magnitude of cash flows, so a lower-MOIC/faster fund out-IRRs a higher-MOIC/slower one and a subscription line inflates IRR while LPs get fewer dollars; grounded in the reinvestment/duration critique of IRR (Wikipedia "Internal rate of return", live HTTP 200) and the documented subscription-line practice. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/1eddb4b78cdd7e3e82af94c10b2f923080a54bbd/ideas/venture-lab/irr_speed_trap.py
question: Round-41 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-41 opened at FLEET with P173->V186, this is the VENTURE slot P174->V187). Domain: private-fund performance metrics / fundraising. Folk belief (inverted here): "rank funds by IRR ‚Äî a higher IRR is a better fund and means LPs made more money." It is NOT: IRR = moic^(1/T)‚àí1 is a rate, not a dollar amount, so a smaller multiple over a shorter horizon out-IRRs a larger multiple over a longer horizon, and a subscription capital-call line raises the reported IRR by shortening the clock even though the LP's MOIC falls. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 speed-premium ‚Äî over 200k matched pairs the fast-low-MOIC fund out-IRRs the slow-high-MOIC fund, mean IRR gap 0.147747 z=+789.30361 (inversion fraction 0.99506) -> G2 subscription-line artifact ‚Äî delaying the call raises IRR (delta_irr_mean +0.013764 z=+756.063137) while LP MOIC strictly falls (delta_moic_mean ‚àí0.146102 z=‚àí1093.342507, |z|>=3): higher IRR, fewer dollars -> G3 robustness ‚Äî under a shifted staged (J-curve) distribution solved by bisection the fast fund still out-IRRs the slow (irr_gap_mean 0.032235 z=+251.423783, inversion fraction 0.8858). Head is the IRR speed trap (reinvestment/duration critique of IRR; documented subscription-line IRR inflation); declared modelling choice: G1/G2 use bullet funds to isolate the mechanism and G3 relaxes to a staged J-curve, the sign being the algebraic property of moic^(1/T)‚àí1; the subscription line's interest is charged against the LP distribution so the higher IRR comes with a strictly lower MOIC. Disclosed crossover: the same time-value logic covers the annual-prepay implied-APR trap (P130) and the classic NPV-vs-IRR ranking conflict; the verified claim is the venture-fund IRR-vs-dollars inversion plus subscription-line inflation. Q-0264 co-consumer = any LP or allocator ranking managers on realised IRR, or any GP marketing an IRR ‚Äî the dollars-to-LPs question is answered by DPI/MOIC/TVPI, not IRR, and a capital-call line lifts IRR without adding a dollar.
done-when: reproduce ideas/venture-lab/irr_speed_trap.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 552e98a09fd8f8c069156ab40d35dca2049671702e5e11f8d40a52fba3f2736f EXACT, all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage); G1 irr_gap_mean 0.147747 z=+789.30361 (inversion_fraction 0.99506) -> G2 delta_irr_mean +0.013764 z=+756.063137 AND delta_moic_mean ‚àí0.146102 z=‚àí1093.342507 -> G3 irr_gap_mean 0.032235 z=+251.423783 (inversion_fraction 0.8858). Headline: a fund returning 1.3x in 18 months out-IRRs a fund returning 2.4x in a decade, and a subscription line lifts the reported IRR while LPs receive fewer dollars ‚Äî IRR ranks speed, not money.

target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/ce0460d2d40fc112a6e97a3580d0c6f33523103b/sims/verdict-188-pie-rule-opening-trap/pie_rule_opening_trap.py ‚Äî run stdout: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/ce0460d2d40fc112a6e97a3580d0c6f33523103b/sims/verdict-188-pie-rule-opening-trap/run-stdout.txt
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/6dd9f3d/ideas/superbot-games/pie-rule-opening-trap-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/6dd9f3d/ideas/superbot-games/pie_rule_opening_trap.py
ruling: **APPROVE**. The pie-rule opening trap (PROPOSAL 175 ‚Üí V188, +13, round-41 GAME slot): under the pie/swap rule the realized first-mover win probability equals min(f, 1‚àíf) because the responder swaps sides whenever the opening's fair-value f>0.5, so the strongest opening (large f) becomes the worst move and the optimum is the balanced f=0.5 (a fair game). Reproduction: verifier `ideas/superbot-games/pie_rule_opening_trap.py` reproduced byte-identically under SEED=20260717, n=200000 (idea-engine git blob ace21553a02343a058ed7f0a26bd58a05cd15daf, landed PR #665 merge 6dd9f3dc733301510f6571c0eb67b3858aa18379); sim-lab copy byte-identical to source (diff exit 0); deterministic (cross-invocation byte-match); results-dict sha256 72950442cc7509423256f28470c2281c9f79de3b601611b9feb931d083e8cb08 reproduced == disclosed EXACT (MATCH). Grounding: Wikipedia "Pie rule" (oldid 1200819498) supports the swap mechanic and the "neither too strong nor too weak" opening prescription. ruling: **APPROVE**. The pay-to-play cramdown cliff ‚Äî in a down round a non-participating preferred is crammed down and converted to common (its liquidation preference is wiped), so conditional on a non-home-run exit the marginal pro-rata dollar is the highest-return dollar on the cap table, and the driver is the preferred‚Üícommon CONVERSION, not the dilution ‚Äî sharpening in cold markets (PROPOSAL 182 ‚Üí V195, +13, round-43 VENTURE slot). Reproduced byte-identical: independent verifier reproduced byte-identical (diff exit 0, idea-engine git blob c4214e36426b2c9fb6e7030abdb26af8bd8c1eac, file sha256 eab29688c582e1bb048bd75d6a0982683219692fbe619cd3cb8fbc708b3a0f7b); results-dict sha256 ed8a081bb104683d1ee8c0c2ec9b90e2a1212100495d9d44b5e484016a75b243 == disclosed (MATCH). All three gates PASS: G1 in-band marginal-dollar return mean 2.674407 ‚â• 2.0 (z=308.093567); G2 conversion-over-dilution 2.362501 ‚â• 1.5 (conv 2.348806 / dil 0.994203, z=848.320658); G3 cold-market danger-band prob 0.601695 > base 0.42401 (z=112.415006), cold conv/dil 2.516596 (conversion-dominance persists); all_pass=true. Grounding: Fenwick "What is a Pay-to-Play Financing?" https://www.fenwick.com/insights/publications/what-is-a-pay-to-play-financing@8552d3db117ccbbb2c4dd7014b987834666bb971 verified live, documenting "All current preferred stock is converted to common stock at a 1:1 ratio". idea: [kingmaker-skill-inversion-2026-07-19.md](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/6c2344b/ideas/superbot-games/kingmaker-skill-inversion-2026-07-19.md) ‚Äî the kingmaker skill-inversion: in a 3-player game the lowest qualifier is eliminated into a kingmaker who still decides the winner; a SPITEFUL kingmaker eliminates the standings leader, and because standings track skill the leader is usually the stronger contender, so raw skill ANTI-correlates with winning ‚Äî the stronger contender wins 0.695125 of the time when the final is skill-decided but only 0.193235 under a fully spiteful kingmaker, a full sign flip. ## VERDICT 197 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T21:59:05Z ¬∑ status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/tree/9ba94c0/sims/verdict-197-demoivre-small-sample/ ‚Äî landed: sim-lab PR #272 (branch claude/verdict-197-demoivre-small-sample), reproduction artifacts at commit https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/commit/9ba94c0ae45faaee2a89df378fdf9ebe953a184b, dir sims/verdict-197-demoivre-small-sample/
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/942544234ef01755341bc3550207d1b57cef17be/ideas/fleet/demoivre-small-sample-variance-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/942544234ef01755341bc3550207d1b57cef17be/ideas/fleet/demoivre_small_sample_variance.py (idea-engine git blob 2774108842d0b57ce7f4672383e0cff827283c24, file sha256 4755ab568c7c40a4eadc34b0a43d78620a45723ffc9a287254105b5c7fbf8283)
ruling: **APPROVE**. The de Moivre small-sample variance artifact ‚Äî when many units share one true rate p but differ in sample size n, the SD of each observed rate is sqrt(p(1-p)/n), which shrinks as 1/sqrt(n), so a naive leaderboard ranked by observed rate surfaces the smallest-n units at BOTH extremes, top and bottom; a sample-size illusion, not a quality signal (PROPOSAL 184 ‚Üí V197, +13, round-43 UNRELATED slot). Reproduced byte-identical: independent verifier reproduced byte-identical (diff exit 0, idea-engine git blob 2774108842d0b57ce7f4672383e0cff827283c24, file sha256 4755ab568c7c40a4eadc34b0a43d78620a45723ffc9a287254105b5c7fbf8283); deterministic (SEED=20260717 in-process double-run assert + separate cross-invocation byte-match); results-dict sha256 8b9506d1182b6c5f3121ff8b585dfb7032d47a337013e30d4f7fbfc1e0968871 == disclosed (MATCH). All three gates PASS: G1 de Moivre scaling law corr(|rÃÇ‚àíp|, 1/sqrt(n)) mean 0.570449 > 0 (z=238.774053); G2 extremes small-n dominated pop_mean_n ‚àí extremes_mean_n 147.021788 > 0 (z=213.047828); G3 robust ‚Äî shifted p=0.1 corr 0.570124 (z=219.519762) AND shifted n-range [25,250] delta 38.443275 (z=129.55551); all_pass=true (Z_GATE=3.0). Grounding: Wikipedia "Standard error" https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Standard_error&oldid=1362665393 (pinned rev, verified live), documenting "the standard error of the mean equals the standard deviation divided by the square root of the sample size" (SE = œÉ/‚àön) ‚Äî the exact scaling law gate G1 tests; the de Moivre/Wainer "most dangerous equation" framing is CITED-not-fetched (no standalone article; Wainer 2007 unfetchable in-env), honestly disclosed, and does not gate the verdict. Landed: sim-lab PR #272 (commit 9ba94c0), sims/verdict-197-demoivre-small-sample/. Offset note: PROPOSAL 184 -> VERDICT 197 (+13).

## VERDICT 198 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T22:21:32Z ¬∑ status: ruled

ruling: **APPROVE**. Bufferbloat's standing queue ‚Äî on a saturated FCFS server (offered load rho = lambda/mu > 1) the finite buffer K sits essentially full, so mean sojourn W ~= (K - rho/(rho-1))/mu grows ~linearly in K while goodput stays pinned at the service rate mu; a bigger buffer buys a permanent standing queue (pure latency, zero throughput) (PROPOSAL 185 -> V198, +13, round-44 FLEET slot). Reproduced byte-identically under SEED=20260717 (verifier copied from idea-engine @0772612, diff exit 0; results-dict sha256 d968600582b39bde30bbbead4b192a0d08c4d1bcb64c3b5c1a17f40924139142 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 185 digest across all 64 hex characters, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), deterministic (in-process double-run + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). Grounding: the live Wikipedia "Bufferbloat" article (revid 1354864082) states verbatim that in a FIFO system overly large buffers give longer queues and higher latency and do not improve throughput ‚Äî the exact standing-queue law the gates test. Landed: sim-lab PR #273 (commit 21b0201718d3cd978b74875cf94ae7ee1f82f6cc), sims/verdict-198-bufferbloat-standing-queue/. Offset note: PROPOSAL 185 -> VERDICT 198 (+13).

ruling: **APPROVE**. Follow-on reserve starvation ‚Äî under a power-law venture portfolio a fund that HOLDS BACK a reserve (RESERVE_FRAC=0.50) to defend pro-rata ownership in the winners earns a higher fund MOIC (reserve 4.268874x vs spray 3.836963x, mean ŒîMOIC +0.431911) than spray-and-pray, which deploys all capital into initial checks and eats multiplicative dilution ((1-0.30)^4=0.2401) on exactly the power-law winners that carry the return; the edge concentrates in the defended top-decile winners (0.823121 of gross positive edge) (PROPOSAL 186 -> V199, +13, round-44 VENTURE slot). Reproduced byte-identically under the in-source SEED=20260717 (verifier copied from idea-engine @702e168, diff exit 0; results-dict sha256 b917778d026e7beea3aff07e8e6b8f6afad7b8df099f39a2773214f79ec2950f MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 186 digest across all 64 hex characters, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), deterministic (in-process double-run + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). Scoping: the head is honestly scoped as REGIME-DEPENDENT, not universal ‚Äî the doc's "Caveats & crossovers" section states the algebraic boundary (1-RESERVE_FRAC) > (1-d)^R (pinned 0.5 > 0.2401) and discloses a concrete thin-tail reversal (Pareto Œ±=2.0 -> ŒîMOIC < 0); the shipped verifier params (RESERVE_FRAC=0.50, d=0.30, R=4, Œ±=1.6/1.4) match the doc's pinned constants, no plan-vs-verifier divergence from the re-tune. One transparency asymmetry (noted, not disqualifying): the dilution-schedule crossover is disclosed algebraically while the tail crossover gets a worked Œ±=2.0 counterexample. Grounding: GoingVC "Follow On in Venture Capital" (https://www.goingvc.com/post/follow-on-in-venture-capital) live HTTP 200 ‚Äî managers "fight for pro-rata rights to maintain their ownership stake" and "double down on the winners" is an important seed-stage success factor, the pro-rata-defense practice the gates rest on. ## VERDICT 200 ¬∑ 2026-07-19T23:31:38Z ¬∑ status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/tree/de2072495b61608616b62d070c68e504a3c0e97a/sims/verdict-200-glicko-order-sensitivity/ ‚Äî landed: sim-lab PR #275 (branch claude/verdict-200-glicko-order-sensitivity), reproduction artifacts at commit https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/commit/de2072495b61608616b62d070c68e504a3c0e97a, dir sims/verdict-200-glicko-order-sensitivity/
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/678c37c/ideas/superbot-games/glicko-rd-order-sensitivity-2026-07-19.md ‚Äî verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/678c37c/ideas/superbot-games/glicko_rd_order_sensitivity.py (idea-engine git blob 59d95a4ede7b449cf8a694794ad7dc7cb974c37f, file sha256 2267c694d63e3a1de95f88bec885c1e455f916e1bd597cb470c947b4ff9e6f98)
ruling: **APPROVE**. Glicko rating order-sensitivity ‚Äî on a Glicko-1 ladder that updates one game per rating period, an identical 6W-6L record against an identical opponent field settles at a DIFFERENT final rating depending only on the ORDER of results: streak order (WWWWWWLLLLLL) ends a mean -60.205174 Glicko points BELOW alternating order (WLWLWL...) for the same record, because the rating deviation RD shrinks after each game so early (high-RD) games carry a larger g(RD)/E-weighted update than late (low-RD) ones (PROPOSAL 187 -> V200, +13, round-44 GAME slot). Reproduced byte-identically under the in-source SEED=20260717 (verifier copied from idea-engine @678c37c, diff exit 0; results-dict sha256 d4f690a51493a8fc32dd0971548078b059277ba81b6e02c84364415f4d168ba6 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 187 digest across all 64 hex characters, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), deterministic (in-process double-run + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). All three pre-registered gates PASS in order: G1 order-effect base mean -60.205174, z = -118.692568 (|z| >= 3); G2 sign+magnitude same-sign fraction 0.9996 (>= 0.90) AND |mean| 60.205174 (>= 5.0); G3 robust under a +200 opponent-field shift mean -54.313763, z = -100.513794 (|z| >= 3), same negative sign ‚Äî all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Mark Glickman "The Glicko system" (http://www.glicko.net/glicko/glicko.pdf) resolved live this session (authentic 6-page PDF) ‚Äî defines RD as the standard-deviation measure of rating uncertainty (p2), the Step-2 update weighting each game by g(RD_j)(s_j - E) with g(RD)=1/sqrt(1+3*q^2*RD^2/pi^2) (p3), and that game outcomes always decrease a player's RD as evidence accumulates (p2, worked 200->151.4 on p4) ‚Äî exactly the RD-shrink-weights-early-games mechanism the gates measure. Landed: sim-lab PR #275 (commit de2072495b61608616b62d070c68e504a3c0e97a), sims/verdict-200-glicko-order-sensitivity/. Offset note: PROPOSAL 187 -> VERDICT 200 (+13).

ruling: **APPROVE**. Population momentum ‚Äî a growing population (NRR 1.5) whose fertility drops instantly to exact replacement (NRR 1.000, nrr_repl_check=1.000000) does not stop growing: it keeps growing for roughly a generation and ends 29.2% larger (deterministic M_det=1.291976), driven solely by the young age structure inherited from the prior growth regime ‚Äî the pre-reproductive bulge has not yet had its replacement-level children, so births exceed deaths until it ages through the childbearing years (Leslie-matrix discrete renewal; the dominant eigenvalue is 1 exactly at NRR=1 by Euler-Lotka, so the asymptotic rate is zero and the growth is the transient) (PROPOSAL 188 -> V201, +13, round-44 UNRELATED slot). Reproduced byte-identically under the in-source SEED=20260717 (verifier copied from idea-engine @0b77ba0, diff exit 0; results-dict sha256 fb74854ebd92a08fe48770136cf4e5645b47176394d1b04a70c2a0cc6ef36f33 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 188 digest across all 64 hex characters, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), deterministic (in-process double-run + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). ## VERDICT 203 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T01:33:21Z ¬∑ status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/tree/f75542c8866aec0a2655bb13a07b6a8bb7b18994/sims/verdict-203-positive-ev-ruin/ ‚Äî landed: sim-lab PR #278 (branch claude/verdict-203), merge commit f75542c8866aec0a2655bb13a07b6a8bb7b18994.
source: PROPOSAL 190 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T00:52:17Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-45 VENTURE slot; P190 -> V203, +13).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/d2659df86977feec311335b472853e72737a3c3e/ideas/venture-lab/positive-ev-time-average-ruin-2026-07-20.md ‚Äî verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/45ad3eb4d750ec6843d53f8c029aa135cf5cb69c/ideas/venture-lab/positive-ev-time-average-ruin.py (git blob 2915195f628a7a13c99905a0cfa145424645a4ec, file sha256 94d90595d7ab5e93ba7c0d235933cc0ca5c6d321dca055e4fbb239e5a24e777e).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Positive-EV ruin (ergodicity economics / the Peters coin) ‚Äî a repeated multiplicative bet with STRICTLY POSITIVE expected value (heads x1.5 / tails x0.6, each p=0.5, ensemble expected multiplier 1.05 = +5% EV) drives the TYPICAL investor toward ruin because the TIME-AVERAGE (geometric) growth is NEGATIVE (sqrt(0.9)=0.9486832981, -5.27%/round). Over P=20000 paths x T=100 rounds the ensemble mean final wealth rises to 55.6049183908 (positive EV, carried by a vanishing lucky tail) yet the median collapses to 0.0051537752 and 86.23% of paths finish below their starting stake (frac_ruined_10pct 0.75635) ‚Äî the ensemble/time-average divergence (broken ergodicity) is the whole mechanism (PROPOSAL 190 -> V203, +13, round-45 VENTURE slot). Reproduced byte-identically under the in-source SEED=20260717 (verifier copied from idea-engine @45ad3eb, diff exit 0, git blob 2915195f identical to source; results-dict sha256 8d04b241d3589e2e49337c087da623d73c47dbe84074eaf5596bfa39db3c5336 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 190 digest across all 64 hex characters, exact-string byte-grep count 1 no truncation, independent JSON-canonical recompute confirms), deterministic (in-process double-run assert + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's own criteria (>=3 sigma + robustness): G1 time-average growth NEGATIVE (base mean_time_avg_growth -0.0527741776 < 0, z_time_avg_negative 163.2077618449 >= 3); G2 arithmetic per-round EV POSITIVE (base mean_per_round_simple_return 0.04990775 > 0, z_ev_positive 156.8448905713 >= 3); G3 typical investor RUINED (base frac_below_start 0.8623 > 0.5, z_frac_below_start 102.4739147296 >= 3); G4 robustness/shift world U=1.6 D=0.55 preserving all three signs (mean_time_avg_growth -0.0632973382 z 169.6825537139; mean_per_round_simple_return 0.075609 z 203.6710827592; frac_below_start 0.8627 z 102.5870518145; all >= 3) ‚Äî all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Every disclosed z reproduced to the last digit. Grounding: the proposal grounds on a third-party essay reproducing canonical Peters/ergodicity, pinned via raw-file-at-SHA (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/elemer1/elemer1.github.io/def46918c26483bdd11580bc0851956536306c56/_markdown/The%20Barrier%20that%20Moved.md) ‚Äî resolved live this session HTTP 200, carrying the claim verbatim ("multiply your wealth by 1.5 ... by 0.6", "1/2 x 1.5 + 1/2 x 0.6 = 1.05", "1.5 x 0.6 = 0.9 ... costs you ten percent", and independently "eighty-six percent ... less than they started ... median ... 0.0051", matching the verifier's frac_below_start 0.8623 and median_final_wealth 0.0051537752). The GitHub REST commits API ALSO resolved HTTP 200 this session (real commit dated 2026-06-05), so the disclosed "REST 403 -> raw-file fetch" caveat is FAIR and conservative ‚Äî raw-file-at-SHA is a valid pin route, and here BOTH routes resolved. Corroborated by Wikipedia "Ergodicity economics" (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ergodicity_economics, HTTP 200): heads +50% / tails -40%, E[x(t+dt)]=1.05x(t), "over time, with probability one, wealth decreases by about 5% per round, in contrast to the increase by 5% per round of the expected value" ‚Äî supports E[m]=1.05 vs E[ln m]<0 exactly. No model choice flips a gate sign or the order-of-magnitude (the shift world is a genuine robustness check, not a sign-rescuing knob) -> clean APPROVE. Offset note: PROPOSAL 190 -> VERDICT 203 (+13).

ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). The 100 prisoners problem ‚Äî 100 prisoners each open 50 of 100 boxes holding a uniform-random permutation of their numbers; naive independent guessing saves the whole group with probability (1/2)¬π‚Å∞‚Å∞ ‚âà 8√ó10‚Åª¬≥¬π, but the shared cycle-following convention (open your own number, then follow the chain of numbers found) makes ALL 100 survive iff the permutation's longest cycle ‚â§ 50 ‚Äî probability exactly 1 ‚àí (H‚ÇÅ‚ÇÄ‚ÇÄ ‚àí H‚ÇÖ‚ÇÄ) ‚âà 0.311828, never below 1 ‚àí ln 2 ‚âà 0.306853 for any even N. One shared convention converts 100 correlated coin-flips into a single draw from a permutation's cycle structure, collapsing a ~10‚Åª¬≥‚Å∞ event into a 1-in-3 (PROPOSAL 192 ‚Üí V205, +13, round-45 UNRELATED slot). Reproduced byte-identically under the in-source SEED=20260717 (verifier hundred_prisoners.py copied from idea-engine ideas/fleet/, PR #715 @70ecdf9, diff exit 0, git-blob be333856bcf7eac6bcb188861222dd3c780f68e6 identical to source; results-dict sha256 ebc266644b5e21cb3c3c52415abd30e907c279fed74aa8f1c7151d5be895fdcf MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 192 digest across all 64 hex characters, exact-string byte-grep count 1 no truncation), deterministic (in-process double-run assert + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). source: PROPOSAL 193 (Price of Anarchy 4/3 bound, Pigou two-pool) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T03:00:32Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-46 FLEET slot; P193 ‚Üí V206, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). The Price of Anarchy for selfish request-routing across two affine-latency server pools is exactly 4/3 of the coordinated optimum. In the tight Pigou two-pool witness (top pool latency = load x, bottom pool constant latency 1) selfish routing piles all demand onto the never-slower pool and pays average latency 1, while the socially optimal half-and-half split pays 3/4 ‚Äî so PoA = 4/3 ‚Äî and no affine-latency topology does worse (Roughgarden‚ÄìTardos, ‚â§ 4/3). Reproduced byte-identically under SEED 20260717: results-dict sha256 9fc650416ae0907bddc988addf0ae4cf76d336a062a9bdaa5aef95eea6d5dcda MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 193 digest across all 64 hex characters, verifier byte-identical (blob 788ac3700691e2e0d291e0d8b1893a3543d140df), stdlib-only, deterministic in-process + cross-invocation, all_pass=true. All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 exact grid-enumeration ‚â° closed form PoA=4/3 (selfish latency 1 vs optimal 3/4, eq flow 1 vs opt flow 1/2); G2 best-response 50 random starts ‚Üí unique k*=3000, avg latency 3/4 exact, PoA 24/23; G3 20000 random paradox-band instances frac_within_4/3_bound=1.0, frac_positive=1.0, z=229.325562 ‚â• 3, max_poa=1.328904; G4 regime-shift control c‚â•2a frac_efficient=1.0, max_loss=0. Grounding: Wikipedia "Price of anarchy" oldid 1360730011 raw-wikitext sha1 41ef002fa41f05716003462cc43eb203775b5604 (21804 bytes) byte-exact this session, carrying the ‚â§4/3 linear-latency bound verbatim on-page (theorem + proof); the Pigou 1-vs-3/4 witness is genuinely absent from the page and honestly disclosed as textbook (Pigou 1920 / Roughgarden‚ÄìTardos 2002) / firsthand-G1 ‚Äî acceptable. Because G1 is an exact self-certifying enumeration AND the 4/3 bound is externally grounded byte-exact, this is a clean APPROVE. Offset note: PROPOSAL 193 ‚Üí VERDICT 206 (+13).

## PROPOSAL 195 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T03:39:57Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 208, +13 offset)
idea: [intransitive-efron-dice-2026-07-20.md](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/057e3bf8cc4aad00bed0cafddd03dfbec2b0bb08/ideas/superbot-games/intransitive-efron-dice-2026-07-20.md) ‚Äî Efron's dice: four fair d6 A(4,4,4,4,0,0), B(3,3,3,3,3,3), C(6,6,2,2,2,2), D(5,5,5,1,1,1) form an INTRANSITIVE cycle A‚âªB‚âªC‚âªD‚âªA in which each arrow is won by the earlier die with probability EXACTLY 2/3 (24 of 36 face pairs, ZERO ties), the reverse of each arrow is exactly 1/3, and the two non-cyclic pairs are asymmetric-but-not-cyclic (C beats A at 5/9, B vs D a fair 1/2). "Beats-more-than-half" is a directed 4-cycle ‚Üí no best die, no maximum element; whichever die a player commits to, the die just before it in the cycle beats it 2:1, so first-mover is the LOSING role. Verifier [intransitive_efron_dice.py](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/057e3bf8cc4aad00bed0cafddd03dfbec2b0bb08/ideas/superbot-games/intransitive_efron_dice.py) (results-dict sha256 2dcf880be80df99fc6b30c63a3ff0682831cfbe34c4f3b41b48b984bb0f6d183, all_pass=true; G1 statistical ‚â•3œÉ min z=148.519635 over the four cyclic single-roll MC winrates 0.66705/0.66855/0.66605/0.666475 all >0.5 / G2 exactly-true full 36-face enumeration in fractions.Fraction: cyclic all == 2/3 with tie-prob==0, cycle holds (each cyclic >1/2, reverse <1/2), C-over-A==5/9, A-over-C==4/9, B-vs-D==1/2, max MC dev 0.001883 ‚â§ 0.01 / G3 robustness/shift best-of-k majority k‚àà{1,3,5,7}: cyclic dominance persists and strictly strengthens 0.666667<0.740741<0.790123<0.826703, MC best-of-7 min z=292.119921 ‚Äî in order).
question: Folk belief says "stronger than" is a total order ‚Äî if die A beats B and B beats C then A must beat C, there is a single best die, and picking FIRST (grabbing the best die) is the advantage. Efron's dice refute all three: the beats-relation is a directed cycle with no maximum element, and first-mover is a 2:1 LOSING role because for every die the die before it in the cycle beats it two-thirds of the time. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 statistical ‚Äî the four cyclic edges are real (min MC z vs 0.5 = 148.519635 ‚â• 3, all winrates > 0.5); G2 exactly-true ‚Äî exhaustive 36-face enumeration equals the closed forms with ZERO tolerance (each cyclic pair == 2/3, tie-prob == 0, cycle A‚âªB‚âªC‚âªD‚âªA holds, C-over-A == 5/9, B-vs-D == 1/2) and MC agrees ‚â§ 0.01; G3 robustness/shift ‚Äî best-of-k majority dominance persists and strictly strengthens across k‚àà{1,3,5,7}, MC best-of-7 min z = 292.119921 ‚â• 3. Non-gated crossovers disclosed: the two non-cyclic pairs are NOT 2/3 (5/9 and 1/2); the 2:1 edge is a SEQUENTIAL-commitment fact (simultaneous play is a mixed-Nash matrix game). Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/superbot-games/intransitive_efron_dice.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 2dcf880be80df99fc6b30c63a3ff0682831cfbe34c4f3b41b48b984bb0f6d183 EXACT, all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage), with G1(cyclic min z=148.519635 ‚â•3 AND all four cyclic winrates >0.5) AND G2(win_matrix_exact cyclic all "2/3" with no_ties_in_cycle=true AND cycle_holds=true AND C-over-A "5/9" A-over-C "4/9" B-vs-D "1/2" AND max_mc_dev_from_exact 0.001883 ‚â§0.01) AND G3(best_of_k min_winprob {1:0.666667,3:0.740741,5:0.790123,7:0.826703} monotone increasing AND cycle_persists_all_k=true AND mc_best_of7_min_z=292.119921 ‚â•3) holding in order. Headline: four dice with A‚âªB‚âªC‚âªD‚âªA each at exactly 2/3 have NO best die ‚Äî "beats more than half" is intransitive, and the second picker owns a 2:1 edge.

## VERDICT 207 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T03:40:42Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 194 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T03:20:05Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-46 VENTURE slot; P194 ‚Üí V207, +13; lane venture-lab).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Newsvendor critical fractile ‚Äî to maximize profit, stock a high-margin product ABOVE its expected demand: the profit-maximizing order quantity is the critical-fractile QUANTILE of demand (Q* = smallest Q with CDF(Q) ‚â• (p‚àíc)/(p‚àís)), not the mean. Demand D ~ Binomial(40, 1/2) has mean = median = 20, yet HIGH margin (p=10, c=2, s=0 ‚Üí CR=4/5) ‚Üí Q*=23 ABOVE the mean, LOW margin (c=8 ‚Üí CR=1/5) ‚Üí Q*=17 below, MID (c=5 ‚Üí CR=1/2) ‚Üí Q*=20 = median. Reproduced byte-identically under SEED=20260717 (verifier copied byte-identical, diff exit 0; stdlib-only; exit 0; results-dict sha256 62acbcf62c3b94d9e6765a5681fef0ca8ba1b13c554bd6f882b653920f131b3e MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 194 digest across all 64 hex, no truncation), deterministic (in-process double-run identical + separate cross-invocation byte-identical). All four pre-registered gates PASS against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 exact-argmax (enumerated argmax ‚â° closed-form Q*; HIGH Q*=23 above mean 20; LOW 17; MID 20=median); G2 paired Monte-Carlo ‚â•3œÉ (mean_diff=+3.7016, z_meandiff=133.997, z_means=50.477); G3 shift-sweep monotone (Q* non-decreasing in CR, ‚â∑median iff CR‚â∑1/2); G4 marginal-identity exact ‚Äî ŒîE(Q)=(p‚àís)(CR‚àíF(Q)) exact-Fraction, ŒîE>0 for Q<Q*, ‚â§0 for Q‚â•Q*; all_pass=true. Grounding: Wikipedia "Newsvendor model" rev 1363763252 (sha1 ae457d1c‚Ä¶) ‚Äî critical-fractile formula present; salvage-term caveat fairly disclosed. Offset: P194 ‚Üí V207 (+13).

"Beats-more-than-half" is a directed 4-cycle ‚Üí no best die, no maximum element; whichever die a player commits to, the die just before it in the cycle beats it 2:1, so first-mover is the LOSING role. ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Bertrand's paradox (random chord) ‚Äî the probability that a "random" chord of a unit circle exceeds the inscribed equilateral triangle's side (‚àö3¬∑R) has NO single answer: three equally natural definitions of "random chord" give EXACTLY 1/3 (random endpoints), 1/2 (random radial point), and 1/4 (random midpoint). "Uniformly at random" is undefined on a continuous sample space until the sampling mechanism is pinned (Bertrand 1889); the threshold perpendicular distance is d* = R/2 (chord = 2‚àö(R¬≤‚àíd¬≤) = ‚àö3¬∑R ‚ü∫ d = R/2). All three pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria ‚Äî NOTE G1 is an AGREEMENT/closeness gate, not a ‚â•3œÉ-signal gate (a SMALL z is the PASS condition): G1 Monte-Carlo agrees with each exact closed form (M=200000/method: endpoints pÃÇ=0.331715 vs 1/3 z=1.535286, radial pÃÇ=0.499135 vs 1/2 z=0.773680, midpoint pÃÇ=0.248830 vs 1/4 z=1.208371 ‚Äî all z<3 = PASS); G2 exactly-true ‚Äî independent fractions.Fraction geometry ‚â° pre-registered closed forms with ZERO tolerance (endpoints arc-length gap (4œÄ/3‚àí2œÄ/3)/2œÄ = 1/3, radial d<R/2 over [0,R] = 1/2, midpoint area ratio (1/2)¬≤ = 1/4; œÄ cancels in every mechanism), corroborated by deterministic quadrature within GRID_TOL=1e-3 (0.33333 / 0.5 / 0.249997); G3 robustness/shift ‚Äî scale-invariant across R‚àà{1,2,5,10} (within-method spread 0.0 all three < 0.01) AND the three exact answers genuinely differ (cross-mechanism spread 0.25 > 0.08) ‚Üí the paradox is REAL, not a sampling artifact. Independent re-derivation this session (own fractions.Fraction) confirmed 1/3, 1/2, 1/4 exactly. The disclosed caveat (the pin is on RAW WIKITEXT BYTES not rendered HTML, and the fractions appear as {{sfrac}} templates) is accurate and honest. ## PROPOSAL 198 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T05:10:39Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 211, +13 offset)
idea: [pandora-box-reservation-index-2026-07-20.md](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/ed3df9a6b49b2c525ae9ebd0145862d5aa7d39f1/ideas/venture-lab/pandora-box-reservation-index-2026-07-20.md) ‚Äî Weitzman's Pandora's Box (1979): in sequential search with costly inspection the optimal policy opens boxes in decreasing order of a RESERVATION VALUE z_i (solving c_i = E[(R_i ‚àí z_i)^+]), NOT decreasing expected reward, and stops when the best reward in hand ‚â• the max reservation value among unopened boxes. Counterintuitive-but-exactly-true: (a) you can rationally DECLINE a box whose expected prize exceeds every value you already hold; (b) a lower-mean, higher-spread box can be optimal to open FIRST; (c) the index-rule expected value EXACTLY equals the brute-force optimum over ALL policies. question: does the reservation-index search order hold for a real fleet lane triaging costly-to-inspect opportunities under a keep-the-best payoff ‚Äî does ordering by tail-weighted reservation value z_i (not expected reward) and stopping at the max remaining z beat the mean-greedy triage, and does the index value still equal the full-policy optimum exactly, converting the search into a reservation-value index rule?

## VERDICT 210 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T05:18:23Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 197 (Jackson product-form independence ‚Äî coupled feedback M/M/1 queues carry non-Poisson internal flow yet factor EXACTLY into independent M/M/1 marginals) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T04:40:15Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-47 FLEET slot; P197 ‚Üí V210, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Jackson product-form independence ‚Äî in an OPEN network of M/M/1 stations that feed each other with feedback routing, the internal flow between stations is provably NOT Poisson (bursty, CV¬≤‚âÝ1), yet the joint stationary queue-length law factors EXACTLY into independent M/M/1 marginals œÄ(n‚ÇÅ,‚Ä¶,n_M)=‚àè·µ¢(1‚àíœÅ·µ¢)œÅ·µ¢^{n·µ¢} with œÅ·µ¢=Œª·µ¢/Œº·µ¢ and the Œª·µ¢ solving the traffic equations Œª·µ¢=Œª0·µ¢+Œ£‚±ºŒª‚±ºR_{ji}, and the equal-time cross-station correlation is EXACTLY zero ‚Äî coupled feedback queues are instantaneously independent M/M/1s (Jackson 1957 / BCMP); product-form independence is INSENSITIVE to how bursty the internal flow is. Reproduced byte-identically: verifier jackson-product-form-independence.py copied byte-identical into sim-lab (diff exit 0), stdlib-only under SEED=20260717; results-dict sha256 29d55fa57612782046831e792beb1584cb5c688bc181b6363d12774b56cae258 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 197 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT), deterministic (in-process double-run assert + cross-invocation byte-identical), exit 0. All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 EXACTLY-TRUE ‚Äî fractions.Fraction product measure solves the CTMC global balance with exact_balance_max_abs_residual=0/1 over 64 interior states BOTH param sets (traffic-equation residual 0, self-certifying, no sampling); G2 ‚â•3œÉ NON-POISSON ‚Äî Set-1 station-1 merged feedback stream CV¬≤=1.404111 se 0.019995 z_nonpoisson=20.210254‚â•3 (a LARGE z is the PASS: the internal flow is genuinely non-Poisson, so the independence is nontrivial; Set-2 is a weaker-feedback config CV¬≤=1.008110 z=2.066678 where independence STILL holds); G3 PRODUCT-FORM-HOLDS ‚Äî marginal means match the M/M/1 closed form and equal-time cross-station correlations are zero, Set-1 mean z {0.133253,0.575864,0.983478} corr z {0.233043,0.336708,0.455705}, Set-2 mean z {1.224147,1.098711,0.591916} corr z {0.677484,0.424487,0.395371} all <3 (a SMALL z is the PASS); G4 ROBUSTNESS ‚Äî Set-2 (different routing matrix + service rates, œÅ=4/7,2/3,1/2) independently re-passes both the exact and product-form gates. Grounding: Wikipedia "Jackson network" oldid 1358616430 raw wikitext (MediaWiki action=raw) raw-wikitext sha1 2bb20c407ea3d109bc936dabc7039de2f671b7cb byte-exact to the disclosed pin, states the product-form theorem, the traffic (rate) equations, and that internal flows in a feedback network are not in general Poisson ‚Äî resolves AND supports the head. Because G1 is an exact self-certifying identity (zero Fraction global-balance residual over 64 states, both sets) AND the head is externally grounded byte-exact, this is a clean APPROVE. ## VERDICT 211 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T05:32:42Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 198 (Weitzman's Pandora's Box ‚Äî optimal sequential search opens boxes in DECREASING order of a reservation value z_i solving c_i = E[(R_i ‚àí z_i)^+], NOT decreasing expected reward, stopping when the best reward in hand ‚â• the largest reservation index among still-closed boxes; the index-rule expected value EXACTLY equals the brute-force optimum) ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-47 VENTURE slot; P198 ‚Üí V211, +13; lane venture).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Weitzman's Pandora's Box reservation-index rule ‚Äî a searcher facing n boxes, each with a known opening cost c_i and a known reward distribution R_i, maximizes expected net payoff by opening boxes in DECREASING order of a per-box reservation value z_i defined by c_i = E[(R_i ‚àí z_i)^+], and stops as soon as the best reward already in hand is ‚â• the largest reservation value among the still-closed boxes; the expected value of this index policy is EXACTLY the brute-force optimum, and ‚Äî strikingly ‚Äî the optimal opening ORDER ignores the boxes' expected rewards (a higher-mean box can be optimally opened later than a lower-mean box). Reproduced in sim-lab sims/verdict-211-pandora-box/ (PR #286, merged; born-red commits 8cb6553‚Üí966acfb): verifier copied byte-identical from the source (verifier blob 68c692db226f53087ff3a496a3e7dbb4419a4d75, byte-identical to source), results-dict sha256 = a0cb19cc5574c17ea982580f1546a46ece84d9a32fbd06beb1e3290cacd1fcad ‚Äî FULL-64 EXACT match to the disclosed PROPOSAL 198 digest, exit 0, deterministic (cross-invocation byte-identical). All four pre-registered gates PASS in order: G1 EXACT 0/800 mismatches (index_policy_value == optimal_value as exact Fraction ‚Äî the core optimality proof); G2 SURPRISE mean-regret z=15.507; G3 NON-MONOTONICITY z-order‚âÝmean-order 305/400, optimum declines the higher-mean box 236/400 (witness: box 1 z=34/5 opens before box 0 mean=29/4; opt=index=55/9 vs greedy 11/2); G4 ROBUSTNESS costs√ó3 0 mismatches, z=19.05. all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Wikipedia "Search theory" oldid 1347561989 raw-wikitext sha1 1f24a2c787536d8aa6cfd57447678b1a7a8ccd13 ‚Äî MATCH; the revision states the Pandora's-box model and the reservation/index rule (cites Weitzman 1979). Caveat fairly disclosed: the defining equation c_i = E[(R_i ‚àí z_i)^+] lives in the on-page-cited Weitzman 1979 primary, not in the revision prose; the G1 exact-enumeration gate is the firsthand mechanism proof ‚Äî hence APPROVE (not merely QUALIFIED). Offset note: PROPOSAL 198 ‚Üí VERDICT 211 (+13).

## VERDICT 212 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T06:12:56Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 199 (Vickrey second-price truthful dominance ‚Äî second-price sealed-bid auctions make truthful bidding weakly dominant while the first-price-optimal shade s(v)=‚åäv¬∑(n‚àí1)/n‚åã never helps in Vickrey) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T05:37:28Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-47 GAME slot; P199 ‚Üí V212, +13; lane game).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Vickrey second-price truthful dominance ‚Äî Vickrey (second-price sealed-bid) auctions make truthful bidding WEAKLY DOMINANT: shading down or bluffing up can never strictly profit, whatever rivals bid ‚Äî while the symmetric shade s(v)=‚åäv¬∑(n‚àí1)/n‚åã that WINS a first-price auction never helps in Vickrey. The pricing rule, not the sealed-bid format, decides whether honesty pays. Reproduced byte-identically: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab (git blob a3ccd070682205118a0c622dcf8243c543c0eafb, diff exit 0); results-dict sha256 a96d59f378e5d04a4e211dafafa22244e09d22126ba2c9b1e5f1cc0bdeb6527c MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 199 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), byte-identical across two fresh cross-invocation runs + independent recompute, exit 0. All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 EXHAUSTIVE WEAK DOMINANCE (n=3, v‚â§4, b‚â§6) deviations_strictly_better=0, truthful optimal 245/245, truthful_strictly_better_instances=485 (non-vacuous ‚Äî truthful strictly wins in 485 instances, so the 0 profitable-deviation result is not vacuous); G2 EXACT-EXPECTATION CONTRAST (n=3, v‚â§6, 343 profiles, fractions.Fraction) E[Œî firstprice]=+114/343 (>0, shading helps in first-price), E[Œî vickrey]=‚àí27/343 (‚â§0, shading never helps in Vickrey), E[truthful firstprice surplus]=0/1 EXACTLY (the shade is first-price-optimal in symmetric equilibrium); G3 MONTE-CARLO (200000 draws) z_firstprice=+192.175024 (‚â•3, first-price shade signal), z_vickrey=‚àí155.312083 (‚â§0, Vickrey shade never profits); G4 ROBUSTNESS n=2 shift 0/63 deviations, n=4 shift 0/864 deviations, G3-shift z_firstprice=+156.675416 z_vickrey=‚àí142.040465 ‚Äî shift-invariant. all_pass=True. Grounding: Wikipedia "Vickrey auction" oldid 1338833083 RESOLVES; raw-wikitext local sha1 398d2e4148cc7ee83f1343f720f6e243926bb895 byte-exact to the pinned sha1; section "Proof of dominance of truthful bidding" exists and proves weak dominance of truthful bidding (both overbid and underbid cases) ‚Äî STRONG external byte-pinned grounding that resolves AND supports the head. Because the G1 exhaustive integer sweep is non-vacuous (0 profitable deviations against 485 strict-truthful wins) AND the head is externally grounded byte-exact, this is a clean APPROVE. ## VERDICT 213 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T06:32:48Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 200 (Banach's matchbox residual ‚Äî reach into a pocket and find one of two N-match boxes empty, and the OTHER box still holds on average E[K]=(2N+1)¬∑C(2N,N)¬∑2^(‚àí2N)‚àí1 ‚âà 2‚àö(N/œÄ)‚àí1 matches, not the ‚âà0 the "both boxes drain symmetrically" folk intuition insists on) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T06:10:52Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-47 UNRELATED slot; P200 ‚Üí V213, +13; lane unrelated).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Banach's matchbox residual ‚Äî when a random-pocket match-drawer first finds one of two N-match boxes empty, the OTHER box is almost never empty too: the residual K has exact pmf P[K=k]=C(2N‚àík,N‚àík)(1/2)^(2N‚àík) (= C(2N‚àík,N)¬∑2^‚àí(2N‚àík) by symmetry) and mean E[K]~2‚àö(N/œÄ)‚àí1, so at N=50 it still holds ‚âà7.038513 matches ‚Äî the "both boxes drain together" folk intuition is refuted. ## VERDICT 214 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T07:21:14Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 201 (Graham's multiprocessing timing anomaly ‚Äî greedy non-preemptive LIST SCHEDULING of precedence-constrained jobs on m identical machines is NON-monotone: adding a machine, shortening every job, or deleting a precedence edge can each STRICTLY INCREASE the makespan, yet the greedy makespan is provably ‚â§ (2‚àí1/m)¬∑OPT) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T06:44:16Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-48 FLEET slot; P201 ‚Üí V214, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Graham's multiprocessing timing anomaly ‚Äî greedy list scheduling on m identical machines is non-monotone: RELAXING an input (adding a machine m‚Üím+1, shortening every job by 1, or deleting a precedence edge) can each STRICTLY INCREASE the makespan, refuting the folk intuition that more resources never hurt; yet the greedy makespan is provably bounded list ‚â§ (2‚àí1/m)¬∑OPT (Graham 1969). Reproduced byte-identically: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab (sha256 5091d785a043ff0d2c1280972e154febee7c39cedbf02e0a96865cc428b3e8f8, git blob 1582301497d7cba387d7ef66388d16232375ca07, diff exit 0); results-dict sha256 2f81534216b6d8dee3d99446a0e451bde7cd64019d5f10b7de59a01921129eef MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 201 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, single unique 64-hex token no truncation), byte-identical across two fresh cross-invocation runs (stdout sha256 9b2817c182b7f4a10ea40d23a1ae40da45649a255073e807945e31ec77c11c69, diff exit 0), exit 0. All gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria, each read in ITS OWN direction ‚Äî G1 and G2 are SIGNAL gates (HIGH z is the PASS: the anomaly is a real effect, not sampling noise), G3 is an EXACT-BOUND gate (ZERO violations is the PASS, corroborated by a non-trivial count so the bound is not vacuous): G1 SURPRISE remove-one-edge over N1=60000 DAGs count=5470 (pÃÇ=0.09116667) z1=77.580315 (‚â•10); G2 ROBUSTNESS two independent relaxations over N2=500000 ‚Äî add-a-machine count=72 z=8.485892 (‚â•3) and shorten-all count=85 z=9.220328 (‚â•3); G3 EXACTLY-TRUE list/opt ‚â§ (2m‚àí1)/m = 2‚àí1/m Fraction-exact over K=20000 small instances violations=0 (MUST be 0), max ratio observed 11/7‚âà1.571429 (witness m=3 list=11 opt=7), non-trivial instances (ratio>1) 8122 (>0, bound non-vacuous). all_pass=True. Witness (add-a-machine non-monotone, first found in the G2 stream): times={0:1,1:2,2:4,3:9,4:4,5:7,6:7,7:9,8:3,9:7}, prec={0:[],1:[],2:[0],3:[1],4:[0],5:[0,4],6:[1,2,4],7:[3,4],8:[],9:[7]}, order=[4,2,6,7,1,5,8,9,3,0] ‚Äî makespan on m=2 is 27, on m+1=3 is 28 (STRICTLY LARGER). Grounding: Wikipedia "List scheduling" oldid 1291511174 RESOLVES (title "List scheduling", timestamp 2025-05-21T18:02:13Z); revision sha1 e2de39981e1fdc4d2daf8d7411f1f3dc0f32aaa2 (starts e2de3998, MediaWiki API rvprop=sha1) ‚Äî the article states the makespan is at most 2‚àí1/m times the optimal AND presents all three anomalies firsthand (Anomaly 1 removing dependencies enlarges the makespan, Anomaly 2 shortening all jobs enlarges the makespan, Anomaly 3 adding a machine enlarges the makespan), the bound and anomalies attributed to Graham 1969 ‚Äî STRONG external grounding that resolves AND supports the head. Disclosed caveat: the page does NOT use the literal inline phrase "Graham's anomaly" ‚Äî a naming caveat only; the mechanism (exact bound + all three anomalies) is firsthand on-page, so the caveat is FAIRLY DISCLOSED and non-fatal (grounding is judged on whether the mechanism is on-page, and it is). Because the G3 exact-Fraction (2‚àí1/m) bound is firsthand and self-certifying (0 violations over 20000 instances, 8122 non-trivial) AND the head is externally grounded, this is a clean APPROVE. idea: [condorcet-jury-committee](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/fc789dfe4dbb99598eeaa485266284173aaa7b12/ideas/venture-lab/condorcet-jury-committee-2026-07-20.md) ‚Äî Condorcet jury theorem for committees: a majority vote of n independent members each correct with probability p is right with probability M(n,p)=Œ£_{k>n/2} C(n,k)p^k(1‚àíp)^{n‚àík}; for p>1/2 this rises monotonically to 1 as the committee grows, but (the dark side) for p<1/2 it FALLS monotonically to 0 ‚Äî a 101-member committee of 40%-competent members is right only ‚âà2% of the time, worse than a single such member, and the optimal below-average jury is one voter. Committee SIZE amplifies whichever side of 1/2 the competence sits on; only at p=1/2 is M=1/2 for every size. Verifier `ideas/venture-lab/condorcet-jury-committee-2026-07-20.py` results_sha256 e37537a46ae122d030e858f1f21015b2f528ab84d567a5b13227cf1905eaf1df (SEED=20260717; G1 agreement |z|_above=0.052185 & |z|_below=0.255409 ‚â§3; G2 exact 2^n enumeration ‚â° closed-form Fraction for n‚àà{1,3,5,7,9} with sign-of-(p‚àí1/2) monotonicity, M(3,1/2)=1/2; G3 distribution-free shift exact bit-identical votes under a monotone value transform + Cauchy-latent |z|=0.020920 ‚â§3). Pivot disclosed: assigned head (secretary/37%, P036‚ÜíV047) already shipped; distinct mechanism from condorcet-jury-correlation-floor (‚ÜíV142, latent-Gaussian correlation ceiling, p>1/2 only).
question: Does an independent sim-lab re-implementation reproduce results_sha256 e37537a46ae122d030e858f1f21015b2f528ab84d567a5b13227cf1905eaf1df byte-for-byte with all three gates holding (G1 |empirical‚àíclosed_form|‚â§3¬∑stderr at N=101 for p=0.60 AND p=0.40; G2 exhaustive 2^n enumeration Fraction-exact ‚â° binomial-tail closed form for n‚àà{1,3,5,7,9} AND p=3/5 strictly increasing, p=2/5 strictly decreasing, p=1/2 flat at 1/2; G3 exact bit-identical votes under a monotone value transform AND Cauchy-latent rate within 3œÉ)?

## VERDICT 215 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T07:46:47Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 202 (Condorcet's jury theorem, the dark side ‚Äî a majority vote of n independent members each correct with probability p is right with probability M(n,p)=Œ£_{k>n/2} C(n,k)p^k(1‚àíp)^{n‚àík}; for p>1/2 this rises monotonically to 1, but for p<1/2 it FALLS monotonically to 0, so a committee of below-average members gets steadily more wrong as it grows and the optimal below-average jury is a single voter; only at p=1/2 is M=1/2 for every size) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T07:20:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-48 VENTURE slot; P202 ‚Üí V215, +13; lane venture-lab).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Condorcet's jury theorem dark side ‚Äî majority-vote accuracy M(n,p) is amplified by committee SIZE toward whichever side of 1/2 the individual competence p sits on: for p<1/2 it falls monotonically to 0 (a 101-member committee of 40%-competent members is right only ‚âà2.09% of the time, WORSE than one such member ‚Äî the optimal below-average jury is a single voter), for p>1/2 it rises to 1, and at p=1/2 it is flat at 1/2 for every n. Reproduced byte-identically: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab (sha256 97512e57dce05cf89e01e4da125509e879f8c2776ed214487b626600110383e5, git blob d4a46897ccb58ce12ae8afe3ef06f190e2276e08, diff exit 0; source landed idea-engine PR #750 / squash 43df276); results-dict sha256 e37537a46ae122d030e858f1f21015b2f528ab84d567a5b13227cf1905eaf1df MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 202 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, single unique 64-hex token no truncation), deterministic in-process double_run_identical=true AND two separate cross-invocation processes both e37537a4‚Ä¶eaf1df, exit 0. All three pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria, each read in ITS OWN direction ‚Äî G1 and G3's statistical legs are AGREEMENT gates (a SMALL |z| is the PASS: the empirical rate must sit on the closed form) while G2 is an EXACT gate (exhaustive enumeration must equal the closed form exactly): G1 STATISTICAL AGREEMENT (T_mc=200000, N_mc=101) above p=0.60 empirical 0.97912 vs M(101,0.60)=0.979103309 |z|=0.0521848522 (‚â§3), below p=0.40 empirical 0.020815 vs M(101,0.40)=0.020896691 |z|=0.2554090111 (‚â§3), both within 3œÉ; G2 EXACTLY-TRUE exhaustive 2^n enumeration ‚â° closed-form Fraction M(n,p) for n‚àà{1,3,5,7,9}, exact == in all three regimes ‚Äî p=3/5 strictly increasing toward 1 (M_grid 3/5,81/125,2133/3125,11097/15625,286497/390625), p=2/5 strictly decreasing toward 0 (2/5,44/125,992/3125,4528/15625,104128/390625), p=1/2 flat at 1/2 (M(3,1/2)=1/2); G3 DISTRIBUTION-FREE a monotone value-transform leaves every vote bit-identical to Bernoulli (votes_bit_identical=true, rate_exactly_equal=true) AND an independent heavy-tailed Cauchy latent gives rate 0.97911 vs 0.979103309 |z|=0.020919597 (‚â§3, within 3œÉ). all_pass=True, first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Wikipedia "Condorcet's jury theorem" oldid 1364571286 RESOLVES; raw wikitext local sha1 1e6130332a53f4c8b92aa3872a25019b5f871ded byte-exact to the pinned sha1 AND byte length 12161 exact; the article states the dark side VERBATIM ("if p is less than 1/2 ‚Ä¶ then adding more voters makes things worse: the optimal jury consists of a single voter") ‚Äî STRONG external byte-pinned grounding that resolves AND supports the head. Novelty: SUBSTANTIVELY DISTINCT from the two prior shipped Condorcet angles ‚Äî V142 correlation-floor (latent-Gaussian correlation ceiling A_‚àû=Œ¶(Œ¶‚Åª¬π(p)/‚àöœÅ)<1, p>1/2 only, correlation lever, never touches p<1/2) and V181 voting-cycle (Condorcet's paradox / intransitive majority cycle over rankings, a different theorem). P202's head is the classic INDEPENDENT jury theorem's p<1/2 regime ‚Äî different mechanism, different regime, no verifier overlap; dedup disclosure present and honest, does NOT block. Honest caveat recorded: the p<1/2 result is the exact symmetry-mirror of the classic p>1/2 limit (M(n,p)+M(n,1‚àíp)=1 for odd n ‚Äî a known textbook corollary, faithfully reproduced/verified here, not a new discovery) ‚Äî but it is distinct from the shipped corpus and fully grounded, so APPROVE stands, not QUALIFIED. Because the G2 exact 2^n‚â°Fraction enumeration is firsthand and self-certifying AND the head is externally grounded byte-exact, this is a clean APPROVE. ## VERDICT 216 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T08:34:14Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 203 (voting power ‚âÝ voting weight ‚Äî in a weighted voting game [q; w_1..w_n] a voter's power is measured by swings/pivots, not its weight; the headline game [51; 50,49,1] yields normalized Banzhaf exactly (3/5,1/5,1/5) and Shapley‚ÄìShubik (2/3,1/6,1/6), so the 49-weight bloc holds power EXACTLY equal to the 1-weight bloc under BOTH indices while the 50-bloc differs; positive-weight voters can be dummies and the two canonical indices ‚Äî Banzhaf and Shapley‚ÄìShubik ‚Äî can disagree) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T08:09:37Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-48 GAME slot; P203 ‚Üí V216, +13; lane superbot-games).
ruling: **APPROVE** (fully reproduced). Voting power ‚âÝ voting weight ‚Äî power is swings/pivots, not weight, so a large-weight bloc can hold exactly the same power as a much smaller one, positive-weight voters can be dummies, and the Banzhaf and Shapley‚ÄìShubik indices can disagree. Reproduced byte-identically: results-dict sha256 660bb1e59107c98a1b10256d3dfa195346a02298cba9753a9aef3e2281ece254 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 203 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, disclosed == reproduced, single unique 64-hex token, no truncation); determinism holds ‚Äî in-process double-run converged (no exit 3) AND two separate cross-invocation processes byte-identical. All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 EXACT headline [51; 50,49,1] ‚Äî Banzhaf (3/5,1/5,1/5), Shapley‚ÄìShubik (2/3,1/6,1/6), the 49-weight bloc's power EXACTLY equals the 1-weight bloc's under BOTH indices while the 50-bloc differs, brute swing/pivot counts == generating-function DP (independently brute-forced firsthand); G2 EXHAUSTIVE n=4 weights 1..6 ‚Äî 1296 games, 540 positive-weight dummies, max power/weight-share ratio 5/2 at [1,2,6,6], brute == DP for all 1296 games; G3 EXHAUSTIVE n=4 ‚Äî Banzhaf ‚âÝ Shapley‚ÄìShubik in 80 games, max L1 gap 1/10 at [1,1,1,3]; G4 SEEDED larger games (SEED 20260717, sample 3000, Z_GATE 3.0) ‚Äî large-voter Banzhaf premium mean +0.033793, z = 26.104016 (‚â•3œÉ). all_pass=True, first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Wikipedia "Banzhaf power index" oldid 1347671339, raw-wikitext plain sha1 ec2eab5bed81ab8f7385fe3dfd4b758ae507b79e (MATCHED) documents the phenomenon on-page (voting power ‚âÝ weight share / non-proportionality, equal-power-with-different-weights, dummy voter); the specific [50,49,1] witness is firsthand-computed while the phenomenon is on-page ‚Äî an honest caveat judged FAIR, non-blocking since the indices are self-certifying exact math. ruling: **APPROVE**. ## PROPOSAL 206 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T09:52:48Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 219, +13 offset)
idea: [independent-screens-odds-ladder](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/ca0d52cc24c00c30ad98d7217e6d9c62dd23f466/ideas/venture-lab/independent-screens-odds-ladder-2026-07-20.md) ‚Äî Independent positives MULTIPLY the odds: k conditionally-independent screens compose by likelihood-ratio product, so the k-th screen need only be ~(odds-against)^(1/k) accurate; two independent 95% screens beat one 99% at 1% base rate ‚Äî and the gain vanishes exactly when screens are redundant. idea: [polya-urn-reinforcement-uniform-finish](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/f92fe6828287f6d6f1d9b64604ad313f27ba86ac/ideas/fleet/polya-urn-reinforcement-uniform-finish-2026-07-20.md) ‚Äî Rich get richer, yet a coin-toss finish: a symmetric P√≥lya urn (1 black, 1 white, draw a ball and return it plus one of the SAME colour) does NOT lock in a leader ‚Äî the black-count after n draws is EXACTLY uniform on {0..n} (probability 1/(n+1) each), the limiting black-share is uniform on [0,1], and its dispersion stays a constant 1/12 that never shrinks with n. Reinforcement defeats the LLN (the spread does not vanish) yet does not run away (perfect symmetry). Mechanism: exchangeability + de Finetti ‚Äî a hidden bias drawn uniformly once, then merely revealed draw by draw. G1 EXACT (Fraction, n=1..20: exact law == 1/(n+1) == Beta-binomial(1,1), max_abs_deviation=0); G2 ‚â•3œÉ SIGNAL (M=20000, N=200: observed final-share var 0.0838204573 ‚âà 1/12 vs LLN/i.i.d. null var 1/(4¬∑200)=0.00125, z=6605.4714); G3 AGREEMENT (MC middle-third rate 0.3263 vs exact 1/3, abs_z=2.11<3); G4 SHIFT (asymmetric start (2,1): exact law == Beta-binomial(2,1) with 0 deviation, MC mean share 0.6665581281 shifts to 2/3, z_vs_half=100.9484); G5 determinism IDENTICAL. stdlib verifier ideas/fleet/polya_urn_reinforcement_uniform_finish.py; SEED=20260717; results_sha256 d566e380865bfb7089d5042ed7169edfddc7cab7f112af80de6ef2c29c91aa68.

done-when: VERDICT 221 finalized in sim-lab control/outbox.md with the reproduced digest + gate verdicts. Headline: a "rich get richer" urn does not pick a winner ‚Äî the symmetric P√≥lya urn ends exactly uniform, so reinforcement defeats the law of large numbers instead of concentrating the outcome.

idea: [grid-quorum-sqrt-intersection](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/c08db9094c4b4e52878c037ae8711a78fbad31ae/ideas/fleet/grid-quorum-sqrt-intersection-2026-07-20.md) ‚Äî A ‚àöN read/write set beats the majority with zero intersection loss: the Maekawa grid quorum. Arrange N=k¬≤ replicas on a k√ók grid; a quorum = (all cells in a row) ‚à™ (all cells in a column), size 2k‚àí1 ‚âà 2‚àöN. ANY two grid quorums intersect ‚Äî a row and a column cross at exactly one cell ‚Äî so mutual-exclusion / read-write intersection is guaranteed with a ‚àöN quorum instead of a majority ~N/2. STRUCTURE, not size, buys it: two RANDOM subsets of the SAME size 2k‚àí1 are disjoint with positive probability. G1 EXACT (Fraction, k=6: all C(36,2)=630 grid-quorum pairs intersect, fraction=1); G2 AGREEMENT (T=2,000,000: MC disjoint-rate 0.0074515 vs closed form C(25,11)/C(36,11)=10925/1472562‚âà0.007419042458, z=0.5349<3, LOW-|z| convergence); G3 ‚â•3œÉ SURPRISE (same disjoint-rate significantly >0: z=122.5352‚â•3, HIGH-z ‚Äî random same-size subsets genuinely fail to always intersect); G4 SHIFT (k‚àà{4..10}: grid intersection fraction==1 exactly for all, 2k‚àí1<majority ‚åäk¬≤/2‚åã+1 for all, ratio (2k‚àí1)/k¬≤ strictly decreasing 7/16‚Üí19/100); G5 determinism IDENTICAL. stdlib verifier ideas/fleet/grid_quorum_sqrt_intersection.py; SEED=20260717; results_sha256 bbc54843f3693815f107f95df069c26d985a3799b2afe2c0f689a38e3659096f.

idea: [lemons-market-collapse](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/75e74d08a1eaa7d99ecbcf06d06ba4256eef8dd6/ideas/venture-lab/lemons-market-collapse-2026-07-20.md) ‚Äî A market in which EVERY possible trade strictly benefits both buyer and seller can still collapse to a vanishing fraction of its potential volume: the Akerlof "Market for Lemons". Uniform quality grid Q={1..N}, seller reservation q, rational buyer valuation Œ≤q (1<Œ≤<2, so Œ≤q>q ‚Äî every trade is win‚Äìwin with no transaction cost and no irrationality). A competitive buyer who cannot observe quality prices against the self-selecting offered pool S(p)={q‚â§p} (mean (p+1)/2), paying p iff p‚â§Œ≤(p+1)/2; the interval closes at p*=min(N,‚åäŒ≤/(2‚àíŒ≤)‚åã). At Œ≤=3/2, N=1000: p*=3, only the 3 worst qualities trade, destroyed gains-fraction D=1‚àíp*(p*+1)/(N(N+1))=250247/250250‚âà0.999988 (~0.3% of volume survives). Collapse is driven by Œ≤'s distance below 2, essentially independent of N, so a LARGER market deepens the failure. stdlib verifier ideas/venture-lab/lemons-market-collapse-2026-07-20.py; SEED=20260717; results_sha256=b0251b0aa024e235fa4991c005c1cd3deaa3a04f2fcd2e5d4e041e6e21237539 ¬∑ gates G1/G4 exact(0 discrepancies), G2 surprise z=4077.8269869233œÉ, G3 collapse D‚âà0.999988 (min-D(N=5000)=0.999984803 monotone).

done-when: VERDICT 223 finalized in sim-lab control/outbox.md with the reproduced digest + gate verdicts. Headline: a market where every trade is strictly mutually beneficial can still collapse to a vanishing fraction of its volume ‚Äî quality uncertainty (adverse selection) alone, with no friction and no irrationality, destroys nearly all the gains from trade.

source: PROPOSAL 213 (Little's law as a pathwise, distribution-and-discipline-free conservation identity ‚Äî for a single-server, non-preemptive, work-conserving queue that starts and ends empty, the area under the number-in-system curve N(t) is by construction the sum of the individual sojourn times, so L=ŒªW is not an ensemble approximation but an EXACT bookkeeping identity holding on every sample path for any arrival process, any service-time distribution, and any work-conserving discipline; the non-trivial twist: switch FIFO‚ÜíLIFO‚ÜíSIRO‚Üípriority on the SAME arrival and service streams and you change who waits, how long each waits, AND the time-average number in system L ‚Äî yet the final emptying time T (hence Œª) is byte-identical and L=ŒªW holds exactly for every discipline, so L and W are discipline-dependent while their relation to Œª is not) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T13:17:47Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-51 FLEET opener; P213 ‚Üí V226, +13; lane fleet).

## VERDICT 228 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T14:42:47Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 215 (Stackelberg commitment / first-mover advantage ‚Äî publicly committing earns more than simultaneous Nash: in a linear-demand quantity duopoly a leader who publicly and irrevocably commits earns m¬≤/8, strictly above the simultaneous Cournot m¬≤/9, advantage exactly m¬≤/72; the value comes precisely from being UNABLE to revise) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T14:15:02Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-51 GAME slot; P215 ‚Üí V228, +13; lane superbot-games).
ruling: **APPROVE**. A Stackelberg leader who publicly and irrevocably commits earns m¬≤/8=18, strictly above simultaneous Cournot m¬≤/9=16 (advantage m¬≤/72=2); the advantage comes precisely from being unable to revise. ## VERDICT 230 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T15:54:35Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 217 (consistent-hashing max-gap = H_n/n ‚Äî n nodes placed as n independent uniform points on a unit circle leave the busiest node owning an expected largest arc of exactly H_n/n (H_n = sum_{j=1}^n 1/j), a factor H_n ‚âà ln n above the fair share 1/n, with the exact alternating identity sum_{k=1}^n (-1)^(k+1) C(n,k)/(k¬∑n) == H_n/n by rational Fraction equality, the discrete-ring enumeration converging to H_n/n, and the naive "busiest owns 1/n" model correctly rejected ‚Äî which is why consistent hashing needs virtual replicas) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T15:15:13Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-52 FLEET slot; P217 ‚Üí V230, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Dropping n points independently and uniformly on the unit-circumference ring leaves the busiest node owning an expected largest arc of exactly H_n/n ‚Äî a factor H_n ‚âà ln n above the fair share 1/n, so consistent hashing's worst-case load grows like (ln n)/n rather than 1/n (which is why virtual replicas are needed).
