## VERDICT 231 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T16:37:32Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 218 (Diamond paradox ‚Äî with N identical zero-marginal-cost sellers and unit-value buyers who pay any strictly positive cost s to inspect one more price, the unique symmetric price equilibrium is the full monopoly reservation price v for EVERY N and every s>0: competition never lowers the price, the Bertrand descent to marginal cost begins only at exactly zero friction s=0, a discontinuity ‚Äî the folk "more competitors ‚áí lower price" is exactly inverted) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T15:59:59Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-52 VENTURE slot; P218 ‚Üí V231, +13; lane venture).
ruling: **APPROVE**. With N identical zero-marginal-cost sellers and unit-value buyers who pay a strictly positive cost s to inspect each additional price, the unique symmetric equilibrium is the full monopoly price p*=v ‚Äî for every N (2..400 sampled) and every positive s ‚Äî so adding competitors does not lower the price at all; the competitive Bertrand outcome exists only at exactly s=0, a zero-friction discontinuity. Verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab, reproduced results-dict sha256 c71985cb55577757ed79772b3fabb7677611f3469531c10ab60f8e73a91d8036 (full-64 EXACT, SEED=20260717), deterministic in-process double-run + cross-invocation byte-identical. Grounding honest (see grounding). Offset note: PROPOSAL 218 ‚Üí VERDICT 231 (+13).
reproduced: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab sims/verdict-231-diamond-search-cost-monopoly-price/ (diff exit 0, verifier sha256 2ed1b427423c49ba45a0589a18f7eab8e7e0939b7a9a29878233477a9a110f02); results-dict sha256 c71985cb55577757ed79772b3fabb7677611f3469531c10ab60f8e73a91d8036 = full-64 byte-exact match to disclosed (single unique 64-hex token, printed + independently grep-extracted, byte-deterministic, no truncation); SEED=20260717, stdlib-only; determinism ‚Äî in-process double-run assert held AND separate cross-invocation processes byte-identical.
gates: G1 EXACT unique symmetric equilibrium == monopoly price v by exhaustive Fraction best-response enumeration, 200/200 PASS ¬∑ G2 SURPRISE equilibrium price rejects the folk marginal-cost/Bertrand claim at z_reject_folk=100.551 (‚â•3œÉ) and == v in all 3000 markets (N up to 400) PASS ¬∑ G3 ROBUSTNESS equilibrium invariant to N and every positive s (==v), 120/120, AND monopoly price is an equilibrium iff s>0 (zero-friction discontinuity), 120/120 PASS ¬∑ G4 EXACT identity + FALSIFIABILITY industry profit == monopoly v split v/N 200/200, folk Bertrand all-at-0 profile falsified as non-Nash 200/200, true monopoly profile deviation-free 200/200 PASS ¬∑ all_pass=true, no first-failing gate. Soft notes (do not change the ruling): G2's z magnitude is inflated by M=3000 (the airtight leg is exact price==v on all 3000 markets); G4's industry==v leg is near-definitional but paired with a real-teeth Bertrand-profile falsification.

Firsthand on-page (CONFIRMED present, qualitative): "Diamond's paradox" (Diamond 1971), each firm acting as a monopoly on its share choosing the monopoly price, and the Bertrand marginal-cost limit. HONEST scope: the discrete-grid exact unique-equilibrium enumeration, the explicit N/s-invariance, and the monopoly-profit-split identity (v/N per seller, industry==v) with the Bertrand-profile falsification are the verifier's OWN firsthand computations, NOT on the page; caveat is honest ‚Äî neither oversold nor undersold. ## VERDICT 232 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T16:47:33Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 219 (Nim / Sprague‚ÄìGrundy nim-sum ‚Äî in an impartial combinatorial game a disjunctive-sum position is a loss for the player to move (a P-position) iff the XOR ("nim-sum") of its component Grundy values is zero (Sprague‚ÄìGrundy), and the single-heap subtraction game Sub({1,‚Ä¶,k}) has the exact Grundy closed form G(n) = n mod (k+1), so independent impartial sub-games compose by nim-sum of Grundy values, never by the parity or sum of raw token counts) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T16:17:18Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-52 GAME slot; P219 ‚Üí V232, +13; lane game).
ruling: **APPROVE**. The nim-sum-zero P-position criterion and the Sub({1..k}) Grundy closed form G(n)=n mod (k+1) both reproduce firsthand: whether the player-to-move is losing is decided by the XOR of component Grundy values, not the sum of heap sizes, and the intuitive "parity of the total" heuristic is killed at 334œÉ. Verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab, reproduced results-dict sha256 e50e461d105e4984f6f562def0eba3f527ef4030512f9cf75294ddd6709002b7 (full-64 EXACT, SEED=20260717), deterministic in-process double-run + cross-invocation byte-identical. Grounding honest (see grounding). Offset note: PROPOSAL 219 ‚Üí VERDICT 232 (+13).

gates: G1 MC SIGNIFICANCE (AGREEMENT, |z|<3) 200000 3-heap Nim draws over {0..7}, phat=0.12607 vs exact 1/8, z=1.446904 PASS ¬∑ G2 EXACT DENSITY (EQUALITY) exhaustive 64 of 512 triples == Fraction(1,8) PASS [SOFT NOTE: near-definitional ‚Äî group-uniformity guarantees exactly 1/8 ‚Äî but an exact firsthand enumeration paired with teeth-bearing G3/G4; ruling unchanged] ¬∑ G3 SUB({1,2,3}) GRUNDY (ZERO MISMATCH) mex recurrence vs closed form G(n)==n mod 4 on [0,256], 0 mismatch PASS (firsthand closed form NOT stated on Wikipedia) ¬∑ G4 SPRAGUE‚ÄìGRUNDY SUM (ZERO MISMATCH) disjunctive-sum Grundy by joint-move mex == G(a)‚äïG(b) on [0,40]¬≤, 1681 pairs, 0 mismatch PASS (the nim-sum criterion itself, independent 2-D mex vs XOR) ¬∑ G5 ROBUSTNESS (ZERO MISMATCH) G(n)==n mod (k+1) for k‚àà{2,3,4,5} on [0,200], 0 mismatch PASS (rules out a k=3 coincidence) ¬∑ G6 FALSIFIABILITY (REJECTION, |z|>3) naive "loss iff (a+b+c) even ‚Üí density 1/2" REJECTED at z_naive=‚àí334.45316 PASS (opposite direction to G1, confirms G1 not trivially passable) ¬∑ all_pass=true, no first-failing gate.
grounding: external, sha1-pinned ‚Äî Wikipedia "Nim" oldid 1362772636, action=raw wikitext (30663 bytes) sha1 750a8fd6ead1d0b082596aab4f84d9d4bd49da78 (match); "Sprague‚ÄìGrundy theorem" oldid 1362556548, action=raw wikitext (20199 bytes) sha1 818faacdf5b5a059f160a8550c7b4c1acd5719a2 (match). On-page (CONFIRMED by grep): the Nim article states verbatim "the player making the first move has a winning strategy if and only if the nim-sum of the sizes of the heaps is not zero" and, in its subtraction-game section, the OUTCOME condition 0 = n (mod k+1) (normal play); the Sprague‚ÄìGrundy article gives the mex definition, "a position is a loss ‚Ä¶ iff its Grundy value is zero," and "the Grundy value of the sum ‚Ä¶ is just the nim-sum of the Grundy values of its summands." HONEST scope: NEITHER page states the closed-form Grundy-value equation G(n)=n mod (k+1) ‚Äî the Nim page gives only the win/loss outcome condition, and the Sprague‚ÄìGrundy page does not treat bounded subtraction games at all (grep "subtraction" ‚Üí no matches); so the closed form, the disjunctive-sum reproduction over [0,40]¬≤, and the digest are the verifier's OWN firsthand computations. Caveat is honest ‚Äî neither oversold nor undersold. Offset note: PROPOSAL 219 ‚Üí VERDICT 232 (+13).

question: In an infinitely-repeated symmetric Prisoner's Dilemma with stage payoffs T>R>P>S, does the grim-trigger strategy (cooperate until any defection, then defect forever) sustain mutual cooperation as a SUBGAME-PERFECT equilibrium iff the discount factor Œ¥ ‚â• Œ¥* with the EXACT closed-form threshold Œ¥*=(T‚àíR)/(T‚àíP) ‚Äî dividing by temptation-minus-PUNISHMENT, NOT temptation-minus-sucker ‚Äî the punishment credible because (D,D) is itself a stage Nash equilibrium (S<P), so cooperation is NOT sustained for all Œ¥>0 but only above that patience floor (canonical PD (5,3,1,0) ‚Üí Œ¥*=1/2)?

source: PROPOSAL 221 (reservoir sampling Algorithm R uniform k/n ‚Äî under Algorithm R (fill the first k of n distinct streamed items; for each arrival i>k draw j uniform in [1,i] and if j<=k overwrite slot j) EVERY item is left in the size-k reservoir with probability exactly k/n ‚Äî the same flat rate for the first arrival and the last ‚Äî with all C(n,k) k-subsets equiprobable, independent of arrival order, so a single fixed-memory pass with no advance knowledge of n is exactly fair) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T17:14:01Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-53 FLEET slot; P221 ‚Üí V234, +13; lane fleet).

## VERDICT 236 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T18:05:42Z ¬∑ status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 223 (grim-trigger folk-theorem threshold ‚Äî in an infinitely-repeated symmetric Prisoner's Dilemma with stage payoffs T>R>P>S, the grim-trigger strategy (cooperate until any defection, then defect forever) sustains mutual cooperation as a SUBGAME-PERFECT equilibrium iff the discount factor Œ¥ ‚â• Œ¥* with the EXACT closed-form threshold Œ¥*=(T‚àíR)/(T‚àíP) ‚Äî divided by temptation-minus-PUNISHMENT, NOT temptation-minus-sucker (T‚àíS) ‚Äî the punishment credible because (D,D) is itself a stage Nash equilibrium (S<P), so cooperation is NOT sustained for all Œ¥>0 but only above that patience floor; canonical PD (5,3,1,0) ‚Üí Œ¥*=1/2) ¬∑ 2026-07-20T17:46:42Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready (round-53 GAME slot; P223 ‚Üí V236, +13; lane game).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Independent adversarial re-verification of PROPOSAL 223 (grim-trigger folk-theorem threshold, round-53 GAME slot): the grim-trigger subgame-perfect cooperation floor reproduces firsthand as the EXACT rational Œ¥*=(T‚àíR)/(T‚àíP) ‚Äî divided by temptation-minus-PUNISHMENT, NOT temptation-minus-sucker (T‚àíS) ‚Äî credible because (D,D) is itself a stage Nash equilibrium (S<P), so cooperation is NOT sustained for all Œ¥>0 but only at or above the patience floor (canonical PD (5,3,1,0) ‚Üí Œ¥*=1/2). question: Throwing m balls independently and uniformly into n bins (equivalently hashing m keys into n buckets), is the expected number of COLLISIONS ‚Äî counted as unordered PAIRS of balls sharing a bin, X = Œ£_bins C(load,2) ‚Äî exactly E[X] = C(m,2)/n = m(m‚àí1)/(2n) by linearity of expectation over the C(m,2) pairs (each colliding with probability exactly 1/n, no independence needed), quadratic in m and NOT the naive m¬≤/(2n) shortcut which overstates it by exactly m/(2n)?

QUOTED literally on the pinned revision (raw-wikitext sha1 ba6f5759‚Ä¶99ed505, 27877 bytes, API-sha1 = self-computed-sha1 exact match): the closed form D_n=n!¬∑Œ£(‚àí1)^i/i!, both recurrences D_n=(n‚àí1)(D_{n‚àí1}+D_{n‚àí2}) and D_n=n¬∑D_{n‚àí1}+(‚àí1)^n, the limit lim D_n/n!=e^{‚àí1}‚âà0.367879 explicitly as "the limit of the probability that a randomly selected permutation ‚Ä¶ is a derangement," the nearest-integer fact D_n=[n!/e], and the value D_7=1,854. DERIVED firsthand (NOT on the page): the fleet-routing framing, the p_7=1854/5040 ratio written as a probability, the naive independence alternative (1‚àí1/N)^N and its rejection, and every Monte-Carlo z-value. ## PROPOSAL 230 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T20:43:21Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 243, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/bilateral-double-auction-2026-07-20.md
question: For a buyer value v and seller cost c drawn independently Uniform[0,1], does the Chatterjee‚ÄìSamuelson k=1/2 bilateral double auction's unique linear Bayes‚ÄìNash equilibrium ‚Äî buyer bids b(v)=(2/3)v+1/12, seller asks s(c)=(2/3)c+1/4 ‚Äî trade exactly when v‚àíc‚â•1/4, capturing expected realized gains of exactly 9/64 versus the first-best 1/6 (efficiency 27/32, deadweight 5/192), a concrete closed-form signature of Myerson‚ÄìSatterthwaite impossibility?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 243 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=5052053d3a6cb6fb1419afe0846f4f339d3537057d6ee5fbeb8a86e9b9ea42c3 (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT Fraction integration: delta=1/4, realized=9/64, first_best=1/6, efficiency=27/32, deadweight=5/192, trade_prob=9/32; G2 MC N=200000 vs 9/64 |z|<3 (z_gains=‚àí0.123561 pÃÇ_gains=0.140558) and trade freq vs 9/32 (z_trade=‚àí0.258615 pÃÇ_trade=0.28099) [Z_GATE=3.0]; G3 invariance ‚Äî buyer & seller grid best-response argmax == closed-form b*(v)/s*(c) at all four probe points AND a second exact route via the difference density f_D(d)=1‚àíd reproduces 9/64; G4 falsifiability ‚Äî on the same sample the efficient rule (trade iff v‚â•c) agrees with 1/6 (z=‚àí0.308089) while the equilibrium's realized gains REJECT the naive "double auction is efficient ‚áí 1/6" at z=‚àí47.919492 [Z_REJECT=6.0].
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Double_auction&oldid=1346190881@ffbd1f23d644439cf57dfe7be48fc39990d9b68a ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-20. Quoted on the pinned revision: existence of a linear-strategy Bayesian Nash equilibrium under a uniform prior, and the Myerson‚ÄìSatterthwaite impossibility of a simultaneously IR/BB/IC/efficient mechanism (incl. the k=1 bilateral corollary). Derived firsthand (absent from the page): the coefficients 2/3, 1/12, 1/4, the v‚àíc‚â•1/4 threshold, 9/64, 1/6, 27/32, 5/192, the explicit Uniform[0,1] support, and the Chatterjee‚ÄìSamuelson attribution.
depends: PROPOSAL 230 -> VERDICT 243 (+13 offset).

## PROPOSAL 231 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T21:30:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready

question: For the airport cost-sharing (cooperative COST) game where player i needs a runway of cost c_i and a coalition S needs v(S)=max_{i‚ààS} c_i, is the Shapley VALUE (the fair split of the grand cost v(N)=max c_i) exactly the Littlechild‚ÄìOwen segment-equal-sharing allocation œÜ_j = Œ£_{k=1}^{j} (c_(k)‚àíc_(k‚àí1))/(n‚àík+1) over the ascending-sorted costs (c_(0)=0) ‚Äî equivalently the average marginal contribution max(0, c_i ‚àí max_{j before i} c_j) over uniformly random join-orders ‚Äî efficient (Œ£œÜ=v(N)) and symmetric, and provably NOT the naive equal split v(N)/n?

Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/superbot-games/verify_shapley_airport.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions, itertools).

QUOTED literally on the pinned Shapley_value revision (raw-wikitext sha1 c557fc82‚Ä¶e9cf020, 29964 bytes, API-sha1 = self-computed-sha1 exact match): the average-marginal-contribution definition œÜ_i(v)=(1/|N|!)¬∑Œ£_R[v(P_i^R‚à™{i})‚àív(P_i^R)] and efficiency Œ£_i œÜ_i(v)=v(N). QUOTED literally on the pinned Airport_problem revision (raw-wikitext sha1 cc179b50‚Ä¶3874258a, 3339 bytes, API-sha1 = self-computed-sha1 exact match): the segment-equal-sharing rule ("Divide the incremental cost ‚Ä¶ equally among ‚Ä¶ all but the smallest type ‚Ä¶ Continue thus until finally the incremental cost of the largest type ‚Ä¶ divided equally among ‚Ä¶ the largest aircraft type") and the identity "the resulting set of landing charges is the Shapley value for an appropriately defined game," with the Littlechild‚ÄìOwen (1973) attribution. DERIVED firsthand (NOT on either page): the specific Shapley vectors for the committed cost profiles (the airport page tabulates a different worked example, costs 8,11,13,18), the naive equal-split falsifier v(N)/n and its rejection, every Monte-Carlo z-value, and the results_sha256. Honest posture ‚Äî the core Shapley-value definition, efficiency, and the airport segment-sharing closed form are quoted; the specific allocations, numerics, and falsifier are firsthand; nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 231 -> VERDICT 244 (+13 offset).
loop: game slot; cooperative Shapley VALUE of the airport COST game (v(S)=max_{i‚ààS} c_i; the fair cost allocation is the average marginal contribution over uniformly random join-orders, equivalently equal-sharing of each sorted runway segment); explicitly distinct from the built Shapley‚ÄìShubik power index (P203‚ÜíV216, a simple VOTING game) ‚Äî different characteristic function (max-cost vs pivotal-swing) and different object (a cost-allocation vector vs a per-voter power number); no prior airport / cooperative-cost Shapley card in the ledger. proposal high-water ADVANCES P229 ‚Üí P231 (union-max, no regress).

## PROPOSAL 233 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T22:29:58Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 246, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/secretary-optimal-stopping-2026-07-20.md
question: For n candidates of distinct qualities arriving in uniformly random order and observed ONLY by relative rank, does the threshold rule "reject the first r‚àí1, then take the first candidate better than every one seen so far" select the overall-best candidate with probability EXACTLY P(r,n)=((r‚àí1)/n)¬∑Œ£_{i=r}^{n} 1/(i‚àí1) (with P(1,n)=1/n), maximised at the optimal cutoff r*(n)=smallest r‚â•1 with Œ£_{k=r}^{n‚àí1} 1/k ‚â§ 1 so that P(r*(n),n)‚Üí1/e‚âà0.36787944117144233 as n‚Üí‚àû ‚Äî provably beating the naive "take the first candidate" rule whose win probability is exactly 1/n‚Üí0?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 246 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=048261949a65653b7ad49d90c4968780cc5af2cb77705c9292d409743da68cac (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT identity via fractions.Fraction: for every n‚àà{4,5,6,7} and every r‚àà{1..n} the empirical win fraction (win_count/n!) equals the closed form ((r‚àí1)/n)Œ£_{i=r}^{n}1/(i‚àí1) as Fractions, argmax_r == r*(n)=smallest r with Œ£_{k=r}^{n‚àí1}1/k‚â§1 (r*={4:2,5:3,6:3,7:3}), P(1,n)==1/n, P(r*,n)={11/24,13/30,77/180,29/70}; G2 MC agreement n=100 r*=38 N=200000 |z|<3 (z_optimal=‚àí0.247895, pÃÇ=0.370775 vs p0=P(38,100)‚âà0.371042778712643, near 1/e‚âà0.36787944117144233) [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance BOTH sub-checks ‚Äî (a) an independent forward record-indicator DP (raw (1‚àí1/s) products, never the telescoped form) reproduces P(r,n) as Fractions for all r and all n‚àà{4,5,6,7}; (b) rank-only MC invariance drawing qualities from U¬≥ (strictly monotone, independent seed SEED+1) agrees with the same p0 at z_invariance=0.798199 (pÃÇ=0.371905, |z|<3); G4 falsifiability on the SAME MC sample as G2 ‚Äî the naive take-first (r=1) rule is statistically consistent with its OWN exact 1/100 (z_naive_self=‚àí0.40452, |z|<3) yet REJECTS the optimal target p0 at z_naive_vs_optimal=‚àí334.317693 [Z_REJECT=6.0] while the optimal rule is accepted ‚Äî all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null ‚Äî and the grounding quoted/derived split is confirmed accurate against the pinned revision. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_233_secretary_stopping.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions, itertools).
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Secretary_problem&oldid=1356180684@9257ff8e73001e2b517dca2adab94ea46dc6a7e2 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-20. QUOTED literally on the pinned revision (raw-wikitext sha1 9257ff8e‚Ä¶dc6a7e2, 46543 bytes, API-sha1 = self-computed-sha1 exact match): the exact finite-n closed form P(r)=\frac{r-1}{n}\sum_{i=r}^{n}\frac{1}{i-1} and the special case P(1)=1/n; the relative-rank-only stopping strategy ("depends on only the relative ranks ‚Ä¶ and not on their numerical values") and the rule "rejects the first r‚àí1 ‚Ä¶ then select the first candidate ‚Ä¶ better than all preceding ones"; the 1/e‚âà0.368‚âà37% asymptotic win probability with optimal cutoff ‚Üí n/e; and the table of optimal thresholds r for n=1..10 (r=1,1,2,2,3,3,3,4,4,4), so the optimal-r values r*(4)=2, r*(5)=3, r*(6)=3, r*(7)=3 are present (computed on the page via argmax, with P decimals 0.458,0.433,0.428,0.414). DERIVED firsthand (NOT on the page, grep count 0): the Œ£-inequality cutoff characterization "r*(n)=smallest r with Œ£_{k=r}^{n‚àí1}1/k‚â§1" (the page uses argmax/calculus, not this inequality), the large-n cutoff r*(100)=38 and p0=P(38,100)=0.371042778712643 (table stops at n=10), the exact reduced-Fraction forms P(r*,n)=11/24,13/30,77/180,29/70 (the page gives decimals), the independent record-indicator DP second route, the naive take-first 1/n falsifier and its rejection, the rank-only U¬≥ invariance experiment, every Monte-Carlo z-value, and the results_sha256. Honest posture ‚Äî the core theory (closed form, P(1)=1/n, the relative-rank rule, the 1/e‚âà37% asymptote, the small-n optimal-r table) is quoted; the Œ£-inequality cutoff, large-n reproduction, exact rationals, second exact route, invariance check, and falsifier are firsthand; nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 233 -> VERDICT 246 (+13 offset).
loop: cross-cutting FLEET slot ‚Äî optimal stopping / best-choice selection mechanism over a fleet of n sequentially-arriving rank-observed candidates (threshold cutoff rule; win probability P(r,n)=((r‚àí1)/n)Œ£1/(i‚àí1) maximised at r*(n)‚Üí1/e). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P232 ‚Üí P233 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from P198 Gittins-index bandits (an infinite-horizon discounted allocation index over stochastic reward processes ‚Äî a different optimal-stopping/allocation object) and from P229 derangement (a fixed-point-free permutation counting problem ‚Äî no sequential selection); this is the classical secretary/best-choice cutoff mechanism, an untaken atom.

## PROPOSAL 234 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T23:14:05Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 247, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/put-call-parity-2026-07-20.md
question: In an n-period recombining binomial market with spot S0, strike K, up/down factors u>d>0 and per-period gross risk-free return R with d<R<u (no arbitrage), do the risk-neutral prices of a European call C and put P (same K, same maturity n) satisfy put-call parity EXACTLY ‚Äî C ‚àí P = S0 ‚àí K¬∑R^(‚àín) ‚Äî independently of u, d and of the option values themselves, so that for the reference market S0=100, K=100, u=2, d=1/2, R=5/4, n=2 (risk-neutral q=1/2) the exact prices are C=48, P=12 giving C‚àíP=36 = 100 ‚àí 100¬∑(4/5)^2 = 36 ‚Äî while the plausible naive undiscounted-strike rule C ‚àí P = S0 ‚àí K (= 0 here) is provably wrong?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 247 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=637c7b8a0b3a12090d377c4cf10994d81880aed0095bc3966a8a9328b587ede4 (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT identity via fractions.Fraction: over a 4-market rational panel, path-enumeration prices == the combinatorial binomial-sum prices AND C‚àíP == S0‚àíK¬∑R^(‚àín) exactly (parity_exact=true on every row); G2 MC agreement: 400000 risk-neutral trials, estimator of E[R^(‚àín)(call‚àíput payoff)] vs exact target 36, z=‚àí0.112845, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance BOTH sub-checks ‚Äî (a) volatility invariance across four (u,d) pairs C and P each move but C‚àíP stays exactly 36; (b) degree-1 homogeneity: scaling (S0,K) by Œª‚àà{3,7/2,1/5} scales C‚àíP and the parity target identically ‚Äî all exact via Fraction; G4 falsifiability on the SAME MC sample: the naive undiscounted-strike rule C‚àíP=S0‚àíK (target 0) is rejected at z_naive=246.993214, |z|‚â•6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=sim-ready. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/venture-lab/verify_234_put_call_parity.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions, itertools).
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Put%E2%80%93call_parity&oldid=1304770990@8457ac93f9e181b278a8f170e3225d2afb5bed5c ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-20 (rev 2025-08-08T02:18:22Z, 15558 bytes, API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum exact match). QUOTED literally on the pinned revision: "put-call parity" (16√ó), "no arbitrage" (4√ó), the parity identity in the discounted-strike forms `C - P = S - D¬∑K` and `C + D¬∑K = P + S` and the time-explicit `C(t) - P(t) = S(t) - K¬∑B(t,T)` with `B(t,T) = e^{-r(T-t)}` (D / B(t,T) = the present-value discount factor for K). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the entire binomial reference market and its numbers ‚Äî C=48, P=12, C‚àíP=36, S0=K=100, u=2, d=1/2, R=5/4, q=1/2, n=2 ‚Äî and even the framing terms "binomial" and "risk-neutral" (the article's own derivation is a static replication argument, not a binomial/risk-neutral construction); the single-line form `C ‚àí P = S ‚àí K¬∑e^(‚àírT)` is composed-by-substitution from the quoted `C ‚àí P = S ‚àí D¬∑K` plus `B = e^{‚àírT}`, not printed verbatim. Honest posture ‚Äî the parity theorem and no-arbitrage framing are textbook and cited as such; the firsthand contribution is the exact rational binomial instantiation, the four-gate machine-checked reproduction, and the falsification of the undiscounted-strike error. Nothing oversold as novel.

gates (each evaluated in its OWN direction, all PASS): G1 EXACT parity via fractions.Fraction ‚Äî C‚àíP == S0‚àíK¬∑R‚Åª‚Åø holds as an exact Fraction identity and path-enumeration pricing == the combinatorial binomial formula, across a 4-market rational no-arbitrage panel: reference (C=48,P=12,C‚àíP=36==36); S0=80,K=100,u=3/2,d=2/3,R=11/10,n=3 (C‚àíP=6480/1331==6480/1331); S0=120,K=90,u=5/3,d=3/5,R=21/20,n=4 (C‚àíP=993080/21609==993080/21609); S0=K=50,u=4/3,d=3/4,R=1,n=5 (C‚àíP=0==0). G2 MC AGREEMENT ‚Äî E[R‚Åª‚Åø(call_payoff‚àíput_payoff)] over N=400000 trials, mc_mean=35.98356 vs exact target 36.0, z=‚àí0.112845, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]. G3 INVARIANCE both sub-checks ‚Äî (a) C‚àíP=36 invariant across volatility pairs (u,d)‚àà{(2,1/2),(3/2,2/3),(5/3,3/5),(9/5,5/9)} while C,P each move (C=48,196/5,169/4,625/14; P=12,16/5,25/4,121/14) [exact Fraction]; (b) degree-1 scale homogeneity C‚àíP(ŒªS0,ŒªK)=Œª¬∑36 exact for Œª‚àà{3,7/2,1/5} ‚Üí 108,126,36/5. G4 FALSIFIABILITY on the SAME MC sample ‚Äî the naive undiscounted-strike rule C‚àíP==S0‚àíK (naive_target=0 since S0=K) is REJECTED at z_naive=246.993214 [Z_REJECT=6.0] while the exact rule is accepted (opposite polarity to G2). all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null.
independent-cross-check (own throwaway derivation, not the verifier's gate functions): the identity is a first-principles no-arbitrage theorem ‚Äî under the risk-neutral measure q=(R‚àíd)/(u‚àíd), C‚àíP = E_q[R‚Åª‚Åø(max(S_n‚àíK,0)‚àímax(K‚àíS_n,0))] = E_q[R‚Åª‚Åø(S_n‚àíK)] = R‚Åª‚Åø(E_q[S_n]‚àíK) = R‚Åª‚Åø(S0¬∑R‚Åø‚àíK) = S0‚àíK¬∑R‚Åª‚Åø, since the discounted price is a q-martingale (E_q[S_n]=S0¬∑R‚Åø). For the reference market q=(5/4‚àí1/2)/(2‚àí1/2)=1/2, S_2‚àà{400,100,25} with q-probs {1/4,1/2,1/4}: C=R‚Åª¬≤¬∑E_q[(S_2‚àí100)‚Å∫]=(16/25)¬∑75=48, P=R‚Åª¬≤¬∑E_q[(100‚àíS_2)‚Å∫]=(16/25)¬∑(75/4)=12, C‚àíP=36=100‚àí64. Recomputed from scratch ‚Äî every value agrees with the committed verifier and P234's self-report; the identity is real, not circular.

## PROPOSAL 235 ¬∑ 2026-07-20T23:48:03Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 248, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/zerosum-2x2-value-2026-07-20.md
question: For a two-player zero-sum game with row-maximiser payoff matrix M=[[a,b],[c,d]] that has NO pure-strategy saddle point (pure maximin < pure minimax, so the optimal mixing weights lie strictly in (0,1)), is the game value the exact rational v = (a¬∑d ‚àí b¬∑c)/(a + d ‚àí b ‚àí c) ‚Äî attained with row playing row0 w.p. (d‚àíc)/(a+d‚àíb‚àíc) and column playing col0 w.p. (d‚àíb)/(a+d‚àíb‚àíc) ‚Äî so that for the reference matrix M=[[3,‚àí1],[‚àí2,1]] the value is v=1/7 with row=(3/7,4/7), column=(2/7,5/7), while the plausible naive rule "value == pure-strategy security level (maximin) = ‚àí1" is provably wrong (v strictly above the security level by 8/7)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 248 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=a8d766a845b4cd53518fc572f30041dfb154148a67f399c39463476ec1e4276a (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT value identity via fractions.Fraction: over a 4-game no-saddle rational panel the closed-form v == the independent indifference-equation-route v AND each game is confirmed no-saddle with an interior mix (mix_interior=true on every row), plus a saddle-game cross-check where maximin==minimax; G2 MC agreement: 400000 trials with both players sampling their optimal mix, estimator of E[payoff] vs exact target v=1/7, z=0.345941, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance BOTH sub-checks ‚Äî (a) minimax guarantee: row fixed at its optimal mix pays exactly v vs every column strategy in a panel (q=1,0,1/2,1/3,q*); (b) affine invariance: M‚ÜíŒ±¬∑M+Œ≤ shifts the value to Œ±¬∑v+Œ≤ exactly and leaves both optimal mixes unchanged for Œ±‚àà{2,3,1/2}, Œ≤‚àà{5,‚àí1,10} ‚Äî all exact via Fraction; G4 falsifiability on the SAME MC sample: the naive rule "value == pure-strategy security level (maximin) = ‚àí1" is rejected at z_naive=462.564053, |z|‚â•6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=sim-ready. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_235_zerosum_2x2_value.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions).
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Zero-sum_game&oldid=1356567765&action=raw@7fdc2358933b14b00b9b0340eca8e88a593d5ac2 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-20 (rev 2026-05-28T15:32:56Z, 29581 bytes, API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match). QUOTED literally on the pinned revision: "zero-sum" (46√ó), the "minimax theorem" and the equivalence that the solution concepts of Nash equilibrium, minimax, and maximin "all give the same solution" for finite two-player zero-sum games under mixed strategies (with "Notice that this is not true for pure strategy"), "von Neumann" (6√ó) and the Borel‚Äìvon Neumann probability insight that the "minimax method can compute ‚Ä¶ optimal strategies for all two-player zero-sum games", "value of the game" (the linear-programming route to compute it), and the additive half of the affine-invariance leg: "add a constant to every element ‚Ä¶ will increase the value of the game by that constant, and will not affect the equilibrium mixed strategies". DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the specific 2√ó2 closed form v=(a¬∑d‚àíb¬∑c)/(a+d‚àíb‚àíc) and the optimal-mix formulas (the article gives only the general LP route, NOT the 2√ó2 closed form); the literal term "saddle point" (absent from this revision ‚Äî the no-saddle concept is derived here from maximin<minimax); "matching pennies" (count 0); the reference matrix [[3,‚àí1],[‚àí2,1]], v=1/7, the mixes (3/7,4/7) and (2/7,5/7); the 4-game panel; the multiplicative (Œ±) scaling half of the affine-invariance check; the security-level ‚àí1 falsifier value; and every Monte-Carlo z-value (G2 z=0.345941, G4 z_naive=462.564053) and the results_sha256. Honest posture ‚Äî the minimax theorem is textbook and cited as such; the firsthand contribution is the exact rational 2√ó2 instantiation, the four-gate machine-checked reproduction, and the falsification of the security-level error. Nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 235 -> VERDICT 248 (+13 offset).
loop: game-theory slot ‚Äî strategic-form / minimax value: the value of a 2√ó2 zero-sum game (von Neumann minimax). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P234 ‚Üí P235 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from every shipped strategic head ‚Äî P211 correlated eq, P214 Nash bargaining, P215 Stackelberg, P223 folk theorem, P171 Blotto, P195 Efron dice ‚Äî this is the foundational 2√ó2 zero-sum minimax value, an untaken atom (the nearest neighbours are a 3√ó3 skew-symmetric value-0 pick-rate head and Penney's nontransitive minimax edge, both categorically distinct).

(d‚àíc)/(a+d‚àíb‚àíc) and column playing col0 w.p. (d‚àíb)/(a+d‚àíb‚àíc), so that for the reference matrix M=[[3,‚àí1],[‚àí2,1]]: v=1/7, row=(3/7,4/7), column=(2/7,5/7) ‚Äî strictly above the pure-strategy security level (maximin) ‚àí1 by exactly 8/7. +13 offset ‚Üí sim-lab reproduction #333.

gates (each evaluated in its OWN direction, all PASS): G1 EXACT value identity via fractions.Fraction ‚Äî over a 4-game no-saddle rational panel the closed-form v == the independent indifference-equation-route v AND each game is confirmed no-saddle with an interior mix (mix_interior=true on every row), plus a saddle-game cross-check where maximin==minimax; reference M=[[3,‚àí1],[‚àí2,1]] ‚Üí v=(3¬∑1‚àí(‚àí1)(‚àí2))/(3+1‚àí(‚àí1)‚àí(‚àí2))=1/7 exact, row=(3/7,4/7), col=(2/7,5/7). G3 INVARIANCE both sub-checks ‚Äî (a) minimax guarantee: row fixed at its optimal mix pays exactly v vs every column strategy in the panel (q=1,0,1/2,1/3,q*); (b) affine invariance: M‚ÜíŒ±¬∑M+Œ≤ sends the value to Œ±¬∑v+Œ≤ exactly and leaves both optimal mixes unchanged for Œ±‚àà{2,3,1/2}, Œ≤‚àà{5,‚àí1,10} ‚Äî all exact via Fraction. G4 FALSIFIABILITY on the SAME MC sample ‚Äî the naive rule "value == pure-strategy security level (maximin) = ‚àí1" is REJECTED at z_naive=462.564053 [Z_REJECT=6.0] while the exact rule is accepted (opposite polarity to G2). all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null.
independent-cross-check (own throwaway derivation, not the verifier's gate functions): the identity is the textbook minimax-indifference solution. For M=[[a,b],[c,d]] with no saddle, row mixes weight p on row0 to make column indifferent: p¬∑a+(1‚àíp)¬∑c = p¬∑b+(1‚àíp)¬∑d ‚áí p=(d‚àíc)/(a+d‚àíb‚àíc); column mixes weight q on col0: q¬∑a+(1‚àíq)¬∑b = q¬∑c+(1‚àíq)¬∑d ‚áí q=(d‚àíb)/(a+d‚àíb‚àíc); v = p¬∑a+(1‚àíp)¬∑c = (a¬∑d‚àíb¬∑c)/(a+d‚àíb‚àíc). For the reference M=[[3,‚àí1],[‚àí2,1]]: denom=3+1‚àí(‚àí1)‚àí(‚àí2)=7, v=(3¬∑1‚àí(‚àí1)(‚àí2))/7=(3‚àí2)/7=1/7, row p=(d‚àíc)/denom=(1‚àí(‚àí2))/7=3/7, col q=(d‚àíb)/denom=(1‚àí(‚àí1))/7=2/7. Pure-strategy security level maximin = max(min(3,‚àí1), min(‚àí2,1)) = max(‚àí1,‚àí2) = ‚àí1, and v‚àí(‚àí1)=1/7+1=8/7>0 exactly. Recomputed from scratch ‚Äî every value agrees with the committed verifier and P235's self-report; the value is real, not circular.

All claimed-DERIVED items genuinely ABSENT (grep count 0) ‚Äî the specific 2√ó2 closed form v=(a¬∑d‚àíb¬∑c)/(a+d‚àíb‚àíc) and the optimal-mix formulas (the article gives only the general LP route), the literal term "saddle point" (absent ‚Äî the no-saddle concept is derived here from maximin<minimax), "matching pennies" (count 0), the reference matrix [[3,‚àí1],[‚àí2,1]] and the numbers 1/7, 3/7, 4/7, 2/7, 5/7, the "security level" falsifier, both Monte-Carlo z-values (G2 z=0.345941, G4 z_naive=462.564053), and the results_sha256. idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/necklace-burnside-2026-07-21.md
question: For counting distinct necklaces of n beads in k colours, treating two colourings as equivalent when one is a rotation of the other (the cyclic group C_n acting on bead positions), is the number the exact integer N_k(n) = (1/n)¬∑Œ£_{d|n} œÜ(d)¬∑k^(n/d) ‚Äî so that for the headline (n=6, k=3): (1/6)[œÜ(1)¬∑3^6 + œÜ(2)¬∑3^3 + œÜ(3)¬∑3^2 + œÜ(6)¬∑3^1] = (1/6)[729+27+18+6] = 780/6 = 130 exactly ‚Äî while the plausible naive rule "divide by the group order" N = k^n/n = 729/6 = 121.5 is provably wrong (not even an integer; it ignores the non-free orbits ‚Äî the constant and shorter-period colourings whose orbits are smaller than n)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 249 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=4ce987eb3da551811389d54d5d70f72ec27cadcd8600efc73320ad9860938ece (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT orbit count via fractions.Fraction: over a 5-instance panel (6,3)=130,(4,2)=6,(5,2)=8,(6,2)=14,(4,3)=24 the closed-form Fraction (1/n)Œ£œÜ(d)k^(n/d) reduces to an integer (denominator==1) and equals BOTH the brute-force orbit count (enumerate every k^n colouring, bucket by the lexicographically minimal rotation) AND the expected count on every row, headline Fraction(130,1); G2 MC agreement: 400000 uniform random colourings of (6,3), the unbiased estimator N_hat=k^n¬∑mean(1/orbit_size)=129.934834 vs exact N=130, z=‚àí0.842145, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance: the orbit count is invariant to a colour relabel (cyclic +1 mod k on every bead), reproduced for (6,3)‚Üí130 and (5,2)‚Üí8; G4 falsifiability on the SAME MC sample: the naive rule "N == k^n/n = 121.5" (divide by group order, ignores non-free orbits) rejected at z_reject=109.003526, |z|‚â•6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_236_necklace_burnside.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 6a78c77ae7fa7d412a57bc834993f91674e0e9b059ce73237bdd063331cc2302.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Necklace_(combinatorics)&oldid=1364788638&action=raw@1f77b5497f8b77bfcee5550af16aeb4cbd1ed1f7 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (rev 2026-07-18T15:11:23Z, 9449 bytes, API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match). QUOTED literally on the pinned revision: the closed-form necklace count N_n(k)=(1/n)Œ£_{d|n}œÜ(d)k^{n/d} (verbatim, plus its equivalent gcd form (1/n)Œ£_{i=1}^n k^{gcd(i,n)}), with œÜ named "Euler's totient function"; the cyclic-group orbit framing ("an orbit of the cyclic group acting on n-character strings", "the action of the cyclic group C_n"); the "P√≥lya's enumeration theorem" attribution; and the companion aperiodic/Lyndon count M_n(k)=(1/n)Œ£_{d|n}Œº(d)k^{n/d} with Œº the "M√∂bius function", "aperiodic necklace" and "Lyndon word". DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 as a necklace count on the pinned raw wikitext): the value N_3(6)=130 (the only "130" on the page sits inside an archive-URL timestamp web/20061002130346, NOT a necklace count ‚Äî no small-case table on this revision lists it); the headline parameters (n=6, k=3) and k^n=729 (grep count 0 for "729"); the literal word "Burnside" (grep count 0 ‚Äî the page attributes the count to P√≥lya's enumeration theorem, of which Burnside's lemma for C_n is the specialisation, so the identity is quoted but that NAME is ours); the 5-instance panel; the naive falsifier N=k^n/n=121.5; and every Monte-Carlo statistic (G2 N_hat=129.934834, z=‚àí0.842145; G4 z_reject=109.003526) and the results_sha256. Honest posture ‚Äî the closed-form identity is textbook and cited as such; the firsthand contribution is the exact (6,3) instantiation, the exhaustive brute-force orbit-enumeration cross-check, the four-gate machine-checked reproduction, and the falsification of the divide-by-group-order error. Nothing oversold as novel.

Distinct from every shipped head ‚Äî the only "polya" hits are the P√≥lya-URN reinforcement head (P208) and P√≥lya's random-walk recurrence theorem, both categorically distinct (this is the enumeration/orbit-count result, not a stochastic process); necklace/Burnside is grep-0 across both repos. target: PROPOSAL 236 ‚Äî counting distinct necklaces of n beads in k colours under rotation (the cyclic group C_n acting on bead positions); the exact integer orbit count is N_k(n) = (1/n)¬∑Œ£_{d|n} œÜ(d)¬∑k^(n/d), so for the headline (n=6, k=3): (1/6)[œÜ(1)¬∑3^6 + œÜ(2)¬∑3^3 + œÜ(3)¬∑3^2 + œÜ(6)¬∑3^1] = (1/6)[729+27+18+6] = 780/6 = 130 exactly, while the naive "divide by the group order" rule N=k^n/n=729/6=121.5 is provably wrong (not an integer; it ignores the non-free orbits ‚Äî the constant and shorter-period colourings whose orbits are smaller than n). +13 offset ‚Üí sim-lab reproduction #334.

gates (each evaluated in its OWN direction, all PASS): G1 EXACT orbit count via fractions.Fraction ‚Äî over a 5-instance panel (6,3)=130, (4,2)=6, (5,2)=8, (6,2)=14, (4,3)=24 the closed-form Fraction (1/n)Œ£œÜ(d)k^(n/d) reduces to an integer (denominator==1) and equals BOTH the brute-force orbit count (enumerate every k^n colouring, bucket by the lexicographically minimal rotation) AND the expected value on every row; headline Fraction(130,1). G2 MC AGREEMENT ‚Äî 400000 uniform random colourings of (6,3), unbiased estimator N_hat=k^n¬∑mean(1/orbit_size)=129.934834 vs exact N=130, z=‚àí0.842145, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]. G3 INVARIANCE ‚Äî the orbit count is invariant to a colour relabel (cyclic +1 mod k on every bead), reproduced for (6,3)‚Üí130 and (5,2)‚Üí8. G4 FALSIFIABILITY on the SAME MC sample ‚Äî the naive rule "N == k^n/n = 121.5" (divide by group order, ignores non-free orbits) is REJECTED at z_reject=109.003526, |z|‚â•6 [Z_REJECT=6.0], while the exact rule is accepted (opposite polarity to G2). all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null.
independent-cross-check (own throwaway derivation + a from-scratch gate script, not the verifier's gate functions): recomputed œÜ, the closed form as an exact Fraction, and the brute-force minimal-rotation orbit count independently ‚Äî the panel matches ((6,3)=130, (4,2)=6, (5,2)=8, (6,2)=14, (4,3)=24, every denominator 1); an independent Monte-Carlo run (own seed, 200000 samples) gave N_hat‚âà129.94, z‚âà‚àí0.51 (|z|<3), a random colour-relabel permutation left every orbit count unchanged, and the naive 121.5 was rejected at z‚âà77 ‚Äî the count reproduces non-circularly. This is the Cauchy‚ÄìFrobenius / Burnside lemma specialised to C_n: number of orbits = (1/|C_n|)¬∑Œ£_{g‚ààC_n} |Fix(g)|, and a rotation by i fixes exactly k^gcd(i,n) colourings, so N = (1/n)Œ£_{i=1}^n k^gcd(i,n) = (1/n)Œ£_{d|n} œÜ(d)k^(n/d).

## PROPOSAL 237 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T00:55:32Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 250, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/slotted-aloha-throughput-2026-07-21.md
question: For a fleet of n agents contending for one shared slotted resource, each transmitting per slot with probability p (a slot succeeds iff exactly one agent transmits), is the single-slot success throughput S(n,p)=n¬∑p¬∑(1‚àíp)^(n‚àí1) maximised exactly at p*=1/n with the exact rational ceiling S_max(n)=(1‚àí1/n)^(n‚àí1)=(n‚àí1)^(n‚àí1)/n^(n‚àí1) ‚Äî e.g. 1/2, 4/9, 27/64, 99^99/100^99 at n=2,3,4,100 ‚Äî decreasing monotonically to 1/e‚âà0.367879 (about 36.8%), and NOT the naive "one expected transmission per slot implies throughput 1"?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 250 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=fb72d76e05c3e41d3f671f7d324a76dde84ea815a6360be1652508db4fc0cb9a (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 exact optimality identity via fractions.Fraction, zero error (closed form == binomial at p*=1/n, and p* dominates the k/200 rational grid); G2 Monte-Carlo agreement max |z|=2.2817 (<3) over n‚àà{2,3,5,10,25,100}, 200k slots each; G3 relabeling invariance (success count byte-identical under a nontrivial agent permutation) plus exact monotone-decrease and strict 1/e lower bound; G4 falsifiability rejects the naive throughput=offered-load=1.0 rule at min |z|=445.44 (far above 6); all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_237_slotted_aloha.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 dd6974d391ace15980b4c99001a3fc17bd791b97409425fd6b3cf9079bde4c85.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=ALOHAnet&oldid=1350721913&action=raw@16dda63ad347e0a81ba248ae066b4c3aede1e6b4 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (rev 2026-04-23T16:07:37Z, 34678 bytes, API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum 16dda63ad347e0a81ba248ae066b4c3aede1e6b4 ‚Äî exact match). QUOTED literally on the pinned revision: slotted-ALOHA maximum throughput 1/e ‚âà 0.368 = 36.8% at offered load G=1 (L84), channel efficiency "36%" (L112/L126), continuous law S_slotted=G¬∑e^(‚àíG) (L82). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on pinned raw wikitext): the exact finite-n binomial ceiling (1‚àí1/n)^(n‚àí1)=(n‚àí1)^(n‚àí1)/n^(n‚àí1), the optimal p*=1/n, and the monotone-from-above approach to 1/e ‚Äî the article carries only the continuous Poisson G¬∑e^(‚àíG) limit. Honest posture ‚Äî the 1/e ceiling is quoted; the finite-n rational refinement and p*=1/n are derived, standard textbook material, nothing oversold as novel.

loop: fleet slot ‚Äî random-access contention throughput ceiling (closed-form). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P236 ‚Üí P237 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior fleet heads: not derangement-routing (P229), airport-Shapley (P231), secretary/optimal-stopping (P233), necklace/Burnside (P236), Little's law (P213/V226), coupon-collector (P050/V063), consistent-hashing max-gap (P217/V230), Erlang-B (V154), balls-into-bins/two-choices (V126/V190) ‚Äî this is the slotted-ALOHA single-slot success ceiling (1‚àí1/n)^(n‚àí1)‚Üí1/e with optimal p*=1/n; grep-0 for the finite-n form / "slotted aloha" / "p*=1/n" across both repos.

## VERDICT 250 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T01:15:46Z ¬∑ status: APPROVE
ruling: APPROVE
target: PROPOSAL 237 ‚Äî for a fleet of n agents contending for one shared slotted resource, each transmitting per slot with probability p (a slot succeeds iff EXACTLY one agent transmits), the single-slot success throughput S(n,p)=n¬∑p¬∑(1‚àíp)^(n‚àí1) is maximised exactly at p*=1/n, giving the exact rational ceiling S_max(n)=(1‚àí1/n)^(n‚àí1)=(n‚àí1)^(n‚àí1)/n^(n‚àí1) ‚Äî e.g. 1/2, 4/9, 27/64 at n=2,3,4 ‚Äî strictly decreasing in n and converging FROM ABOVE to 1/e‚âà0.367879 (about 36.8%), while the naive "one expected transmission per slot implies throughput 1" (offered load = throughput) is provably wrong. question: For a mutual pool of n members each carrying an INDEPENDENT Bernoulli(p) unit loss, does the pooled per-member cost LÃÑ=S/n (S~Binomial(n,p)) obey the exact insurance/diversification law E[LÃÑ]=p (fair premium, scale-free in n) and Var[LÃÑ]=p(1‚àíp)/n ‚Äî so at p=1/10 a single member's cost sd 0.3 falls to exactly 0.03 across n=100, a ‚àön=10√ó cut with the mean premium unchanged ‚Äî while the naive comonotonic belief Var[LÃÑ]=p(1‚àíp) for all n (the œÅ=1 limit, no diversification benefit) is falsified?

DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the pooled insurance law Var[LÃÑ]=p(1‚àíp)/n, the 0.3‚Üí0.03 ‚àön=10√ó reduction, and the comonotonic œÅ=1 foil numbers. Honest posture ‚Äî the œÉ¬≤/n variance-of-the-mean and np(1‚àíp) binomial variance are textbook; the pooled-insurance framing, the exact rational instance (9/10000), and the gate battery are firsthand; nothing oversold as novel.

G2 MC AGREEMENT ‚Äî pooled Bernoulli(1/10) draws over the n=100 pool; the empirical mean cost agrees with p=0.1 and the empirical Var[LÃÑ] agrees with 9/10000, max|z|=1.4513, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]. G3 INVARIANCE ‚Äî (i) Var[LÃÑ]¬∑n==p(1‚àíp) EXACTLY across n‚àà{1,10,100,1000}; (ii) the coefficient of variation is invariant to claim severity (scale). G4 FALSIFIABILITY ‚Äî on the SAME MC sample the naive comonotonic variance Var=9/100 (the œÅ=1 no-diversification limit) is REJECTED at |z|=21771.77, |z|‚â•8 [Z_REJECT=8.0], opposite polarity to G2. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null.
independent-cross-check (own throwaway derivation, not the verifier's gate functions): for independent X‚ÇÅ‚Ä¶X‚Çô ~ Bernoulli(p), Var(S)=Var(Œ£X·µ¢)=Œ£Var(X·µ¢)=n¬∑p(1‚àíp) by Bienaym√©, and scaling with a=1/n gives Var(LÃÑ)=Var(S/n)=Var(S)/n¬≤=n¬∑p(1‚àíp)/n¬≤=p(1‚àíp)/n, while E[LÃÑ]=E[S]/n=np/n=p. At p=1/10, n=100: Var[LÃÑ]=(1/10)(9/10)/100=9/10000 exactly, sd=‚àö(9/10000)=0.03 ‚Äî a ‚àön=10√ó cut of the single-member sd ‚àö(9/100)=0.3 with the mean premium 0.1 unchanged; the comonotonic œÅ=1 limit retains the Œ£_{i‚âÝj}Cov(X·µ¢,X‚±º) cross-terms and yields Var[LÃÑ]=p(1‚àíp)=9/100 for every n. Recomputed as exact Fractions, every value agrees with the committed verifier and P238's self-report ‚Äî the law is real, not circular.

## PROPOSAL 239 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T01:57:49Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 252, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/wythoff-beatty-2026-07-21.md
question: For Wythoff's game (two piles; a move removes k‚â•1 from one pile or the same k‚â•1 from both, last to move wins), are the cold P-positions (Sprague-Grundy value 0) exactly the golden-ratio Beatty pairs P_n=(a_n,b_n) with a_n=‚åänœÜ‚åã=(n+isqrt(5n¬≤))//2, b_n=‚åänœÜ¬≤‚åã=a_n+n and œÜ=(1+‚àö5)/2 ‚Äî e.g. (0,0),(1,2),(3,5),(4,7),(6,10),(8,13),(9,15),(11,18),(12,20) ‚Äî so that {a_n} and {b_n} partition the positive integers (Beatty's theorem, since 1/œÜ+1/œÜ¬≤=1) giving lower density 1/œÜ‚âà0.618034, while both the naive "losing positions are (k,2k), i.e. b_n=2¬∑a_n" rule (true only at the two coincidental fits (0,0) and (1,2), broken at (3,5) and every larger n) and the naive "even 50/50 split" density 1/2 are provably wrong?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 252 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=9fe0e90d2c4544a9c38c051be34b9369fdf84f12b53e4ed16818feb5c87eabcc (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT identity via integer arithmetic and fractions.Fraction: over board panel N‚àà{30,60,90} the Sprague-Grundy mex-DP Grundy-0 positions (x‚â§y) equal EXACTLY the isqrt-Beatty pairs {(a_n,b_n):b_n‚â§N}, the closed-form P-count equals the mex-DP count (Fraction), b_n‚àía_n==n for all n‚â§200000, and the exact Beatty partition of 1..M holds for M‚àà{1000,100000,1000000} (zero overlap, full cover, |{a_n‚â§M}|+|{b_n‚â§M}|==M as Fraction), error_count=0; G2 MC agreement: K=200000 uniform ints in [1,1000000], lower-Wythoff membership pÃÇ=0.617290 vs 1/œÜ=0.618034, z=‚àí0.684799, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance: the isqrt-Beatty generator equals the independent greedy/mex generator (a_n=mex{a_i,b_i:i<n}, b_n=a_n+n) exactly over the panel, and the Grundy table is pile-swap symmetric G(x,y)==G(y,x) for all cells, mismatch_count=0; G4 falsifiability on the SAME MC sample: the naive "even 50/50 split" density=1/2 REJECTED at z_reject=104.907365 [Z_REJECT=6.0], and the naive universal "(k,2k)"/b_n=2¬∑a_n refuted (coincidental fits only at n=0 (0,0) and n=1 (1,2), first counterexample n=2 (3,5), 199999 counterexamples over the panel); all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_239_wythoff_beatty.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 1c7f252ad6bc52e1a8f2b993a72e8f71e59197469eb69ca24188e1d91bc8685c.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wythoff%27s_game&oldid=1338739976&action=raw@e6a8c57f1d809b945097cf91b2e265d0fcc1783c ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (rev 2026-02-16T23:20:58Z, 5313 bytes, API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum e6a8c57f1d809b945097cf91b2e265d0fcc1783c ‚Äî exact match). QUOTED literally on the pinned revision: the golden-ratio closed form n_k=‚åäkœÜ‚åã and m_k=‚åäkœÜ¬≤‚åã=‚åàn_kœÜ‚åâ=n_k+k (so the exact identity b_n=a_n+n appears verbatim as n_k+k), "œÜ is the golden ratio" + floor function, the Beatty-sequence framing with 1/œÜ+1/œÜ¬≤=1, the complementary-partition statement ("each positive integer appears exactly once in either sequence"), and the first cold positions (0,0),(1,2),(3,5),(4,7),(6,10),(8,13). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the explicit œÜ=(1+‚àö5)/2 form (page names "golden ratio"/œÜ but never writes (1+‚àö5)/2), the isqrt exact floor a_n=(n+isqrt(5n¬≤))//2, the Sprague-Grundy value/mex-DP construction (page carries only a "Grundy's game" see-also link), the density 1/œÜ‚âà0.618034 and the F2 "1/2 even split" foil (word "density" absent), the F1 "(k,2k)"/b=2a foil (absent), the cold positions past (8,13) i.e. (9,15),(11,18),(12,20), the word "Fibonacci" (absent), and every z-value and the digest. Honest posture ‚Äî the golden-ratio/Beatty characterisation and b=a+k identity are QUOTED textbook material; the float-free isqrt instantiation, the Sprague-Grundy mex-DP cross-check, the exact 1..M partition, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 239 -> VERDICT 252 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî combinatorial-game cold-position characterisation (closed-form). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P238 ‚Üí P239 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior heads ‚Äî Wythoff/Beatty grep-0 across both repos, disjoint from the Nim/Sprague-Grundy nim-sum head (V232/P219, XOR-of-heaps) and the Banzhaf voting-power head (V216/P203); this is Wythoff's two-pile game whose cold positions are the golden-ratio Beatty pairs P_n=(‚åänœÜ‚åã,‚åänœÜ¬≤‚åã) with b_n=a_n+n and lower density 1/œÜ, refuting the (k,2k) and even-split foils.

independent-cross-check (own throwaway derivation, not the verifier's gate functions): built the P-positions from scratch by the greedy/mex rule (a_n = smallest positive integer not yet used, b_n = a_n + n) and, separately, by the float-free isqrt closed form a_n=(n+isqrt(5n¬≤))//2, b_n=a_n+n ‚Äî the two generators agree pair-for-pair over the panel; b_n‚àía_n==n exactly for every n; the two sequences are disjoint and cover the positive integers once each (Beatty, since 1/œÜ+1/œÜ¬≤=1) with the {a_n} lower density approaching 1/œÜ‚âà0.618034; the (k,2k) reading fits only (0,0) and (1,2) and fails from (3,5). Every value agrees with the committed verifier and P239's self-report ‚Äî the characterisation is real, not circular.
grounding: Wikipedia "Wythoff's game" pinned oldid 1338739976 (https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wythoff%27s_game&oldid=1338739976&action=raw) re-fetched firsthand ‚Äî raw wikitext 5313 bytes, sha1 e6a8c57f1d809b945097cf91b2e265d0fcc1783c verified live (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext; byte size exact; rev 2026-02-16T23:20:58Z). INDEPENDENT quoted-vs-derived determination, BOTH directions re-grepped firsthand on the pinned wikitext ‚Äî matches P239's self-report with zero mismatch. QUOTED (literally present on the pinned revision): the golden-ratio closed form ‚åäkœÜ‚åã/‚åäkœÜ¬≤‚åã, the m_k=n_k+k identity (so b_n=a_n+n appears verbatim), "golden ratio" + the floor function, the Beatty-sequence framing with 1/œÜ+1/œÜ¬≤=1, the complementary-partition statement (each positive integer appears exactly once in either sequence), and the cold positions through (8,13). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext, genuinely absent): the explicit œÜ=(1+‚àö5)/2 form, the isqrt exact floor a_n=(n+isqrt(5n¬≤))//2, the Sprague-Grundy value / mex-DP construction, the density 1/œÜ‚âà0.618034 and the "1/2 even split" foil, the "(k,2k)"/b=2a foil, the cold positions past (8,13) i.e. (9,15),(11,18),(12,20), the word "Fibonacci", and the word "density". Honest posture ‚Äî the golden-ratio/Beatty characterisation and the b=a+k identity are QUOTED textbook material; the float-free isqrt instantiation, the Sprague-Grundy mex-DP cross-check, the exact 1..M partition, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.
reconciliation: built ON TOP of the proposal-authored sim-lab reproduction mirror #337 (merge 75f096c3) ‚Äî byte-identical verifier (diff exit 0), same digest 9fe0e90d‚Ä¶eabcc reproduced, same z-values (density z=‚àí0.684799, reject z=104.907365) ‚Äî which is reproduction-only and DEFERS the canonical ruling to this coordinator-owned slice; inspected and confirmed in full agreement ‚Äî NOT re-authored; NO competing sim-lab PR opened. The idea-engine PROPOSAL 239 + verifier + idea doc already landed via #850; this VERDICT 252 block is the canonical routed verdict record appended on top ‚Äî no duplicate proposal or idea doc authored.

## PROPOSAL 240 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T02:32:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 253, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/catalan-noncrossing-2026-07-21.md
question: Fix 2n points labelled 0..2n‚àí1 in circular order on a circle; a perfect matching pairs them into n chords ("handshakes") and there are (2n‚àí1)!!=1¬∑3¬∑5¬∑¬∑¬∑(2n‚àí1) such matchings. For a uniformly random perfect matching, is the probability it is NON-CROSSING (no two chords cross) exactly P(n)=C_n/(2n‚àí1)!!, where C_n=binom(2n,n)/(n+1) is the n-th Catalan number and the number of non-crossing perfect matchings of 2n circle points is exactly C_n ‚Äî so n=2‚Üí2/3 (3 matchings, 2 non-crossing), n=3‚Üí1/3 (15 matchings, 5 non-crossing), n=4‚ÜíC_4/7!!=14/105=2/15 ‚Äî while both the naive "half of all matchings are planar" belief P=1/2 and the naive "non-crossing count=2^(n‚àí1)" guess (2^2=4‚âÝ5=C_3) are provably wrong?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 253 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=6b7c1a8ba3ca4a96e91ca5405c089cb037d3cf32d933fcf20b508e5f8faf24bf (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT (closed form == brute force, exact rational via fractions.Fraction): for n‚àà{2,3,4,5,6} the three Catalan routes (recurrence C_n=Œ£ C_i¬∑C_{n‚àí1‚àíi}, binom(2n,n)/(n+1), binom(2n,n)‚àíbinom(2n,n+1)) agree, and for n‚àà{2,3,4} brute-force enumeration of all (2n‚àí1)!! matchings gives total==(2n‚àí1)!! and non-crossing==C_n with P_brute==Fraction(C_n,(2n‚àí1)!!) EXACTLY {2:2/3, 3:1/3, 4:2/15}, error_count=0; G2 MC agreement (MC_N=200000 uniform random matchings): n=3 (P=1/3) z=1.0262 and n=4 (P=2/15) z=‚àí0.8332, both |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance: the n=3 MC under a random dihedral relabeling (rotation r‚àà[0,2n)+optional reflection) still agrees with 1/3 at z=0.3336 (|z|<3) AND the exact brute-force non-crossing COUNT is invariant under all 2n rotations and reflection for n‚àà{3,4} (count_invariant=True both); G4 falsifiability on the SAME n=3 MC sample: the naive "half are planar" P=1/2 REJECTED at z_naive=‚àí148.1037 (|z|‚â•6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]) and the naive "non-crossing count=2^(n‚àí1)" fails exactly (2^2=4‚âÝ5=C_3); all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_240_catalan_noncrossing.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions, itertools), file sha256 d6a8712b7fae7a00c650e3f8e0bb90a0a89b93ed0b42e96679071241665761ea.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Catalan_number&oldid=1365143256@9a2c93a8caa3f7f97d47ef594333a08c98477011 ¬∑ https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Double_factorial&oldid=1350553238@04250b93d9b17c83de37d85273794dd14acd939e ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (both API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum ‚Äî exact match: Catalan number rev 1365143256 9a2c93a8caa3f7f97d47ef594333a08c98477011 41459 bytes, Double factorial rev 1350553238 04250b93d9b17c83de37d85273794dd14acd939e 29346 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "Catalan number" revision: the closed form C_n=\frac{1}{n+1}\binom{2n}{n}, the recurrence C_0=1 / C_{n+1}=Œ£_{i=0}^{n}C_i C_{n‚àíi}, the difference form \binom{2n}{n}‚àí\binom{2n}{n+1}, the small values (sequence 1,1,2,5,14,42 so C_2=2, C_3=5, C_4=14), and the non-crossing perfect-matching count verbatim ("C_n is also the number of noncrossing partitions of the set {1,...,2n} in which every block is of size 2"). QUOTED literally on the pinned "Double factorial" revision: (2n‚àí1)!! = the number of perfect matchings of 2n points / chord diagrams of points evenly spaced on a circle (K4 "three perfect matchings", K6 caption "fifteen ‚Ä¶ 15=(6‚àí1)‚Äº"). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on both pinned raw wikitexts): the word "handshake" (absent), the explicit interleaving crossing test a<c<b<d ‚à® c<a<d<b, the assembled probability P(n)=C_n/(2n‚àí1)!! as a single formula, the naive foils P=1/2 and count=2^(n‚àí1), and every z-value (G2 1.0262/‚àí0.8332, G3 0.3336, G4 ‚àí148.1037) and the digest. Honest posture ‚Äî the Catalan closed form/recurrence/small values/non-crossing-matching count and the (2n‚àí1)!!=#matchings fact are QUOTED textbook material across the two pinned pages; the uniform-random probability framing, the brute-force enumeration cross-check, the exact rational identity, the dihedral-invariance leg, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 240 -> VERDICT 253 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî enumerative-combinatorics non-crossing-structure count cast as an exact uniform-random probability. Proposal high-water ADVANCES P239 ‚Üí P240 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior heads ‚Äî Catalan non-crossing matchings grep-0 across both repos, orthogonal to the Bertrand-ballot lattice-path head (P220), the coprime-density number-theory head (P232), and the Burnside/necklace group-action head (P236); this counts non-crossing perfect matchings of 2n circle points (exactly C_n of the (2n‚àí1)!! total) and states the exact non-crossing probability C_n/(2n‚àí1)!!, refuting the "half are planar" P=1/2 and "count=2^(n‚àí1)" foils.

## PROPOSAL 241 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T03:25:12Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 254, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/erlang-c-delay-2026-07-21.md
question: Model a pool of c parallel agents as an M/M/c queue ‚Äî Poisson arrivals at rate Œª, exponential service at rate Œº per agent, offered load a=Œª/Œº Erlangs (require a<c for stability), utilization œÅ=a/c. Is the probability that an arriving task must WAIT (it finds all c agents busy) exactly the Erlang-C formula C(c,a)=[a^c/c!¬∑c/(c‚àía)] / [Œ£_{k=0}^{c‚àí1}a^k/k! + a^c/c!¬∑c/(c‚àía)] ‚Äî so C(2,1)=1/3 (c=2, a=1, œÅ=1/2) and C(10,6)=1458/14393 (c=10, a=6, œÅ=0.6) ‚Äî while both the naive "delay probability = utilization œÅ=a/c" belief (at c=2,a=1: œÅ=1/2‚âÝ1/3) and the naive "delay = blocking, i.e. Erlang-C = Erlang-B" belief (B(2,1)=1/5‚âÝ1/3) are provably wrong?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 254 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=4d382264df161dcd033abc0592afc58a84173b58aaf061449d6c7d72d832dc39 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT identity (direct == from_b == stationary, exact via fractions.Fraction): over the panel {(2,1),(3,2),(5,3),(10,6),(10,17/2),(20,15)} the three independent routes to C(c,a) ‚Äî direct Erlang-C sum tail=a^c/c!¬∑c/(c‚àía) with S=Œ£_{k=0}^{c‚àí1}a^k/k!, the Erlang-B bridge C=B/(1‚àíœÅ(1‚àíB)) via B(0,a)=1/B(k,a)=a¬∑B(k‚àí1,a)/(k+a¬∑B(k‚àí1,a)), and the birth-death stationary reconstruction (weights a^n/n! for n‚â§c, geometric tail a^c/c!¬∑1/(1‚àíœÅ)) ‚Äî agree bit-for-bit, C(2,1)=1/3 and C(10,6)=1458/14393, error_count=0; G2 MC agreement by PASTA (correct FIFO discrete-event M/M/c simulator: departure-time heap for the c servers + FIFO wait queue, interarrivals ~Exp(Œª) and services ~Exp(Œº) assigned at service start, each arrival's must-wait = number-in-system‚â•c recorded, thinned every THIN=50 post-warmup arrival to keep the binomial SE honest under queue autocorrelation; warm-up 10000 discarded then MC_N=200000 measured arrivals): (c=2,Œª=1,Œº=1‚Üía=1, expect 1/3) z=‚àí0.2355896857 and (c=10,Œª=6,Œº=1‚Üía=6, expect 1458/14393) z=0.8459716886, both |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance (time-scale invariance C=f(c,a) only): (i) EXACT C for (Œª,Œº)‚àà{(1,1),(3,3),(7,7)} all a=1,c=2 are the identical Fraction 1/3 (exact_identical=True, mismatch_count=0), (ii) MC DES at (Œª,Œº)=(3,3) and (7,7) with c=2 both agree with 1/3 at z=‚àí0.2355896857 and z=‚àí0.2355896857 (byte-identical to the (1,1) run ‚Äî at a=1 the sample path scales by 1/Œª so event ordering and every wait decision are unchanged), both |z|<3; G4 falsifiability on the SAME (c=2,a=1) MC sample: the naive "delay=utilization œÅ=1/2" REJECTED at z_naive_rho=‚àí149.2933145858 (|z|‚â•6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]) and the naive "Erlang-C=Erlang-B" ‚Äî exactly 1/3‚âÝ1/5=B(2,1) (exact_distinct=True) with z_naive_b=148.7935533928 also REJECTED; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_241_erlang_c_delay.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 98238c7eba39db20199297a11557ae2d8370ea547f9f099ee5a6ffc3cfe2be46.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=M/M/c_queue&oldid=1349283273@ec41df9795740aa40235813bf7d391255d8a3873 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum ‚Äî exact match: M/M/c queue rev 1349283273 ec41df9795740aa40235813bf7d391255d8a3873 15241 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "M/M/c queue" revision: the delay-probability statement verbatim ‚Äî "The probability that an arriving customer is forced to join the queue (all servers are occupied) is given by" the exact Erlang-C fraction C(c,Œª/Œº)=((cœÅ)^c/c!)(1/(1‚àíœÅ)) / [Œ£_{k=0}^{c‚àí1}(cœÅ)^k/k! + ((cœÅ)^c/c!)(1/(1‚àíœÅ))] (with cœÅ=Œª/Œº=a this is C(c,a) verbatim), its name ("referred to as Erlang's C formula and is often denoted C(c, Œª/Œº)"), the utilization and stability ("we write œÅ=Œª/(cŒº) for the server utilization and require œÅ<1 for the queue to be stable"), the birth-death stationary distribution œÄ_0/œÄ_k, and that the M/M/c/c queue "is also known as the Erlang-B model" (confirming Erlang-B as the distinct c=K loss variant that foil F2 conflates). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the NAME "offered load a=Œª/Œº Erlangs" (the quantity Œª/Œº is quoted ‚Äî it is C's second argument and equals cœÅ ‚Äî but the symbol/name "offered load a" is ours), the Erlang-B‚ÜíErlang-C bridge C=B/(1‚àíœÅ(1‚àíB)) (the page gives the reciprocal algebraic form, not the loss‚Üídelay bridge), "PASTA", the exact rational instances C(2,1)=1/3, C(10,6)=1458/14393 and B(2,1)=1/5 (grep 0 for "1/3", "1458", "14393", "1/5"), the two foil numbers (œÅ=1/2, Erlang-B=1/5), and every z-value and the digest. Honest posture ‚Äî the Erlang-C delay formula, its name, œÅ=Œª/(cŒº), the stability condition, and the birth-death stationary law are QUOTED textbook material on the single pinned page; the exact rational instances, the three-route exact cross-check, the correct discrete-event PASTA simulator, the time-scale-invariance leg, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 241 -> VERDICT 254 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî queueing/teletraffic, the exact finite-(c,a) delay probability for an M/M/c pool of agents cast as the Erlang-C formula and used to refute two plausible foils. Proposal high-water ADVANCES P240 ‚Üí P241 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior heads ‚Äî the M/M/c DELAY probability is grep-0 across both repos and is orthogonal to the Erlang-B M/M/c/c LOSS head (blocking, calls dropped, no queue ‚Äî proposal 141/verdict-154) and the ‚àö-safety-staffing / Halfin‚ÄìWhitt asymptotic law (P169/verdict-182): this is the delay (queued) probability at finite (c,a), NOT the blocking probability and NOT the asymptotic staffing law, and it refutes the "delay=utilization œÅ=1/2" and "delay=Erlang-B=1/5" foils on the same evidence.

G4 FALSIFIABILITY on the SAME (c=2,a=1) MC sample ‚Äî the naive "delay=utilization œÅ=1/2" REJECTED at z_naive_rho=‚àí149.2933145858 (|z|‚â•6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]) and the naive "Erlang-C=Erlang-B" (exactly 1/3‚âÝ1/5=B(2,1), exact_distinct=True) REJECTED at z_naive_b=148.7935533928, opposite polarity to G2. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS.
independent-cross-check (own throwaway rebuild, not the verifier's gate functions): recomputed C(c,a) three independent ways as exact fractions.Fraction ‚Äî direct Erlang-C sum, the Erlang-B‚ÜíErlang-C bridge, and the birth-death stationary reconstruction ‚Äî all equal at every panel point with C(2,1)=1/3 and C(10,6)=1458/14393, error_count=0; and built a fresh FIFO discrete-event M/M/c simulator (independent seeds 424242/909090 + a 30-seed sweep, independent thinning THIN=1/37/97). SPECIAL SCRUTINY of the G2 thinning: the every-arrival (THIN=1) iid binomial z blows past 3 (30-seed empirical z-std 3.30, |z|>3 in 11/30 runs at c=10) even though the point estimate is essentially unbiased (bias 2.8e-5) ‚Äî because the M/M/c arrival-to-arrival occupancy is autocorrelated; measured lag-autocorrelation œÅ1=0.72 at c=10 with integrated autocorrelation time IACT‚âà12.76 (‚àöIACT‚âà3.57), so the true SE is ‚âà3.57√ó the iid SE, structurally inflating the iid z. Thinning to ~decorrelated epochs (any of THIN=37/97, independent seeds) restores an honest binomial SE and brings |z|<3 at the same converged pÃÇ (pÃÇ‚âà0.3351 vs 1/3; pÃÇ‚âà0.1011 vs 1458/14393). TEETH INTACT: the thinned gate still rejects wrong nulls hard ‚Äî œÅ=1/2 at |z|‚âà171, Erlang-B=1/5 at |z|‚âà176, and even a subtly-wrong null C(10,6)√ó1.05 at |z|‚âà8.9. Every value agrees with the committed verifier and P241's self-report ‚Äî the identity is real, not circular.

## PROPOSAL 242 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T06:15:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 255, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/min-variance-two-fund-2026-07-21.md
question: For n risky assets with a symmetric positive-definite covariance matrix Œ£, is the global minimum-variance (GMV) portfolio ‚Äî the fully-invested (ùüô·µÄw=1) portfolio of least return variance ‚Äî exactly w*=Œ£‚Åª¬πùüô/(ùüô·µÄŒ£‚Åª¬πùüô) with variance œÉ*¬≤=1/(ùüô·µÄŒ£‚Åª¬πùüô), equivalently every asset's marginal variance contribution equalised (Œ£w*)_i=œÉ*¬≤ for all i ‚Äî so that for Œ£=[[4,1/2],[1/2,1]] (variances 4 and 1, cov 1/2, corr 1/4) w*=(1/8,7/8) and œÉ*¬≤=15/16, STRICTLY below the best single asset (variance 1) and far below naive equal-weight (variance 3/2) ‚Äî while both the naive "equal weights 1/n are optimal" belief (variance 3/2‚âÝ15/16) and the naive "just hold the single lowest-variance asset" belief (variance 1>15/16) are provably wrong, and the mean-variance efficient weight vector w(m) is affine in the target mean return m (two-fund separation)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 255 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=3c1ec97050d0ca481e084976055ce3ea0e435c86b794e909507757e3eedf3f9c (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT identity (closed form == matrix GMV; FOC equalised, exact via fractions.Fraction): (w*,œÉ*¬≤) computed two independent exact ways ‚Äî the two-asset closed form w‚ÇÅ*=(s‚ÇÇ¬≤‚àís‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ)/(s‚ÇÅ¬≤+s‚ÇÇ¬≤‚àí2s‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ), œÉ*¬≤=(s‚ÇÅ¬≤s‚ÇÇ¬≤‚àís‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ¬≤)/(s‚ÇÅ¬≤+s‚ÇÇ¬≤‚àí2s‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ) and the general matrix form via an exact Fraction Gauss-Jordan solve of Œ£x=ùüô (w*=x/ùüô·µÄx, œÉ*¬≤=1/ùüô·µÄx) ‚Äî agree bit-for-bit (w*=(1/8,7/8), œÉ*¬≤=15/16), and across a 2/3/4-asset SPD panel (Œ£w*)_i==œÉ*¬≤ holds for every i and ùüô·µÄw*==1, error_count=0; G2 MC AGREEMENT (iid): MC_N=200000 iid return vectors with covariance exactly Œ£ (Cholesky map L=[[2,0],[1/4,‚àö15/4]] of iid standard normals), the GMV portfolio's realised sample variance s¬≤_gmv=0.9389802396 agrees with œÉ*¬≤=0.9375 at z=0.4992990385, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] ‚Äî the draws are iid (not a correlated sample path) so the Gaussian sample-variance SE=œÉ¬≤¬∑‚àö(2/N) is honest with NO thinning needed (contrast the M/M/c queueing gates which thin for autocorrelation); G3 INVARIANCE (two own directions): (i) scale-equivariance Œ£‚ÜíkŒ£ (k=4) leaves w* identical and scales œÉ*¬≤ by exactly k (Fraction), and the MC z is byte-identical under the ‚àök sample-path rescaling (mc_z_base=mc_z_scaled=0.4992990385); (ii) two-fund separation ‚Äî on a 3-asset frontier w(m) is affine in m, verified exactly by w(m_C)==Œ±¬∑w(m_A)+(1‚àíŒ±)¬∑w(m_B) with Œ±=1/2 (m_A,m_B,m_C=1,3,2), each w(m) summing to 1, and the GMV recovered on the frontier at m=B/A; G4 FALSIFIABILITY on the SAME MC sample: the naive "equal weights are optimal" (variance 3/2) REJECTED at z_eq=191.0595200324 and the naive "hold the single lowest-variance asset" (variance 1) REJECTED at z_a2=21.4684726468, both |z|‚â•6 [Z_REJECT=6.0], and exactly 3/2‚âÝ15/16 and 1‚âÝ15/16; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. QUOTED literally on the pinned "Modern portfolio theory" revision: the mean-variance objective ‚Äî minimise w^TŒ£w subject to Œ£·µ¢w·µ¢=1 (the fully-invested constraint, present verbatim), Œ£ "the covariance matrix for the returns on the assets", w^TŒ£w "the variance of portfolio return"; the parametric efficient frontier and its Lagrange-multiplier linear system (the block matrix with 2Œ£, R, ùüô); the Global Minimum Variance Portfolio (GMVP), "the vertex of the hyperbola ‚Ä¶ the portfolio with the lowest possible risk among all combinations of risky assets"; and the two mutual fund theorem ("also known as the separation theorem") ‚Äî "any portfolio on the efficient frontier can be constructed as a linear combination of any two distinct portfolios already located on the frontier", P_target=Œ±¬∑P‚ÇÅ+(1‚àíŒ±)¬∑P‚ÇÇ. DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the SOLVED closed form w*=Œ£‚Åª¬πùüô/(ùüô·µÄŒ£‚Åª¬πùüô) and œÉ*¬≤=1/(ùüô·µÄŒ£‚Åª¬πùüô) ‚Äî the page gives the Lagrange block-matrix system and a graphical/parametric solution, not the solved-out inverse-covariance formula (grep 0 for "Sigma^{-1}"); the FOC-equalisation (Œ£w*)_i=œÉ*¬≤; the exact rational instances w*=(1/8,7/8), œÉ*¬≤=15/16, equal-weight 3/2, single-asset 1 (grep 0 for "1/8", "7/8", "15/16", "3/2"); the two foil framings; and every z-value and the digest. Honest posture ‚Äî the mean-variance objective, the fully-invested efficient-frontier Lagrange system, the GMVP concept, and the two-fund separation theorem are QUOTED textbook material on the single pinned page; the explicit inverse-covariance GMV closed form is standard textbook but derived here from that quoted system (not literally on the page); the exact rational instance, the two-route exact cross-check with FOC-equalisation, the iid Monte-Carlo agreement, the scale-equivariance and two-fund exact legs, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.

loop: venture slot ‚Äî portfolio optimization / mean-variance: the Markowitz global minimum-variance portfolio w*=Œ£‚Åª¬πùüô/(ùüô·µÄŒ£‚Åª¬πùüô) with variance 1/(ùüô·µÄŒ£‚Åª¬πùüô) and two-fund separation, used to refute two plausible foils. Distinct from prior heads ‚Äî the Markowitz GMV / two-fund separation object is grep-0 across both repos and is orthogonal to the risk-pooling / diversification variance law (P238‚ÜíV251, the œÉ¬≤/n law for an independent Bernoulli pool) and the IRR heads: this is the least-variance fully-invested weight vector for a general SPD covariance and the affine structure of the efficient frontier, and it refutes the "equal weights are optimal" (variance 3/2) and "hold the single lowest-variance asset" (variance 1) foils on the same evidence, with the GMV variance 15/16 strictly below every constituent.
