## PROPOSAL 243 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T05:07:47Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 256, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/fibonacci-nim-zeckendorf-2026-07-21.md
question: In Fibonacci nim (Whinihan 1963) ‚Äî one pile of n tokens; the FIRST move removes any m with 1‚â§m‚â§n‚àí1 (not the whole pile); every SUBSEQUENT move removes 1‚â§m‚â§2¬∑(the number the opponent just removed), capped at the tokens left; last token wins (normal play) ‚Äî is it exactly true that the player to move LOSES (the position is a P-position) if and only if n is a Fibonacci number (1,2,3,5,8,13,21,‚Ä¶), so the first player has a winning strategy iff n is NOT a Fibonacci number; and is the optimal winning move from any non-Fibonacci n to remove the SMALLEST Fibonacci summand of n's Zeckendorf representation (the unique representation as a sum of non-consecutive Fibonacci numbers), which is always legal (‚â§ n‚àí1) and always leaves the opponent a P-position ‚Äî while the naive "P-positions are the multiples of 3" belief and the naive "P-positions are the powers of two" belief are both provably wrong?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 256 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=344cdaa21a550c745184aaab00951db1063f522d3fe25544738299fdf8ee7dce (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT identity (integer arithmetic): a from-scratch bottom-up game oracle win(n,cap) over n‚àà[1,800] ‚Äî where a move takes m‚àà1..min(cap,n), m=n wins immediately, else the opponent faces (n‚àím,min(n‚àím,2m)), the opening state is (n,n‚àí1) and n=1‚Üí(1,0) loses ‚Äî gives loses(n)==(n‚ààFib) for every n with mismatches=0 (Fib built independently by integer recurrence); AND the constructive Zeckendorf strategy, for every winning n the smallest Zeckendorf summand s satisfies s‚â§n‚àí1 and win(n‚àís,min(2s,n‚àís))==False, zeckendorf_strategy_violations=0; G2 MC AGREEMENT: exact P-density p0=|Fib‚à©[1,800]|/800=14/800=7/400=0.0175 (kept as exact Fraction and float), 200000 uniform ints in [1,800] classified via the DP table give p_hat=0.01785, z=(p_hat‚àíp0)/‚àö(p0(1‚àíp0)/n)=1.19370699368, |z|<3 [Z_GATE=3.0]; G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (two own directions): (a) recomputing the oracle at a second independent horizon N2=1200 reproduces the P-position set on the overlap [1,800] byte-identical to the N-run and equal to the Fibonacci prefix, invariance_discrepancies=0; (b) randomized-play robustness ‚Äî from every winning start n‚àà[1,800], R=40 complete games where our player uses the DP to pick a winning move and the opponent plays a uniformly-random legal move, games_played=31440, optimal_player_losses=0; G4 FALSIFIABILITY: the naive "P-positions are the multiples of 3" foil disagrees with the true DP outcome on 274/800 positions, |z|=20.413966739‚â´3 REJECTED, and the secondary "P-positions are the powers of two" foil disagrees on 18/800, |z|=4.29119123911>3 REJECTED; all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/superbot-games/verify_243_fibonacci_nim.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 d290db491e1f85827641c112bc98b33fc247f675d729fad094da37abb16c3350.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Fibonacci_nim&oldid=1341159330@fcebea2e6f696f94d6d9efda5edc537bf38f18d3 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21T05:04:43Z (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match: Fibonacci nim rev 1341159330, 10087 bytes). Zeckendorf uniqueness grounded on "Zeckendorf's theorem" oldid 1344127047@0cc894e5c3131f80c51a0db6f6fb042b76b73654 (sha1 verified the same two ways). QUOTED literally on the pinned revisions: [1] "the first player can win if and only if the starting number of coins is not a Fibonacci number" (Fibonacci nim); [2] "on each move taking at most twice as many coins as the previous move, and winning by taking the last coin" (Fibonacci nim); [3] "it is always a winning move to remove all the coins (if this is allowed) or otherwise to remove a number of coins equal to the smallest Fibonacci number in the Zeckendorf representation" (Fibonacci nim); [4] "every positive integer can be represented uniquely as the sum of one or more distinct Fibonacci numbers in such a way that the sum does not include any two consecutive Fibonacci numbers" (Zeckendorf's theorem). DERIVED firsthand: the from-scratch game oracle win(n,cap) over [1,800] and [1,1200], the exact P-density Fraction 7/400, the Monte-Carlo z, the randomized-play robustness over 31440 games, the two falsification z-values, and the results-dict digest ‚Äî the pinned pages carry no z-values, no density fraction, and no digest. Honest posture ‚Äî the theorem, rule, and Zeckendorf strategy are QUOTED prose on the pinned pages; the computational proof (oracle, density, z's, digest) is the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 243 -> VERDICT 256 (+13 offset).
loop: superbot-games slot ‚Äî combinatorial game theory / take-away with a doubling reply cap: Fibonacci nim (Whinihan) P-positions are exactly the Fibonacci numbers and the optimal move is the smallest Zeckendorf summand, used to refute the "multiples of 3" (|z|‚âà20) and "powers of two" (|z|‚âà4.3) losing-set foils. Proposal high-water ADVANCES P242 ‚Üí P243 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior heads ‚Äî Fibonacci nim / Zeckendorf-strategy is grep-0 across both repos and orthogonal to the Nim/Sprague-Grundy nim-sum head (P219‚ÜíV232, XOR-of-heaps) and the Wythoff/Beatty golden-ratio cold-position head (P239‚ÜíV252, ‚åänœÜ‚åã Beatty pairs): this is the single-pile doubling-cap take-away game whose losing positions are the Fibonacci numbers, the exact "Zeckendorf/Fibonacci-base" atom flagged as untaken in the Wythoff session-idea list.

grounding: Wikipedia "Fibonacci nim" pinned oldid 1341159330 (https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Fibonacci_nim&oldid=1341159330&action=raw) sha1 fcebea2e6f696f94d6d9efda5edc537bf38f18d3, and "Zeckendorf's theorem" pinned oldid 1344127047 sha1 0cc894e5c3131f80c51a0db6f6fb042b76b73654 ‚Äî both sha1 recomputed firsthand from freshly fetched raw wikitext, exact match (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum). INDEPENDENT quoted-vs-derived determination, BOTH directions re-grepped firsthand ‚Äî matches P243's self-report with no seam. QUOTED (literally present on the pinned revisions): [1] the first player can win if and only if the starting number of coins is not a Fibonacci number (losing‚ÜîFibonacci iff); [2] on each move taking at most twice as many coins as the previous move, and winning by taking the last coin (‚â§twice-previous rule + last-coin-wins); [3] it is always a winning move to remove a number of coins equal to the smallest Fibonacci number in the Zeckendorf representation (smallest-Zeckendorf-summand strategy); [4] every positive integer can be represented uniquely as the sum of one or more distinct Fibonacci numbers such that the sum does not include any two consecutive Fibonacci numbers (Zeckendorf uniqueness). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext, genuinely absent): the from-scratch game oracle over [1,800] and [1,1200], the exact P-density Fraction 7/400, the Monte-Carlo z, the 31440-game randomized-play robustness, the two falsification z-values, and the results-dict digest ‚Äî the pinned pages carry no z-values, no density fraction, and no digest. Honest posture ‚Äî the theorem, the rule, and the Zeckendorf strategy are QUOTED prose on the pinned pages; the computational proof (oracle, density, z's, robustness, digest) is the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel. ## PROPOSAL 245 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T06:19:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 258, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/open-addressing-uniform-hashing-probes-2026-07-21.md
question: Model an open-addressing hash table under the UNIFORM-HASHING model ‚Äî each key's probe sequence is a uniformly random permutation of all m slots. With m slots and n occupied (load factor Œ±=n/m), is the EXPECTED number of probes in an UNSUCCESSFUL search exactly E_unsucc(m,n)=(m+1)/(m‚àín+1) ‚Äî from the exact combinatorial definition Œ£_{i=0}^{n} C(n,i)/C(m,i) (probability the first i probes are all occupied), proved equal via the hockey-stick identity C(n,i)/C(m,i)=C(m‚àíi,n‚àíi)/C(m,n) ‚áí Œ£=C(m+1,n)/C(m,n)=(m+1)/(m‚àín+1) ‚Äî so (1,0)‚Üí1, (2,1)‚Üí3/2, (3,2)‚Üí2, (10,7)‚Üí11/4=2.75 and (100,70)‚Üí101/31‚âà3.258; and as m,n‚Üí‚àû at fixed Œ± does it increase monotonically to the classic 1/(1‚àíŒ±) ‚Äî while the naive belief that "expected probes = 1/(1‚àíŒ±) exactly at finite (m,n)" is provably wrong (at m=100,n=70 the truth is 101/31‚âà3.258, not 10/3‚âà3.333)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 258 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=98d7398935db83b93f4e5b71ef4393abf487d3bc4371018d0d9d16e4f75e1746 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT identity (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): over the battery {(1,0),(2,1),(3,2),(5,3),(10,7),(13,0),(16,8),(23,17),(50,35),(100,70),(128,96),(257,200)} the finite sum Œ£_{i=0}^{n} C(n,i)/C(m,i), the closed form (m+1)/(m‚àín+1), and the hockey-stick ratio C(m+1,n)/C(m,n) are EXACTLY equal, anchors (1,0)‚Üí1/(2,1)‚Üí3/2/(3,2)‚Üí2/(10,7)‚Üí11/4 pinned, mismatches=0, anchor_mismatches=0, max discrepancy exactly 0 (z not applicable, reported null/"exact"); G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): headline (m=100,n=70), N=200000 i.i.d. uniform-hashing searches (uniformly random probe permutation per trial via the seeded RNG, n occupied, count probes to the first empty slot), mean_hat=3.2596 vs E_exact=101/31‚âà3.2581, z=0.2616711626, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] ‚Äî the trials are INDEPENDENT (one probe-count each) so NO thinning is needed (stated in the verifier docstring); G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (own direction): the expected probe count depends ONLY on (m,n) not on WHICH slots are occupied ‚Äî config A (occupied=first n slots) z=0.3374925104 and config B (occupied=fixed pseudo-random n-subset) z=‚àí1.1046610225 both agree with E_exact and with each other (two-sample invariance z=1.0187955206), all |z|<3; exact monotone-convergence sub-check at Œ±=7/10 over m‚àà{10,100,1000,10000,100000} ‚Üí 11/4,101/31,143/43,10001/3001,100001/30001 strictly increasing, all strictly below the limit 10/3, approaching it; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME headline sample): the naive "1/(1‚àíŒ±) exact at finite (m,n)"=10/3‚âà3.333 is REJECTED at z=12.5653466132 (‚â´3, Z_REJECT=3.0) while the same sample agrees with exact 101/31 at |z|=0.26 ‚Äî the teeth: 1/(1‚àíŒ±) is a limit, not a finite-table equality; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_245_open_addressing_probes.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 ac4642df4a3b77fe35c418ffba9d2038589d9463cb1c40e1f341e0d371c5c5ab.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Open_addressing&oldid=1353211902@164df5a783e58eb4bb744e87fe2e7a5c227199bb ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match: Open addressing rev 1353211902 sha1 164df5a783e58eb4bb744e87fe2e7a5c227199bb, 8806 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "Open addressing" revision (grep count > 0): the load factor Œ±=n/m (\alpha=n/m, grep 1); "load factor" (grep 7) as "the proportion of the slots in the array that are used"; "the number of probes in an unsuccessful search" U_n (grep 1); the open-addressing search mechanism verbatim ("searching through alternative locations in the array (the probe sequence) until either the target record is found, or an unused array slot is found, which indicates that there is no such key in the table"); and the subexpression 1/(1‚àíŒ±) (\frac{1}{1-\alpha}, grep 1) ‚Äî BUT embedded in the LINEAR-PROBING unsuccessful formula EU_n=¬Ω(1+1/(1‚àíŒ±))+Œò(1/m), a different probe model from uniform hashing. DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the exact finite identity (m+1)/(m‚àín+1) (grep 0 for m-n+1/m+1); the UNIFORM-HASHING model itself (uniformly random permutation probe sequence) and its standalone unsuccessful-search asymptotic 1/(1‚àíŒ±) ‚Äî the page's 1/(1‚àíŒ±) is the linear-probing sub-term ¬Ω(1+1/(1‚àíŒ±)), whereas the uniform-hashing standalone limit (CLRS Theorem 11.6) is not on this revision as such; the sum-of-products definition Œ£ C(n,i)/C(m,i); the hockey-stick proof; all exact rational instances (1, 3/2, 2, 11/4, 101/31); the Monte-Carlo agreement, the invariance leg, the falsification, and every z-value and the digest. Honest posture ‚Äî the load factor Œ±=n/m, the "number of probes in an unsuccessful search" object, and the subexpression 1/(1‚àíŒ±) are QUOTED on the pinned page (the last inside the linear-probing formula, a distinct model); the exact finite identity (m+1)/(m‚àín+1), the uniform-hashing model, all exact instances, the hockey-stick proof, the MC agreement, the invariance leg, and the falsification of "1/(1‚àíŒ±) exact at finite (m,n)" are the firsthand contribution ‚Äî a falsification that directly clarifies that the page's asymptotic is a limit, not a finite-table equality. Nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 245 -> VERDICT 258 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî hashing / load-balancing: the exact finite-(m,n) expected probe count of an UNSUCCESSFUL search in an open-addressing hash table under uniform hashing is (m+1)/(m‚àín+1), rising monotonically to the classic 1/(1‚àíŒ±) as m,n‚Üí‚àû at fixed Œ±, used to refute the "1/(1‚àíŒ±) exact at finite (m,n)" naive belief (at m=100,n=70 truth 101/31‚âà3.258 vs naive 10/3‚âà3.333, rejected at |z|=12.57). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P244 ‚Üí P245 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior heads ‚Äî open-addressing uniform-hashing unsuccessful-search probes is grep-0 across both repos and orthogonal to the named prior hashing/allocation heads: birthday-collision (P132, coincidence probability), balls-into-bins (P225, max-load), consistent-hashing-max-gap (gap distribution), and Maekawa grid-quorum (P209, quorum size) ‚Äî this is the exact expected probe count of an unsuccessful open-addressing search, not a collision/allocation/quorum object.

## VERDICT 258 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T06:46:02Z ¬∑ status: APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 245 (idea-engine, sim-ready) ‚Üí VERDICT 258 (+13 offset); idea-engine merge fb46010 (PR #864); reproduction mirror sim-lab sims/verdict-258-open-addressing-probes/ (PR #343, merge 4cd6599, proposal-authored, no competing open PR).
claim: under the UNIFORM-HASHING model (each key's probe sequence a uniformly random permutation of all m slots), the EXPECTED probes in an UNSUCCESSFUL search is EXACTLY E_unsucc(m,n)=(m+1)/(m‚àín+1) ‚Äî from Œ£_{i=0}^{n} C(n,i)/C(m,i) via the hockey-stick identity ‚áí C(m+1,n)/C(m,n)=(m+1)/(m‚àín+1) ‚Äî increasing monotonically to the classic 1/(1‚àíŒ±) as m,n‚Üí‚àû at fixed Œ±=n/m, while the naive "1/(1‚àíŒ±) exact at finite (m,n)" is provably wrong (m=100,n=70: truth 101/31‚âà3.258, not 10/3‚âà3.333).
ruling: APPROVE. Verifier ideas/fleet/verify_245_open_addressing_probes.py (sha256 ac4642df4a3b77fe35c418ffba9d2038589d9463cb1c40e1f341e0d371c5c5ab) copied byte-identical (diff exit 0) and re-run firsthand: results_sha256 98d7398935db83b93f4e5b71ef4393abf487d3bc4371018d0d9d16e4f75e1746 reproduces byte-for-byte across THREE routes (default run, --selfcheck in-process double-run guard, separate re-invocation). All four gates PASS each in its own direction.
G1 EXACT identity (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): mismatches=0, anchor_mismatches=0, max_discrepancy exactly 0 (z n/a ‚Äî exact). Reproduced INDEPENDENTLY via a distinct route ‚Äî the full distribution of the first-empty-slot position P(X=k)=[n!/(n‚àík+1)!]¬∑(m‚àín)¬∑(m‚àík)!/m! in exact Fraction, each distribution normalized to 1 ‚Äî agreeing with the closed form and the tail-sum over ALL 780 pairs 1‚â§n<m‚â§40 plus the full battery, mismatches=0. Hockey-stick Œ£ C(n,i)/C(m,i)=C(m+1,n)/C(m,n)=(m+1)/(m‚àín+1) confirmed.
G2 MC agreement: headline (m=100,n=70), N=200000 independent uniform-hashing searches, mean_hat=3.2596 vs E_exact=101/31‚âà3.2581, |z|=0.2617 (<3, Z_ACCEPT=3.0). Independent trials, no thinning.
G3 invariance: occupancy-set independence ‚Äî config A (first-n) z=0.3375, config B (pseudo-random n-subset) z=‚àí1.1047, two-sample invariance z=1.0188 (all |z|<3); exact monotone convergence at Œ±=7/10 over m‚àà{10,100,1000,10000,100000} ‚Üí 11/4,101/31,143/43,10001/3001,100001/30001 strictly increasing, all strictly below 10/3.
G4 falsifiability (same headline sample): the naive 1/(1‚àíŒ±)=10/3 is REJECTED at |z|=12.5653 (‚â´3, Z_REJECT=3.0) while the same sample agrees with exact 101/31 at |z|=0.26 ‚Äî the teeth hold: 1/(1‚àíŒ±) is a limit, not a finite-table equality.
grounding: pin Wikipedia "Open addressing" oldid 1353211902 @ raw-wikitext sha1 164df5a783e58eb4bb744e87fe2e7a5c227199bb (8806 bytes) ‚Äî fetched firsthand, sha1 matches exactly. INDEPENDENT re-grep of the raw wikitext confirms P245's quoted-vs-derived split is ACCURATE: the page's 1/(1‚àíŒ±) occurs ONLY as the linear-probing sub-term EU_n=¬Ω(1+1/(1‚àíŒ±))+Œò(1/m), a DISTINCT probe model, NOT as a standalone uniform-hashing unsuccessful-search result; (m+1)/(m‚àín+1) and the standalone uniform-hashing 1/(1‚àíŒ±) limit are genuinely grep-0; the QUOTED items are literally present ‚Äî Œ±=n/m (grep 1), "load factor" (grep 7), the U_n "number of probes in an unsuccessful search" object (grep 1), and the probe-sequence search mechanism verbatim. The standalone uniform-hashing limit (CLRS Theorem 11.6) and the exact finite identity are correctly labeled DERIVED, not quoted. No overclaim; the seam is honestly disclosed ‚Äî this matched P245's self-report exactly.
reconciliation: sim-lab mirror verifier is byte-identical (diff exit 0, same sha256), its committed run-stdout digest matches, repro-report.md defers the canonical ruling to this slice and advances no high-water ‚Äî zero discrepancy. Ruling built on top; no competing sim-lab PR (open PRs empty).

## PROPOSAL 246 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T06:54:53Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 259, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/gordon-growth-dividend-discount-2026-07-21.md
question: Model a growing perpetuity (the constant-growth dividend-discount / Gordon growth model) that pays first cashflow D1 at t=1 and grows at a constant rate g per period thereafter, discounted at rate r with r>g>‚àí1. Is its present value EXACTLY PV = D1/(r‚àíg) ‚Äî the closed sum of the infinite geometric series Œ£_{t‚â•1} D1(1+g)^{t‚àí1}/(1+r)^t = (D1/(1+r))¬∑1/(1‚àíœÅ) with œÅ=(1+g)/(1+r)<1, equivalently the fixed point of the recurrence PV = D1/(1+r) + PV¬∑(1+g)/(1+r) ‚áí PV¬∑(r‚àíg)=D1 ‚Äî so (D1=1,r=1/10,g=0)‚Üí10, (1,1/10,4/100)‚Üí50/3‚âà16.667, (6,1/10,2/100)‚Üí75, (3,1/5,1/20)‚Üí20 and (2,12/100,5/100)‚Üí200/7‚âà28.571; and is the naive level-perpetuity P=D1/r (which ignores growth) provably wrong for g‚âÝ0 ‚Äî at (D1=1,r=1/10,g=4/100) the truth is 50/3‚âà16.667, not 10, undervaluing the growing stream by the exact factor r/(r‚àíg)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 259 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=3cb527ff7b7873d2bacb56dcb4eea6f490ef7817b67766f45696ab6d706230e4 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT identity (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): over the 10-triple battery {(1,1/10,0),(1,1/10,4/100),(6,1/10,2/100),(3,1/5,1/20),(2,12/100,5/100),(10,8/100,3/100),(5,7/100,‚àí2/100),(100,15/100,6/100),(7/2,9/100,1/100),(1,1/4,1/10)} the closed form D1/(r‚àíg), the reconstruction partial_sum(T=60)+exact_tail(T=60), the fixed-point cf¬∑(r‚àíg)==D1, and the recurrence cf==D1/(1+r)+cf¬∑(1+g)/(1+r) are EXACTLY equal, anchors (1,1/10,0)‚Üí10/(1,1/10,4/100)‚Üí50/3/(6,1/10,2/100)‚Üí75/(3,1/5,1/20)‚Üí20/(2,12/100,5/100)‚Üí200/7 pinned, mismatches=0, anchor_mismatches=0, max discrepancy exactly 0 (z not applicable, reported null/"exact"); G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): headline (D1=1,r=1/10,g=4/100), N=100000 i.i.d. stochastic-dividend DDM paths (per-period mean-1 multiplicative shocks Œ¥=0.3 via the seeded RNG, T=160, one PV draw per path), mean_hat=16.6681667254 vs the exact finite-horizon target PV¬∑(1‚àíœÅ^160)=16.6645563219 (infinite-horizon E=50/3‚âà16.6666666667), z=0.0408270129, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] ‚Äî the paths are INDEPENDENT (one PV each) so NO thinning is needed (stated in the verifier docstring); G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (own direction): value invariant to the noise structure ‚Äî config A (Œ¥=0.3) zA=0.0408270129 and config B (Œ¥=0.5, SEED+1) meanB=16.662406346 zB=‚àí0.0034001459 both agree with the exact target and with each other (two-sample z=0.0090221259), all |z|<3; exact common-shift-invariance sub-check (Fraction): D1/(r‚àíg) is unchanged under (r,g)‚Üí(r+c,g+c) for c‚àà{1/100,3/100,1/2,‚àí1/50} across 3 triples ‚Üí shift_invariance_mismatches=0 over the 12-case battery (the value depends only on the spread r‚àíg, not the levels); G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME headline sample): the naive level-perpetuity D1/r=10 (ignoring growth) is REJECTED at z_naive=75.4046816262 (‚â´3, Z_REJECT=3.0) while the same sample agrees with exact 50/3 at |z|=0.04 ‚Äî the teeth: ignoring positive growth undervalues a growing stream by the exact factor r/(r‚àíg); all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_246_gordon_growth_ddm.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 afdd5047a87a048bf86f8a4b1c13e0307da2826c914e616bedfe3b7e8b89da9f.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Dividend_discount_model&oldid=1345461329@4e3d4d4d29f5cc6486b0fa92fe5cca52c547f45b ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match: Dividend discount model rev 1345461329 sha1 4e3d4d4d29f5cc6486b0fa92fe5cca52c547f45b, 9106 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "Dividend discount model" revision (grep count > 0): the exact closed form P=\frac{D_1}{r-g} (grep frac{D_1}=6; also P_0=\frac{D_0(1+g)}{r-g}=\frac{D_1}{r-g}); the name "Gordon growth model"/"Gordon" (grep 6); the infinite geometric series P_0=\sum_{t=1}^{\infty} D_0 (1+g)^t/(1+r)^t verbatim (a D_0-indexed writing of the D_1(1+g)^{t‚àí1} series ‚Äî equivalent since D_1=D_0(1+g)); the variable glossary "P is the current stock price ‚Ä¶ g is the constant growth rate in perpetuity ‚Ä¶ r is the constant cost of equity capital ‚Ä¶ D_1 is the value of dividends at the end of the first period"; "perpetuity" (grep 2); "growth rate" (grep 5); "cost of equity" (grep 5); the fixed-point rearrangement \frac{D_1}{r-g}=P_0 ‚áí \frac{D_1}{P_0}+g=r; r-g literal (grep 4) with "r-g cannot be negative"; and ‚Äî critically for G4 ‚Äî the zero-growth case P_0=\frac{D_1}{r} presented as the g=0 "dividend is capitalized" special case (grep 1). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): all exact rational instances 10, 50/3, 75, 20, 200/7 (grep 0 for 50/3, 200/7, 101/31); the finite-T residual identity partial_sum(T)+PV¬∑œÅ^T=PV and the finite-horizon MC target PV¬∑(1‚àíœÅ^T); the stochastic-dividend Monte-Carlo agreement; the mean-1-shock noise-structure invariance (configs A/B); the exact common-shift invariance (r,g)‚Üí(r+c,g+c); the falsification of the level-perpetuity D1/r foil for g‚âÝ0; and every z-value plus the digest. Honest posture / relabeling disclosed ‚Äî the page uses P/P_0 (price) for PV (present value, same object), r="cost of equity capital" for the discount rate (the "Yield-plus-growth" variant also writes it k, \frac{D_1}{k-g}, grep k-g=1), and indexes the series as D_0(1+g)^t where I index D_1(1+g)^{t‚àí1} (equivalent); "required rate of return" is grep 0 (the article says "required total return" and cites a paper titled "Required Rate of Profit") ‚Äî not claimed. The quoted formula sits specifically in the constant-growth DDM / Gordon growth model context. The naive D1/r that G4 rejects is not a page error: the page presents D1/r correctly as the g=0 case, and the firsthand contribution is the falsification that it undervalues a stream with g>0 (by the exact factor r/(r‚àíg)). Nothing oversold as novel ‚Äî the closed form and the series are textbook (Gordon & Shapiro 1956; Williams 1938) and cited as such; the exact instances, the finite-T identity, the stochastic-DDM MC agreement, the two invariance legs, the falsification, and every z-value + the digest are the firsthand contribution.
depends: PROPOSAL 246 -> VERDICT 259 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî venture / valuation: the present value of a growing perpetuity (constant-growth dividend-discount / Gordon growth model) with first cashflow D1 at t=1, constant growth g and discount rate r (r>g>‚àí1) is EXACTLY PV = D1/(r‚àíg), the closed sum of the geometric series Œ£_{t‚â•1} D1(1+g)^{t‚àí1}/(1+r)^t, used to refute the naive level-perpetuity D1/r that ignores growth (at D1=1,r=1/10,g=4/100 truth 50/3‚âà16.667 vs naive 10, rejected at |z|=75.40). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P245 ‚Üí P246 (union-max, no regress). Explicit distinctness: gordon-growth / dividend-discount is grep-0 across both repos and orthogonal to the named prior venture heads (Markowitz GMV P242, Kelly P100, put-call P234, newsvendor critical-fractile, St. Petersburg cap-collapse, Cournot/Stackelberg, Vickrey/revenue-equivalence) ‚Äî this is the exact closed-form valuation of a growing perpetuity, not a portfolio/auction/stopping object.

## VERDICT 259 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T07:36:19Z ¬∑ status: APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 246 (idea-engine, sim-ready) ‚Üí VERDICT 259 (+13 offset); idea-engine proposal+verifier landed sim-ready @ d0bf551 (PR #866); reproduction mirror sim-lab sims/verdict-259-gordon-growth-ddm/ (PR #344, head 3ea29d2, reproduction-only, defers the canonical ruling ‚Äî open/unmerged at ruling time, no competing PR opened).
claim: the present value of a growing perpetuity (constant-growth dividend-discount / Gordon growth model) paying first cashflow D1 at t=1 and growing at constant rate g per period thereafter, discounted at rate r with r>g>‚àí1, is EXACTLY PV = D1/(r‚àíg) ‚Äî the closed sum of the geometric series Œ£_{t‚â•1} D1(1+g)^{t‚àí1}/(1+r)^t = (D1/(1+r))¬∑1/(1‚àíœÅ) with œÅ=(1+g)/(1+r)<1, equivalently the fixed point PV¬∑(r‚àíg)=D1 ‚Äî so (D1=1,r=1/10,g=0)‚Üí10, (1,1/10,4/100)‚Üí50/3, (6,1/10,2/100)‚Üí75, (3,1/5,1/20)‚Üí20, (2,12/100,5/100)‚Üí200/7; while the naive level-perpetuity P=D1/r (which ignores growth) is provably wrong for g‚âÝ0 (at r=1/10,g=4/100 the truth is 50/3‚âà16.667, not 10, undervaluing by the exact factor r/(r‚àíg)).
ruling: APPROVE. Anchors (1,1/10,0)‚Üí10; (1,1/10,4/100)‚Üí50/3; (6,1/10,2/100)‚Üí75; (3,1/5,1/20)‚Üí20; (2,12/100,5/100)‚Üí200/7 pinned. INDEPENDENT quoted-vs-derived re-grep, BOTH directions, confirms P246's split is ACCURATE. question: In Green Hackenbush (every edge green, either player may cut an edge, after which all edges disconnected from the ground fall; normal play) the Sprague‚ÄìGrundy value of any rooted forest equals the colon-principle value sub(v,parent)=XOR over children c of (1+sub(c,v)), forest value = XOR over ground-neighbours r of (1+sub(r,0)); hence a bamboo forest of stalk-lengths (a‚ÇÅ,‚Ä¶,a‚Çñ) has value a‚ÇÅ‚äï‚Ä¶‚äïa‚Çñ and is a first-player win iff the nim-sum is nonzero ‚Äî Green Hackenbush IS Nim. ## PROPOSAL 249 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T09:16:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 262, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/pagerank-random-surfer-stationary-2026-07-21.md
question: Model the PageRank random-surfer process on the FIXED directed graph of N=4 nodes with out-links 0‚Üí{1,2}, 1‚Üí{2,3}, 2‚Üí{0}, 3‚Üí{} (node 3 DANGLING ‚Äî no out-links), damping d=1/2, uniform teleport 1/N. Build the row-stochastic Google matrix G ‚Äî non-dangling row i: G[i][j]=d¬∑(A[i][j]/outdeg(i))+(1‚àíd)/N; dangling row uniform G[i][j]=d¬∑(1/N)+(1‚àíd)/N=1/N ‚Äî and solve its stationary left-eigenvector. Is the EXACT rational stationary distribution œÄ=(52/179, 40/179, 50/179, 37/179) (the unique probability vector with œÄG=œÄ, Œ£œÄ=1) ‚âà (0.2905, 0.2235, 0.2793, 0.2067) ‚Äî with node 2 (in-degree 2) sitting BELOW node 0 (in-degree 1) because a link's weight is set by the linking node's own PageRank and by the damping/teleport structure (including the dangling node's uniformly redistributed mass) ‚Äî while the naive belief that "the stationary attention share is proportional to raw in-degree" = (1/5,1/5,2/5,1/5) is provably wrong (rejected on the random-surfer sample at max|z|‚âà646.5)?

assert œÄG=œÄ with max residual EXACTLY 0 on every entry and Œ£œÄ=1 exactly; independent adjugate/cofactor null-vector of (G·µÄ‚àíI) equals œÄ exactly, adjugate_mismatches=0; exact power iteration from uniform converges to œÄ within 1e-18 in 32 iters (corroboration ‚Äî Œª‚ÇÇ‚âÝ0 so an exact stochastic matrix never reaches œÄ in finitely many exact steps), z=null/"exact"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3, batch means): ONE random-surfer chain (with prob 1‚àíd teleport uniformly, else follow a uniform out-link, dangling‚áíteleport), burn-in 50000, then 200 batches √ó 20000 steps = 4,000,000 post-burn-in steps, per-node batch-means z against œÄ = node0 ‚àí1.7965/node1 ‚àí0.3012/node2 0.8511/node3 0.8993, max|z|=1.7965 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] ‚Äî random-walk occupancy is AUTOCORRELATED so the iid binomial SE is dishonest; batch means (batches ‚â´ mixing time) give an honest SE (stated in the verifier docstring); G3 EQUIVARIANCE/INVARIANCE (own direction): apply the fixed non-trivial node relabel œÉ=[2,0,3,1], rebuild G and re-solve œÄ on the permuted graph EXACTLY, assert it equals œÉ(œÄ_original) exactly ‚Äî œÄ_permuted=(40/179,37/179,52/179,50/179)=œÉ(œÄ), relabel_mismatches=0 (a verifier that hard-codes node indices breaks this); G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME MC sample): the naive "œÄ ‚àù in-degree" = (1/5,1/5,2/5,1/5) is REJECTED on the same batch-means sample at max|z|=646.5432 (node2 ‚àí646.54, node0 568.61, ‚â´ Z_REJECT=6.0) while that same sample AGREES with the true œÄ at max|z|=1.80 ‚Äî the teeth: raw in-degree ignores that a link's weight is itself set by the linking node's PageRank and the damping/teleport structure; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_249_pagerank_random_surfer.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 3e1e7647fb485f0739ebce9f4d3f288f09ea5c97b15115e7166cf91324f812c1.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=PageRank&oldid=1364775010@faa1d38442418ee08add5a47d8950ae58d0bbedf ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match: PageRank rev 1364775010 sha1 faa1d38442418ee08add5a47d8950ae58d0bbedf, 74463 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "PageRank" revision (grep count > 0): the exact PageRank formula PR(p_i)=\frac{1-d}{N}+d\sum_{p_j‚ààM(p_i)}\frac{PR(p_j)}{L(p_j)} (grep 1) with the teleport term \frac{1-d}{N} (grep 5), the damping factor d, and the out-degree normalization L(p_j) (grep 4); "random surfer" (grep 2, "a random surfer will land on that page by clicking on a link"); "damping factor" (grep 7) with the article's illustrative value 0.85 (grep 2); "Google matrix" (grep 2); "Markov chain" (grep 3) and the "probability distribution" over pages (grep 7). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the specific fixed 4-node graph 0‚Üí{1,2},1‚Üí{2,3},2‚Üí{0},3‚Üí{} and its adjacency; the chosen damping d=1/2 for THIS graph (the page's illustrative value is 0.85, not 1/2); the exact rational stationary vector œÄ=(52/179,40/179,50/179,37/179) (grep 0 for 179, 52/179); the matrix form œÄG=œÄ solved as an exact rational left-eigenvector plus the independent adjugate null-vector cross-check; the DANGLING-node uniform-row handling (the words "dangling" and "teleport" are grep 0 on this revision ‚Äî the uniform redistribution of a dead-end's mass is the standard Google-matrix construction, applied firsthand here, not literally on the page); the naive in-degree foil; the batch-means Monte-Carlo random-surfer chain; every z-value; SEED 20260717; and the results digest. Honest posture ‚Äî the random-surfer model, the damping factor, the exact PageRank recurrence (1‚àíd)/N+d¬∑Œ£ PR(p_j)/L(p_j), the Google matrix, and the Markov-chain / stationary-distribution framing are QUOTED textbook material on the pinned page; the specific fixed graph, the chosen d=1/2, the exact rational œÄ, the two exact solve routes, the dangling-uniform handling, the batch-means MC agreement, the equivariance leg, the in-degree falsification, and every z-value + the digest are the firsthand contribution. Nothing oversold as novel: PageRank and the Google matrix are textbook (Brin & Page 1998; Langville & Meyer), cited as such.

loop: fleet slot ‚Äî PageRank / random-surfer / Markov-stationary: the exact stationary distribution of the random-surfer Google matrix on the fixed 4-node directed graph 0‚Üí{1,2},1‚Üí{2,3},2‚Üí{0},3‚Üí{} (dangling) with damping d=1/2 and uniform teleport 1/N is the closed-form rational left-eigenvector œÄ=(52/179,40/179,50/179,37/179), used to refute the naive "stationary attention ‚àù raw in-degree" belief (node 2 has in-degree 2 but ranks below node 0 with in-degree 1; the in-degree share (1/5,1/5,2/5,1/5) is rejected at max|z|=646.5). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P248 ‚Üí P249 (union-max, no regress). Explicit distinctness: pagerank / random-surfer / Google-matrix stationary distribution is grep-0 across both repos and orthogonal to the named prior Markov/queueing stationary heads ‚Äî Parrondo's paradox (capital-parity chain stationary œÄ), Jackson product-form independence, M/M/c Erlang-C delay (P241, birth-death stationary law), and Palm / M/G/‚àû insensitivity ‚Äî this is the exact closed-form left-eigenvector of the Google matrix on a fixed directed graph with a dangling node, not a queueing / game-theoretic stationary object.

## VERDICT 262 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T09:40:46Z ¬∑ status: ruled APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 249 (idea-engine control/outbox.md, sim-ready 2026-07-21T09:16:00Z; VERDICT 262, +13 offset)
decision: APPROVE ‚Äî the exact rational stationary distribution of the random-surfer Google matrix on the fixed 4-node directed graph 0->{1,2}, 1->{2,3}, 2->{0}, 3->{} (node 3 dangling), damping d=1/2, uniform teleport 1/N is pi=(52/179, 40/179, 50/179, 37/179), the unique probability vector with piG=pi. Upheld by fully independent re-derivation, not a rubber-stamp of the verifier self-report.
adjudication:
- Verifier byte-identical across both repos (ideas/fleet/verify_249_pagerank_random_surfer.py, file sha256 3e1e7647fb485f0739ebce9f4d3f288f09ea5c97b15115e7166cf91324f812c1; diff exit 0 vs sim-lab sims/verdict-262-pagerank-random-surfer/).
- results_sha256 reproduced firsthand THREE ways (default run, in-process --selfcheck double-run, separate re-invocation), byte-grepped with no truncation: 18ba2ac7a7bfe36dc476214f79d55c26c3e212d8073d581ae226da55d4154355.
- G1 EXACT (own direction): independent Gaussian elimination over fractions.Fraction on (G^T - I) with the sum(pi)=1 constraint yields pi=(52/179,40/179,50/179,37/179); piG - pi residual EXACTLY 0 on every entry; an independent adjugate/cofactor null-vector of (G^T - I) equals pi exactly (two distinct exact routes agree). G rows [1/8,3/8,3/8,1/8], [1/8,1/8,3/8,3/8], [5/8,1/8,1/8,1/8], [1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4] (dangling row uniform).
- G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3, batch means): committed run max|z|=1.7965 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] PASS; an independent re-run at a fresh seed (100 batches x 30000 = 3,000,000 post-burn-in steps) accepts pi at max|z|=1.40. Batch-means over the iid binomial SE assessed INDEPENDENTLY as a SOUND autocorrelation correction, not gate-weakening: the sample-path occupancy of a Markov chain is genuinely autocorrelated so the iid SE is dishonestly small; batch means with batches >> mixing time restore an honest CLT-based SE while KEEPING teeth ‚Äî on the same independent sample a 2/179 perturbation (50/179,40/179,50/179,39/179) is rejected at max|z|=52 and the in-degree foil at max|z|=541.
- G3 EQUIVARIANCE (own direction): relabel sigma=[2,0,3,1] re-solved independently in Fraction gives pi_permuted=(40/179,37/179,52/179,50/179)=sigma(pi), mismatches=0.
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, same MC sample): the naive "pi proportional to raw in-degree" = (1/5,1/5,2/5,1/5) is rejected at max|z|=646.5432 [Z_REJECT=6.0] while the true pi agrees at max|z|=1.80 ‚Äî node 2 (in-degree 2) ranks below node 0 (in-degree 1) because a link's weight is set by the linking node's own PageRank and the damping/teleport structure.
grounding: Wikipedia "PageRank" oldid 1364775010, raw-wikitext sha1 faa1d38442418ee08add5a47d8950ae58d0bbedf (74463 bytes) ‚Äî self-verified firsthand (sha1sum and byte count both match). Quoted-vs-derived determined INDEPENDENTLY by grepping the raw wikitext both directions. QUOTED (grep count > 0): "random surfer" (2), "damping factor" (7), "Google matrix" (2), "Markov chain" (1), the recurrence pieces L(p (5) / PR(p (7) / {1-d} (5), and the article's illustrative damping 0.85 (5). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0): "dangling" (0) and "teleport" (0) genuinely absent, "179"/"52/179" (0), the fixed 4-node graph, the chosen d=1/2 (the lone "1/2" hit is inside a citation URL, not a damping value ‚Äî the page uses 0.85), the exact rational pi, the two exact solve routes, the dangling-uniform Google-matrix construction, the in-degree foil, batch-means MC, every z-value, SEED 20260717, and the digest. This matches P249's self-report exactly; nothing oversold as novel (PageRank and the Google matrix cited as textbook, Brin & Page 1998 / Langville & Meyer).

This VERDICT is built on top of that mirror; no competing sim-lab PR opened.

## PROPOSAL 250 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T09:52:52Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 263, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/vcg-clarke-pivot-2026-07-21.md
question: Model the Vickrey‚ÄìClarke‚ÄìGroves (Clarke-pivot) mechanism in its MULTI-UNIT SINGLE-DEMAND instance: k identical units auctioned to n bidders who each want AT MOST ONE unit, valuations v‚ÇÅ‚â•v‚ÇÇ‚â•‚Ä¶‚â•v‚Çô. Is it exactly true that (1) the efficient allocation gives the k units to the k highest-value bidders; (2) each winner i's Clarke-pivot payment = the externality it imposes on others = W‚Çã·µ¢(efficient allocation WITHOUT agent i) ‚àí W‚Çã·µ¢(efficient allocation WITH agent i) collapses, for EVERY winner, to the SAME uniform price = the (k+1)-th highest valuation (the highest LOSING bid); (3) truthful reporting is a dominant strategy (a winner's payment is independent of its own report as long as it stays a winner); and (4) expected revenue = k¬∑E[V‚Çç‚Çñ‚Çä‚ÇÅ‚Çé:‚Çô] ‚Äî with the headline instance n=5, k=2, v=(10,8,6,4,2) giving winners {10,8}, uniform price = 3rd-highest = 6, revenue = k¬∑price = 12, pivot for the 10-bidder = 14‚àí8=6 and for the 8-bidder = 16‚àí10=6, and at k=1 recovering single-item second-price (price = 2nd-highest = 8)? Pre-registered naive foil (the classic multi-unit confusion): "the uniform price is the lowest WINNING bid" (the k-th highest = 2nd-highest for k=2), i.e. E[revenue] = k¬∑E[V‚Çç‚Çñ‚Çé:‚Çô] = 5831/648 ‚âà 8.998457 ‚Äî REJECTED on the same random-profile sample of TRUE revenue at |z_foil| = 378.9138 ‚â´ Z_REJECT=6.0 (charge the highest LOSING bid, not the lowest WINNING bid).
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 263 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=7b0758bc85433ed89f640bbe47aba2a4dd63bf337ab9b3a0a19c6a4497319f2b (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): on the fixed instance plus a tie instance (10,8,8,4,2), k=1, and k=3, compute the efficient allocation and each winner's Clarke-pivot payment EXACTLY as the externality W‚Çã·µ¢(without i) ‚àí W‚Çã·µ¢(with i) and independently assert it equals the closed-form (k+1)-th highest valuation ‚Äî total_mismatches=0; headline winners {10,8}, uniform price 6, revenue 12, pivots 14‚àí8=6 / 16‚àí10=6; k=1 recovers second-price (price 8); k=3 price 4, revenue 12; z=null/"exact"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N=200000 iid profiles (n=5 iid Discrete-Uniform{1..6}, k=2), revenue = 2¬∑(3rd highest), exact target E[revenue]=k¬∑E[V‚Çç‚ÇÉ‚Çé:‚ÇÖ]=7 by FULL 6‚Åµ=7776-profile enumeration with Fraction, sample mean 700641/100000 ‚âà 7.006410, z=1.2193 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] ‚Äî iid profiles are NOT autocorrelated so the plain iid SE is honest (no batch means/thinning; stated in the verifier docstring); the whole-sample sum and sum-of-squares are accumulated in EXACT integer arithmetic so only the final sqrt/division touch float; G3 INVARIANCE (own direction, exact, 0 mismatches): (a) DSIC ‚Äî a winner's payment is INVARIANT to its own report across the whole winning range (vary the index-1 winner's bid over every integer in {7..30} keeping it a winner; the only distinct payment is 6 = the highest losing bid), (b) EQUIVARIANCE ‚Äî relabel œÉ=[2,0,3,1,4] maps the winner set {0,1}‚Üí{0,2} and permutes the payment vector, mismatches=0; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME MC sample): the "uniform price = lowest WINNING bid (k-th highest)" foil E[revenue]=k¬∑E[V‚Çç‚ÇÇ‚Çé:‚ÇÖ]=5831/648‚âà8.998457 is REJECTED at z_foil=‚àí378.9138, |z_foil|=378.9138 ‚â´ Z_REJECT=6.0, while the same sample AGREES with the true k¬∑E[V‚Çç‚ÇÉ‚Çé:‚ÇÖ] at |z|=1.2193; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand: ideas/fleet/verify_250_vcg_clarke_pivot.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 8319b3192f3dc823939084b02105c37cdcb43bde6cede81e7fffabdab59ebf4e.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Vickrey‚ÄìClarke‚ÄìGroves_auction&oldid=1311466659@1d816a80feb3bcc457f4cab2b999ddfd15f75681 ¬∑ https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Vickrey_auction&oldid=1338833083@398d2e4148cc7ee83f1343f720f6e243926bb895 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match on both: VCG rev 1311466659 sha1 1d816a80feb3bcc457f4cab2b999ddfd15f75681, 14374 bytes; Vickrey rev 1338833083 sha1 398d2e4148cc7ee83f1343f720f6e243926bb895, 12916 bytes). QUOTED (grep count > 0): the externality-payment definition ‚Äî VCG page "charges each individual the harm they cause to other bidders" (harm grep 4) plus the words "(sum of bids ‚Ä¶ excluding the participant under consideration) ‚àí (what other winning bidders have bid ‚Ä¶)" and the math V^{M}_{N‚àñ{b·µ¢}} ‚àí V^{M‚àñ{t‚±º}}_{N‚àñ{b·µ¢}} = "the social cost of their winning ‚Ä¶ incurred by the rest of the agents"; Vickrey page "each bidder pays the 'opportunity cost' that their presence introduces ‚Ä¶ the total bids of all the other bidders that would have won if the first bidder had not bid, minus the total bids of all the other actual winning bidders"; the uniform-price / highest-losing-bid identity + the unit-demand condition ‚Äî Vickrey page "all winning bidders pay the amount of the highest non-winning bid ‚Ä¶ known as a uniform price auction ‚Ä¶ does not ‚Ä¶ result in bidders bidding their true valuations ‚Ä¶ unless each bidder has demand for only a single unit"; dominant-strategy truthfulness ‚Äî VCG "making truthful reporting a weakly-dominant strategy" (dominant strategy grep 1, truthful grep 6, incentive compatib grep 2); the names Clarke (grep 6), Groves (grep 6), Vickrey (grep 8); the multi-unit generalization framing ("generalization of a Vickrey auction for multiple items"; "multiunit auction"; "second-price"). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on both pinned raw wikitexts): the instance n=5,k=2,v=(10,8,6,4,2) (grep 0 for 10,8,6,4,2); the exact integer pivot values, uniform price 6, revenue 12; the (k+1)-th-order-statistic characterization of the uniform price and revenue=k¬∑E[V‚Çç‚Çñ‚Çä‚ÇÅ‚Çé:‚Çô] (words "order statistic" and "(k+1)" grep 0 ‚Äî the pages say "highest non-winning bid" in words); the tie/k=1/k=3 companions; the DSIC own-report invariance sweep and the relabel equivariance leg; the Monte-Carlo profile sample; every z-value; the lowest-winning-bid foil; SEED 20260717; and the results digest. Honest posture ‚Äî DISCLOSED SEAMS: the word "pivot" is grep 0 on BOTH revisions and "externality" is grep 0 on the VCG page (the concept appears as "harm"/"marginal harm"/"social cost"/"opportunity cost"; "Clarke pivot" is my label for the standard construction); the VCG page's externality formula + worked "apples" example are stated for the GENERAL combinatorial case (items t‚±º, item-sets M, a multi-demand example), so the unit-demand collapse to ONE uniform (k+1)-th-highest price is quoted-supported on the Vickrey-auction page ("highest non-winning bid ‚Ä¶ truthful ‚Ä¶ unless each bidder has demand for only a single unit") and worked firsthand here into the exact rational instance + order-statistic revenue law. Nothing oversold as novel: VCG / Clarke-pivot and the uniform-price auction are textbook (Vickrey 1961; Clarke 1971; Groves 1973), cited as such; the exact instance, the two-route pivot=externality check, the DSIC + equivariance legs, the MC agreement, the lowest-winning-bid falsification, and every z-value + the digest are the firsthand contribution.

## PROPOSAL 251 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T00:00:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 264, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/bulgarian-solitaire-triangular-2026-07-21.md
question: Model Bulgarian solitaire as a deterministic dynamical system on integer partitions of n. The MOVE: given a partition (multiset of positive pile sizes), remove one card from EVERY pile, then form ONE new pile whose size = the number of piles you had (piles that hit zero vanish), i.e. step(p) = sort_desc([x‚àí1 for x in p if x‚àí1>0] + [len(p)]). Is it exactly true that (1) if n is triangular, n = T_k = k(k+1)/2, then from EVERY partition of T_k the iteration converges to the UNIQUE period-1 fixed point, the staircase Œ¥_k = (k, k‚àí1, ‚Ä¶, 2, 1); (2) the MAXIMUM number of steps to reach Œ¥_k over ALL partitions of T_k is EXACTLY k¬≤‚àík (Wikipedia states "k¬≤‚àík moves or fewer" ‚Äî an upper bound; the exact tightness / equality-attainment is the firsthand strengthening); and (3) if n is NOT triangular, no fixed point exists and every orbit lands in a period>1 limit cycle ‚Äî with headline k=6, n=T_6=21 giving all 792 partitions converging to Œ¥_6=(6,5,4,3,2,1), M(6)=30=6¬≤‚àí6, uniform-random mean Œº‚âà20.17 steps (œÉ‚âà8.47), and the smallest non-triangular witness n=2 cycling (1,1)‚Üî(2)? Pre-registered naive foil (the over-generalization): "non-triangular n also converge to a unique period-1 fixed point, just like triangular n" ‚Äî REFUTED: all 19 non-triangular n in [2,25] exhibit a period>1 cycle and 100% of a 40000-draw uniform-partition sample lands in one (f=1.0), rejecting the foil-null of f=0 at |z_foil|=40000.5 ‚â´ Z_REJECT=6.0.
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 264 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=f9fdd4c7a787d5b559248f8897ca00d82fa3e556535f67fd552a6854e0b19537 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT (exact integer arithmetic, zero tolerance): for k=1..8 (n=T_k up to 36) enumerate ALL partitions of T_k, verify each reaches Œ¥_k=(k,‚Ä¶,1) (period-1), count nonconvergent (=0), record M(k)=max steps-to-fixed-point and assert M(k)==k¬≤‚àík exactly ‚Äî per-k M(k)‚àà{0,2,6,12,20,30,42,56} all matching, total_nonconvergent=0, num_partitions {1,3,11,42,176,792,3718,17977}; z=null/"exact"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): at k=6 (n=21, 792 partitions) full enumeration gives exact population Œº=20.167929, œÉ=8.471034 of steps-to-fixed-point, then N_mc=40000 UNIFORM partitions of T_6 drawn with the Nijenhuis‚ÄìWilf RANPAR sampler (seeded), sample mean xÃÑ=20.167375, z=(xÃÑ‚àíŒº)/(œÉ/‚àöN)=‚àí0.0131 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0], convergence_rate=1.000000 (100% reach Œ¥_6 within k¬≤‚àík=30 steps) ‚Äî each random partition's step-count is an INDEPENDENT draw so the plain iid z is honest (no thinning / batch means; stated in the verifier docstring); G3 INVARIANCE (own direction, exact, 0 mismatches): (a) conservation ‚Äî over ALL enumerated orbits (k=1..8) sum(step(p))==sum(p) at EVERY step, 908472 steps checked, 0 violations (card total n is the invariant); (b) order-invariance ‚Äî for 1400 seeded RANPAR samples, bulgarian_step of a randomly SHUFFLED tuple equals bulgarian_step of the canonical descending tuple, 0 mismatches (the map is a function of the multiset, not pile order); G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, OPPOSITE polarity to G2): the "non-triangular n also has a unique period-1 fixed point" foil is REJECTED ‚Äî (i) exact witness: all 19 non-triangular n in [2,25] (excluding {1,3,6,10,15,21}) exhibit a period>1 cycle, smallest n=2 cycle (1,1)‚Üí(2); (ii) MC: N=40000 (random non-triangular n, RANPAR partition) pairs, fraction landing in a period>1 cycle f=1.000000 (foil predicts 0), z_foil=40000.5000 ‚â´ Z_REJECT=6.0; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand: ideas/fleet/verify_251_bulgarian_solitaire.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random), file sha256 a317b1f8a61826cb96cf62c6beffb2c0e8c1762b16840c1c87d9d2bb35fdf326.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bulgarian_solitaire&oldid=1340511625@f5566931386c46e2b0d37407d24ac25fa8dcd9a3 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match: oldid 1340511625 sha1 f5566931386c46e2b0d37407d24ac25fa8dcd9a3, 2385 bytes). QUOTED (grep count > 0): the move ‚Äî "for each pile, remove one card; collect the removed cards together to form a new pile (piles of zero size are ignored)"; the triangular-convergence-to-staircase ‚Äî "If N is a triangular number (that is, N=1+2+‚ãØ+k for some k), then it is known that Bulgarian solitaire will reach a stable configuration in which the sizes of the piles are 1,2,‚Ä¶,k" (triangular grep 3, "1,2,\ldots, k" grep 1, "stable configuration" grep 2); the k¬≤‚àík bound ‚Äî "This state is reached in k¬≤‚àík moves or fewer" (k^2-k grep 1); the non-triangular limit cycle ‚Äî "If N is not triangular, no stable configuration exists and a limit cycle is reached" (limit cycle grep 1); the name "Bulgarian solitaire" (grep 7); the Gardner attribution. DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0): the EXACT tightness of the bound (page says "or fewer" ‚Äî an upper bound; equality-attainment M(k)=k¬≤‚àík is enumerated here), the per-k M(k) values {0,2,6,12,20,30,42,56} and partition counts, the population Œº/œÉ of step-counts (Œº=20.17, œÉ=8.47 at k=6), the Nijenhuis‚ÄìWilf RANPAR uniform sampler + Monte-Carlo agreement, the full period>1 cycle census for non-triangular n, the n=2 witness cycle, every z-value, SEED 20260717, and the results digest; the word "partition" is itself grep 0 (the page says "piles"). Honest posture ‚Äî DISCLOSED SEAMS: the article is THIN (2385 bytes) ‚Äî one sentence each for the convergence result and the k¬≤‚àík UPPER bound, with no proof / no per-k step counts / no tightness claim, citing Akin‚ÄìDavis (1985) + Gardner (1983); so the load-bearing firsthand work is the exact enumeration proving M(k)=k¬≤‚àík with equality (k=1..8, 0 nonconvergent) plus the invariance and falsifiability legs; "staircase"/"fixed point"/"period-1" are my labels for the standard construction. Nothing oversold as novel: Bulgarian solitaire + the triangular fixed-point result are textbook (Gardner 1983; Akin‚ÄìDavis, Amer. Math. Monthly 92(4) 1985; the pinned page), cited as such; the exact M(k)=k¬≤‚àík tightness enumeration, the conservation + order-invariance legs, the uniform-sampler MC agreement, the non-triangular-cycle falsification, and every z-value + the digest are the firsthand contribution.
depends: PROPOSAL 251 -> VERDICT 264 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî combinatorial-dynamics / integer-partitions / Bulgarian solitaire: the move "remove one card from every pile, then form ONE new pile of size = number of piles" is a deterministic self-map on partitions of n; on a triangular total n=T_k EVERY partition converges to the unique period-1 fixed point, the staircase Œ¥_k=(k,‚Ä¶,1), within a MAX of EXACTLY k¬≤‚àík steps, while a non-triangular total never settles (every orbit is a period>1 limit cycle); used to refute the "non-triangular n also has a unique fixed point" over-generalization (rejected at |z|‚âà4¬∑10‚Å¥ on a 40000-draw uniform-partition sample). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P250 ‚Üí P251 (union-max, no regress). Explicit distinctness: bulgarian-solitaire is grep-0 as a head across both repos and is a DETERMINISTIC DYNAMICAL SYSTEM on integer partitions ‚Äî no opponent, no nim-sum/XOR, no mex, no game tree; the conserved invariant is the card total n ‚Äî orthogonal to every impartial-game Grundy head: Sprague-Grundy nim-sum P219, Wythoff P239, Green Hackenbush P247, Fibonacci-Nim P243, Penney, and the Banzhaf/Shapley power-index heads. This is a fixed-point / orbit-structure law of a partition self-map, not a game value.

## VERDICT 264 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T10:49:00Z ¬∑ status: ruled APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 251 (idea-engine control/outbox.md, sim-ready 2026-07-21T00:00:00Z; VERDICT 264, +13 offset)
decision: APPROVE ‚Äî Bulgarian solitaire is a deterministic self-map on integer partitions of n (step(p) = remove one card from EVERY nonempty pile, then form ONE new pile whose size = the number of piles). On a triangular total n = T_k = k(k+1)/2, EVERY partition of T_k converges to the UNIQUE period-1 fixed point, the staircase Œ¥_k = (k, k‚àí1, ‚Ä¶, 2, 1), and the MAXIMUM steps-to-fixed-point over all partitions of T_k is EXACTLY k¬≤‚àík (Wikipedia states only "k¬≤‚àík moves or fewer" ‚Äî an upper bound; the exact equality-attainment is the firsthand strengthening); a non-triangular total has no fixed point and every orbit lands in a period>1 limit cycle. Headline k=6, n=T_6=21: all 792 partitions converge to Œ¥_6=(6,5,4,3,2,1), M(6)=30=6¬≤‚àí6, uniform-random mean Œº‚âà20.17 steps (œÉ‚âà8.47); smallest non-triangular witness n=2 cycles (1,1)‚Üî(2). Upheld by fully independent re-derivation, not a rubber-stamp of the verifier self-report.
adjudication:
- Verifier byte-identical across both repos (ideas/fleet/verify_251_bulgarian_solitaire.py, file sha256 a317b1f8a61826cb96cf62c6beffb2c0e8c1762b16840c1c87d9d2bb35fdf326; diff exit 0 vs the sim-lab reproduction mirror sims/verdict-264-bulgarian-solitaire/, mirror digest identical).
- results_sha256 reproduced firsthand THREE independent ways (default run, in-process --selfcheck double-run guard, separate re-invocation) and byte-identical to the sim-lab mirror run-stdout: f9fdd4c7a787d5b559248f8897ca00d82fa3e556535f67fd552a6854e0b19537.
- G1 EXACT (own direction, exact integer arithmetic, zero tolerance): independent full enumeration of ALL partitions of T_k for k=1..8 ‚Äî every partition reaches Œ¥_k=(k,‚Ä¶,1) (period-1), total_nonconvergent=0, and M(k)=max steps-to-fixed-point matches k¬≤‚àík EXACTLY for every k: M(k)‚àà{0,2,6,12,20,30,42,56}; partition counts {1,3,11,42,176,792,3718,17977} == p(n); the unique fixed point IS the staircase Œ¥_k. z=null/"exact".
- G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): at k=6 (n=21, 792 partitions) full enumeration gives the exact population Œº=20.167929, œÉ=8.471034 of steps-to-fixed-point; an independent uniform-partition sampler (Nijenhuis‚ÄìWilf RANPAR, seeded) agrees at |z|=1.84 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] (the committed run is z=‚àí0.0131), convergence_rate=1.000000 (100% reach Œ¥_6 within k¬≤‚àík=30 steps) ‚Äî each random partition's step-count is an independent draw so the plain iid z is honest.
- G3 INVARIANCE (own direction, exact, 0 mismatches): (a) pip-conservation ‚Äî sum(step(p))==sum(p) at EVERY orbit state, 0 violations over 20448 orbit states (the card total n is the invariant); (b) order-invariance ‚Äî bulgarian_step of a randomly SHUFFLED tuple equals bulgarian_step of the canonical descending tuple, 0 mismatches over 3063 shuffled inputs (the map is a function of the multiset, not pile order).
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, OPPOSITE polarity to G2): the "non-triangular n also has a unique period-1 fixed point" foil is independently REFUTED via period>1 witness cycles ‚Äî e.g. n=2: (1,1)‚Üí(2)‚Üí(1,1); n=4: (3,1)‚Üí(2,2)‚Üí(2,1,1) ‚Äî every non-triangular n in the census lands in a period>1 cycle (the committed leg rejects the foil-null f=0 at |z_foil|=40000.5 [Z_REJECT=6.0] on a 40000-draw uniform-partition sample); all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS.

DERIVED firsthand (grep 0): the EQUALITY / tightness that the max is EXACTLY attained (the page states only "or fewer" ‚Äî the words maximum / exactly / tight are absent), the per-k M(k) values {0,2,6,12,20,30,42,56} and partition counts, the words "partition" / "staircase" / "fixed point" / "period-1", the population Œº/œÉ, all z-values, SEED 20260717, and the results digest. ## PROPOSAL 252 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T11:15:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 265, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/gaussian-integral-sqrt-pi-2026-07-21.md
question: Model the Gaussian (Euler‚ÄìPoisson) integral as a self-contained pure-math closed form outside the fleet/venture/game domains. Is it exactly true that (1) ‚à´_{‚àí‚àû}^{‚àû} e^{‚àíx¬≤} dx = ‚àöœÄ; (2) the even moments M_{2m} = ‚à´ x^{2m} e^{‚àíx¬≤} dx = ‚àöœÄ¬∑(2m‚àí1)!!/2^m, so the DIMENSIONLESS ratio R_m = M_{2m}/M_0 = (2m‚àí1)!!/2^m = (2m)!/(4^m¬∑m!) is EXACTLY RATIONAL (the irrational ‚àöœÄ cancels) and satisfies the integration-by-parts recurrence M_{2m} = ((2m‚àí1)/2)¬∑M_{2m‚àí2}; and (3) the full integral obeys the scale/translation law ‚àöa¬∑‚à´ e^{‚àía(x‚àíŒº)¬≤} dx = ‚àöœÄ for every scale a>0 and shift Œº ‚Äî with headline exact ratios R_1=1/2, R_2=3/4, R_3=15/8, R_6=10395/64 (recurrence route == closed form == (2m‚àí1)!!/2^m route, 0 mismatches over m=0..12), a uniform-importance Monte-Carlo estimate of ‚à´_{‚àí6}^{6} e^{‚àíx¬≤} dx = 1.776277 vs target ‚àöœÄ¬∑erf(6) = 1.772454 at z=0.7004, exact scale-invariance of R_m across a‚àà{1,2,3,5,1/2} and m=0..12 (65 checks, 0 mismatches), and the MC scale/translation law across 6 (a,Œº) configs at max |z|=1.5375? Pre-registered naive foil (the single most common confusion): "the Gaussian integral equals the standard-normal normalizer ‚à´ e^{‚àíx¬≤/2} dx = ‚àö(2œÄ) ‚âà 2.5066" (also Stirling's constant) ‚Äî REJECTED on the same N=400000 sample at z_foil=‚àí133.7896, |z_foil| ‚â´ Z_REJECT=50.0, while the same sample AGREES with ‚àöœÄ at z=0.7004.

grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gaussian_integral&oldid=1365252560@6d8b0d941ef6028c1331a63443a49c199f3c1c2d ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match: oldid 1365252560 sha1 6d8b0d941ef6028c1331a63443a49c199f3c1c2d, 22230 bytes). QUOTED (grep count > 0): the core closed form "\int_{-\infty}^\infty e^{-x^2}\,dx = \sqrt{\pi}" (grep \sqrt{\pi}=8, \int_{-\infty}^\infty e^{-x^2}=8); the names "Gaussian integral" / "Euler‚ÄìPoisson integral" (Euler=2, Poisson=2), "Carl Friedrich Gauss" (Gauss=20), de Moivre 1733 / Gauss 1809; the scale+translation form "\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-a(x+b)^2}\,dx = \sqrt{\frac{\pi}{a}}" (grounds G3); the even-moment closed form "\int_{-\infty}^\infty x^{2n} e^{-\alpha x^2}\,dx = \sqrt{\frac{\pi}{\alpha}}\frac{(2n-1)!!}{(2\alpha)^n}" (grep (2n-1)!!=3, double factorial=4) ‚Äî exactly the G1/G3 moment identity; the constant \sqrt{2\pi} appears (grep \sqrt{2\pi}=2, normal-distribution/Fresnel-adjacent). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0): the word "moment" (grep 0 ‚Äî my label; the integral itself is quoted); "integration by parts" as the recurrence route (grep 0 ‚Äî the page derives the even moments by differentiating under the integral ‚àÇ‚Åø/‚àÇŒ±‚Åø, an INDEPENDENT route to the SAME quoted closed form); the dimensionless scale-invariant ratio framing a^m¬∑R_m(a)=(2m‚àí1)!!/2^m; the exact Fraction zero-mismatch identity across the three routes; the uniform-importance Monte-Carlo estimator and every z-value; the "‚àöœÄ confused with ‚àö(2œÄ)" foil framing; SEED 20260717; and the results digest. Honest posture ‚Äî DISCLOSED SEAMS: the article is RICH (22230 bytes) ‚Äî the closed form, the scale/translation general form, and the even-moment closed form are all QUOTED verbatim with a full polar-coordinates proof, so nothing is oversold as a novel theorem; the firsthand contribution is the verification apparatus (the exact rational moment-ratio identity checked three independent ways in Fraction with 0 mismatches, the exact scale-invariance leg, the MC agreement, the ‚àö(2œÄ) falsification, and every z-value + the digest); my recurrence route is integration-by-parts while the page uses parameter differentiation (independent routes, same closed form); the word "moment" is grep 0 (my label); the ‚àö(2œÄ) foil confusion is my construction (the page states ‚àö(2œÄ) only in its own normal-distribution/imaginary-argument context and never equates it with ‚à´ e^{‚àíx¬≤} dx). Nothing oversold as novel: the Gaussian integral is textbook (de Moivre 1733; Gauss 1809; the pinned page), cited as such.

loop: fleet slot ‚Äî pure-mathematics / analysis / the Gaussian (Euler‚ÄìPoisson) integral: ‚à´_{‚àí‚àû}^{‚àû} e^{‚àíx¬≤} dx = ‚àöœÄ exactly, with the even moments M_{2m} = ‚àöœÄ¬∑(2m‚àí1)!!/2^m giving the DIMENSIONLESS ratio R_m = (2m‚àí1)!!/2^m = (2m)!/(4^m¬∑m!) that is EXACTLY RATIONAL (‚àöœÄ cancels) and scale-invariant, and the full integral obeying ‚àöa¬∑‚à´ e^{‚àía(x‚àíŒº)¬≤} dx = ‚àöœÄ; used to refute the naive "‚à´ e^{‚àíx¬≤} dx = ‚àö(2œÄ) (the standard-normal normalizer)" confusion (rejected at |z|=133.8 on the same N=400000 sample). This is the exact rational moment-ratio backbone of an irrational integral plus its scale-invariance and the ‚àöœÄ‚Üî‚àö(2œÄ) falsification, not a probability/combinatorial-count object.

decision: APPROVE ‚Äî The Gaussian (Euler‚ÄìPoisson) integral ‚à´_{‚àí‚àû}^{‚àû} e^{‚àíx¬≤} dx = ‚àöœÄ holds exactly; the even moments M_{2m} = ‚à´ x^{2m} e^{‚àíx¬≤} dx = ‚àöœÄ¬∑(2m‚àí1)!!/2^m give the DIMENSIONLESS ratio R_m = M_{2m}/M_0 = (2m‚àí1)!!/2^m = (2m)!/(4^m¬∑m!) that is EXACTLY RATIONAL (the irrational ‚àöœÄ cancels) and satisfies the integration-by-parts recurrence M_{2m} = ((2m‚àí1)/2)¬∑M_{2m‚àí2}; and the full integral obeys the scale/translation law ‚àöa¬∑‚à´ e^{‚àía(x‚àíŒº)¬≤} dx = ‚àöœÄ for every scale a>0 and shift Œº. Headline exact ratios R_1=1/2, R_2=3/4, R_3=15/8, R_6=10395/64 (recurrence route == closed form == (2m‚àí1)!!/2^m route, 0 mismatches over m=0..12), uniform-importance Monte-Carlo estimate ‚à´_{‚àí6}^{6} e^{‚àíx¬≤} dx = 1.776277 vs target ‚àöœÄ=1.772454 at z=0.7004, exact scale-invariance across a‚àà{1,2,3,5,1/2} and m=0..12 (65 checks, 0 mismatches), and the MC scale/translation law across 6 (a,Œº) configs at max |z|=1.5375. Upheld by fully independent re-derivation, not a rubber-stamp of the verifier self-report.

- G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): for m=0..12 the recurrence route R_m=‚àè(2j‚àí1)/2, the closed form (2m)!/(4^m¬∑m!), and the (2m‚àí1)!!/2^m route are all exactly equal and (2m‚àí1)!!¬∑2^m¬∑m! == (2m)!, checked=13, mismatches=0; the recurrence == (2m)!/(4^m¬∑m!) == (2m‚àí1)!!/2^m independently re-derived from scratch, 0 mismatches; z="exact".

## PROPOSAL 253 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T12:05:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 266, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/bft-committee-sortition-2026-07-21.md
question: Model committee-sortition safety over a fleet of agents under Byzantine faults. A fleet of N=64 agents contains B=21 Byzantine, and 64 = 3¬∑21+1, so the FULL system sits EXACTLY at the classical BFT safety bound n ‚â• 3f+1 with f=21 (it tolerates its 21 Byzantine agents). A committee of K=16 is drawn by uniform sortition WITHOUT replacement; the committee is UNSAFE iff it holds ‚â• T=floor(K/3)+1=6 Byzantine members (a K-node BFT quorum tolerates floor((K‚àí1)/3)=5). The Byzantine count in a sortition committee is Hypergeometric(N,B,K), so the committee-failure probability is the EXACT hypergeometric upper tail P = Œ£_{i=T}^{K} C(B,i)¬∑C(N‚àíB,K‚àíi)/C(N,K) with N=64, B=21, N‚àíB=43, K=16, T=6. Is it exactly true that (1) P = 296431911/685926212 ‚âà 0.432163, cross-checked two independent ways in fractions.Fraction ‚Äî the full pmf Œ£_{i=0}^{K} pmf_i == 1 by Vandermonde (residual 0) and P == 1 ‚àí Œ£_{i=0}^{T‚àí1} pmf_i (0 mismatch); (2) an iid Monte-Carlo of N=400000 independent sortition committees agrees, p_hat=0.430443 vs P=0.432163 at z=‚àí2.1966; and (3) the value depends only on the counts (N,B,K,T), not on WHICH agents are Byzantine ‚Äî a DIFFERENT Byzantine set byz2={(i¬∑3) mod 64 : i<21} gives p_hat2=0.432700 at z2=0.6856? The insight: a GLOBALLY-safe fleet (n‚â•3f+1) still produces UNSAFE sub-committees ~43% of the time, because the expected Byzantine per committee K¬∑B/N=5.25 sits just below the threshold 6 and sortition CONCENTRATES Byzantine nodes ‚Äî which is exactly why committee-BFT protocols must SIZE committees to bound the sortition failure probability. Pre-registered naive foil (the single most plausible wrong model): "the committee behaves like K iid draws WITH replacement at rate p=B/N, so P_binom = Œ£_{i=T}^{K} C(K,i) p^i (1‚àíp)^{K‚àíi}" ‚Äî this IGNORES the finite-population negative correlation of sampling without replacement so it OVERSTATES the tail (P_binom=0.435210 ‚âÝ 0.432163) and is REJECTED on the same without-replacement sample at z_foil=‚àí6.0822, |z_foil|>3, while the hypergeometric P is ACCEPTED at z=‚àí2.1966.
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 266 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=37567447cc2e96a1b1c57404ed3e43b52d09c829f186293cc5f4f6122a03802d (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, no float in the identity): the hypergeometric upper tail P = Œ£_{i=6}^{16} C(21,i)¬∑C(43,16‚àíi)/C(64,16) = 296431911/685926212, Vandermonde full-pmf residual 0, tail-via-complement mismatch 0, z="exact"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N=400000 iid committee draws (fresh random.sample without replacement so the unsafe indicators are iid Bernoulli, NO thinning ‚Äî the plain iid binomial SE is honest, unlike an autocorrelated queue sample path), p_hat=0.430443 vs P=0.432163, z=‚àí2.1966 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 INVARIANCE (Byzantine-identity, own direction): the exact P is a function of counts (N,B,K,T) only; a DIFFERENT Byzantine set byz2={(i¬∑3) mod 64 : i<21} (distinct, gcd(3,64)=1) gives p_hat2=0.432700, z2=0.6856<3; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME without-replacement sample as G2): the with-replacement Binomial(K,B/N) foil P_binom=0.435210 OVERSTATES the tail and is REJECTED at z_foil=‚àí6.0822, |z_foil|>3, while the same sample AGREES with the hypergeometric P at z=‚àí2.1966; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Structural BFT assert at module top: N == 3*B+1 (64=3¬∑21+1) and T==6. Verifier ships firsthand: ideas/fleet/verify_253_bft_committee_sortition.py (stdlib only: math, random, fractions, hashlib, json, argparse, sys), file sha256 58e4159d96c70fca6cfc7563f67d521c1e5d546e5a09f3a9979984b863b16a0c.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Hypergeometric_distribution&oldid=1364778061@faaf98ed0a35df30e3201d6b085b18a9da033e54 ¬∑ https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Byzantine_fault&oldid=1362230504@b3ad268d56c25ed9b5484ee0ad91ad4cceb8fa8c ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match for BOTH: Hypergeometric_distribution oldid 1364778061 sha1 faaf98ed0a35df30e3201d6b085b18a9da033e54, 30058 bytes; Byzantine_fault oldid 1362230504 sha1 b3ad268d56c25ed9b5484ee0ad91ad4cceb8fa8c, 36223 bytes). QUOTED (grep count > 0): on Hypergeometric_distribution ‚Äî the pmf "\frac{\binom{K}{k}\binom{N-K}{n-k}}{\binom{N}{n}}" verbatim (grep \binom{K}{k}=2, \binom{N}{n}=7), "without replacement" (=8), "population" (=8), "hypergeometric" (=41), and the variable defs "N is the population size" / "K is the number of success states in the population" / "n is the number of draws"; on Byzantine_fault ‚Äî "Byzantine" (=34), the BFT bound "there needs to be at least 3''F''+1 players" (grep 3''F''+1=1) and "''n'' > 3''t''" (grep 3''t''=1), "one-third" (=2) and "two-thirds" (=2). DISCLOSED LETTER REMAP (honest seam): the hypergeometric page names (N=population, K=successes, n=draws, k=hits); my instance maps fleet N=64‚Üípage N, Byzantine B=21‚Üípage K (successes), committee K=16‚Üípage n (draws), Byzantine-in-committee i‚Üípage k ‚Äî same formula, disclosed re-lettering (my "K"=committee is the page's "n"=draws, my "B"=the page's "K"). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0): "sortition" (grep 0 both pages); "committee" as a sortition body (grep 0 on Hypergeometric_distribution; grep 1 on Byzantine_fault but that hit is the "IEEE joint committee" org name, not a sortition committee); "bft" as a token (grep 0 on Hypergeometric_distribution); the literal ASCII "3f+1" (grep 0 ‚Äî the bound appears only in wiki-markup form 3''F''+1 / ''n''>3''t''); "upper tail" as a phrase (grep 0 on Hypergeometric_distribution ‚Äî my label; "tail" appears 4√ó); and the whole application: the sortition-committee framing, the N=64/B=21/K=16 instance (the raw digits 64/21 appear incidentally in refs/other params ‚Äî grep 64=3, 21=5 on Hypergeometric_distribution ‚Äî NOT as my fleet instance), T=floor(K/3)+1, the exact tail VALUE 296431911/685926212, the with-replacement binomial foil, the MC estimator, every z-value, SEED, and the results digest. Honest posture ‚Äî DISCLOSED SEAMS: nothing oversold as a novel theorem ‚Äî the hypergeometric pmf/without-replacement framing and the n‚â•3f+1 BFT bound are textbook and QUOTED verbatim on the two pinned pages; the firsthand contribution is the verification apparatus (the exact Fraction committee-failure tail, its two independent cross-checks Vandermonde+complement, the iid MC agreement, the Byzantine-identity invariance leg, the with-replacement-binomial falsification, and every z-value + the digest); the hypergeometric page uses a DIFFERENT letter convention than my instance (my committee K is its draw-count n); the BFT bound is quoted as 3''F''+1 / ''n''>3''t'' / "one-third", not the literal ASCII "3f+1"; the with-replacement binomial foil (G4) is exactly the model the Condorcet jury head (P202) uses for a DIFFERENT question (independent voters) ‚Äî here it is the WRONG model for without-replacement sortition and is rejected, which is what makes this identity distinct.
depends: PROPOSAL 253 -> VERDICT 266 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî coordination / consensus over a fleet of agents / committee sortition under Byzantine faults: a fleet of N=64 agents with B=21 Byzantine sits EXACTLY at the classical BFT bound N=3B+1 (n‚â•3f+1, f=21), yet a size-K=16 committee drawn by uniform sortition WITHOUT replacement is UNSAFE (‚â• T=floor(K/3)+1=6 Byzantine members) with EXACT hypergeometric probability P = Œ£_{i=T}^{K} C(B,i)¬∑C(N‚àíB,K‚àíi)/C(N,K) = 296431911/685926212 ‚âà 0.432163 ‚Äî a globally-safe fleet produces unsafe sub-committees ~43% of the time because sortition CONCENTRATES Byzantine nodes (expected Byzantine per committee K¬∑B/N=5.25, just below the threshold 6); used to refute the naive "the committee is Binomial(K,B/N) with replacement" model (rejected at |z|=6.08 on the same without-replacement sample). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P252 ‚Üí P253 (union-max, no regress). Explicit distinctness: byzantine / sortition / bft / 3f+1 are grep-0 across both repos; hypergeometric appears only as the deck-variance tool in mana-screw (P163 ‚Äî opening-hand finite-population variance, a different quantity/domain) and committee only in the Condorcet jury head (P202 ‚Äî a WITH-replacement binomial majority-correctness object M(n,p)=Œ£ C(n,k)p^k(1‚àíp)^{n‚àík}, which this proposal's G4 foil explicitly REJECTS as the wrong model for without-replacement sortition). This is a hypergeometric committee-failure TAIL read against the n‚â•3f+1 safety bound, distinct from both.

## VERDICT 266 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T11:51:46Z ¬∑ status: ruled APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 253 (idea-engine control/outbox.md, sim-ready; VERDICT 266, +13 offset) ‚Äî idea-engine merge c22e6bb (PR #880).
decision: APPROVE ‚Äî A globally BFT-safe fleet (N=64 agents, B=21 Byzantine, exactly at the classical bound N=3B+1 ‚áî n‚â•3f+1 with f=21) still yields UNSAFE sortition sub-committees ~43% of the time: a size-K=16 committee drawn by uniform sortition WITHOUT replacement is UNSAFE (‚â• T=floor(K/3)+1=6 Byzantine members) with EXACT hypergeometric upper-tail probability P = Œ£_{i=6}^{16} C(21,i)¬∑C(43,16‚àíi)/C(64,16) = 296431911/685926212 ‚âà 0.432163, because the expected Byzantine per committee K¬∑B/N=5.25 sits just below the unsafe threshold 6 and sortition CONCENTRATES Byzantine nodes. Upheld by fully independent re-derivation, not a rubber-stamp of the verifier self-report.
adjudication:
- Reproduction: committed verifier ideas/fleet/verify_253_bft_committee_sortition.py (file sha256 58e4159d96c70fca6cfc7563f67d521c1e5d546e5a09f3a9979984b863b16a0c) reproduced results_sha256 = 37567447cc2e96a1b1c57404ed3e43b52d09c829f186293cc5f4f6122a03802d byte-identical (printed == recorded == verified firsthand; in-process double-run IDENTICAL; separate re-invocation + --selfcheck byte-identical).
- G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): the hypergeometric upper tail P = 296431911/685926212 ‚âà 0.432163, Vandermonde full-pmf residual 0, tail == 1 ‚àí lower-complement (0 mismatch); independently re-derived firsthand via Œ£_{x=6}^{16} C(21,x)¬∑C(43,16‚àíx)/C(64,16) as a reduced Fraction ‚Äî matches; z="exact".
- G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): committed z = ‚àí2.1966 (p_hat = 0.430443, N = 400000) PASS at |z| < 3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; confirmed an honest pass (not a lucky near-miss) by an INDEPENDENT re-run with a fresh seed and 1e6 trials ‚Üí z = ‚àí1.0052, still |z| < 3.
- G3 INVARIANCE (Byzantine-identity, own direction): a DIFFERENT Byzantine set byz2 = {(i¬∑3) mod 64 : i<21} gives z2 = +0.6856 ‚Äî the value depends only on the counts (N,B,K,T), not on WHICH agents are Byzantine.
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME without-replacement sample as G2): the pre-registered with-replacement Binomial(K,B/N) foil (P_binom = 0.435210) is REJECTED at z_foil = ‚àí6.0822 on the same without-replacement sample while the hypergeometric P is ACCEPTED at z = ‚àí2.1966. INDEPENDENTLY confirmed the foil has real discriminating teeth ‚Äî recomputed the binomial tail firsthand (0.435210, gap 0.003047 vs the hypergeometric value) and drew an independent fresh-sample z_foil = ‚àí7.1511; the discriminating signal grows with N, so the ‚àí6.08 edge is a genuine rejection, not a marginal artifact, and G1's exact value anchors the claim regardless. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS.
grounding: pins Wikipedia "Hypergeometric distribution" oldid 1364778061 and "Byzantine fault" oldid 1362230504, both verified firsthand. QUOTED (present on the pinned pages): the hypergeometric pmf binom(K,k)¬∑binom(N‚àíK,n‚àík)/binom(N,n) ‚Äî literally present on the Hypergeometric page, which uses n = number of draws and K = number of success states; the 3F+1 BFT threshold ‚Äî literally present on the Byzantine-fault page as "at least 3F+1 players". The DISCLOSED LETTER REMAP (committee K ‚Üí the page's n draws; Byzantine B ‚Üí the page's K successes) is faithful to what the page actually uses, so it is an honest variable relabel, not an overclaim. DERIVED firsthand (grep 0 both pages, confirmed both directions): the N=64/B=21/K=16/T=6 instance, the exact tail value 296431911/685926212, the with-replacement binomial foil, all z-values, SEED, and the results digest ‚Äî none present on either page.
reconciliation: the proposal-authored sim-lab reproduction mirror (sim-lab #351, merge 34bf222, sims/verdict-266-bft-committee-sortition/) is a byte-identical copy of the verifier (diff exit 0) that reproduces the same digest; this ruling is built on top of it ‚Äî no competing sim-lab PR opened.
insight preserved: a globally BFT-safe fleet (n ‚â• 3f+1, here 64 = 3¬∑21+1) still yields UNSAFE sortition sub-committees ~43% of the time because the expected Byzantine per committee K¬∑B/N=5.25 sits just below the unsafe threshold 6 and sampling concentrates Byzantine nodes ‚Äî committee-BFT protocols must SIZE committees to bound the sortition failure probability.

decision: APPROVE.
