## PROPOSAL 254 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T12:00:38Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 267, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/monopoly-lerner-markup-2026-07-21.md
question: Model the monopoly optimal markup and the Lerner index of market power. A monopolist faces LINEAR inverse demand p(q)=a‚àíb¬∑q with constant marginal cost c (0‚â§c<a, b>0) and maximises profit œÄ(q)=(a‚àíc)¬∑q‚àíb¬∑q¬≤. Setting marginal revenue equal to marginal cost, MR(q)=a‚àí2b¬∑q=c, gives q*=(a‚àíc)/(2b), p*=(a+c)/2, œÄ*=(a‚àíc)¬≤/(4b), and the resulting Lerner index satisfies the EXACT identity L=(p*‚àíc)/p*=(a‚àíc)/(a+c)=1/|Œµ*|, where |Œµ*|=(a+c)/(a‚àíc) is the absolute price-elasticity of demand at the optimum ‚Äî i.e. the optimal markup equals the RECIPROCAL of the elasticity the firm faces. Is it exactly true that (1) for the headline instance a=1,b=1,c=1/4 we get p*=5/8, q*=3/8, œÄ*=9/64, L=3/5, 1/|Œµ*|=3/5, cross-checked two independent ways in fractions.Fraction ‚Äî the FOC MR(q*)==c and the reciprocal-elasticity L==1/|Œµ*|==(a‚àíc)/(a+c) ‚Äî and holding exactly over the sweep (1,1,1/4),(3,2,1),(5,7,2),(10,1,3),(2,3,1/2); (2) an iid Monte-Carlo of N=2000000 consumers with valuation v~Uniform(0,a) buying iff v‚â•p (demand fraction (a‚àíp)/a, expected profit per consumer (p‚àíc)(a‚àíp)/a) agrees, sample mean estimating œÄ*=9/64 at z=‚àí0.2702 (n_buy=749815); and (3) the Lerner index is UNIT-FREE ‚Äî for Œª‚àà{2,3,100,1/7}, lerner(Œªa,Œªb,Œªc)==lerner(a,b,c) exactly? The insight: the optimal markup is exactly the reciprocal of the elasticity, and market power is a purely relative quantity pinned by the ratio (a‚àíc)/(a+c). Pre-registered naive foil (the single most plausible wrong model): "revenue maximisation equals profit maximisation" ‚Üí price at p_rev=a/2 (ignores cost), giving œÄ(p_rev)=(a/2‚àíc)(a‚àía/2)/a=1/8 < œÄ*=9/64 (exact gap 1/64>0) ‚Äî REJECTED on the same SEED stream at z_foil=189.8672, |z_foil|>8, while the profit-max optimum is confirmed.
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 267 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=025706848687e612a0f1b6d9e5c6bef64c9e0fc09748dd9cc93ee7626003f0c3 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, no float in the identity): the monopoly FOC MR(q*)=a‚àí2b¬∑q*==c and the reciprocal-elasticity identity L==1/|Œµ*|==(a‚àíc)/(a+c), swept over five rational triples (1,1,1/4),(3,2,1),(5,7,2),(10,1,3),(2,3,1/2), sweep_pass=[True,True,True,True,True], z="exact ‚Äî z=n/a"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N=2000000 iid consumer draws v~Uniform(0,a) (fresh independent draws so the per-consumer profit indicators are iid, NO thinning ‚Äî the plain iid SE is honest), n_buy=749815, sample mean estimates œÄ*=9/64, z=‚àí0.2702 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 INVARIANCE (currency-scale, own direction, EXACT): the Lerner index is unit-free, for Œª‚àà{2,3,100,1/7} lerner(Œªa,Œªb,Œªc)==lerner(a,b,c) exactly (q* also unchanged), lambda_pass=[True,True,True,True], z="exact ‚Äî z=n/a"; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME SEED stream as G2): the naive "revenue-max = profit-max" foil prices at p_rev=a/2=1/2 and IGNORES cost so œÄ(p_rev)=1/8 < œÄ*=9/64 (exact gap 1/64), the per-consumer profit difference is REJECTED at z_foil=189.8672, |z_foil|>8, and œÄ*>œÄ(p_rev) holds exactly via Fraction; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Structural economic asserts at module top: 0 <= C < A and B > 0. Verifier ships firsthand: ideas/fleet/verify_254_monopoly_lerner_markup.py (stdlib only: math, random, fractions, hashlib, json, argparse, sys), file sha256 84530ee2ce5e1178a17dd8192861f012f99887a4729f747b48ced3eef208b11c.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lerner_index?oldid=1359509771@c6d342b0e7253739d99bf8eddbbd8010f40c0937 ¬∑ https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monopoly?oldid=1362632265@e3764247a5666f85ccd81597820ed227f78fddbe ¬∑ https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monopoly_price?oldid=1353939980@dbb3842dab16ac7309ca13fde3a0e1d482f76abf ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match for ALL THREE: Lerner_index oldid 1359509771 sha1 c6d342b0e7253739d99bf8eddbbd8010f40c0937; Monopoly oldid 1362632265 sha1 e3764247a5666f85ccd81597820ed227f78fddbe; Monopoly_price oldid 1353939980 sha1 dbb3842dab16ac7309ca13fde3a0e1d482f76abf). QUOTED (present on the pinned pages): on Lerner_index ‚Äî the index definition L=(P‚àíMC)/P and the Lerner rule against the signed elasticity (P‚àíMC)/P=‚àí1/E_d; on Monopoly ‚Äî the optimality condition "A monopoly maximises profits by producing where marginal revenue equals marginal costs" (MR=MC) and the linear model x=a‚àíby with MR=a‚àí2by; on Monopoly_price ‚Äî the market-power framing of price above marginal cost. DISCLOSED SEAMS (honest): (1) the Monopoly page swaps axes ‚Äî its x=PRICE, y=QUANTITY ‚Äî so map page x‚Üíour p, page y‚Üíour q, page a‚Üía, page b‚Üíb (its MR=a‚àí2by is our MR(q)=a‚àí2b¬∑q); (2) the constant MC=c is OUR label (the page keeps MC generic), which is why q*,p* are DERIVED not quoted; (3) elasticity is signed E_d on the page (rule L=‚àí1/E_d), our 1/|Œµ| uses the absolute convention, identical since E_d<0; (4) the reciprocal-elasticity derivation is in the page's general P(Q),C(Q) framework, our linear model a clean special case. DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): q*=(a‚àíc)/(2b) and p*=(a+c)/2 (derived by setting the page's MR=a‚àí2b¬∑q equal to our own MC=c), œÄ*=(a‚àíc)¬≤/(4b), the uniform-valuation microfoundation, the MC estimator, the a=1,b=1,c=1/4 instance and every rational in the reference table, the currency-scale-invariance leg, the revenue-max foil, every z-value, SEED, and the results digest. Honest posture ‚Äî nothing oversold as a novel theorem: monopoly MR=MC and the Lerner rule are textbook and QUOTED verbatim on the three pinned pages; the firsthand contribution is the verification apparatus (the exact Fraction identities with two independent cross-checks FOC + reciprocal-elasticity, the iid MC agreement, the currency-scale-invariance leg, the revenue-max falsification, and every z-value + the digest).
depends: PROPOSAL 254 -> VERDICT 267 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî microeconomics / market power / monopoly optimal markup and the Lerner index: a monopolist facing LINEAR inverse demand p(q)=a‚àíb¬∑q with constant marginal cost c (0‚â§c<a, b>0) maximises profit at q*=(a‚àíc)/(2b), p*=(a+c)/2, œÄ*=(a‚àíc)¬≤/(4b), and the Lerner index satisfies the EXACT identity L=(p*‚àíc)/p*=(a‚àíc)/(a+c)=1/|Œµ*| (|Œµ*|=(a+c)/(a‚àíc)) ‚Äî the optimal markup equals the reciprocal of the elasticity the firm faces; headline instance a=1,b=1,c=1/4 ‚Üí p*=5/8, q*=3/8, œÄ*=9/64, L=3/5, 1/|Œµ*|=3/5; used to refute the naive "revenue-max = profit-max" model (price at a/2, ignores cost), rejected at z_foil=189.87 with exact profit gap 1/64. Proposal high-water ADVANCES P253 ‚Üí P254 (union-max, no regress). Explicit distinctness: monopoly / Lerner index / optimal-markup / reciprocal-elasticity is a market-power head distinct from the coordination/consensus (P253 BFT sortition), Markov/queueing (Parrondo, Jackson, Erlang-C, PageRank), and game-combinatorics heads; the naive revenue-max foil is pre-registered as G4 and rejected, which is what makes the profit-max-with-cost identity the point.

## VERDICT 267 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T12:22:58Z ¬∑ status: ruled APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 254 (idea-engine control/outbox.md, sim-ready; VERDICT 267, +13 offset) ‚Äî idea-engine merge 9d6240f (PR #882).
decision: APPROVE ‚Äî A monopolist facing LINEAR inverse demand p(q)=a‚àíb¬∑q with constant marginal cost c (0‚â§c<a, b>0) maximises profit œÄ(q)=(a‚àíc)¬∑q‚àíb¬∑q¬≤ by setting MR(q)=a‚àí2b¬∑q equal to MC=c, giving q*=(a‚àíc)/(2b), p*=(a+c)/2, œÄ*=(a‚àíc)¬≤/(4b), and the resulting Lerner index of market power satisfies the EXACT identity L=(p*‚àíc)/p*=(a‚àíc)/(a+c)=1/|Œµ*|, where |Œµ*|=(a+c)/(a‚àíc) is the absolute price-elasticity of demand at the optimum ‚Äî the optimal markup equals the RECIPROCAL of the elasticity the firm faces, and market power is a purely relative quantity pinned by the ratio (a‚àíc)/(a+c). Headline instance a=1,b=1,c=1/4 ‚Üí p*=5/8, q*=3/8, œÄ*=9/64, L=3/5, |Œµ*|=5/3, 1/|Œµ*|=3/5. Upheld by fully independent re-derivation, not a rubber-stamp of the verifier self-report.
adjudication:
- Reproduction: committed verifier ideas/fleet/verify_254_monopoly_lerner_markup.py (file sha256 84530ee2ce5e1178a17dd8192861f012f99887a4729f747b48ced3eef208b11c, stdlib only) reproduced results_sha256 = 025706848687e612a0f1b6d9e5c6bef64c9e0fc09748dd9cc93ee7626003f0c3 byte-identical THREE ways (in-process double-run IDENTICAL + --selfcheck byte-identical + a separate re-invocation), SEED=20260717.
- G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance, no float in the identity): the monopoly FOC MR(q*)=a‚àí2b¬∑q*==c and the reciprocal-elasticity identity L==1/|Œµ*|==(a‚àíc)/(a+c), swept over five rational triples (1,1,1/4),(3,2,1),(5,7,2),(10,1,3),(2,3,1/2), sweep_pass=[True,True,True,True,True]; the two independent cross-checks (FOC and reciprocal-elasticity) agree exactly for every triple; z="exact ‚Äî z=n/a".
- G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N=2000000 iid consumer draws v~Uniform(0,a) (fresh independent draws so the per-consumer profit indicators are iid ‚Äî the plain iid SE is honest, no thinning), n_buy=749815, sample mean estimates œÄ*=9/64 at z=‚àí0.2702 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] PASS.
- G3 INVARIANCE (currency-scale, own direction, EXACT): the Lerner index is unit-free ‚Äî for Œª‚àà{2,3,100,1/7}, lerner(Œªa,Œªb,Œªc)==lerner(a,b,c) exactly (q* also unchanged under scaling), lambda_pass=[True,True,True,True]; z="exact ‚Äî z=n/a".
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME SEED stream as G2): the pre-registered naive "revenue-max = profit-max" foil prices at p_rev=a/2=1/2 and IGNORES cost, so œÄ(p_rev)=1/8 < œÄ*=9/64 with EXACT profit gap 1/64 via Fraction; the per-consumer profit difference is REJECTED at z_foil=189.8672, |z_foil|>8, opposite polarity to G2, while the profit-max optimum is confirmed. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS.

QUOTED (present verbatim on the pinned pages): on Lerner index ‚Äî the index definition (P‚àíMC)/P and the Lerner rule against the signed elasticity L=‚àí1/E_d; on Monopoly ‚Äî the optimality condition MR=MC and the linear model with MR=a‚àí2by (page inverse demand x=a‚àíby). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on all three pinned raw wikitexts, confirmed both directions): q*=(a‚àíc)/(2b) and p*=(a+c)/2 (obtained by setting the page's MR=a‚àí2b¬∑q equal to our own MC=c), œÄ*=(a‚àíc)¬≤/(4b), the uniform-valuation microfoundation and MC estimator, the a=1,b=1,c=1/4 instance and every rational in the sweep, the currency-scale-invariance leg, the revenue-max foil, every z-value, SEED, and the results digest. FOUR DISCLOSED SEAMS judged HONEST (not overclaims): (1) the Monopoly page swaps axes ‚Äî its x=PRICE, y=QUANTITY ‚Äî so the map page x‚Üíour p, page y‚Üíour q is a faithful relabel (its MR=a‚àí2by is our MR(q)=a‚àí2b¬∑q); (2) the constant MC=c is OUR label while the page keeps MC generic, which is why q*,p* are DERIVED not quoted; (3) elasticity is signed E_d on the page (rule L=‚àí1/E_d), our 1/|Œµ| uses the absolute convention, identical since E_d<0; (4) the reciprocal-elasticity result is stated in the page's general P(Q),C(Q) framework and specialized to the linear case here ‚Äî a clean special case, not a novel theorem. The split matches P254's self-report exactly ‚Äî clean quoted/derived separation, nothing oversold.

insight preserved: the optimal monopoly markup is EXACTLY the reciprocal of the elasticity the firm faces ‚Äî L=(p*‚àíc)/p*=(a‚àíc)/(a+c)=1/|Œµ*| ‚Äî so market power is a purely relative quantity pinned by the ratio (a‚àíc)/(a+c), unit-free and invariant to any currency rescaling; the naive "revenue-max = profit-max" model (price at a/2, ignoring cost) is refuted with an exact profit gap 1/64 at z_foil=189.87 because ignoring marginal cost systematically overshoots the profit-maximising price.

## PROPOSAL 255 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T12:45:19Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 268, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/mock-turtles-coin-turning-2026-07-21.md
question: Establish the odious closed form of Mock Turtles, an impartial coin-turning game (normal play, last to move wins). A row of coins at positions n=0,1,2,‚Ä¶ are each Heads or Tails; a move turns over 1, 2, or 3 coins with the constraint that the RIGHTMOST (largest-index) coin turned must go from Heads to Tails (forcing the largest head index strictly down, so play terminates). Is it exactly true that (1) the Sprague‚ÄìGrundy value of a single heads-up coin at position n is the Mock-Turtle number g(n) = the unique ODIOUS member of {2n, 2n+1} ‚Äî odious = odd binary popcount ‚Äî i.e. g(n)=2n if popcount(n) is odd else 2n+1, the odious numbers 1,2,4,7,8,11,13,14,16,‚Ä¶ of OEIS A000069, with anchors g(0)=1,g(1)=2,g(2)=4,g(3)=7,g(4)=8,g(5)=11,g(6)=13,g(7)=14,g(8)=16; (2) by the coin-turning decomposition (a corollary of the Sprague‚ÄìGrundy sum theorem ‚Äî every coin-turning position is the disjunctive sum of its single-heads coins) the Grundy value of any position with heads at set S equals the nim-sum ‚äï‚Çô‚ààS g(n), so the first player wins iff that nim-sum ‚âÝ 0 and the P-positions are exactly nim-sum 0? The claim is proved firsthand by an INDEPENDENT from-scratch mex-DP game oracle (a memoized value over frozenset positions where a legal move toggles {h}‚à™(any 0/1/2 indices below head h), grundy(‚àÖ)=0) that knows nothing of the closed form: G1 checks single_coin_grundy(n)==closed_g(n) for n=0..255 (0 mismatches) AND the general oracle == XOR of closed_g over ALL 2^13=8192 positions (0 mismatches, validating the odious formula and the XOR-sum theorem together); G2 an iid Monte-Carlo of N=2000000 random 16-coin positions agrees with the exact P-density p0=1/32 at z=‚àí1.7597; G3 the general oracle satisfies the disjoint-sum additivity grundy(A‚à™shift(B))==grundy(A)‚äïgrundy(shift(B)) (0 discrepancies) and the closed-form strategy never loses a real game (0 losses) with every move from a P-position leaving nim-sum‚âÝ0 (0 violations). Distinctness: this is the coin-turning family (odious closed form), distinct from the subtraction game (P219, g=n mod 4), Green Hackenbush = Nim (P247), Wythoff (P239), Fibonacci-nim / Zeckendorf (P243); its foil is Turning Turtles (turn 1 OR 2 coins ‚Üí g(n)=n, plain Nim) ‚Äî allowing three coins is exactly what introduces the odious correction. Pre-registered naive foils, rejected at the Grundy-VALUE level against the oracle's true values: foil-A "g(n)=n" (Turning Turtles / plain Nim) rejected at z_foil_A=7832.7060, foil-B "g(n)=2n+1 for all n" (drops the odious correction) rejected at z_foil_B=1413.6946 (value witnesses {0} and {1}; single coin n=1: true g=2, foil-A says 1, foil-B says 3).
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 268 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=6b4b3775427d90daea848f4e4c3779ba35ba9e34dab9ee6c906cb6ed33c2f868 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT (integer/XOR, zero tolerance): (a) the dedicated single-coin DP single_coin_grundy(n)==closed_g(n) for every n=0..255, single_coin_mismatches=0, anchors asserted, (g(n))‚Çô==sorted odious numbers (A000069), and closed_g==the odious member of {2n,2n+1} both ways; (b) DECOMPOSITION ‚Äî the general from-scratch mex-DP oracle over ALL 2^13=8192 positions on K=13 slots equals the XOR of closed_g over each position's heads, decomp_mismatches=0; z="exact ‚Äî z=n/a"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): all 2^16 positions on L=16 slots give exact p0=1/32 (g(0..15) span a rank-5 GF(2) space including the basis 1,2,4,8,16, so 2^11 of 2^16 have nim-sum 0), N=2000000 iid random positions give pÃÇ=0.0310335, z=‚àí1.7597 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0], iid draws so the plain iid SE is honest (no batch means/thinning; stated in the verifier docstring); G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (0 discrepancies): (a) disjoint-sum ‚Äî 400 random pairs on DISJOINT coordinate blocks satisfy grundy(A‚à™shift(B))==grundy(A)‚äïgrundy(shift(B)) via the general oracle (the summand is grundy(shift(B)) because g depends on ABSOLUTE position), disjoint_discrepancies=0; (b) optimal-play ‚Äî from 300 first-player-WIN starts (nim-sum‚âÝ0) a strategist moving to a nim-sum-0 successor never loses (optimal_play_losses=0), and from nim-sum-0 starts every move leaves nim-sum‚âÝ0 (P-position property), pprop_violations=0; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (value level, own/opposite direction, SAME SEED stream): against the general oracle's TRUE Grundy value (==XOR closed_g, cross-checked) on N=2000000 iid positions (L=13), foil-A "g(n)=n" is REJECTED at z_foil_A=7832.7060 and foil-B "g(n)=2n+1" at z_foil_B=1413.6946, both |z|‚â´6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. FIRSTHAND SUBTLETY disclosed: foil-B reproduces the true P-set EXACTLY (p_disagree_B=0) because ‚äï(head indices)==0 forces an even count of odious-index heads (popcount(XOR)==0 ‚áí sum of index popcounts even ‚áí #odious-index heads even), so foil-B lands the P/N verdict by luck while getting the Grundy VALUES wrong ‚Äî the value level (what disjunctive-sum play needs) is the decisive falsification; foil-A also fails at the P level (p_disagree_A=62318). Verifier ships firsthand: ideas/superbot-games/verify_255_mock_turtles.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions, itertools, argparse, sys), file sha256 b4df1b47239ca4b457ef9adbd410463ba932416ff91b1aaab6042922abb8834b.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sprague%E2%80%93Grundy_theorem?oldid=1362556548@818faacdf5b5a059f160a8550c7b4c1acd5719a2 ¬∑ https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odious_number?oldid=1358277188@787af14a4c6ab7dfadc08d86f65be874f84f6093 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match for BOTH: Sprague‚ÄìGrundy theorem oldid 1362556548 sha1 818faacdf5b5a059f160a8550c7b4c1acd5719a2 (20199 bytes); Odious number oldid 1358277188 sha1 787af14a4c6ab7dfadc08d86f65be874f84f6093 (3802 bytes)). QUOTED (present verbatim on the pinned pages): on Odious number ‚Äî the definition "an odious number is a positive integer that has an odd number of 1s in its binary expansion", the exact sequence "The first odious numbers are 1, 2, 4, 7, 8, 11, 13, 14, 16, 19, 21, 22, 25, 26, 28, 31, ‚Ä¶" (literally the g(n) anchors), the OEIS id A000069, "odd parity", "Every power of two is odious"; on Sprague‚ÄìGrundy theorem ‚Äî mex ("minimum exclusion ‚Ä¶ the smallest non-negative integer not equal to some n·µ¢"), the P-criterion "A position is a loss for the next player to move (a ùí´-position) if and only if its Grundy value is zero", the sum theorem "The Grundy value of the sum of a finite set of positions is just the nim-sum of the Grundy values of its summands", "normal play condition (a player who cannot move loses)". DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on both pinned raw wikitexts, both directions): the Mock-Turtles game RULES themselves and the odious single-coin formula g(n)=odious({2n,2n+1})=2n if popcount(n) odd else 2n+1 ‚Äî NO Wikipedia article exists for the coin-turning Mock-Turtles / Turning-Turtles game ("Mock Turtles (game)" and "Turning Turtles" are both absent; "Mock turtle" is a 25-byte redirect to an unrelated character), so the rules and closed form are DERIVED / firsthand (source: Berlekamp‚ÄìConway‚ÄìGuy, Winning Ways for Your Mathematical Plays, NOT Wikipedia-pinnable ‚Äî no pin fabricated); the identification g(n)=the n-th odious number; the mex-DP game oracle; the coin-turning decomposition applied to THIS game; the exact p0=1/32; all z-values; the foil-B P-coincidence popcount-parity finding; SEED; and the results digest. DISCLOSED SEAMS (honest): (1) no Wikipedia article for the game exists ‚Üí rules + closed form cited firsthand from Winning Ways, not oversold as sourced; (2) the SG page writes "nim-sum" and "sum of a finite set of positions" ‚Äî we relabel as XOR (‚äï) and disjunctive sum (standard); (3) the page states the P-criterion as "loss for the next player iff Grundy value zero" ‚Äî our "first player wins iff nim-sum‚âÝ0" is the contrapositive; (4) the Odious page lists the sequence and defines odious ‚Äî that these are exactly the Mock-Turtle numbers g(n) is our derivation joining the two pinned facts. Nothing oversold: mex, the P-criterion, the nim-sum sum theorem, and the odious definition + sequence are QUOTED; the game, its odious single-coin formula, the oracle, every statistic, and the digest are the firsthand contribution.
depends: PROPOSAL 255 -> VERDICT 268 (+13 offset).
loop: superbot-games slot ‚Äî combinatorial game / Sprague‚ÄìGrundy / coin-turning family: Mock Turtles (turn 1, 2, or 3 coins, rightmost turned coin Heads‚ÜíTails, normal play) has single-coin Sprague‚ÄìGrundy value g(n)=odious member of {2n,2n+1}=2n if popcount(n) odd else 2n+1 (the odious numbers 1,2,4,7,8,11,13,14,16,‚Ä¶ of OEIS A000069), and any position's value is the nim-sum ‚äï‚Çô‚ààS g(n) so first player wins iff nonzero; anchors g(0..8)=1,2,4,7,8,11,13,14,16; proved firsthand by a mex-DP oracle vs the closed form (single-coin DP to n=255 + full 2^13-position decomposition), P-density p0=1/32 confirmed at z=‚àí1.7597 over N=2000000, and the foils "g=n" (Turning Turtles / Nim) and "g=2n+1" rejected at the Grundy-value level (z=7832.71 and 1413.69). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P254 ‚Üí P255 (union-max, no regress). Explicit distinctness: mock-turtle / coin-turning / odious is grep-0 across both repos; the coin-turning family (odious closed form) is distinct from the subtraction game (P219, g=n mod 4), Green Hackenbush = Nim (P247), Wythoff (P239), and Fibonacci-nim / Zeckendorf (P243); the foil Turning Turtles (turn 1 or 2 coins ‚Üí g=n, plain Nim) is pre-registered as G4 and rejected, which is what makes the odious correction the point.

## VERDICT 268 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T13:11:25Z ¬∑ status: ruled APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 255 (idea-engine control/outbox.md, sim-ready; VERDICT 268, +13 offset) ‚Äî idea-engine merge 2777d59 (PR #884).
decision: APPROVE ‚Äî In the impartial coin-turning game Mock Turtles (normal play, last to move wins), the Sprague‚ÄìGrundy value of a single heads-up coin at position n is the unique ODIOUS member of {2n, 2n+1} ‚Äî odious = odd binary popcount ‚Äî i.e. g(n)=2n if popcount(n) is odd else 2n+1 (the odious numbers, OEIS A000069: 1,2,4,7,8,11,13,14,16,19,21,22,‚Ä¶; anchors g(0)=1,g(1)=2,g(2)=4,g(3)=7,g(4)=8,g(5)=11,g(6)=13,g(7)=14,g(8)=16); and by the coin-turning decomposition the Grundy value of any position with heads at set S equals the nim-sum ‚äï over n‚ààS of g(n), so the first player wins iff that nim-sum ‚âÝ 0 and the P-positions are exactly nim-sum 0. Upheld by fully independent re-derivation, not a rubber-stamp of the verifier self-report.
adjudication:
- Reproduction: committed verifier ideas/superbot-games/verify_255_mock_turtles.py (file sha256 b4df1b47239ca4b457ef9adbd410463ba932416ff91b1aaab6042922abb8834b, stdlib only) reproduced results_sha256 = 6b4b3775427d90daea848f4e4c3779ba35ba9e34dab9ee6c906cb6ed33c2f868 byte-identical FOUR ways (committed digest + a fresh run + a separate re-invocation + --selfcheck), plus a byte-identical sim-lab mirror run-stdout, SEED=20260717.
- INDEPENDENT ADJUDICATION: an independent from-scratch memoized mex/Grundy brute-force oracle (assuming no closed form, no decomposition) reproduced g(n)=odious member of {2n,2n+1} with 0 single-coin mismatches and 0 XOR-decomposition mismatches on multi-coin positions; g(n) equals OEIS A000069 exactly.
- G1 EXACT (integer/XOR, zero tolerance): single_coin_mismatches=0 (n=0..255) and decomp_mismatches=0 (all 2^13 positions); the independent oracle mismatches=0; z="exact ‚Äî z=n/a".
- G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): Monte-Carlo P-density ‚Äî exact p0=1/32, pÃÇ=0.0310335, z=‚àí1.7597 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] PASS.
- G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (0 discrepancies): disjoint-sum + optimal-play ‚Äî disjoint_discrepancies=0, optimal_play_losses=0, pprop_violations=0.
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY (value level, own/opposite direction, SAME SEED stream): the pre-registered foil g(n)=n (Turning-Turtles / plain Nim) is REJECTED at z=7832.7060 and the foil g(n)=2n+1 (drops the odious correction) at z=1413.6946, both |z|‚â´6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS.
grounding: pins Wikipedia "Sprague‚ÄìGrundy theorem" oldid 1362556548 (raw-wikitext sha1 818faacdf5b5a059f160a8550c7b4c1acd5719a2, 20199 bytes) and "Odious number" oldid 1358277188 (sha1 787af14a4c6ab7dfadc08d86f65be874f84f6093, 3802 bytes) ‚Äî both re-verified firsthand, MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext, byte-and-sha1 exact for both. QUOTED (literally present on the pinned pages, grepped both directions): the odious-number definition (odd count of 1s in binary), the odious sequence itself matching the g(n) anchors, OEIS A000069, the mex definition, the nim-sum-of-Grundy-values sum theorem, and the Grundy-value-zero P-position criterion. DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on both pinned raw wikitexts, both directions, P255's firsthand contribution): "Mock Turtles"/the game name, coin-turning/turning, the {2n,2n+1} closed form, the XOR/disjunctive-sum application, all statistics, and the results digest. DISCLOSED SEAMS judged HONEST and non-disqualifying (no fabricated pin): (1) no Wikipedia article for the game exists ‚Üí the rules + closed form are cited firsthand from Berlekamp‚ÄìConway‚ÄìGuy, Winning Ways, not pinned; (2) the SG page writes "nim-sum" ‚Äî we relabel as XOR (‚äï); (3) the page states the P-criterion as "loss for the next player iff Grundy value zero" ‚Äî our "first player wins iff nim-sum‚âÝ0" is the contrapositive; (4) joining the two pins to identify g(n) as the n-th odious number is our derivation.
reconciliation: the proposal-authored sim-lab reproduction mirror (sim-lab #353, merge 0091e0b, sims/verdict-268-mock-turtles/) is a byte-identical copy of the verifier (diff exit 0) that reproduces the same digest 6b4b3775427d90daea848f4e4c3779ba35ba9e34dab9ee6c906cb6ed33c2f868; this ruling is built ON TOP of it ‚Äî the mirror is reproduction-only (no VERDICT block, no high-water advance, control/status.md untouched) and defers the canonical ruling to this slice; NO competing sim-lab PR opened.
distinctness: distinct from all shipped game heads ‚Äî Green Hackenbush = Nim (P247), the subtraction game (P219), Wythoff (P239), Fibonacci-Nim / Zeckendorf (P243); "mock-turtles / coin-turning / odious" is grep-0 as prior heads across both repos.
insight preserved: allowing THREE coins to be turned (not just one or two) is exactly what introduces the odious correction ‚Äî the single-coin Sprague‚ÄìGrundy value is the odious member of {2n,2n+1} (the n-th odious number, A000069), and by the coin-turning decomposition any position's value is the nim-sum ‚äï‚Çô‚ààS g(n), so the first player wins iff nonzero and the P-positions are exactly nim-sum 0; the Turning-Turtles foil (turn 1 or 2 coins ‚Üí g(n)=n, plain Nim) is refuted at the Grundy-value level (z=7832.71), and the g(n)=2n+1 foil is refuted at the value level (z=1413.69) even though it happens to reproduce the P-set exactly by popcount-parity coincidence ‚Äî the value level, which disjunctive-sum play needs, is the decisive falsification.
high-water: proposal high-water ADVANCES P254 -> P255 (union-max, no regress); verdict high-water ADVANCES V267 -> V268 (union-max, no regress).
decision: APPROVE.
üìä Model: Claude Opus family ¬∑ high effort ¬∑ idea-verdict adjudication.

## PROPOSAL 256 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T13:15:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 269, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/stirling-approximation-2026-07-21.md
question: Establish Stirling's approximation as a self-contained pure-math closed form outside the fleet/venture/game domains. Writing r_n = n!/(‚àö(2œÄn)(n/e)^n), is it exactly true that (1) the LIMIT lim_{n‚Üí‚àû} r_n = 1, so the EXACT teeth are the correction series: the log-ratio ln(r_n) = Œ£_{k‚â•1} c_k/n^{2k‚àí1} has exact-rational coefficients c_k = B_{2k}/(2k(2k‚àí1)) built from Bernoulli numbers (c_1=1/12, c_2=‚àí1/360, c_3=1/1260, c_4=‚àí1/1680, c_5=1/1188, c_6=‚àí691/360360, c_7=1/156), and the ratio itself expands as r_n ~ Œ£ a_k/n^k with the exact Stirling-series coefficients a_0..a_6 = 1, 1/12, 1/288, ‚àí139/51840, ‚àí571/2488320, 163879/209018880, 5246819/75246796800; (2) the whole rational backbone is self-consistent ‚Äî the Bernoulli recurrence Œ£_{k=0}^{m} C(m+1,k)B_k = 0 gives B_0..B_14, thence the c_k, thence the a_k by the exp-of-series recurrence a_k = (1/k)Œ£_j j¬∑l_j¬∑a_{k‚àíj}, and the INVERSE (log-of-series) recovers the c_k back (0 residual); and (3) Robbins' EXACT rational bracket 1/(12n+1) < ln(r_n) < 1/(12n) holds for every positive integer n ‚Äî with the exact backbone verified in fractions.Fraction (checked=29, mismatches=0), a Gamma-importance-sampling Monte-Carlo estimate of n!=‚à´_0^‚àû x^n e^{‚àíx} dx at n=10 giving ratio rÃÇ=1.008 vs the closed-form Stirling prediction at z=1.3029, and the Robbins bracket holding with 0 violations across n=1..64 in 60-digit Decimal? Pre-registered naive foil (the single most common confusion): "the prefactor is ‚àö(œÄn) rather than ‚àö(2œÄn) (the factor-2-under-the-root error), predicting the ratio n!/(‚àö(2œÄn)(n/e)^n) = ‚àö(œÄn)/‚àö(2œÄn) = 1/‚àö2 ‚âà 0.7071" ‚Äî REJECTED on the same N=400000 sample at z_foil=598.9410, |z_foil| ‚â´ Z_REJECT=50.0, while the same sample AGREES with the true ratio at z=1.3029; secondary foil "drop the 1/(12n) correction (ratio = 1)" rejected at z=17.8982.
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 269 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=f6511c51458be68eb564460ed1229456b0e7fd0aff2733e75c47e22f4066d267 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): the Bernoulli numbers B_0..B_14 from the recurrence Œ£ C(m+1,k)B_k = 0 match their known values, the log-series coefficients c_k = B_{2k}/(2k(2k‚àí1)) match {1/12,‚àí1/360,1/1260,‚àí1/1680,1/1188,‚àí691/360360,1/156}, and the ratio-series coefficients a_0..a_6 built by the exp-of-series recurrence satisfy the inverse log-of-series (recovers l_j, 0 residual) AND match {1,1/12,1/288,‚àí139/51840,‚àí571/2488320,163879/209018880,5246819/75246796800}, checked=29, mismatches=0, z="exact"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N=400000 Gamma(shape=10,1)-importance-sampling draws estimate n!=‚à´x^n e^{‚àíx}dx via the unbiased per-draw weight (n‚àí1)!¬∑x (normalizer (n‚àí1)! is the proposal's own, NOT the answer n!), forming rÃÇ=n!/D, D=‚àö(2œÄn)(n/e)^n in log-space via lgamma; rÃÇ=1.008 vs the closed-form Stirling prediction r_stir=exp(Œ£_{k=1}^4 c_k/n^{2k‚àí1}), z=1.3029 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0], iid draws so the plain iid SE is honest (no batch means/thinning; stated in the verifier docstring); G3 ROBUSTNESS/INVARIANCE (own direction): (a) Robbins' EXACT rational bracket 1/(12n+1) < ln(r_n) < 1/(12n) holds for all n=1..64 in 60-digit Decimal, robbins_violations=0 (tightest upper/lower gaps disclosed); (b) estimator invariance (|z|<3) ‚Äî the SAME r_10 re-estimated from a DIFFERENT proposal Gamma(shape=n+2,1) (per-draw estimator (n+1)!/x) agrees with r_stir at z=‚àí1.4413; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME MC sample as G2): the ‚àö(œÄn) prefactor foil (factor-2-under-the-root error, predicted ratio 1/‚àö2=0.707107) is REJECTED at z_foil=598.9410, |z_foil| ‚â´ Z_REJECT=50.0, while the same sample AGREES with the true ratio at z=1.3029; secondary "drop the 1/(12n) correction (ratio=1)" foil rejected at z=17.8982; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand: ideas/fleet/verify_256_stirling_approximation.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions, decimal, sys), file sha256 d2e5054bcc38b0367152832fd9d21bd50a9cd91db2a291bf5479ea875bdfd407.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Stirling%27s_approximation&oldid=1360453793@5b7a91f2e93f54a950126ac06be9d35bcf4d8095 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match: oldid 1360453793 sha1 5b7a91f2e93f54a950126ac06be9d35bcf4d8095, 34026 bytes). QUOTED (grep count > 0): the main asymptotic n! \sim \sqrt{2\pi n}\left(\frac{n}{e}\right)^n (grep \sqrt{2\pi n}=9, \left(\frac{n}{e}\right)=10); the full ratio-series 1 + \frac{1}{12n} + \frac{1}{288n^2} ‚àí \frac{139}{51840n^3} ‚àí \frac{571}{2488320n^4} + \frac{163879}{209018880n^5} ‚àí ‚ãØ (so a_1..a_5 quoted verbatim; grep 139=3, 51840=1, 571=1, 2488320=1, 163879=1); the log-series coefficient formula c_k = \tfrac{B_{2k}}{2k(2k-1)} (grep Bernoulli=3); Robbins' EXACT bracket \sqrt{2\pi n}(\frac{n}{e})^n e^{\frac{1}{12n+1}} < n! < \sqrt{2\pi n}(\frac{n}{e})^n e^{\frac{1}{12n}} (grep frac{1}{12n}=14, Robbins=2, cited "A Remark on Stirling's Formula", 1955); the names "Stirling's formula" (grep=15), "de Moivre" (grep=5), "Bernoulli number". DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the coefficient a_6=5246819/75246796800 (grep 5246819=0, 75246796800=0 ‚Äî the on-page series truncates at a_5 then "‚ãØ"); the Bernoulli recurrence route Œ£ C(m+1,k)B_k=0 as the computation (grep C(m+1=0, binom{m+1=0); the exp-of-series ‚Üí log-of-series inverse-consistency check; the Monte-Carlo Gamma-importance-sampling estimator of n! and every z-value (grep Monte=0, importance=0, gammavariate=0); the ‚àö(œÄn) factor-2-under-root foil and its predicted ratio 1/‚àö2=0.7071 (grep 1/sqrt(2)=0, 0.7071=0); the numeric Robbins sweep "0 violations across n=1..64" (the bracket is quoted; the high-precision numeric verification across the sweep is mine); SEED 20260717 (grep=0); and the results digest (grep sha256=0). Honest posture ‚Äî DISCLOSED SEAMS: the article is RICH (34026 bytes) ‚Äî the main asymptotic, the full ratio-series (a_1..a_5), the c_k=B_{2k}/(2k(2k‚àí1)) formula, and Robbins' bracket are all QUOTED verbatim, so nothing is oversold as a novel theorem; the firsthand contribution is the exact-Fraction backbone built end-to-end (Bernoulli recurrence ‚Üí c_k ‚Üí a_k) and cross-checked against its own inverse (which extends one coefficient, a_6, beyond the page), the Monte-Carlo Gamma-integral agreement, the high-precision Robbins sweep, the ‚àö(œÄn) falsification, and every z-value + the digest; the page states Robbins' bracket while the numeric verification that it holds with 0 violations across n=1..64 is mine; the ‚àö(œÄn)-prefactor foil is my construction (the page states the correct ‚àö(2œÄn) prefactor and never makes the error). Nothing oversold as novel: Stirling's approximation is textbook (de Moivre 1733; Stirling; Robbins 1955; the pinned page), cited as such.

## PROPOSAL 257 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T13:55:32Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 270, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/valiant-permutation-routing-2026-07-21.md
question: Establish the exact phase-1 backbone of Valiant's two-phase (random-intermediate) oblivious routing on the hypercube Q_n (N = 2^n nodes), a routing/load-balancing head outside the shipped max-load / derangement / power-of-two-choices / consistent-hashing set. Valiant routes each packet first to a UNIFORMLY RANDOM intermediate node d, then on to its true destination by left-to-right bit-fixing. Writing L = H(s, d) for the phase-1 route length (Hamming distance source‚Üíintermediate), is it exactly true that (1) L is distributed EXACTLY Binomial(n, 1/2) ‚Äî pmf p_k = C(n,k)/2^n ‚Äî so E[L] = n/2 and Var[L] = n/4, INDEPENDENT of the source s (because d is uniform on {0,1}^n and independent of s, s^d is uniform, so L = popcount(s^d) ~ Binomial(n,1/2) regardless of s; this source-invariance is exactly why the random intermediate defeats an adversarial permutation); (2) the probability generating function is G(x) = E[x^L] = ((1+x)/2)^n exactly (from Œ£_k C(n,k) x^k = (1+x)^n); and (3) the average congestion over the N¬∑n directed edges of Q_n is E[total edge-crossings]/(N¬∑n) = (N¬∑(n/2))/(N¬∑n) = 1/2 exactly ‚Äî with the whole rational backbone verified in fractions.Fraction (PGF identity + E[L]=n/2 via the pmf AND G'(1) + Var[L]=n/4 via the pmf AND G''(1)+G'(1)‚àíG'(1)¬≤ + pmf normalization/symmetry + congestion 1/2, checked=75, mismatches=0), a Monte-Carlo estimate of the phase-1 route length at n=12 over N_mc=400000 uniform pairs (s,d) giving mean_len=5.997945 vs E[L]=6.0 at z=‚àí0.749864, source-permutation invariance under the bit-reversal adversary at adv_z=0.79122, and n-invariance across n‚àà{8,10,12,14} (all |z|<3)? These exact expectations are the backbone of Valiant's O(n)=O(log N) w.h.p. delivery result (the Chernoff tail rests on E[L]=n/2). Pre-registered naive foil (the single most common confusion): "every packet crosses all n dimensions, so the route length = n = 12 and the edge congestion = 1" ‚Äî REJECTED on the same N=400000 sample at z_foil=‚àí2190.134211, |z_foil| ‚â´ Z_REJECT=50.0, while the same sample AGREES with the true mean at z=‚àí0.749864; secondary foil "route length = n/2 but each edge is traversed deterministically so congestion = 1.0 not 1/2" rejected at z=‚àí2190.134211.
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 270 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=fd0d970057a36cfb2f2ad3dfe200acd09d3ce303d8c1eb37e88f6d3330fa759f (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): the PGF identity Œ£_k C(n,k) x^k = (1+x)^n hence G(x)=((1+x)/2)^n holds at rational x‚àà{1,2,1/2,3,‚àí1/3} for n‚àà{1,2,3,8,12}, E[L]=n/2 via the pmf and via G'(1), Var[L]=n/4 via the pmf and via G''(1)+G'(1)‚àíG'(1)¬≤ (G''(1)=n(n‚àí1)/4), the pmf sums to 1 and is symmetric p_k=p_{n‚àík}, and the average directed-edge congestion (N¬∑(n/2))/(N¬∑n)=1/2 with N=2^n, checked=75, mismatches=0, z="exact"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N_mc=400000 uniform 12-bit pairs (s,d), route length L=popcount(s^d) an iid Binomial(12,1/2) draw, mean_len=5.997945 vs E[L]=6.0, plain iid SE=0.00274 (iid draws so the plain iid SE is honest ‚Äî no batch means/thinning; stated in the verifier docstring), z=‚àí0.749864 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0], derived edge-congestion estimate mean/n=0.499829 vs 1/2 at z_congestion=‚àí0.749864; G3 ROBUSTNESS/INVARIANCE (own direction): (a) SOURCE-PERMUTATION INVARIANCE ‚Äî sources taken from the bit-reversal permutation of the node index (an adversarial, non-uniform source ordering) with uniform-random intermediates give adv_mean=6.002165 at adv_z=0.79122<3, the adversary cannot inflate phase-1 length because the intermediate is uniform independent of the source; (b) n-INVARIANCE ‚Äî E[L]/n=1/2 (edge congestion 1/2) across n‚àà{8,10,12,14}, z=‚àí0.215486,‚àí0.112088,1.232978,2.686444, all |z|<3; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME MC sample as G2): the primary foil "route length = n = 12 (all dimensions crossed) / edge congestion = 1" is REJECTED at z_foil=‚àí2190.134211, |z_foil| ‚â´ Z_REJECT=50.0, while the same sample AGREES with the true mean at z=‚àí0.749864; secondary "congestion = 1.0 not 1/2" foil rejected at z=‚àí2190.134211; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand: ideas/fleet/verify_257_valiant_permutation_routing.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions, sys), file sha256 7e204af1e5ee84e8c89fccc975d6e3ffa3440571e5d049f16c5af3faf819c7d4.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=STC104&oldid=1317632376@9945529d84c498378e412e0d6dcd464fec499fd2 ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match: oldid 1317632376 sha1 9945529d84c498378e412e0d6dcd464fec499fd2, 9688 bytes). The STC104 is the INMOS/T9000-family packet router that implemented Valiant's two-phase randomized routing; the article names the scheme and cites Valiant 1982. QUOTED (grep count > 0): the scheme name "two-phase randomized routing" (grep=2); the mechanism "randomly chosen intermediate node" (=1) and "before routing it to the destination" (=1) ‚Äî verbatim "routing packets to a randomly chosen intermediate node, before routing it to the destination"; "random destination" (=1, the implementation of the random intermediate ‚Äî "prepend a header with a random destination"); the load-balancing effect "average worst case" (=1, "reduce all traffic to an average worst case with predictable latency and bandwidth"); "Valiant" (=2), the citation "A scheme for fast parallel communication" (=1, Valiant, SIAM J. Comput. 11(2), 1982), and "hypercube" (=1, a supported network topology). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the phase-1 route-length identity L=popcount(s^d) ~ Binomial(n,1/2) and its pmf C(n,k)/2^n (grep Binomial=0); E[L]=n/2, Var[L]=n/4 (grep n/2=0); the PGF G(x)=((1+x)/2)^n; the average directed-edge congestion=1/2 (grep congestion=0); the source-invariance and the bit-reversal-adversary check (grep Hamming=0); the O(log N)/O(n) delivery bound (grep O(log=0, O(n=0 ‚Äî attributed to the cited Valiant 1982 paper but NOT stated on this page); every z-value and both foils; SEED 20260717 (grep=0); the results digest (grep sha256=0). Honest posture ‚Äî DISCLOSED SEAMS: the pinned STC104 article is an ENGINEERING description ‚Äî it quotes the scheme name, the random-intermediate mechanism, Valiant's attribution, and the hypercube topology, but states NONE of the route-length distribution, the moments, the PGF, the congestion value, or the O(log N) bound (all grep-0), so the firsthand contribution is exactly the exact Binomial(n,1/2) phase-1 backbone (pmf, PGF, moments, congestion) verified end-to-end in fractions.Fraction + the MC agreement + the source-permutation/n-invariance legs + the two falsifications; in particular the O(log N) w.h.p. guarantee is Valiant's well-known result (attributed to the cited 1982 paper) but is not quoted on this page ‚Äî disclosed; the bit-reversal adversary, the n-invariance sweep, every z-value, both foils, SEED, and the digest are mine. Nothing oversold as novel: Valiant's two-phase randomized routing is textbook (Valiant 1982; Valiant‚ÄìBrebner 1981; the pinned STC104 page), cited as such.
depends: PROPOSAL 257 -> VERDICT 270 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî randomized parallel routing / Valiant two-phase (random-intermediate) oblivious routing on the hypercube Q_n: route each packet first to a uniformly random intermediate node d, then on to its destination by left-to-right bit-fixing, so the phase-1 route length L = H(s, d) ~ Binomial(n, 1/2) exactly (p_k = C(n,k)/2^n, E[L] = n/2, Var[L] = n/4, source-invariant), the PGF G(x) = ((1+x)/2)^n, and the average congestion over the N¬∑n directed edges = (N¬∑(n/2))/(N¬∑n) = 1/2; the whole rational backbone verified end-to-end in fractions.Fraction (PGF identity + both moments via the pmf AND G'(1)/G''(1) + pmf normalization/symmetry + congestion 1/2, checked=75, 0 mismatches), an MC estimate of the phase-1 route length agreeing with E[L]=6.0 at z=‚àí0.749864, source-permutation invariance under the bit-reversal adversary at adv_z=0.79122, and n-invariance across n‚àà{8,10,12,14} all |z|<3; used to refute the naive "route length = n = 12 / congestion = 1" confusion (rejected at |z|=2190.13 on the same N=400000 sample). Proposal high-water is NOT advanced by this slice (this is a PROPOSAL-only fleet slot; the union-max proposal/verdict high-water advance is the coordinator's call in a later isolated step). Explicit distinctness: valiant / random-intermediate / two-phase are grep-0 as heads across both repos (control/outbox.md, ideas/, sims/), and this is a route-length-distribution + edge-congestion + source-invariance object (NOT a max-load object), orthogonal to every shipped routing/load-balancing head ‚Äî balls-into-bins max-load P225, derangement / no-home-task P229, power-of-two-choices (two shipped heads), consistent-hashing. This is the exact Binomial(n,1/2) phase-1 route-length backbone + the ((1+x)/2)^n PGF + the 1/2 edge-congestion identity + the source-permutation invariance that underpins Valiant's O(log N) guarantee.

## VERDICT 270 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T14:08:27Z ¬∑ status: ruled APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 257 (idea-engine control/outbox.md, sim-ready; VERDICT 270, +13 offset) ‚Äî Valiant two-phase (random-intermediate) oblivious routing on the hypercube Q_n, phase-1 backbone; idea-engine merge f74988b (PR #888).
decision: APPROVE ‚Äî verified firsthand; every claim reproduced with zero discrepancy. Valiant routes each packet first to a UNIFORMLY RANDOM intermediate d, then on by left-to-right bit-fixing; the phase-1 route length L = H(s, d) = popcount(s^d) is EXACTLY Binomial(n, 1/2) ‚Äî pmf p_k = C(n,k)/2^n, E[L] = n/2, Var[L] = n/4, INDEPENDENT of the source s (d uniform and independent of s ‚áí s^d uniform), which is exactly why the random intermediate defeats an adversarial permutation; the PGF is G(x) = ((1+x)/2)^n and the average congestion over the N¬∑n directed edges of Q_n is (N¬∑(n/2))/(N¬∑n) = 1/2. Upheld by fully independent re-derivation, not a rubber-stamp of the verifier self-report.
adjudication:
- Reproduction: committed verifier ideas/fleet/verify_257_valiant_permutation_routing.py (file sha256 7e204af1e5ee84e8c89fccc975d6e3ffa3440571e5d049f16c5af3faf819c7d4, stdlib only; diff copy-vs-original exit 0, unmodified) reproduced results_sha256 = fd0d970057a36cfb2f2ad3dfe200acd09d3ce303d8c1eb37e88f6d3330fa759f character-for-character, deterministic THREE ways (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + a separate re-invocation, all byte-identical), SEED=20260717.
- G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): independently re-derived the backbone ‚Äî the PGF identity Œ£_k C(n,k) x^k = (1+x)^n hence G(x)=((1+x)/2)^n, E[L]=n/2 via the pmf AND via G'(1), Var[L]=n/4 via the pmf AND via G''(1)+G'(1)‚àíG'(1)¬≤ (G''(1)=n(n‚àí1)/4), pmf normalization Œ£ p_k = 1 and symmetry p_k = p_{n‚àík}, and the average directed-edge congestion (N¬∑(n/2))/(N¬∑n) = 1/2 with N = 2^n, all hold; checked=75, mismatches=0; z="exact".
- G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N_mc=400000 uniform 12-bit pairs (s, d), route length L = popcount(s^d) a genuinely iid Binomial(12, 1/2) draw (independent s, d = getrandbits(n)), mean_len=5.997945 vs E[L]=6.0, z=‚àí0.749864 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] PASS; the plain iid SE is honest ‚Äî reproduced SE=0.002739, not the autocorrelation-inflation mode; derived edge-congestion estimate 0.499829 vs 1/2 at the same z.
- G3 ROBUSTNESS/INVARIANCE (own direction): (a) SOURCE-PERMUTATION INVARIANCE ‚Äî sources drawn from the bit-reversal permutation (an adversarial, non-uniform source ordering) with uniform intermediates give adv_z=0.79122<3; the adversary cannot inflate phase-1 length because the intermediate is uniform independent of the source; (b) n-INVARIANCE ‚Äî E[L]/n = 1/2 (edge congestion 1/2) across n‚àà{8,10,12,14}, all |z|<3.
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME MC sample as G2): the pre-registered naive foil "route length = n = 12 (all dimensions crossed) / edge congestion = 1" is REJECTED at z_foil=‚àí2190.13, |z_foil| ‚â´ Z_REJECT=50.0, while the same sample AGREES with the true mean at z=‚àí0.749864; the secondary "congestion = 1.0 not 1/2" foil is rejected on the same sample. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS.
grounding: pins Wikipedia "STC104" oldid 1317632376 (raw-wikitext sha1 9945529d84c498378e412e0d6dcd464fec499fd2, 9688 bytes) ‚Äî re-verified firsthand, MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext, byte-and-sha1 exact. The STC104 is the INMOS/T9000-family packet router that implemented Valiant's two-phase randomized routing. QUOTED (literally present on the pinned page, grep count > 0): the scheme name "two-phase randomized routing"; the mechanism "routing packets to a randomly chosen intermediate node, before routing it to the destination"; "random destination" (the implementation of the random intermediate); the load-balancing effect "average worst case"; "Valiant" and the citation "A scheme for fast parallel communication" (Valiant, SIAM J. Comput. 11(2), 1982); and "hypercube". DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext, correctly labeled derived): the phase-1 route-length identity L = popcount(s^d) ~ Binomial(n,1/2) and its pmf C(n,k)/2^n, the moments E[L]=n/2 and Var[L]=n/4, the PGF G(x)=((1+x)/2)^n, the average directed-edge congestion = 1/2, the source-invariance and the bit-reversal-adversary check, the O(log N)/O(n) delivery bound, every z-value and both foils, SEED 20260717, and the results digest. DISCLOSED SEAM (disclosed, non-disqualifying, no fabricated pin): the pinned STC104 article is an engineering description that quotes the scheme name, the random-intermediate mechanism, Valiant's attribution, and the hypercube topology but states NONE of the route-length distribution, the moments, the PGF, the congestion value, or the O(log N) bound (all grep-0); in particular the O(log N) w.h.p. guarantee is Valiant's well-known result (attributed to the cited Valiant 1982 paper) but is not quoted on this page ‚Äî disclosed as a seam. No mislabels; no flags raised. Nothing oversold as novel: Valiant's two-phase randomized routing is textbook (Valiant 1982; Valiant‚ÄìBrebner 1981; the pinned STC104 page), cited as such.
reconciliation: the proposal-authored sim-lab reproduction mirror (sim-lab #355, merge 2e3b2a6, sims/verdict-270-valiant-permutation-routing/) is a byte-identical copy of the verifier (diff exit 0, file sha256 7e204af1e5ee84e8c89fccc975d6e3ffa3440571e5d049f16c5af3faf819c7d4) whose run-stdout reproduces the same digest fd0d970057a36cfb2f2ad3dfe200acd09d3ce303d8c1eb37e88f6d3330fa759f (re-run firsthand, digest reproduces); this ruling is built ON TOP of it ‚Äî the mirror is reproduction-only (no VERDICT block, no high-water advance, control/status.md untouched) and defers the canonical ruling to this slice; NO competing sim-lab PR opened. The idea-engine `## VERDICT 270` block is the canonical routed verdict record.
distinctness: distinct from every shipped routing/load-balancing head ‚Äî balls-into-bins max-load P225, derangement / no-home-task P229, power-of-two-choices, consistent-hashing; "valiant / random-intermediate / two-phase" is grep-0 as prior heads across both repos, and this is a route-length-distribution + edge-congestion + source-invariance object (NOT a max-load object).
insight preserved: routing each packet first to a UNIFORMLY RANDOM intermediate makes the phase-1 route length L = H(s, d) ~ Binomial(n, 1/2) exactly (pmf C(n,k)/2^n, E[L]=n/2, Var[L]=n/4) INDEPENDENT of the source ‚Äî the source-invariance is exactly what defeats an adversarial permutation; the PGF is G(x)=((1+x)/2)^n and the average congestion over the N¬∑n directed edges is exactly 1/2; the whole rational backbone is self-consistent end-to-end (PGF identity + both moments via the pmf AND G'(1)/G''(1) + normalization/symmetry + congestion 1/2, checked=75, 0 mismatches), an MC estimate of the phase-1 route length agrees with E[L]=6.0 at z=‚àí0.749864, source-permutation invariance holds under the bit-reversal adversary at adv_z=0.79122, and n-invariance holds across n‚àà{8,10,12,14}; the naive "route length = n / congestion = 1" confusion is refuted at |z|=2190.13 on the same N=400000 sample. These exact expectations are the backbone of Valiant's O(n)=O(log N) w.h.p. delivery result (the Chernoff tail rests on E[L]=n/2).
high-water: proposal high-water ADVANCES P256 -> P257 (union-max, no regress); verdict high-water ADVANCES V269 -> V270 (union-max, no regress).
decision: APPROVE.
üìä Model: Claude Opus family ¬∑ high effort ¬∑ idea-verdict adjudication.

## PROPOSAL 258 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T15:10:00Z ¬∑ status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 271, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/prophet-inequality-threshold-2026-07-21.md
question: Establish the single-item prophet inequality with an EXPLICIT single-threshold rule, an optimal-stopping / online-algorithms head over the fleet lane. For independent nonnegative random variables X_1..X_n revealed in order, define ALG_œÑ that accepts the first X_i ‚â• œÑ and stops (and, if none is accepted, takes the last variable X_n), with the threshold set to half the expected prophet value œÑ = E[max_i X_i]/2. Is it exactly true that (1) E[ALG_œÑ] ‚â• (1/2)¬∑E[max_i X_i] on every product of nonnegative distributions ‚Äî verified by exact Fraction product-support enumeration on six concrete instances, the guarantee holding on all six; (2) the constant 1/2 is OPTIMAL ‚Äî the tight family X_1=1 (sure), X_2=M w.p. 1/M gives ratio E[ALG_œÑ]/E[max] = M/(2M‚àí1) = 1/(2‚àí1/M), a decreasing sequence 2/3, 3/5, 4/7, 5/9 for M=2,3,4,5 converging DOWN to 1/2, so no online rule beats 1/2; and (3) a THRESHOLD is genuinely necessary ‚Äî on the teeth instance (X_1=1 sure, then X_2=10 sure) the threshold rule attains ratio 1 while the greedy take-first policy collapses to ratio 1/10? Headline generic instance (X_1‚àà{1,0} each w.p. 1/2, X_2‚àà{2,0} each w.p. 1/2): E[max]=5/4, œÑ=5/8, E[ALG_œÑ]=1, ratio 4/5. The whole rational backbone verified in fractions.Fraction (six-instance guarantee sweep + exact headline fractions 5/4‚Üí4/5, 7/4‚Üí4/7, teeth 10‚Üí1, take-first necessity 1/10, all mismatches=0), a seeded Monte-Carlo estimate of E[ALG_œÑ] on the generic instance at N=200000 giving mean 0.9969950000 vs the exact E[ALG_œÑ]=1 at z=‚àí1.903527, positive-scale invariance of the ratio under X‚ÜíŒªX for Œª‚àà{2,3,100,1/7} (exact 4/5), and the tight family monotone toward 1/2. Pre-registered naive foil (the single most common confusion): "the gambler matches the prophet" (E[ALG_œÑ] == E[max] = 1.25) ‚Äî REJECTED on the same N=200000 sample at z_foil=‚àí160.266876, |z_foil| > 6, while the same sample AGREES with the true E[ALG_œÑ]=1 at z=‚àí1.903527; the tight family confirms 1/2 cannot be improved (ratios monotone decreasing 2/3‚Üí5/9, strictly above 1/2).
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 271 reproduces SEED=20260721 results_sha256=1c235e4991e2775e0c5813966f6cec313c296580c83a27195b287d9e4e3068fb (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ‚Äî G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): exact product-support enumeration of E[max], E[ALG_œÑ], and the ratio for six instances (generic + tight M‚àà{2,3,4,5} + teeth), the guarantee E[ALG_œÑ] ‚â• E[max]/2 holds on all six; the headline fractions assert exactly ‚Äî generic (E[max]=5/4, E[ALG_œÑ]=1, ratio 4/5), tight M=4 (7/4, 1, 4/7), teeth (10, 10, 1) ‚Äî and threshold-necessity take-first ratio = 1/10 < 1/2 on the teeth instance, z="exact"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N=200000 seeded iid draws of the generic instance (random.Random(SEED), all variables sampled in order) run through the same ALG_œÑ, mean 0.9969950000 vs exact E[ALG_œÑ]=1, plain iid SE=0.0015786481, z=‚àí1.903527 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0], iid draws so the plain iid SE is honest (no batch means/thinning; stated in the verifier docstring); G3 INVARIANCE (positive scale, own direction, EXACT): the ratio E[ALG_œÑ]/E[max] is invariant under X‚ÜíŒªX for Œª‚àà{2,3,100,1/7}, staying exactly 4/5 on the generic instance via Fraction (œÑ scales with the values, acceptance decisions unchanged), z="exact"; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME MC sample as G2): the "gambler matches the prophet" foil (E[ALG_œÑ] == E[max] = 1.25) is REJECTED at z_foil=‚àí160.266876, |z_foil| > 6, while the same sample AGREES with the true E[ALG_œÑ]=1 at z=‚àí1.903527; corroboration ‚Äî the tight family ratios 2/3, 3/5, 4/7, 5/9 are monotone decreasing toward 1/2 and strictly above 1/2 (r_5 < r_2, both > 1/2); all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand: ideas/fleet/verify_258_prophet_inequality_threshold.py (stdlib only: fractions, hashlib, itertools, json, math, random, sys), file sha256 e3a0f0a271920b0ac91c062a26469515c2f2edefac5dbdebfbf1ca37bbecf1c9.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Prophet_inequality&oldid=1329732914@2630c796b06718744230a4e60e007f1bda1d9edb ¬∑ fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ‚Äî exact match: oldid 1329732914 sha1 2630c796b06718744230a4e60e007f1bda1d9edb, 8483 bytes; the article exists and is not a redirect). QUOTED (grep count > 0, both directions): the name "prophet inequality" (grep=6) and the framing "optimal stopping" (grep=3, incl. the short description "Bound on optimal stopping in random sequences"); the 1/2 guarantee ‚Äî verbatim "an online algorithm ‚Ä¶ whose expected value is at least half that of the prophet: \tfrac12 E[max_i X_i]" (half=1, \tfrac12=4); the single-threshold rule at œÑ=E[max]/2 ‚Äî verbatim "A different threshold, œÑ=\tfrac12 E[max_i X_i], also achieves at least this same expected value" and the general "threshold algorithm that sets a parameter œÑ" (threshold=5, the median-threshold p=1/2 construction also on-page); optimality ‚Äî "No algorithm can achieve a greater expected value for all distributions of inputs" (prose) with the "tight" form (grep=1) credited to Garling; the originators "Krengel" (=2), "Sucheston" (=2), "Garling" (=1), Krengel‚ÄìSucheston 1978. DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext, both directions): the literal string "1/2" (0), "one-half" (0), the exact phrases "best possible" (0) / "cannot be improved" (0); the six enumerated instances and every rational in the reference table ‚Äî 5/4, 4/5, 7/4, 4/7, 1/10 (all grep 0); the numeric application of œÑ=E[max]/2 to each instance; the greedy take-first foil and its 1/10 ratio (take-first=0, greedy=0); the seeded Monte-Carlo estimator and every z-value (Monte=0); the fractions.Fraction enumeration (Fraction=0); the positive-scale-invariance leg; SEED 20260721 (0); the results digest (sha256=0). Honest posture ‚Äî DISCLOSED SEAMS: the pinned article is RICH for this head ‚Äî the single-threshold rule œÑ=\tfrac12 E[max_i X_i], the 1/2 guarantee, and its optimality ("No algorithm can achieve a greater expected value") are all QUOTED verbatim, so nothing is oversold as a novel theorem; the prophet inequality is textbook (Krengel‚ÄìSucheston 1978; Garling's tight form; the pinned page), cited as such. The firsthand contribution is the verification apparatus ‚Äî the exact Fraction enumeration of E[max], E[ALG_œÑ], and the ratio on six concrete instances (with the "take the last variable if none accepted" tie-break made explicit and computed), the tight family exhibiting the monotone approach to 1/2, the teeth instance quantifying threshold-necessity (take-first=1/10), the seeded Monte-Carlo agreement, the positive-scale-invariance leg, the "gambler matches the prophet" falsification, and every z-value + SEED + the digest. Convention note: the page states the guarantee for the accept/stop process with value 0 if nothing is accepted; the verifier's tie-break "take the last variable X_n if none reaches œÑ" only ever helps the algorithm (adds value, never subtracts), so the ‚â• 1/2 guarantee it checks is at least as strong as the on-page statement ‚Äî disclosed.
depends: PROPOSAL 258 -> VERDICT 271 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ‚Äî optimal stopping / online algorithms / the single-item prophet inequality with an explicit single threshold: for independent nonnegative X_1..X_n revealed in order, the rule ALG_œÑ that accepts the first X_i ‚â• œÑ (else takes the last X_n) with œÑ = E[max_i X_i]/2 satisfies E[ALG_œÑ] ‚â• (1/2)¬∑E[max_i X_i], and 1/2 is optimal ‚Äî the tight family X_1=1, X_2=M w.p. 1/M gives ratio 1/(2‚àí1/M) ‚Üí 1/2 (2/3, 3/5, 4/7, 5/9), while greedy take-first collapses to 1/10 on the teeth instance (1 then 10); the whole rational backbone verified end-to-end in fractions.Fraction (six-instance guarantee sweep + exact headline fractions + take-first necessity 1/10, mismatches=0), a seeded MC estimate of E[ALG_œÑ] agreeing with the exact value 1 at z=‚àí1.903527 on N=200000, positive-scale invariance of the ratio (exact 4/5 under Œª‚àà{2,3,100,1/7}), and the tight family monotone toward 1/2; used to refute the naive "gambler matches the prophet" confusion (predicted E[ALG_œÑ]=E[max]=1.25, rejected at |z_foil|=160.27 on the same N=200000 sample). Explicit distinctness: prophet / threshold-stopping / E[max]/2 are grep-0 as heads across both repos (control/outbox.md, ideas/, sims/), and this is an optimal-stopping / online-algorithms guarantee object (a competitive-ratio-in-expectation head), orthogonal to every shipped head ‚Äî the nearest neighbours are the secretary-flavoured / online-selection lanes and the routing/load-balancing heads (Valiant P257, balls-into-bins P225, power-of-two-choices), none of which is the single-threshold 1/2 prophet guarantee. This is the exact E[ALG_œÑ] ‚â• (1/2)E[max] single-threshold backbone + the tight-family optimality of 1/2 + the take-first threshold-necessity (1/10) that underpins the classical prophet inequality. Proposal high-water ADVANCES P257 -> P258 (union-max, no regress); verdict high-water unchanged at V270 (this slice authors NO verdict).
üìä Model: Claude Opus family ¬∑ high effort ¬∑ idea-proposal authoring.

## VERDICT 271 ¬∑ 2026-07-21T14:38:38Z ¬∑ status: ruled APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 258 (idea-engine control/outbox.md, sim-ready; VERDICT 271, +13 offset) ‚Äî single-item prophet inequality with an EXPLICIT single-threshold rule at œÑ = E[max_i X_i]/2, an optimal-stopping / online-algorithms head over the fleet lane.
decision: APPROVE ‚Äî verified firsthand; every claim reproduced with zero discrepancy. For independent nonnegative X_1..X_n revealed in order, the rule ALG_œÑ that accepts the first X_i ‚â• œÑ and stops (and, if none is accepted, takes the last variable X_n), with œÑ = E[max_i X_i]/2, satisfies E[ALG_œÑ] ‚â• (1/2)¬∑E[max_i X_i] on every product of nonnegative distributions; the constant 1/2 is OPTIMAL ‚Äî witnessed as a LIMIT (not overclaimed as an equality) by the tight family X_1=1 (sure), X_2=M w.p. 1/M giving ratio E[ALG_œÑ]/E[max] = M/(2M‚àí1) = 1/(2‚àí1/M), a decreasing sequence 2/3, 3/5, 4/7, 5/9 for M=2,3,4,5 strictly above 1/2 and converging DOWN to 1/2; and a THRESHOLD is genuinely necessary ‚Äî on the teeth instance (X_1=1 sure, then X_2=10 sure) the threshold rule attains ratio 1 while the greedy take-first policy collapses to ratio 1/10. Upheld by fully independent re-derivation, not a rubber-stamp of the verifier self-report.
adjudication:
- Reproduction: committed verifier ideas/fleet/verify_258_prophet_inequality_threshold.py (file sha256 e3a0f0a271920b0ac91c062a26469515c2f2edefac5dbdebfbf1ca37bbecf1c9, stdlib only; diff copy-vs-original exit 0, unmodified) reproduced results_sha256 = 1c235e4991e2775e0c5813966f6cec313c296580c83a27195b287d9e4e3068fb character-for-character, deterministic THREE ways (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + a separate fresh re-invocation, all byte-identical), SEED=20260721.
- G1 EXACT (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): independently re-derived the backbone ‚Äî exact product-support enumeration of E[max], E[ALG_œÑ], and the ratio on six concrete instances (generic + tight M‚àà{2,3,4,5} + teeth), the ‚â•1/2 guarantee E[ALG_œÑ] ‚â• E[max]/2 checked as an INEQUALITY and holding on all six; the headline fractions assert exactly ‚Äî generic instance (X_1‚àà{1,0}, X_2‚àà{2,0} each w.p. 1/2) E[max]=5/4, E[ALG_œÑ]=1, ratio 4/5, tight M=4 (7/4, 1, 4/7), teeth (10, 10, 1); the tight family ratio M/(2M‚àí1)‚àà{2/3,3/5,4/7,5/9} is strictly above 1/2 and ‚Üí1/2, witnessing optimality as a LIMIT not an equality; teeth ratio 1; threshold-necessity take-first ratio = 1/10 < 1/2 on the teeth instance; optimality-universality is grounded on the on-page textbook statement, not a novel code-proof (disclosed seam); mismatches=0, z="exact".
- G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N=200000 seeded iid draws of the generic instance (random.Random(SEED), all variables sampled in order) run through the same ALG_œÑ, mean 0.9969950000 vs exact E[ALG_œÑ]=1, plain iid SE=0.0015786481, z=‚àí1.903527 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] PASS; the iid draws make the plain binomial SE honest ‚Äî no autocorrelation inflation, no batch means/thinning (stated in the verifier docstring).
- G3 INVARIANCE (positive scale, own direction, EXACT): the ratio E[ALG_œÑ]/E[max] is invariant under X‚ÜíŒªX for Œª‚àà{2,3,100,1/7}, staying exactly 4/5 on the generic instance via fractions.Fraction (œÑ scales with the values, acceptance decisions unchanged), z="exact".
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME MC sample as G2): the pre-registered naive foil "the gambler matches the prophet" (E[ALG_œÑ] == E[max] = 1.25) is REJECTED at z_foil=‚àí160.266876, |z_foil| > 6, while the same sample AGREES with the true E[ALG_œÑ]=1 at z=‚àí1.903527 ‚Äî the teeth confirmed; corroboration ‚Äî the tight family ratios 2/3, 3/5, 4/7, 5/9 are monotone decreasing toward 1/2 and strictly above 1/2. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS.
grounding: pins Wikipedia "Prophet inequality" oldid 1329732914 (raw-wikitext sha1 2630c796b06718744230a4e60e007f1bda1d9edb, 8483 bytes) ‚Äî re-verified firsthand, MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext, byte-and-sha1 exact character-for-character; the article exists and is not a redirect. QUOTED (literally present on the pinned page, grep count > 0, both directions): the name "prophet inequality" and the framing "optimal stopping"; the 1/2 guarantee ‚Äî verbatim "expected value is at least half that of the prophet: ¬ΩE[max_i X_i]"; the single-threshold rule at œÑ=¬ΩE[max] ‚Äî verbatim "A different threshold, œÑ=¬ΩE[max_i X_i], also achieves at least this same expected value" and the general "threshold algorithm that sets a parameter œÑ"; optimality ‚Äî "No algorithm can achieve a greater expected value for all distributions of inputs" with the "tight" form credited to Garling; the originators "Krengel", "Sucheston", "Garling" (Krengel‚ÄìSucheston 1978). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext, correctly not claimed on-page): the numeric backbone ‚Äî the literal "1/2" as a decimal, the reference fractions 5/4, 4/5, 7/4, 4/7 and the tight-family fractions, the greedy take-first foil and its 1/10 ratio, the seeded Monte-Carlo estimator and every z-value, the fractions.Fraction enumeration, the positive-scale-invariance leg, SEED 20260721, and the results digest (sha256 grep-0). DISCLOSED SEAM (disclosed, non-disqualifying, no fabricated pin): the pinned article is RICH for this head ‚Äî the single-threshold rule œÑ=¬ΩE[max_i X_i], the 1/2 guarantee, and its optimality ("No algorithm can achieve a greater expected value") are all QUOTED verbatim, so optimality-universality rests on the on-page textbook statement rather than a novel code-proof, and nothing is oversold as a novel theorem; the verifier's tie-break "take the last variable X_n if none reaches œÑ" only ever helps the algorithm (adds value, never subtracts), so the ‚â•1/2 guarantee it checks is at least as strong as the on-page accept/stop statement (value 0 if nothing accepted) ‚Äî disclosed. No mislabels; no flags raised. The firsthand contribution is the verification apparatus over textbook results ‚Äî the exact Fraction enumeration on six instances, the tight family exhibiting the monotone approach to 1/2, the teeth instance quantifying threshold-necessity (take-first=1/10), the seeded Monte-Carlo agreement, the positive-scale-invariance leg, the "gambler matches the prophet" falsification, and every z-value + SEED + the digest. Nothing oversold as novel: the prophet inequality is textbook (Krengel‚ÄìSucheston 1978; Garling's tight form; the pinned page), cited as such.
